# Deferred-Anchor Registry

> The canonical source of truth for every `D-*` anchor cited in `src/` but
> deferred to a future cycle. The leading underscore sorts this file before
> the numbered plans in directory listings тАФ call this out as the registry,
> not a plan. Per-plan ┬з3.1 rows are still the preferred home for anchors
> whose feature area maps cleanly onto a plan; this file catches the orphans
> + cross-cutting anchors that don't belong to any single plan.
>
> **CI/pre-commit guard** (`tools/check-anchor-registry.{sh,ps1}`): every
> `D-*` identifier appearing in `src/` MUST resolve to a row in this file
> OR a citation in any `.plans/*.md`. Pre-fix this recurred TWICE
> (memory: "the same gap silent-failure caught for MULTI-PAGE-GOT").
> The guard makes the system enforce the discipline тАФ same shape as the
> agnosticism veto + the `CallConv` static_assert.
>
> **V2-4.X / full-C ownership (2026-06-10):** the C-language-feature deferral
> CLUSTER тАФ modulo codegen (`D-CSUBSET-MOD-OP-CODEGEN`, тАж), primitive widths
> (`D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH`), linkage/visibility syntax
> (`D-CSUBSET-LINKAGE-VISIBILITY-SYNTAX`, `D-DECL-PREFIX-STRIP-SHARED-HELPER`),
> rodata aggregates (`D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-NONSTRING-ARRAY`), non-main implicit
> return (`D-LK10-ENTRY-MAIN-IMPLICIT-RETURN` тАФ the OPEN non-main successor row,
> cross-ref `D-CSUBSET-ENTRY-NAME-SPLIT`), variadic FFI
> (`D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE`), the apple-arm64 ABI divergence
> (`D-FF3-APPLE-ARM64-ABI-DIVERGENCE`), and peers тАФ is now OWNED by **stepper
> V2-4.X / [`23-full-c-plan`](./23-full-c-plan%20-%20tbd.md)** (c-subset тЖТ full
> C23). Each closes inside the relevant FC phase (see the plan ┬з3.1).
>
> **NOT in this cluster тАФ already shipped, do NOT re-claim as new FC work**
> (caught by the 2026-06-10 plan-lock accuracy review). Two genuinely-CLOSED
> anchors: `D-CSUBSET-DIVISION-OP-CODEGEN` (тЬЕ 2026-06-04 тАФ signed division shipped;
> the modulo successor `D-CSUBSET-MOD-OP-CODEGEN` тЬЕ since closed too тАФ FC1 cycle 1, 2026-06-10, above) and `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH`
> (тЬЕ 2026-06-08 тАФ direct-plt/indirect-slot dispatch shipped; the OPEN variadic concern
> is `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE`, above). PLUS one closed FEATURE with no
> anchor row: the `main` implicit-return-0 (shipped 2026-06-02, live in
> `c-subset.lang.json`, config-only) тАФ the anchor `D-LK10-ENTRY-MAIN-IMPLICIT-RETURN`
> itself remains the still-OPEN non-main successor (above), NOT a closed item. The
> full-C arc supersedes each predecessor with its named successor, never by reopening it.
>
> New full-C exotic-tail exclusions land as `D-FULLC-*` rows as clusters ship.
> Individual rows are NOT rewritten here yet (the plan is the live map until a
> cycle actually closes the anchor).
>
> **Schema (one row per anchor)**:
> ```
> | Anchor | Trigger | Closing work | Cross-refs |
> ```
>
> When an anchor closes, replace its row's status with `тЬЕ CLOSED <date>`
> and cite the commit/SymbolId. Don't delete the row тАФ the audit trail is
> load-bearing for future regressions.
>
> **Authorization log тАФ robustness sweep (2026-06-24, branch `feature/0-0-2-p14`):**
> the user authorized a "robustness sweep," which FIRED the trigger for three
> trigger-gated anchors and CLOSED them in one sweep (393/393 ctest, MSVC
> Release + MinGW): `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE`,
> `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE`, and
> `D-SEMANTIC-ASSIGN-STMT-ASSIGNABILITY-BYPASS` (see their rows below). The
> sweep surfaced one new backlog row, `D-CSUBSET-TENTATIVE-DEFINITION-MERGE`
> (C 6.9.2 tentative definitions тАФ still fail-loud, trigger-gated). [That backlog
> row was later тЬЕ CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c33) when the sqlite3.c
> re-probe fired its trigger тАФ see its row below.]

## Anchor Index

|---|---|---|---|
| `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD` | тЬЕ **GUARD DELIVERED 2026-06-20 (FC13 hardening cycle); CAP RAISED 16тЖТ256 on 2026-06-20 (FC13 cycle 2) once both cap-raise blockers (`D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK` + `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`) closed тАФ the cap is now a real SEMANTIC limit, not a host-stack artifact (parse is O(N) + the frontend's deep recursion [CU build + `analyze`] runs on a 64 MiB worker stack via `core/substrate/large_stack_call`). The "lowered to 16" note below is HISTORICAL.** The Pratt-walker expression-depth guard (`ParserConfig::maxExpressionDepth`, enforced at the single chokepoint `parseExpressionAt` in `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp`) was a `fatal()`/`std::abort()` on overflow, and its cap (256) was ~10x too high for the host C++ stack: the heaviest path (the paren atom re-entry `parsePrimary->parseUntilFrameDepth->stepOnce->walkExpression->parseExpressionAt`) overflows a Windows ~1 MB Debug stack at depth ~25 тАФ BEFORE the guard could fire тАФ so deeply-nested input RAW-crashed (rc 127) or hung. **DELIVERED:** (1) the `fatal()` is now a POSITIONED `P_ExpressionTooDeep` (0x0018) at the offending token + graceful RECOVERY (`recoverExpressionTooDeep_`: emit-once + Error leaf via `panicRecover` + unwind), never an abort/overflow; (2) the default cap lowered 256тЖТ16 so the guard fires with comfortable margin below the Debug paren overflow (~25) while still admitting realistic hand-written C nesting (тЙд15 deep). STEP-1 empirical finding: (B) on-path (NOT a bypass) тАФ `expressionDepth` DOES grow per nesting level; the cap was simply unreachable before the stack blew. **CAP-RAISE follow-up тЖТ `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`:** EXPLORED this cycle and found INSUFFICIENT тАФ a parser-only large-stack worker thread does NOT enable a higher cap, because stage markers localized the ~25-level overflow DOWNSTREAM of the parser (semantic `analyze` / HIR-MIR lowering recurse over the expr tree on the host's MAIN ~1 MB stack); raising the cap needs the downstream stages deep-safe (`D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`) AND the speculative cost bounded (`D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`). **SECOND follow-up (distinct, pre-existing) тАФ c-subset speculative-operand O(N┬▓):** the depth guard eliminates the CRASH, but on PATHOLOGICALLY deep parens the c-subset full parse is SLOW (CLI @ cap=16: depth 100 ~2s, 150-200 ~12s, 250+ >25s) because the `operand` rule is `speculative:true` (compoundLiteral/cast/paren disambiguation, lookahead 64 = 1024-token probe budget): each of the cap-many paren levels re-probes the deep tail, and nested speculation (`maxSpeculationDepth=8`) rollback-re-parses it. This is INDEPENDENT of the cap (cap=8 still times out @ depth 1000) and of THIS fix (pre-fix the deep parens crashed in Pratt recursion before the speculation finished; the fix EXPOSES the speculation cost, it does not cause it). A NON-speculative grammar (`kParenSchema`) parses 1000 parens in ~10ms тАФ the headline unit test pins exactly that (the GUARD mechanism, isolated from c-subset speculation). The fix is a speculation-engine / grammar concern (memoize probe results, or bound speculation by expression depth), NOT the depth guard. Note: only manifests on ALREADY-INVALID input (depth > cap); valid C nests тЙд4 in the entire corpus. Tests: `PrattWalker.{ExceedingMaxExpressionDepthDiagnosesAndRecovers (lowered-cap structural pin), DeeplyNestedParensDiagnoseAtDefaultCapWithoutCrashing (1000 parens @ default cap тЖТ positioned diag, no rc-127), DeepPrefixChainDiagnosesAndRecovers, DeepTernaryChainDiagnosesAndRecovers}` (test_pratt_walker.cpp) PLUS the c-subset end-to-end golden pin `tests/corpus/diagnostics/c-subset/expression_too_deeply_nested.c` (real frontend тЖТ `P_ExpressionTooDeep 1:41-1:42` golden, the exact code+position at the 17th paren where cap=16 trips). | (guard delivered; cap-raise тЖТ the 2 dedicated anchors) | plan 05 (PA2 walker); `D-PP-FATAL-HALTS-PARSE` (sibling); `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK` + `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC` |
| `D-PP-FATAL-HALTS-PARSE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-20 (FC13 hardening cycle).** The compilation-unit PP branch (`compilation_unit.cpp` `parseAndAdd_`) parsed UNCONDITIONALLY even when a FATAL preprocessor backstop had TRUNCATED the synthesized stream (the >256 macro-expansion-nesting guard or the include-nesting guard), feeding the truncated tokens to the parser тАФ producing an inscrutable secondary cascade / hang on top of the real PP cause. **CLOSED:** added `PreprocessResult::fatal` (set ONLY by the two stream-TRUNCATING backstops: `MacroExpander::truncated_` at the >256 guard, `SynthBuilder::fatal` ref-threaded through the recursive include builders at the include-depth guard); the CU gate now keys on `pp.fatal` and, when true, feeds the parser an EOF-ONLY stream тАФ yielding a minimal well-formed tree that STILL carries the (remapped) PP diagnostics, halting the parse before the cascade. Crucially the gate is NARROW: a RECOVERABLE PP error (missing `#include` file, malformed directive, redefinition) or a folded LEXER error leaves the stream INTACT and the parser STILL runs (an earlier `diagnostics->hasErrors()` gate was too broad тАФ it swallowed the rest of a file on an unresolved `#include`; caught by `HirLoweringCSubset.IncludeDirectiveIsSkippedNotFailed` + the FFI ingest tests). Tests: `CompilationUnitPPGate.{FatalMacroNestingTruncationGatesParser, OrdinaryMacroCompileIsNotGated, UnresolvedIncludeStillParsesRestOfFile}` (test_compilation_unit.cpp). | (closed) | `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD` (sibling); plan 05; FC13 |
| `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-20 (FC13 cycle 2 тАФ deep-expression-nesting end-to-end).** Built the standard compiler facility for deep recursion: `src/core/substrate/large_stack_call.{hpp,cpp}` тАФ `runOnLargeStack(stackBytes, fn)` runs `fn` on a dedicated worker thread with a `stackBytes`-RESERVE stack, JOINs (synchronous тАФ no concurrency), and re-throws any exception `fn` threw (captured into `std::exception_ptr` on the worker, rethrown on the joiner; never escapes the entry тЖТ no `std::terminate`); value-returning convenience `callOnLargeStack`; `kDeepRecursionStackBytes = 64 MiB` (cap 256 ├Ч ~40 KB/level тЙИ 10 MB, lazily-committed reserve costs ~nothing). FAIL-LOUD (`std::abort` + stderr) on every thread-API failure AND caller bug (empty `fn`, `stackBytes==0`, Windows `stackBytes > UINT_MAX` тАФ reject, never truncate the 32-bit `_beginthreadex` arg). Windows arm = `_beginthreadex` + `STACK_SIZE_PARAM_IS_A_RESERVATION` + `WaitForSingleObject`/`CloseHandle`; POSIX arm = `pthread_attr_setstacksize`/`pthread_create`/`pthread_join` (CI-verified тАФ no `find_package(Threads)`, raw symbols resolve as `std::thread`'s do). AGNOSTIC: pure host/OS utility, names no language/CPU/format; the `_WIN32`/POSIX `#ifdef` is host-portability. Self-test `tests/core/substrate/test_large_stack_call.cpp` (RunsTheCallable; RethrowsEscapedException value-preserving; ProvidesStackForDeepRecursion = ~1000 frames ├Ч `volatile char[8192]` тЙИ 8 MB that the default stack can't hold). **KEY EMPIRICAL CORRECTION of the localization below:** wrapping JUST `analyze` was INSUFFICIENT тАФ stage markers in the full c-subsetтЖТPE-exec pipeline showed the analyze-wrap lifted the DOWNSTREAM ceiling well past 25 (n=100..180 compile + run clean), but at the raised cap (256) a NEW overflow appeared UPSTREAM: the **CU build (parse / preprocess / type-name oracle)** in `Program::compileFiles`/`compileUnits` (`UnitBuilder::addFile`тЖТ`finish`) recurses building the expression tree and overflows the host main stack at ~n=185 (Debug-MSVC) тАФ the low cap (16) had MASKED it (the guard fired at 16, no deep tree was ever built). So the CU build, `analyze`, AND the BUILD-half `buildCuMir` (HIR + MIR) are ALL wrapped on the 64 MiB stack: `analyze` via a file-static `analyzeImpl` + a thin public `analyze` (`callOnLargeStack(kDeepRecursionStackBytes, тАж)`); the two CU-build sites in `program.cpp` via `callOnLargeStack` returning the `CompilationUnit`. The BUILD half `buildCuMir` (= analyze + CST->HIR + HIR->MIR) is wrapped via a file-static `buildCuMirImpl` + a thin `buildCuMir` (`compile_pipeline.cpp`). **THIRD incompleteness, independent-audit-caught (this cycle's dss-audit):** wrapping JUST parse + analyze was STILL insufficient тАФ CST->HIR (`cst_to_hir.cpp` `lowerExpr`/`lowerOperand`) and HIR->MIR (`hir_to_mir.cpp` `lowerExpr`) ALSO recurse one host-stack frame per nesting level, running inline on the MAIN thread inside `buildCuMir` AFTER `analyze`'s worker has joined; so a valid 41..256-deep expression that parsed + analyzed could still overflow downstream (it HAPPENED to fit at n<=250 on Debug-MSVC, but that is host/frame-size luck, NOT a guarantee тАФ exactly the uncontrolled-margin the wrap removes). FIXED by the `buildCuMir` wrap (analyze nests as a benign inner worker; peak live-deep stack ~10 MB << 64 MiB). Only the LOWER half (MIR->LIR->codegen->link, `lowerMirModuleToAssembly`) genuinely needs no wrap тАФ it iterates a FLAT SSA arena (`mir.hpp`), NOT a tree, so it is non-recursive in nesting depth (audit-confirmed). Cap raised 16тЖТ**256** (`parser.hpp`); now a real SEMANTIC limit (parse O(N) via `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`'s LL(k) prune + analyze/parse on the big stack), so deep-but-legal C compiles end-to-end and only pathological nesting past 256 trips the positioned `P_ExpressionTooDeep` guard. **Verified:** n=100/200/250 c-subset compile (rc 0, ~0.2s) + run (exit 0); n=300 fails LOUD fast (`P0018`, no hang/crash). Test harnesses that build deep CUs unwrapped were also routed onto the facility (the corpus harness `test_diagnostic_corpus.cpp` for the >256-paren fixture; the headline `PrattWalker.DeeplyNestedParensDiagnoseAtDefaultCapWithoutCrashing` 1000-paren pin at the raised default cap) and the duplicate local `runOnLargeStack` in `test_parser_speculation.cpp` was CONSOLIDATED onto the substrate facility (Win32 `CreateThread` trampoline deleted). Re-added + DEEPENED the runnable witness `examples/c-subset/deeply_nested_expression` (200 nested parens тЖТ exit 7; PE-windows + ELF-linux) тАФ a VALID deep expression that compiles AND runs through the WHOLE BUILD+LOWER pipeline at 200 nesting levels (closes the audit's M2: the prior 40-paren example + the guard-tripping fixtures never lowered a valid deep tree past HIR). Corpus fixture `expression_too_deeply_nested.c` regenerated to 300 parens (golden `P_ExpressionTooDeep 1:281-1:282` + `P_UnexpectedToken 1:582-1:583`). 335 ctest green. тАФ *Original localization (audit trail):* Frontend stages DOWNSTREAM of the parser (semantic `analyze`, HIR/MIR lowering) recurse over the expression tree on the host's MAIN ~1 MB stack (Windows) and OVERFLOW at ~25 nested levels (rc-127, NO diagnostic), independent of the parser (which handles deeper). This is what caps `ParserConfig::maxExpressionDepth` at 16. Localized empirically via stage markers in `program.cpp` `runCusToTargets` (parse completes; the crash is inside `compileOneTarget`тЖТ`analyze`). A parser-only large-stack worker thread was EXPLORED this cycle and does NOT help (the crash is downstream). [SUPERSEDED: the crash was ALSO upstream in the CU build once the cap allowed a deep tree тАФ see the close note above.] | (closed тАФ `large_stack_call` substrate; CU build + `analyze` + BUILD-half `buildCuMir` [HIR/MIR] on a 64 MiB worker stack; cap 16тЖТ256) | `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD`; `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`; `parser.hpp` maxExpressionDepth; `core/substrate/large_stack_call.hpp` |
| `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-20 тАФ fixed by a config-driven LL(k) predictive prune (the `Correct direction (b)` below, IMPLEMENTED): `computePredictivePrefixes` (grammar_schema_json.cpp) precomputes a per-rule FIRST_k `predictivePrefix` at schema-load (the leading single-token run + the first variable element's FIRST set, STOPPING before a NULLABLE stop element so each recorded offset is an EXACT admissible-token set тЗТ a SOUND over-approximation); `predictivePrefixPrunes` (parser.cpp) prunes a speculative candidate when an OBSERVED next-token is outside its prefix; a lone unguarded survivor DESCENDS DIRECTLY (no probe). `((` тЖТ castExpr/compoundLiteral pruned (token[+1]=`(` тИЙ FIRST(castTypeRef)) тЗТ O(N); n=300 154sтЖТ0.17s (~1100├Ч); the lone-`(Identifier)` ambiguity still speculates (binder triage unchanged). Agnostic (walks FIRST sets + the position table, names NO token) + config-driven (FIRST + the rule's `lookahead`); independent review-folded тАФ nullable-stop soundness guard + contextual-keyword precondition тЖТ `D-PARSE-PREDICTIVE-PRUNE-CONTEXTUAL-KEYWORD` + synthetic RED-on-disable tests (`ParserSpeculation.{DeepNestCastVsParenIsLinear, PrunesFirstBranchAtOffsetOneAndDirectDescends, NullableStopElementDoesNotOverPrune}`); 333 ctest. A RESIDUAL mild wall-clock super-linearity in the deep parse itself was CHARACTERIZED 2026-06-22 and promoted to its own row `D-PARSE-DEEP-NEST-RECURSION-MEMORY` тАФ a controlled flat-chain-vs-nested experiment PROVED the parse WORK is O(N) (the "TreeBuilder/arena bookkeeping" guess first sketched here is REFUTED: a flat `0+0+тАж+0` chain of equal node count parses with FLAT per-element cost), so the residual [measured ~exp 1.7, not the ~exp 1.2 first estimated] is a constant-factor cost of the N-deep HOST recursion (inferred BY ELIMINATION to be memory-hierarchy behavior тАФ not directly cache-instrumented), bounded/moot under the 256 depth cap тАФ LOW-priority / trigger-gated. тАФ Original characterization (audit trail):** The c-subset `operand` rule is `speculative:true` (compoundLiteral/cast/paren cast-vs-paren disambiguation; lookahead 64 = 1024-token probe budget). Deeply-nested input parses SUPER-LINEARLY (O(N┬▓)+) in depth тАФ measured @ cap=16: depth 20 ~0s, 100 ~3s, 300 TIMES OUT (>40s) тАФ so deep input HANGS before the depth guard can fail loud, even at a LOW cap (the speculation descends the deep tail at the early levels, before the guard depth is reached). INDEPENDENT of the cap (repro @ 16/64/256) and of the guard (a non-speculative `kParenSchema` parses 1000 parens in ~10ms). FIX ATTEMPT 1 FAILED 2026-06-20: a code-trace MIS-diagnosed the cost as O(depth) `SpeculationProbe` state-snapshots (walker `cursorStack_`/`wrapFrameFlags_`, builder `open_`/`scopes_`); the O(1) size-truncation fix had ZERO timing effect AND broke the general non-append-only `SchemaWalker::snapshot/restore` API (`core/test_schema_walker`) тАФ REVERTED. **MEASURED root cause 2026-06-20 (instrumented counters, NOT a code-trace):** the O(N┬▓) is PANIC-MODE RECOVERY running INSIDE doomed speculative `castExpr` probes тАФ `panicInSpec == panicRecovers` (100% of panics are in-probe) and `panicScanTokens тЙИ tokensAdvanced` (the parser advances tokens almost solely to panic-scan inside failing cast probes, then rolls them back). For `((((0))))` the `castExpr` probe (tried BEFORE `parenExpr` in the `operand` alt) fails at each inner `(` (castTypeRef can't start with `(`) and `panicRecover` scans forward to the end-of-expression sync point = O(min(N,64)) tokens ├Ч N levels. **FIX ATTEMPT 2 FAILED 2026-06-20:** suppressing the panic-scan inside probes (`panicRecover` early-return when `speculationDepth > 0`) was correctness-safe (ctest 333-green) but COUNTERPRODUCTIVE тАФ deep input HUNG WORSE (n=100: ~3 s тЖТ timeout, NO diagnostic emitted): the panic-scan is LOAD-BEARING for the failing probe to TERMINATE / make forward progress, NOT pure waste. REVERTED. **Correct direction (UNTRIED тАФ a real engine rework, not a one-liner):** a CHEAP fail-fast for the cast-probe mismatch that PRESERVES termination тАФ either (a) dispatch-level: fail the probe immediately on a required-alt no-candidate while `speculationDepth > 0` (instead of routing through panic recovery), or (b) a k-token candidate prune at the `operand` AltChoice (don't ENTER the cast/compound-literal probe when the token after `(` cannot start `castTypeBase`); both must stay agnostic (FIRST-set / generic dispatch, no language identity) and must not break cast / compound-literal / sizeof / the binder-sketch commit triage. Only manifests on deeply-nested input (valid C nests тЙд4 in the corpus). | (closed тАФ LL(k) predictive prune, direction (b)) | `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD`; `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`; `D-PARSE-PREDICTIVE-PRUNE-CONTEXTUAL-KEYWORD`; `c-subset.lang.json` operand rule |
| `D-PARSE-PREDICTIVE-PRUNE-CONTEXTUAL-KEYWORD` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out cycle 13, branch p14).** Closing the end-to-end "a contextual keyword in a speculative candidate's fixed prefix parses correctly" needed BOTH a PRUNE half (the registry's prescribed fix) AND a deeper PROBE half (the build DISCOVERED it; the anchor did not scope it тЖТ ┬зB-surfaced, user chose build-deep). **(1) PRUNE:** a loader-derived token-id-keyed `contextualKinds` set (`computeContextualKinds` in `grammar_schema_json.cpp`, from the per-`LexemeMeaning.contextual` flags) + an O(1) `GrammarSchema::isContextualKind(SchemaTokenId)` query; `predictivePrefixPrunes` (`parser.cpp`) now `continue`s at any offset whose OBSERVED token is contextual тЖТ the candidate survives the prune. **(2) PROBE (the crux the build exposed):** even surviving the prune, the candidate's speculative probe `recoverAt(P_UnexpectedToken)`'d тАФ the TokenLeaf MATCH GATE in `stepOnce` tests the UN-demoted keyword kind (`effectiveKind` by design does not model demotion), so the token never reached the builder's `pushToken` contextual-demotion. FIX: the match gate now MIRRORS the builder's demotion (a contextual `tokKind` тИЙ expected тЖТ treat as a match when `identifierKind` IS expected) and advances the PARSER's walker with the demoted `identifierKind`, keeping it in lockstep with the builder's walker (no spurious desync-latch trip тЖТ the probe commits). Both halves AGNOSTIC (config-derived `isContextualKind`; the demotion target REUSES the parser's EXISTING `identifierKind` тАФ the schema's resolved `Identifier` token kind, IDENTICAL to the builder's demotion this mirrors тЖТ walker lockstep) тАФ EMPTY `contextualKinds` for a non-contextual grammar тЗТ ZERO behavior change, the deep-nest O(N) prune win preserved. (Review-noted pre-existing residual `D-PARSE-IDENTIFIER-KIND-HARDCODED-VS-CONFIG`: the parser+builder resolve the demotion target via the literal name `"Identifier"` rather than the live `semantics.identifierToken`; latent тАФ all shipped grammars declare `identifierToken: "Identifier"` so they coincide; trigger-gated, NOT introduced here.) 374 ctest green, ZERO regression incl. **tsql** (the `reservedWordPolicy: contextual` grammar where the gate CAN fire). Pinned RED-on-disable with BOTH halves toggled INDEPENDENTLY (prune-skip off тЖТ caseId pruned; match-gate off тЖТ caseId survives prune but probe rejected тЖТ replays wrong candidate) by the synthetic contextual-keyword speculative-alt schema in `test_parser_speculation.cpp`: `ParserSpeculation.{ContextualKeywordInSpeculativePrefixNotMispruned` (soft keyword `kw` in caseId's `[A,Identifier,;]` prefix demotes + commits + asserts the `P_ContextualKeywordResolution` demotion diagnostic fired)`, HardKeywordInSpeculativePrefixStillPruned` (negative control тАФ a HARD keyword stays pruned; the fix doesn't over-broaden)`}`. RESIDUAL: none for this anchor. тАФ *Original OPEN characterization (audit trail):* **registered 2026-06-20 (FC13 predictive-prune soundness fold).** The LL(k) speculative-alt PREDICTIVE PRUNE (`Parser::predictivePrefixPrunes`, the O(N) deep-nest lever that landed alongside `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC`) compares a candidate's exact per-offset FIRST_k prefix against the observed token's `effectiveKind`. `effectiveKind` DELIBERATELY does NOT model contextual-keyword demotion (`parser.cpp` ~L63 тАФ a soft keyword keeps its KEYWORD kind here; demotion to Identifier happens later in the builder against the live cursor expectedSet + scope stack). So for a grammar with contextual keywords (`contextual: true` per entry, or grammar-wide `reservedWordPolicy: "contextual"` тАФ e.g. tsql), at an offset whose admissible set holds the demotion target (Identifier) the prune could see the keyword kind, find it absent, and WRONGLY drop a candidate the demoted token would in fact match тАФ a silent mis-parse. NOT live today: c-subset's speculative prefixes are all hard keywords / punctuation, and tsql's lone `nameOrCall` speculative alt discriminates at offset 1 on `(` / qualified-name punctuation, not on a soft-keyword Identifier slot тАФ so no shipped speculative candidate puts a contextual keyword in its FIXED prefix. The precondition ("sound only for grammars with no contextual / scope-resolvable kind in a speculative candidate's fixed prefix") is DOCUMENTED prominently at both `Parser::predictivePrefixPrunes` and `computePredictivePrefixes`. A clean conservative fix is deferred because no token-id-keyed "is this kind contextual" query exists today тАФ the `contextual` flag is per-`LexemeMeaning` (lexeme-string-keyed); only the grammar-wide `reservedWordPolicy()` lever is exposed, which would NOT cover a per-keyword `contextual: true` under a Strict policy. | Add an agnostic token-id-keyed "is this SchemaTokenId a contextual/scope-resolvable kind" query (derive a `contextualKinds` set in the loader from the `contextual` LexemeMeaning flags + `reservedWordPolicy`), then make `predictivePrefixPrunes` CONSERVATIVE тАФ skip the prune at any offset whose OBSERVED token has a contextual/scope-resolvable kind (do not constrain that offset). Must stay agnostic (no token/language naming) and preserve the deep-nest O(N) win for non-contextual grammars. Pin with a synthetic contextual-keyword speculative-alt schema whose candidate prefix admits Identifier at offset тЙе1 + a soft-keyword input (RED-on-disable: the un-skipped prune drops the valid candidate). USER FIRED THE TRIGGER (4├Ч sign-off, "execute as many cycles as needed"):* full iterative expr+stmt+const-eval traversal across SIX recursion families (parser / cst_to_hir expr+stmt / hir_to_mir expr+stmt / semantic / const-eval ├Ч2), replacing host recursion with explicit heap work-stacks so deep nesting is flat O(N) host-stack. Plan-locked (REWORKтЖТreworked) design at [plan 24](24-iterative-expression-traversal%20-%20tbd.md); backup branch `feature/0-0-2-p14-before-rework`. Acknowledged: NOT a crash fix (already mitigated by the 64 MiB worker), NO production consumer тАФ a robustness goal. Cap is RAISED not removed (`P_ExpressionTooDeep` backstop kept). тАФ *Original characterization (audit trail):* **OPEN (trigger-gated) тАФ registered 2026-06-22 (residual deep-expr parse super-linearity characterization cycle).** A deeply-nested expression like `((((0))))` parses with a residual MILD WALL-CLOCK super-linearity (measured ~exp 1.7, Debug, cap=4000 on a large-stack thread: depth 250 тЙИ23 ms тЖТ 500 тЙИ71 ms тЖТ 1000 тЙИ240 ms тЖТ 2000 тЙИ873 ms тЖТ 3000 тЙИ1897 ms; ns/level rises 93kтЖТ632k). DISTINCT from `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC` (the now-FIXED O(N┬▓) cast-vs-paren panic-scan blowup тАФ that made n=300 take 154 s; GONE, n=3000 is now 1.9 s); it is the SAME residual a sibling session flagged (matching ~exp-1.7). **ROOT CAUSE тАФ decisively localized by a CONTROLLED experiment, not a code-trace:** deep `((((0))))` (host-recursion depth = N) vs a flat `0+0+тАж+0` left-assoc chain (recursion depth ~2, SAME O(N) node/token count, built ITERATIVELY by the Pratt climb). The FLAT chain is perfectly O(N) тАФ ns/level FLAT at ~20.5 ┬╡s across 250тЖТ3000, node count exactly linear тАФ while only the NESTED case grows. Both exercise the IDENTICAL shared machinery (TreeBuilder frame open/close, the node-arena `std::vector` GEOMETRIC growth, schema-walker advance), so the "TreeBuilder/arena bookkeeping" hypothesis from the QUADRATIC row is **REFUTED**. Exhaustive code reading confirms every per-level op on the direct-descent paren path is O(1) (operand AltChoice dispatch, `candidateBranches` over the fixed 6-branch set, the LL(k) `predictivePrefixPrunes` over the fixed prefix, `peekSignificantKind`, frame open/close, ALL `SchemaWalker` ops, `finish()` once) and the lone-survivor `parenExpr` DIRECT-DESCENDS with NO `SpeculationProbe`/checkpoint/snapshot (so the O(N┬▓)-snapshot path is not taken). The residual is therefore NOT algorithmic (PROVEN: the flat control isolates the shared WORK as O(N)) тАФ it is a constant-factor cost of the N-deep HOST recursion, attributed BY ELIMINATION (not directly cache-instrumented) to that recursion's memory-hierarchy behavior: the live C++ call stack тЙИ N ├Ч {parseExpressionAt, parsePrimary, parseUntilFrameDepth, stepOnce, walkExpression} frames PLUS the strided unwind re-access of the depth-indexed `frames`/`frameRules`/`cursorStack`/`pendingChildren` vectors as the working set outgrows cache (the rising-not-constant local exponent ~1.6тЖТ1.9 is consistent with cache-tier transitions, not a clean algorithmic O(N┬▓)). **BOUNDED / MOOT:** `ParserConfig::maxExpressionDepth` caps depth at 256 (`D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD`; valid C nests тЙд4 in the whole corpus; >256 FAILS LOUD via `P_ExpressionTooDeep`, never parses) AND the whole downstream frontend (semantic/HIR/MIR) is itself recursion-bound to the same 256 cap (`D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`) тАФ so the residual only appears in a synthetic cap-raised micro-benchmark, never in production. Pinned by `ParserSpeculation.FlatChainParseWorkIsLinear` (the O(N)-WORK regression guard) + the existing `DeepNestCastVsParenIsLinear` (the deep-path O(N┬▓)-regression guard, whose generous 8├Ч bound is explained by this residual). | Convert the recursive-descent EXPRESSION parser (`parseExpressionAt`/`parsePrimary`/`parseUntilFrameDepth` + the `walkExpression` re-entry) to an explicit heap worklist/stack so deep nesting carries no host-recursion memory cost тАФ AND, for any END-TO-END benefit, do the same for the recursion-bound downstream passes (`D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK`'s semantic/HIR/MIR), since the 256 cap is set by THEM. Stay agnostic (no token/rule/language identity). Pin with a deep-nest wall-clock ratio the iterative form keeps ~linear. Priority: **normal (trigger-gated тАФ fires only if a real consumer needs expressions nested beyond the 256 cap AND the cap is first lifted by an iterative frontend; NO in-cap benefit otherwise).** | `D-PARSE-SPECULATION-OPERAND-QUADRATIC` (the O(N┬▓) sibling this is the residual OF); `D-PARSE-DEEP-FRONTEND-STACK` (the downstream recursion bound that sets the 256 cap); `D-PARSE-EXPR-DEPTH-GUARD` (the cap); `parser.cpp` `parseExpressionAt`; tests `FlatChainParseWorkIsLinear` + `DeepNestCastVsParenIsLinear` |

| `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-MEMORY-CLASS-STRUCT` | тЬЕ **SHIPPED 2026-06-20 (FC12a-struct-memory).** SysV x86_64 struct-by-value at the variadic boundary for the MEMORY-class (>16B) + register-exhaustion-split shapes. **┬зB (user 2026-06-20): Option C тАФ a first-class BY-VALUE-AGGREGATE STACK-ARG carrier** (a prior "push eightbytes as ordinary operands" design was REJECTED тАФ an audit proved it a SILENT MISCOMPILE: the operands land in FREE registers, not the stack). SysV ┬з3.2.3/┬з3.5.7 requires a MEMORY-class aggregate AND a register-exhaustion-split InRegisters aggregate to be passed ENTIRELY in the outgoing overflow (stack) area UNCONDITIONALLY (even when arg registers are free), never split. The greedy register-then-overflow callconv had no per-arg force-to-stack lever, so the carrier IS the lever: a NEW MIR op `ByValueStackArg` (operand 0 = the `freshAggregateTemp` copy address via `lowerByteWiseCopy`, same as the by-ref arg; payload = aggregate byte size; result = pointer, threaded through) sits as ONE Call operand at the aggregate's left-to-right position. `mir_to_lir` lowers it to a `Reg` (the address тАФ kept live by liveness/regalloc) immediately followed by a NEW `LirOperandKind::ByValueStackAgg` size marker (mirroring the leading-Reg + trailing-descriptor memory-operand convention). `lir_callconv` (BOTH `computeMaxOutgoingStackArgs` pre-scan AND the materialize placement loop, via the shared `byValueStackAggSlots` helper) places it at the next overflow offset(s) тАФ reserving ceil(size/outgoingSlot) slots + byte-copying the temp into `[sp + shadowSpaceBytes + overflowIdx*outgoingSlotSize]` (caller-saved scratch GPR avoiding every carrier addr reg + the indirect callee + the count reg + every pending arg-move / FP-dup SOURCE register тАФ the silent-arg-clobber avoid-set, the review-caught BLOCKER-3 fold: the byte-copies run BEFORE the register arg-moves read their sources, so the scratch must not be a live arg source; a blanket argGpr exclusion was REJECTED as over-broad тАФ a dest-only argGpr is written by its own arg-move after the copy, so its transient clobber is dead). It consumes NO arg register (runGpr/runFpr NOT advanced; class counters NOT advanced) and does NOT inflate the SSE/AL count (the GPR-class address Reg fails the FPR test; the marker is non-Reg). CC-NEUTRAL by construction (the byte size is the only datum; the overflow geometry is config-driven) so the Win64 + AAPCS64 struct-vararg cycles REUSE this carrier. Caller routing (`hir_to_mir.cpp` Call arg loop): MEMORY-class ByReference тЖТ carrier; register-exhaustion split (`runGpr+numGp > gpSaveCount || runFpr+numFp > fpSaveCount`) тЖТ WHOLE struct to carrier. Callee `va_arg(>16B)` (`lowerVaArgAggregate`): an EARLY ByReference dispatch тЖТ the overflow-ONLY arm (addr = overflow_arg_area, bump roundUp(size, gpSlotBytes)), never the register-gather diamond (numGp=numFp=0 would make the atomic-fit trivially-true тЖТ gather ZERO pieces). Wall-3 (fixed >16B struct param in a variadic fn): the fail-loud removed тАФ `receiveByValueParam`'s ByReference arm receives the hidden pointer as 1 GPR arg (counted in `currentFnFixedGpr_`), so the common case (1 GPR + тЙд5 scalars тЙд 6) lowers cleanly. | `D-FC12B-WIN64-VARIADIC-CALLEE` | тЬЕ **SHIPPED 2026-06-19 (FC12b).** **┬зB (user 2026-06-19): Option A тАФ a closed `VaListStrategy` enum with the va_list TYPE DERIVED from the strategy, chosen over Option D (an explicit per-CC `vaListType` config field); derived avoids an unused-flexibility knob that must be kept consistent with the strategy. (Rejected extremes: a va_arg recipe-DSL, and a unified ptr-to-opaque-blob va_list тАФ both break the bar.)** Microsoft x64 (ms_x64) variadic CALLEE support тАФ `va_start`/`va_arg`/`va_end` for SCALAR + FP types, config-driven + source/target/linker-AGNOSTIC. The per-CC va_list TYPE + lowering STRATEGY is a NEW closed enum `VaListStrategy` (`aggregate_layout.hpp`, beside `AggregateClassKind`/`BitFieldStrategy` so the SEMANTIC va_list-type injection reads it without pulling the link/target substrate): `SysVRegisterSave` (FC12a, unchanged-but-now-strategy-tagged), `HomogeneousPointer` (Win64, this cycle), `Aapcs64DualCursor` (declared, fail-loud-until-FC12c). `VaListLayout` gains `strategy` (first field) + `namedArgSlotBytes`; `x86_64.target.json` ms_x64 declares `{"strategy":"homogeneous_pointer","namedArgSlotBytes":8}`. **BLOCKER-2 (va_list TYPE per ABI):** `analyze()` gains an `optional<VaListStrategy>` param threaded from the resolved CC (pipeline reads it where it reads `aggregateLayout`); the sema injection switches on it тАФ SysV тЖТ `__va_list_tag[1]` (24B), Win64 тЖТ `char*` (8B), AAPCS64 тЖТ loud `S_VariadicCalleeUnsupported`. A wrong sizeof(va_list) would mis-size the `ap` local + corrupt the stack тАФ pinned `sizeof(va_list)`==8 ms_x64 / 24 sysv. **BLOCKER-1 (no-shadow contiguous base):** Win64's named-arg HOME space (rcx/rdx/r8/r9 slots = the caller's shadow space) and the 5th+ stack args are CONTIGUOUS from `totalFrameSize + callPushBytes` (NO shadowSpaceBytes тАФ adding it would skip the home and read past every named arg). A NEW MIR leaf `VaHomeArgAreaAddr` (payload = the slot-aligned named-arg count) + LIR `va_home_arg_area` op materializes to `lea [sp + totalFrameSize + callPushBytes + payload*outgoingSlotSize]`; the Win64 prologue spills the named int regs into the SAME base (spill-target == va_arg-read-target). HIRтЖТMIR lowers va_start (Win64) to `Store(VaHomeArgAreaAddr, ap)`, va_arg to a LINEAR pointer bump (NO diamond: Load ap тЖТ Load T тЖТ Gep +namedArgSlotBytes тЖТ Store ap), va_end to nothing. **FP-dup (PART 5):** Win64 passes each FP vararg in BOTH the xmm AND the matching integer (home) reg; the callee reads va_arg(double) from the home GPR slot, so the CALLER duplicates each register-resident FP VARARG (operand past `fixedOperandCount`) into argGprs[slot] via a NEW dedicated opcode `movq_xmm_to_gpr` (`66 REX.W 0F 7E /r`; byte-pin `movq rdx, xmm1` = `66 48 0F 7E CA`). A dedicated opcode (not `movq_xclass`) because the encoding-variant guard keys only on (operandKinds, width) and cannot disambiguate the two MOVQ directions, and `movq_xclass` is consumed bidirectionally by Bitcast тАФ overloading would force a silent miscompile on GPRтЖТFPR Bitcast. Every va seam dispatches on `VaListStrategy` with a fail-loud default (no half-migrated site silently runs the SysV path on a Win64 layout). Run-witnessed on PE x86_64 (this Windows host): `examples/c-subset/varargs_win64_sum` (int+double varargs, anti-folded via a mutable-global seed). Every va seam dispatches on `VaListStrategy` with a fail-loud default. The callee reg-save-area zone is gated on strategy тИИ {SysVRegisterSave, Aapcs64DualCursor}. Re-keyed to an unbounded per-class `unordered_set` of global ordinals, mirroring `buildFreeLists` (lir_regalloc.cpp). Pin `LirRewrite.Arm64HighFprSpillScratchPoolHandlesOrdinalsBeyond64`. FIXED scratch-free at the ENCODER (zero `lir_callconv.cpp`/`mir_to_lir.cpp` change): a new `EncodingSlotKind::Imm12HiLo24` slot + a 2-word shifted-imm12 macro variant per `add`/`sub`/`lea` тАФ `op Xd,Xn,#(V&0xFFF)` (sh=0) then `op Xd,Xd,#(V>>12),LSL #12` (sh=1, fixedWord base|0x400000), magnitude-routed by a new optional `immMin`/`immMax` variant guard (1-word тЙд4095, 2-word 4096..0xFFFFFF). Reaches 16 MiB; >16 MiB тЖТ `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB` (row below). AGNOSTIC (x86_64 has no imm12 slot тЖТ never reached; only slot-kind `==` + value-magnitude arithmetic). Byte-pinned (sub sp #36032 тЖТ 0xD13303FF/0xD14023FF; add тЖТ 0x913303FF/0x914023FF; lea тЖТ split) + boundary pins (4095/4096/16777215/16777216) + a callconv two-inst prologue/epilogue count pin; **run-witnessed `examples/c-subset/large_frame_arm64` exit 42 on qemu-aarch64 + x86_64-linux** (release pipeline). NON-leaf (saved-reg spill + call) AND register-pressure-spill large frames also confirmed correct at runtime (exit 42 / 104) тАФ the frame layout keeps saved-regs/spills LOW (in imm12), so only the high array-element address goes beyond imm12 and routes through the now-fixed lea. Closed + RUN-witnessed by a NEW deterministic corpus `examples/c-subset/large_spill_frame_arm64` (a 30-fixed-param AAPCS64 callee `g` called with 30 args тЖТ the 9th+ INCOMING stack-arg loads in `g` + the OUTGOING stack-arg stores + caller spill reload/store all exceed imm9 at offsets 256/264 тЖТ scaled `load_u`/`store_u`; returns p28+p29+p08 = 1+40+1 = 42, miscompile-sensitive; arm64-only because the un-optimized x86_64 caller exhausts its 16-GPR scratch pool materializing 30 args тАФ a SEPARATE pre-existing regalloc limit, not this fix; qemu-aarch64 exit 42 VERIFIED + macho arm64 leg; RED-on-disable: revert the selector тЖТ the arm64 compile fails loud `A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange` at offsets 256 AND 264 (store AND load) тЖТ cannot build) + the host-independent structural callconv pin `Aarch64HighStackParamUsesScaledImm12FrameLoad` (a 40-param callee тЖТ asserts `load_u` present in the materialized module + clean assembly, red-on-disable, runs on EVERY CI leg) + the agnostic counter-pin `X8664HighStackParamHasNoScaledImm12FrameOps` (x86_64 declares no `load_u`/`store_u`) + imm9/scaled BOUNDARY byte-pins (offset 255тЖТimm9 STUR, 256тЖТscaled STR, 32760тЖТscaled imm12-max, 256 on the unscaled `store`тЖТfail-loud). The genuinely-UNENCODABLE tail (offset NEGATIVE outside imm9, aligned >4095*accessSize=32760, or non-access-aligned outside imm9 тАФ needs a register-offset / address-materialization form) STAYS fail-loud тЖТ the narrower residual `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-SCALED-IMM12` (row below). | `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c12, branch feature/0-0-2-p14, 374 ctest + corpus `large_frame_beyond_16mib` exit 42 qemu-aarch64 baseline+release).** AS-BUILT (┬зB Option A тАФ encoder macro, x16 baked): a NEW `EncodingSlotKind::Imm32MovzMovk` (value-bearing fixed32 slot; the encoder splits a 32-bit value lo16тЖТword0 / hi16тЖТword1 into the MOVZ/MOVK imm16 windows, mirroring `materializeViaMovkLadder`'s chunk split) + a NEW 3-word `fixedWords` variant per `add`/`sub`/`lea` in `arm64.target.json`, magnitude-routed by the variant `immMin:16777216`/`immMax:2147483647` guard. The macro is `MOVZ Xs,#(V & 0xFFFF)` + `MOVK Xs,#((V>>16) & 0xFFFF),LSL #16` + an EXTENDED-register op: for the sp-adjust `sub sp,sp,x16` (0xCB3063FF) / `add sp,sp,x16` (0x8B3063FF) with **x16 (AAPCS64 IP0 scratch) BAKED** into all 3 base words (NO resultSlot тАФ sp=31 baked into the op word, validate's rule-G `imm32.movzmovk` baked-dest exemption); for the lea `add Xd,sp,Xd` (0x8B2063E0 base) SCRATCH-FREE тАФ the displacement materializes into the lea's OWN dest reg Xd, threaded through all 3 words via resultSlot+extraResultSlots (the operand-0тЖТrn wire sets Rn from the ACTUAL frame-base operand тАФ sp here, so Rn=31=sp, byte-pinned; the override is general so a non-sp base would get its own Rn). The EXTENDED-register form (bit 21, option=011 UXTX) is the make-or-break: the shifted-register form (0xCB000000) would write XZR not sp тЖТ corrupt frame. AGNOSTIC (x86_64 has no imm16/movzmovk slot тЖТ never reached; slot-kind `==` + value-magnitude arithmetic only; the x16 identity is config-baked like XZR=31/sp=31). Byte-pinned (sub sp #0x1234000 тЖТ MOVZ/MOVK + 0xCB3063FF full 32-bit word; lea тЖТ MOVZ/MOVK + 0x8B2263E2; boundary pins 0xFFFFFFтЖТ2-word / 0x1000000тЖТ3-word / 0x7FFFFFFFтЖТ3-word / 0x80000000тЖТfail-loud) + a callconv F4 structural pin (prologue's first word = `MOVZ x16` + x16 not value-allocated, durable scratch-safety) + **objdump-confirmed + run-witnessed `examples/c-subset/large_frame_beyond_16mib` exit 42 under qemu-aarch64 (baseline+release, Inlining ON) + x86_64-linux** (RED-ON-DISABLE: removing the variant's reachable range тЖТ A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant on the sub/add sp-adjust тЖТ the arm64 leg cannot produce a binary). Genuinely-unencodable residual тЖТ `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-2GIB` (row below; a >2 GiB frame goes negative through the int32 frame size тЖТ no variant matches тЖТ fail-loud). _Original deferral below._ NEW RESIDUAL of `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12` (FC12-deferral 2, 2026-06-22): the SCRATCH-FREE shifted-imm12 word-pair (`EncodingSlotKind::Imm12HiLo24` + the `add`/`sub`/`lea` 2-word variants `op Xd,Xn,#lo` then `op Xd,Xd,#hi,LSL #12` in `arm64.target.json`, magnitude-routed by the variant `immMin`/`immMax` guard) now lands an `add`/`sub`/`lea` IMMEDIATE in (4095, 0xFFFFFF] тАФ a 16 MiB frame reach, covering every realistic frame (e.g. `int big[9000]` = 36000B prologue + `&big[8999]` lea). A value > 0xFFFFFF (16 MiB) STILL fails loud: the add/sub/lea variant selector rejects it (`A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant`, since `immMax:16777215` excludes it), and the encoder's `writeHiLo24` 24-bit gate (`A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange`) is the defense-in-depth backstop. No shipped corpus has a > 16 MiB single frame; this row keeps the ledger honest about the genuinely-unencodable tail. Priority: **low** (only a single function with > 16 MiB of locals). | Add a THIRD word to the macro (`#hi2,LSL #24` reaching 36 bits) OR materialize the offset into a scratch register via MOVZ/MOVK + a register-form `add/sub Xd,Xn,Xm` and select it when the value exceeds 0xFFFFFF. | `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12`; `D-ASM-AARCH64-LARGE-FRAME-IMM12`; plan 13 (asm encoding) |

NOT reachable by any shipped corpus: case (a) needs a base-relative negative beyond тИТ256 (frame offsets are non-negative sp-relative), (b) needs a single frame spill/saved-reg/stack-arg offset > 32760 B (a ~32 KB spill area тАФ far past the deepest corpus pressure), (c) needs an unaligned frame slot (all slots are slotSize-aligned). | `D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c11, branch p14, 372 ctest + corpus `large_frame_win64` exit 42 native Windows baseline+release).** AS-BUILT: an INLINE page-walking stack probe emitted by the x86_64 encoder from a NEW `stack_probe` LIR op (chosen over `__chkstk` because dss PE exes are FREESTANDING тАФ import only kernel32, not msvcrt where __chkstk lives; verified). Config-driven `stackProbePageBytes` on TargetCallingConvention (ms_x64=4096, all others 0/absent тЖТ no probe; validate() requires a power of two). `emitPrologue` (threaded `cc`) emits `StackProbe(sp, frameBytes, pageBytes)` when `cc.stackProbePageBytes>0 && totalFrameSize>pageBytes`, else the plain `sub rsp,F` (byte-identical small-frame/non-Windows path). The encoder hand-emits the loop `mov r11d,frame / .L: sub rsp,page / or qword[rsp],0 / sub r11d,page / cmp r11d,page / ja .L (0x77, compile-time rel8, no reloc) / sub rsp,r11` тАФ r11 is ms_x64 caller-saved, not arg/return, probe runs before arg-home. AGNOSTIC (no `if arch/format==`; page step from the op operand, not hardcoded; arm64 OMITS the op тЖТ A_NoEncodingDeclared if ever emitted). RED-ON-DISABLE (the strongest kind): the corpus CRASHES (SIGSEGV exit 139) without the probe, exit 42 with тАФ native on the Windows box. Byte-pins (frame 36000 non-multiple + 8192 exact-page, exact 0x77 ja) + prologue structural pins (ms_x64>4096тЖТprobe; тЙд4096 + sysv>4096тЖТplain). **тШЕ the unforgeable native witness caught a real bug in the build: a first `sub rsp,r11` mis-encoded as `49 29 DB` (=`sub r11,rbx`) segfaulted EVEN WITH the probe тЖТ fixed to `4C 29 DC`, byte-pinned.** ORIGINALLY: **OPEN тАФ a SILENT RUNTIME CRASH** (surfaced 2026-06-22 while adding the `large_frame_arm64` corpus, but ENTIRELY ORTHOGONAL to the arm64 fix тАФ a pre-existing x86_64-Windows gap). DSS did NOT emit a stack-probe (`__chkstk` / page-walk) for `ms_x64` / `pe64` functions whose frame exceeds one guard page (4096B). A function like `int main(){ int big[9000]; ... }` for `x86_64:pe64-x86_64-windows-exec` emits a bare prologue `sub rsp, ~36016` that jumps ~9 pages PAST the stack guard page WITHOUT touching the intermediate pages тЖТ the first deep-slot access raises `STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION` (0xC0000005) at runtime. The Windows x64 ABI REQUIRES a function allocating >1 page to probe each page (`__chkstk`, or an inline `sub rsp,0x1000`/`test [rsp],eax` loop) so the guard page walks down one page at a time and the kernel commits each. Empirically verified: a 400B + a 4400B frame exit 42, a 36000B frame segfaults. Linux x86_64 + AArch64-ELF do NOT need it (kernel stack-growth guard-gap absorbs the multi-page jump тАФ which is why `large_frame_arm64` exits 42 on x86_64-linux + qemu-aarch64). This is WHY the `x86_64:pe64` target was excluded from `examples/c-subset/large_frame_arm64/expected.json` (see its `$comment`). Priority: **normal** (a real silent-crash class on >1-page Windows frames тАФ large local buffers; converting it to FAIL-LOUD is a cheap interim). | Implement Windows large-frame stack probing in the prologue SP-adjust (`lir_callconv.cpp` emitPrologue/emitSpAdjust), gated AGNOSTICALLY on a CC/format-declared stack-probe-threshold field (mirroring `redZoneBytes`/`shadowSpaceBytes`, NOT `if(arch=="x86_64")`): when the frame exceeds the page size, emit the page-walk probe (or a `call __chkstk`) before/in place of the single `sub rsp,N`; add a runtime corpus witness (re-add the `x86_64:pe64` target to `large_frame_arm64`) + a structural pin. INTERIM (cheaper, kills the silent crash now): a fail-loud guard refusing a >1-page ms_x64 frame until probing lands. | plan 13 (asm encoding); plan 14 (linker); `D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12` (the arm64 sibling, closed) |
| `D-WIN64-PDATA-XDATA-UNWIND` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-06 (p19 Cluster G c114) тАФ the pe64 leg emits Windows x64 SEH/unwind tables (`.pdata` RUNTIME_FUNCTION[] + `.xdata` UNWIND_INFO) per function and points `IMAGE_DATA_DIRECTORY[3]` (EXCEPTION) at `.pdata`; sqlite3.exe compiles SEH-only (no `SQLITE_DISABLE_INTRINSIC`) + RUNS on Windows (`--version`тЖТ3.54.0, canonical smoke `SELECT sum`тЖТ42, full CRUD 52, index-filter 40).** WHY: the x64 ABI needs each function's PROLOGUE described so `RtlVirtualUnwind` can reconstruct the caller's context; without `.pdata` a fault (or, later, a `__try`) cannot unwind. AS-BUILT (agnostic, format-gated): (1) a NEW format-neutral `FrameUnwindInfo`/`FrameSavedReg` (asm.hpp) carries each function's `{totalFrameSize, usesStackProbe, stackProbePageBytes, savedRegs[]}`; (2) `compile_pipeline.cpp` PROJECTS it from the callconv pass's per-function `FrameLayout` (`cc.perFunc[fi]`) onto `assembled.functions[fi].unwind` post-`assemble()`, mapping the DSS physical register ORDINAL тЖТ its x64 hardware encoding via `target.registerInfo(ordinal)->hwEncoding` (GPR ordinal==hwEncoding was coincidental; FPR ordinal 16..31 must map to xmm 0..15) тАФ pure frame data, ONLY the pe writer reads it (ELF/Mach-O populate a dead optional, no `if(format==)`); (3) `pe.cpp buildFunctionUnwindInfo` RECOMPUTES the prologue byte-offsets deterministically (allocLen 7 for `sub rsp,imm32` / `9+28*pages+3` for the inline stack-probe; +8 per GPR save, +8/9 per FPR `movsd`), emits `UWOP_ALLOC_{SMALL,LARGE}` + `UWOP_SAVE_NONVOL` per GPR sorted DESCENDING by CodeOffset, DWORD-aligns each blob, and a first-byte prologue-shape guard (0x48 sub / 0x41 probe) fail-louds if the emitted bytes desync; Edits AтАУF chain `.xdata`+`.pdata` sections, headers, raw-size/pointers, `SizeOfImage`, and data-dir[3]. тШЕ THE FPR DECISION: DSS spills xmm callee-saves as low-64 `movsd` (not the 16-byte `movaps` that `UWOP_SAVE_XMM128` assumes), so the xmm save is OMITTED from the unwind codes (no matching UWOP; an xmm save does NOT move RSP тЖТ the stack/return-address walk stays exact) while its store STILL advances the CodeOffset cursor so following GPR saves stay right тАФ the handler-case RESTORE deferred to [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] (c116). WITNESSES: host-independent byte pin `PeExecWriter.FunctionUnwindInfoEmitsPdataXdataAndExceptionDataDir` (frame 0x20 + saves [xmm6@0, rbx@8, rbp@16] тЖТ CountOfCodes=5 [xmm6 omitted, else 7], rbx CodeOffset 23 [proves xmm width 8 via hwEncoding 6, not 9 via ordinal 30], EXCEPTION dirтЖТ.pdata; RED-on-disable=revert FPR-omitтЖТfail-loud, or drop emissionтЖТno .pdata) + runtime corpus `examples/c-subset/pe_unwind_selftest` (main takes `&target_fn`, calls kernel32 `RtlLookupFunctionEntry`, asserts the OS-found entry's BeginAddress==the fn RVA тЖТ exit 42; RED-on-disableтЖТNULLтЖТ91; pe64-only, `release`-pipeline arm). objdump on sqlite3.exe: `Entry 3 тАж Exception Directory [.pdata]`, 3200 RUNTIME_FUNCTIONs + 86 KB `.xdata`. **Trigger:** FIRED тАФ sqlite's os_win.c saves xmm callee-saves, so the compile hit the FPR path. | (closed тАФ `.pdata`/`.xdata` emission + the EXCEPTION data dir + the FrameUnwindInfo projection) | [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] (c116 handler restore) ┬╖ [[D-WIN64-CHKSTK-LARGE-PROLOGUE]] + [[D-WIN64-HUGE-FRAME-ALLOC]] (fail-loud siblings) ┬╖ `D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE` (the probe whose length allocLen recomputes) ┬╖ the SEH arc c115 (`__try`/`__except` parse) + c116 (funclets); plan 14 linker / plan 23 SQLite-green Windows leg |
| `D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-06 (c116) тАФ the x64 SEH catch WORKS: sqlite compiles with SEH ON (NO defines) + runs (`--version` 3.54.0 / `SELECT sum` 42 / full CRUD 62), and `SQLITE_OMIT_SEH` is DROPPED from the harness.** c116 AS-BUILT: the `__except` FILTER is EXTRACTED into a synthesized `ms_x64` FUNCLET (arg0/rcx=EXCEPTION_POINTERS, arg1/rdx=establisher frame тАФ NO new CC, ms_x64 already maps them; `SehExceptionInfo`тЖТarg0, `SehExceptionCode`тЖТ`*(u32*)*(void**)arg0`, `SehFilterReturn`тЖТReturn); the parent's c114 UNWIND_INFO gains `UNW_FLAG_EHANDLER` (byte0 0x01тЖТ0x09) + the `__C_specific_handler` THUNK RVA + a SCOPE_TABLE `{Begin, End, Handler(=filter funclet RVA), JumpTarget(=__except body RVA)}` in `.xdata` (all link-time RVAs; the personality import is SEH-GATED тАФ synthesized on demand, NOT in windows.json symbols, the c101 0xC0000139 law); parent locals (sqlite `pWal`) are recovered in the funclet via a NEW GENERIC `RecoverParentFrameSlot(base, slotIdx)` MIR op тЖТ `[establisherArg + parentLayout.localAreaOffset() + slot*slotSize]` (base = the funclet's establisher SSA param тАФ NEVER a hardcoded rdx; offset from the parent's FINALIZED FrameLayout via sibling-layout threading into `materializeOneFunc` тАФ the audit-Claim-3 pipeline change; NO frame pointer [establisher==parent post-prologue RSP for FrameRegister=0]; NO arch/format identity in the substrate тАФ AGNOSTICISM VERIFIED by the code-audit); the c115 `SehFilterReturnтЖТhandlerBB` edge is KEPT as a prune/verifier FICTION that emits no runtime branch (the OS resumes at the scope-table JumpTarget); multi-block guarded bodies are laid out CONTIGUOUSLY (`SehParentPolicy::selectBlocks` relayout, enforced-not-assumed) so `[Begin,End)` is ONE PC range; the by-value-param gap (sqlite `pWal` is a bare `Arg`, not an alloca) fixed by the localescape model (force filter-referenced symbols memory-backed, `collectSehFilterReferencedSymbols`). H5 [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] RESOLVED (see its row). WITNESSES (runOn windows): `examples/c-subset/seh_catch_av` (a real AVтЖТ42), `seh_catch_parent_local` (H1 тАФ the filter reads a parent localтЖТ42, negative control `marker=7`тЖТsegfault, unforgeable), `seh_catch_multiblock` (a loop-in-`__try` that faultsтЖТ42) + byte-exact `PeExecWriter.SehScopeTableEmitsEhandlerFlagAndScopeRecord` + `.SehGuardingFunctionSavingNonVolatileXmmFailsLoud` + 4 `SynthSehFunclets.*` MIR pins. Independent DESIGN-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (6 folded) + pre-commit CODE-audit GO (0 CRITICAL/MAJOR). 3-leg gate 563/563 (Win Debug + WSL x86_64 + arm64-qemu); both linux legs a proven no-op off the SEH path. c115 SHIPPED the FRONTEND (as-built): `__try compound __except ( expr ) compound` is a c-subset keyword+grammar (`sehTryStmt`, config-only), a core `HirKind::SehTryExcept` {tryBody, filterExpr, handlerBody}, and 5 neutral MIR ops тАФ `SehTryBegin` (terminator, succs [tryEntry, filterEntry], region-id payload), `SehFilterReturn` (terminator, [i32 filterValue]тЖТ[handlerEntry]), `SehTryEnd` (single fall-through marker), `SehExceptionCode`/`SehExceptionInfo` (0-op value ops = `_exception_code()`/`_exception_info()`, MSVC excpt.h intrinsics, exposed as builtins + windows.json `GetException*` macros). Optimizer soundness kit (each pinned): `opcodeClobbersMemory += SehTryBegin/SehTryEnd/SehFilterReturn` (CSE/LICM never cross a region boundary тАФ the FilterReturn membership is the audit-F1 filterтЖТhandler fix), mem2reg SKIPS any SEH-containing function (fault-time locals stay memory-true), SimplifyCfg never elides/merges a SEH-successor block, the inliner refuses SEH callees + routes SEH hosts through the single-block rebuild (MSVC parity). `MirVerifier::checkSehStructure` (I_SehStructure) pairs Begin/FilterReturn/End on the region id + single-pred filter/handler. HIR-verifier `checkSehContext`: `_exception_code`/`_exception_info` legal only in filter/handler (H_SehBuiltinContext); no jump INTO a region (H_SehJumpIntoRegion); option-(C) early-exit ban [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT]]; no `&&label` into a region [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LABEL-ADDR]]. `mir_to_lir` FAILS LOUD on all 5 ops (L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode citing THIS anchor, every target). c115 WITNESS: sqlite compiles WITHOUT `SQLITE_OMIT_SEH` тАФ the 87 P0001/P0009 parse errors collapse to exactly 35 SEH-funclet-wall diagnostics (frontend fully handles the ~13 wal.c SEH sites). **CLOSING WORK (c116):** emit the x64 filter + handler FUNCLETS (separate mini-fns sharing the parent frame via the establisher-frame pointer / RBP-based parent-local access), the `__C_specific_handler` scope table + set `UNW_FLAG_EHANDLER` + the handler RVA on the c114 UNWIND_INFO, wire the dispatcher, resolve [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] (handler-resume xmm restore), refine mem2reg to region-precise (perf), and DROP `SQLITE_OMIT_SEH` from build-and-test.sh. Witness (c116): a corpus `__try` catching a REAL access violation (PAGE_NOACCESS VirtualAlloc read) тЖТ 42, native Windows; sqlite compiles NO-defines + runs CRUD. | (closed c116 тАФ funclets + `__C_specific_handler` scope tables + the generic `RecoverParentFrameSlot` op + OMIT_SEH dropped; commit at cross-plan) | [[D-WIN64-PDATA-XDATA-UNWIND]] (c114, the UNWIND_INFO this sets EHANDLER on) ┬╖ [[D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE]] (handler-resume restore) ┬╖ [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-FINALLY]]/[[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE]]/[[D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT]]/[[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LABEL-ADDR]] (the c115 trigger-gated fail-louds) ┬╖ plan 23 SQLite-green Windows leg |
| `D-CSUBSET-SEH-FINALLY` | тП│ **TRIGGER-GATED / FAIL-LOUD-IN-PLACE (opened c115 2026-07-06; trigger = a shipped consumer of `__try { } __finally { }`).** c115 PARSES the `__finally` termination-handler arm (`sehFinallyArm` grammar rule) so a use gets a POSITIONED fail-loud at CSTтЖТHIR (`H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind`, "SEH __finally тАж not supported (D-CSUBSET-SEH-FINALLY)") rather than a raw P0001 parse cascade тАФ but there is NO HirKind/lowering for it. sqlite ground-truth (amalgamation-swept): ZERO `__finally` uses (only `__except`). CLOSING WORK (when a consumer appears): `__finally` unwinds via a TERMINATION funclet that runs on BOTH normal fall-through AND exceptional unwind тАФ a genuinely different lowering from `__except`'s exception funclet (it needs `__try`/`__leave` exit interception + a scope-table termination entry). | тП│ trigger = a shipped `__finally` consumer | [[D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS]] (parent, c116) ┬╖ [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE` | тП│ **TRIGGER-GATED / FAIL-LOUD-IN-PLACE (opened c115 2026-07-06; trigger = a shipped consumer of `__leave`).** c115 PARSES `__leave ;` (`sehLeaveStmt`) so a use gets a positioned CSTтЖТHIR fail-loud (D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE) not a parse cascade; NO HirKind/lowering. sqlite: ZERO uses. `__leave` jumps to the end of the innermost `__try` body (structured early-exit within a guarded region тАФ interacts with the [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT]] single-exit invariant, so it needs the SehTryEnd-target machinery when it lands). | тП│ trigger = a shipped `__leave` consumer | [[D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS]] (parent) ┬╖ [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-SEH-EARLY-EXIT` | тП│ **TRIGGER-GATED / FAIL-LOUD-IN-PLACE (opened c115 2026-07-06, the c115 design-audit's option (C); trigger = a shipped consumer needing return/goto-out/break-out of a `__try` guarded body).** c115 REJECTS (HirVerifier `H_SehEarlyExit`) any `return` / goto-out / break-out / continue-out / computed-goto from inside a `__try` guarded body, so the guarded body has EXACTLY ONE exit (the fall-through `SehTryEnd`). WHY: this keeps c116's scope-table region membership CFG-DERIVABLE (reachable-from-tryEntry-without-crossing-the-join) and makes the SimplifyCfg boundary-absorb hazard STRUCTURALLY impossible. The audit ground-truth-swept sqlite: ZERO early exits in any of the ~13 wal.c SEH bodies, so this narrows NO real consumer. CLOSING WORK (option (A), when a consumer needs MSVC-complete early exit): emit a `SehTryEnd(regionId)` on EVERY region-leaving edge (fall-through + each goto-out/return/break/continue-out), and c116 emits instruction-precise (possibly split) scope ranges keyed on the markers (`.xdata` natively supports multiple scope entries per region). | тП│ trigger = a shipped early-exit-from-`__try` consumer | [[D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS]] (parent) ┬╖ [[D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-SEH-LABEL-ADDR` | тП│ **TRIGGER-GATED / FAIL-LOUD-IN-PLACE (opened c115 2026-07-06; trigger = a shipped consumer taking `&&label` of a label inside a `__try` statement).** c115 REJECTS (HirVerifier `H_SehLabelAddress`) a GNU computed-goto label-address (`&&label`, [[D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO]]) naming a label inside any part of a `__try` тАФ a subsequent `goto *ptr` could then enter the guarded PC range UNDETECTABLY at compile time (the jump-into-region rule can only see textual gotos). sqlite: ZERO (it uses neither computed goto near SEH). CLOSING WORK: model the address-taken block's SEH-region membership so the indirect edge is either proven region-internal or rejected precisely. | тП│ trigger = a shipped `&&label`-into-`__try` consumer | [[D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS]] (parent) ┬╖ [[D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO]] |
| `D-WIN64-XMM-UNWIND-RESTORE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-06 (c116) via option (b) PROVE-NO-CONSUMER + an ENFORCED fail-loud invariant.** The trigger fired (the c116 handler funclets landed). RESOLUTION: sqlite's 7 `__try`-guarding WAL-recovery functions spill ZERO non-volatile xmm (empirically тАФ `.pdata`/`.xdata`-extracted; WAL recovery is pure integer/pointer code), so no `__except` handler resumes into a frame reading an unrestored xmm тЖТ the omitted `SAVE_XMM128` is harmless for the shipped corpus. This proof is now an ENFORCED INVARIANT, not a hope: `pe.cpp buildFunctionUnwindInfo` FAILS LOUD (`K_NoMatchingObjectFormat`) if a scope-table-bearing (SEH-guarding) function saves a NON-volatile xmm тАФ so a future SEH-fn-with-xmm cannot silently ship a broken unwind table; it forces the option-(a) work (spill xmm6-15 with `movaps` + emit `UWOP_SAVE_XMM128`) to land first. PINNED red-on-disable: `PeExecWriter.SehGuardingFunctionSavingNonVolatileXmmFailsLoud` (a scope-bearing fn + an xmm6 save тЖТ the encoder errors; drop the `guardsSeh` arm тЖТ it silently omits the restore) + the paired non-SEH negative (`FunctionUnwindInfoEmitsPdata...` тАФ the same xmm6 save on a NON-SEH fn encodes fine). [SUPERSEDED OPEN TEXT:] c114 OMITS FPR callee-saves (MS-x64 xmm6..15) from the `.xdata` unwind codes because DSS spills them low-64 via `movsd`, for which there is NO matching x64 UWOP (`UWOP_SAVE_XMM128` restores a full 16-byte `movaps` slot at an offset/16 node). This is CORRECT for c114's scope тАФ an xmm save does not affect RSP/return-address reconstruction, so `RtlVirtualUnwind`'s STACK walk stays exact тАФ but a language-level exception HANDLER that RESUMES after catching (SEH `__try/__except`, c116) needs the non-volatile xmm6..15 RESTORED to their entry values, which the unwinder can only do from a described `SAVE_XMM128` slot. CLOSING WORK (at c116): either (a) spill xmm callee-saves with a 16-byte `movaps` to a 16-aligned slot + emit `UWOP_SAVE_XMM128` (widen the DSS FPR save form on ms_x64), or (b) prove no `__C_specific_handler` scope resumes into a frame reading a clobbered xmm. Until then a handler that reads a non-volatile xmm after unwinding gets a stale value тАФ a LATENT gap with NO shipped consumer (c114 has no exception handlers). | (closed c116 тАФ prove-no-consumer + the fail-loud invariant) | [[D-WIN64-PDATA-XDATA-UNWIND]] (parent, c114) ┬╖ [[D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS]] (c116) |

The inline stack-probe ([[D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE]], `9+28*pages+3` bytes) crosses 255 B at ~8 guard pages (~32 KiB frame). No shipped corpus reaches it today. | тП│ trigger = a pe64 fn prologue > 255 B in a shipped/corpus build | [[D-WIN64-PDATA-XDATA-UNWIND]] (parent) ┬╖ `D-WIN64-LARGE-FRAME-STACK-PROBE` (the probe length that would cross 255 B) |

| `D-FC12B-WIN64-STRUCT-VARARG` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-20 (FC12b-struct).** Win64 (ms_x64, `VaListStrategy::HomogeneousPointer`) struct-by-value at the variadic boundary + `va_arg(ap, struct)`. **NO new MIR op / LIR kind / carrier, NO lir_callconv / aggregate_abi change** тАФ the design REUSED proven mechanisms: the Win64 ABI passes a by-value struct in EXACTLY ONE arg slot (pow2-тЙд8B тЖТ `classifyAggregate(Win64BySize)` InRegisters[1 GPR piece] = the bytes BY VALUE; every other size тЖТ ByReference = a hidden caller-copy POINTER), placed like a scalar by lir_callconv's existing `cc.slotAligned` walk (Win64 has NO SysV MEMORY-class-to-stack rule тАФ one struct = one slot, register-or-stack BY POSITION). **Caller (`hir_to_mir.cpp` Call arg loop, both walls restructured into a clean VaListStrategy branch):** the two fail-loud HomogeneousPointer guards (ByReference wall + InRegisters wall) тЖТ `appendByValueArg(operands, kids[i], argTy, *abi)` (the SAME helper the non-variadic path uses: InRegisters тЖТ copy-to-temp + load 1 I64 тЖТ 1 value operand; ByReference тЖТ copy-to-temp + push the temp POINTER тЖТ 1 operand) + `runGpr += numGp` (==1) so a subsequent vararg's slot index is right; the SysV atomic-fit/`gpSaveCount` split + `appendByValueStackArg` carrier + the AAPCS64 guard are SKIPPED for HomogeneousPointer and PRESERVED byte-unchanged for SysVRegisterSave/Aapcs64DualCursor. **Callee (`lowerVaArgAggregate` HomogeneousPointer arm):** classify via `byValueClassify(T)` тАФ InRegisters (тЙд8B) тЖТ the slot IS the storage, return apVal (the slot addr), NO extra Load (mirrors the SysV overflow-arm `return ovfArea`); ByReference (>8B) тЖТ Load the pointer from `*apVal` тЖТ the caller-copy addr; both bump `ap` by `namedArgSlotBytes` (one slot, value or pointer тАФ NOT roundUp(size,8)), LINEAR (no diamond), mirroring the scalar HomogeneousPointer va_arg. **Pins (red-on-disable verified):** the 2 fail-loud pins INVERTED to success + 2 new (`Win64VaArgStructLeq8ReturnsSlotAddr` / `Gt8DerefsPointer` тАФ no ICmpUle/carrier, the >8B deref adds a Load; `Win64StructByValueVarargCallLeq8OneSlot` / `Gt8OnePointer` тАФ the Call has exactly 1 value/pointer operand, no carrier). RUN-WITNESSED PE-x86_64 on THIS Windows host: `examples/c-subset/varargs_win64_struct` (a тЙд8B by-value `struct Small`={x,y} AND a >8B by-ref `struct Big`={a,b,c} both through `va_arg(struct)`, anti-folded via mutable global `g_seed`, baseline + full-release-like opt arm тЖТ exit 45). | (closed) | `D-FC12A-VARIADIC-MEMORY-CLASS-STRUCT`; `D-FC12B-WIN64-VARIADIC-CALLEE`; `D-FC12C-AAPCS64-HFA-STRUCT-VARARG` (тЬЕ CLOSED 2026-06-20); `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN` |
| `D-OPT-VALIST-ALLOCA-MEM2REG` | тЬЕтЬЕ **ROOT-CAUSE COMPLETE 2026-06-20 тАФ the code fix is DONE (7f321fd + 061fa23); the persistent CI red was NOT a 3rd code bug but a STALE GitHub PR merge ref.** `refs/pull/28/merge` = `e36dea16` ("Merge **7f321fd** into base", parents `[f563956, 7f321fd]`) = PRE-061fa23 source, while the PR head is correctly `061fa23` (mergeable) тАФ GitHub never regenerated the merge ref after 061fa23, and the `pull_request` CI checks out the merge ref, so EVERY PR #28 run built pre-fix source (proven: byte-identical `.so` BuildId across the 7f321fd + 061fa23 runs despite the changed `.o`). The 061fa23 `lowerAggregateCopy` span fix is verified CORRECT + SUFFICIENT тАФ gcc-13/clang-19 ├Ч libstdc++/libc++ ├Ч O0/O2 ├Ч ASan/valgrind/Electric-Fence + a CI-matching Ubuntu-24.04 container (unfixed 5/5 fail / fixed 5/5 clean); NO third compiler bug exists. REMEDY: a fresh push to feature/0-0-2-p12 regenerates the merge ref тЖТ CI then tests the fix. тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-20** тАФ e0234d8 CI GREEN on ALL 5 legs (run 27885691108: windows-msvc-release + macos-clang-release + linux-clang-asan + linux-gcc-release + linux-arm64-gcc-release all success). ORIGINAL STATUS BELOW: тЬЕ **FIX SHIPPED 2026-06-20 (mark CLOSED after the non-Windows CI legs go green тАФ TWO layered host-dependent-UB bugs surfaced on this corpus, fix commits 7f321fd + this one). THE ┬зB Mem2Reg/va_list HYPOTHESIS WAS REFUTED by investigation тАФ the name is a MISNOMER kept for traceability to the ┬зB that spawned it.** ACTUAL ROOT CAUSE: a PRE-EXISTING use-after-`pop_back` in `TreeBuilder::closeFrame_` (`src/core/types/tree_builder.cpp`) тАФ `Frame& fr = open_.back()` тЖТ `open_.pop_back()` тЖТ then read `fr.openerSpan`/`fr.rule` from the dead back slot. UB: MSVC-release reads the stale-but-intact bytes by luck (passed windows-msvc + the Windows-only local gate), gcc/clang-release + ASan abort (the 4 non-Windows legs). NOT optimizer- or variadic-related тАФ `varargs_win64_struct` merely drove the parser into the speculative-branch cascade-close shape (`trySpeculativeBranch` тЖТ `closeFrameOnce` тЖТ `closeFrame_`); the crash fires during PARSING, before MIR/the optimizer. FIX: snapshot `openerSpan`+`rule` into locals BEFORE `open_.pop_back()`, then `leaveRule` uses the copies тАФ a generic, source/target/format-AGNOSTIC parser-lifetime fix (no identity branch). VERIFIED: 345/345 ctest (no regression) + MSVC-ASan red-on-disable on BOTH the corpus AND `dss_core_test_tree_builder` (revert тЖТ `container-overflow` READ at `closeFrame_` tree_builder.cpp:500 тЖТ restore тЖТ clean). REGRESSION GUARD: the existing TreeBuilder cascade-close tests (`OutOfOrderCloseCascadesAndDoesNotDoubleDiagnose` + every close-path test) are host-independent red-on-disable under any ASan leg; the corpus is a hard-crash witness on gcc-release too. COVERAGE LESSON: the Windows-only local gate STRUCTURALLY cannot catch sanitizer-class UB тАФ the CI ASan/release legs are that gate, so confirm each commit's CI green BEFORE stacking the next cycle (the MSVC-ASan recipe is now a known local tool: `build-asan/` + `/fsanitize=address` RelWithDebInfo + global `/bigobj`, PCH on). **SECOND BUG (HIRтЖТMIR lowering, UNMASKED by the 7f321fd parser fix, fixed in this commit):** a dangling-`std::span`-into-`TypeInterner::operandPool_` heap-use-after-free in `lowerAggregateCopy` (`hir_to_mir.cpp:2490`) тАФ it held `interner.operands(aggTy)` (a span into operandPool_) across the field-copy loop's `interner.pointer(...)` interns, which REALLOCATE the pool тЖТ dangling read. ASan legs = heap-use-after-free; release legs misread the freed bytes тЖТ an invalid TypeId тЖТ `MirBuilder fatal: addInst: 'load' result type invalid`. Host-dependent (MSVC vector growth 1.5├Ч usually has spare capacity тЖТ Windows + local gate pass; libstdc++/libc++ 2├Ч realloc тЖТ the 4 non-Windows legs abort). The TWIN of the already-fixed FC7-C1c hazard at `hir_to_mir.cpp:4660`. FIX: copy the span into an owning `std::vector<TypeId>` before the loop (agnostic; matches the 4660 precedent). VERIFIED: 345/345 ctest + MSVC-ASan forced-realloc red-on-disable (revertтЖТheap-use-after-free at lowerAggregateCopyтЖТrestoreтЖТclean; NO third bug). The investigation audited every `operands()`/`fnParams()`/`scalars()` span site тАФ 2490 was the SOLE remaining unfixed instance. Recurring class (4660 + 2490) тЖТ preventive hardening pinned `D-TYPEINTERNER-OPERAND-SPAN-LIFETIME-GUARD`. _Original (refuted-hypothesis) blocker text below._ ЁЯФ┤ OPEN тАФ ACTIVE BLOCKER (immediate next cycle), discovered 2026-06-20 by the FC12c-struct Step-8.5 self-audit + CI verification. `examples/c-subset/varargs_win64_struct` (FC12b тСб, the ONLY variadic corpus with an `optimizedPipelines` arm) crashes the dss COMPILER ("Subprocess aborted") on ALL 4 non-Windows CI legs (linux-clang-asan, macos-clang-release, linux-gcc-release, linux-arm64-gcc-release) while compiling its optimized pipeline; **windows-msvc-release + the Windows-only local gate PASS** тАФ which masked it through both the тСб and тСв cycles. DETERMINISTIC (41a64d7's single CI run aborted on all 4 legs together тАФ the earlier "flaky/same-sha-flip" read was a sha misattribution; the 15:08 pass was on `c74aeae` тСа which predates the corpus). ┬зA.6 (all CI legs green) is VIOLATED on the branch tip. Leading hypothesis (self-audit forensic, **UNCONFIRMED тАФ needs a linux/asan repro**): Mem2Reg (`src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp`) promotes the `va_list`/`ap` backing alloca to SSA тАФ unsound, because a va_list aliases ABI-defined memory (the prologue-spilled register-save / home area), so the post-promotion access OOBs. Trigger HAS fired. | тЬЕ DONE тАФ the real fix DIFFERS from this hypothesis-based plan (see the description column for the actual TreeBuilder root cause + the copy-before-pop fix). SUPERSEDED plan was: Reproduce on linux (WSL build or the linux-clang-asan config) тЖТ confirm/refute the Mem2RegтЖФva_list root cause тЖТ fix AGNOSTICALLY: disqualify any alloca whose address feeds `VaStart`/`VaArg`/`VaEnd` from Mem2Reg promotion (the rule "a va_list slot is ABI-aliased memory", uniform across every `VaListStrategy`), OR mark the `ap` alloca non-promotable at lowering тАФ NO target/CC/format identity branch. | `D-TYPEINTERNER-OPERAND-SPAN-LIFETIME-GUARD` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-22** тАФ shipped the Option-1 DEBUG generation-guard: `operands()`/`scalars()`/`fnParams()` now return a `GuardedSpan<T>` that captures a `poolGen_` counter (bumped once per pool-mutating intern at the sole `internContent` chokepoint, AFTER the dedup early-return) and ABORTS DETERMINISTICALLY on a read after a later intern тАФ on EVERY host, no ASan / realloc-luck needed; under `NDEBUG` it is a zero-overhead `using` alias to `std::span<T const>`, so the public contract + codegen + all ~234 call sites are byte-identical (built clean with ZERO call-site edits). Pinned by `StaleOperandSpanAfterInternAborts` (debug death test тАФ RED-ON-DISABLE DEMONSTRATED: guard off тЖТ "failed to die"; `GTEST_SKIP` in release where the guard is intentionally absent) + `FreshSpanAndOwningCopySurviveIntern` (no false-fire on immediate read + the own-before-mutate fix pattern survives later interns). The conservative guard found NO 3rd latent instance across the suite (347/347 ctest). Independent code-review CLEAN. `name()` (a `string_view` into the SEPARATE `names_` interner) is deliberately OUT of scope (no incident; extend the same pattern if a names_-realloc hazard ever surfaces). _Original OPEN text:_ PREVENTIVE hardening for a RECURRING bug class; all current instances fixed. `TypeInterner::operands()`/`fnParams()`/`scalars()` return a `std::span` INTO `operandPool_`'s heap buffer; any caller that retains such a span across a subsequent intern (`pointer`/`array`/`internContent` тЖТ `operandPool_.insert` may REALLOCATE) gets a dangling-span heap-use-after-free. This class has bitten TWICE, both fixed reactively via copy-to-owning-vector тАФ `hir_to_mir.cpp:4660` (FC7-C1c, function params) + `hir_to_mir.cpp:2490` (lowerAggregateCopy, the `D-OPT-VALIST-ALLOCA-MEM2REG` cycle) тАФ both host-dependent (MSVC vector growth masks it; libstdc++/libc++ realloc тЖТ non-Windows CI aborts) and INVISIBLE to the Windows-only non-ASan local gate. Trigger: a 3rd instance, OR the next substantive edit to the interner/lowering span sites. | Add a DEBUG-only span-lifetime guard so a dangling-span read aborts DETERMINISTICALLY on every host (not only under ASan / the non-Windows legs): e.g. a generation counter on `operandPool_` bumped on each mutation + a thin debug `SpanView` wrapper capturing the gen at creation and asserting it unchanged on access (zero-cost in release; `operands()` returns the wrapper in debug), OR make `operands()`/`fnParams()`/`scalars()` return an owning small_vector by default (eliminates the class at a small copy cost). | `D-CSUBSET-LINKAGE-UNKNOWN-SPECIFIER-DIAGNOSTIC` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-04 (cycle 14).** Built as decided (agnostic specifier-identity + fail-loud ERROR). **Mechanism**: new per-decl facet `linkageSpecifierIgnoredKinds` (the prefix's STRUCTURAL token KINDS to skip тАФ c-subset: AttributeKeyword/ParenOpen/ParenClose); loader resolves namesтЖТSchemaTokenId (unknownтЖТC_UnknownToken, mirrors variadicMarker; non-array/non-stringтЖТC_InvalidSemantics); `linkageFrom` skips ignored-kind tokens and emits `H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier=0xF00C` on any non-ignored prefix token missing from `linkageSpecifiers` (skip-list DEFAULT = fail-loud). All THREE decl-lowering arms (lowerTopLevel func+var, lowerFunctionDecl, lowerExternDecl) route through the `linkageFrom` chokepoint тАФ coverage by-construction; the **Step-3.5 design-audit caught + closed the lowerExternDecl bypass (Gate 1)**. 2 tests (func+var `__attribute__((bogus))` тЖТ H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier + hir->ok false), REGRESSION-PROVEN (disable emit тЖТ RED). Agnostic (SchemaTokenId + text compare, no hardcode), 193/193. Step-8.5 self-audit VERIFIED-CLEAN. **Latent follow-ups (deferred тАФ no current consumer)**: (i) `linkageFrom`'s `linkageSpecifiers.empty()` early-return means a future lang with prefix + ignoredKinds + EMPTY effects-map would silent-skip тАФ add a loader reject for that contradictory config when a real consumer appears; (ii) `lowerVarLike` (the non-topLevelDecl local-var path) uses raw `visible` тАФ needs `declVisible` IF a future VarDecl-dispatch rule ever gains a `specifierPrefix`. Both fold into `D-DECL-PREFIX-STRIP-SHARED-HELPER`'s shared-chokepoint extraction. тАФ ORIGINAL ┬зB + decision retained below. The ┬зB is resolved тАФ do NOT re-litigate. **Decided design**: a per-language config distinguishes specifier-IDENTITY tokens (lookup-able in `linkageSpecifiers`) from STRUCTURAL syntax (`__attribute__`, parens) тАФ likely a small facet listing the prefix's structural token KINDS to skip (e.g. `AttributeKeyword`/`ParenOpen`/`ParenClose`), so any OTHER prefix token (`StaticKeyword`, the attr `Identifier`) NOT found in `linkageSpecifiers` emits a fail-loud Error + diagnostic; the exact facet shape to be refined via feature-dev + dss-audit next cycle (keep it agnostic тАФ the language declares the structural set, the engine just walks it). Behavior = Error (NOT GCC warn-and-ignore), per the original design-lock + ┬зA.4. Priority: **high (next cycle).** тАФ Cycle-13 review finding (silent-failure-hunter, MEDIUM): an UNRECOGNIZED specifier identifier inside `__attribute__((...))` тАФ a typo (`((wek))`) OR a valid-C attribute c-subset doesn't model (`((noinline))`, `((packed))`) тАФ is SILENTLY ignored by `linkageFrom` (facet miss тЖТ no effect, no diagnostic); the decl silently keeps default (Global) linkage. NOT a valid-code miscompile (static/weak are correct + proven) and GCC-compatible leniency (GCC ignores unknown attributes with a `-Wattributes` warning), but misses the project's fail-loud preference on unrecognized input. **┬зB DECISION (needs user input before building)**: (a) the AGNOSTIC MECHANISM тАФ `linkageFrom`'s flat token-walk cannot distinguish an unknown SPECIFIER from structural SYNTAX (`__attribute__`, parens) without language config; the reviewer's "Identifier-directly-under-attrSpec" distinguisher is c-subset-grammar-specific (would break agnosticism if hardcoded), so a clean fix needs the language to DECLARE which prefix tokens/positions are specifier-IDENTITIES vs pure-syntax (a new facet or a per-rule marker), then the engine emits on an identity-token that misses the facet while still skipping syntax; (b) the BEHAVIOR тАФ Error (strict) vs Warning (GCC `-Wattributes`) vs silent-ignore (current). Priority: **normal** (missing-diagnostic on unrecognized input, not a correctness bug for valid input). | Resolve the ┬зB (mechanism + behavior) with the user, then implement the agnostic specifier-identity recognition + emit the chosen diagnostic; pin with `__attribute__((bogus))` тЖТ diagnostic. Revisit the earlier "unknown-attribute fork = fail-loud error" design note in light of GCC-compat + the agnostic-mechanism cost. | `D-CSUBSET-LINKAGE-SPECIFIERS`; plan 22 pre-OPT7 P1 |
