| `D-AS4-ARM64-BASE-INDEX-LEA` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-16 (arm64 indexed-lea cycle, branch p8).** `lowerGep` (target-blind) emits a 4-op `lea` `[base, index, MemBase(1), MemOffset(0)]` for an indexed pointer access `p[n]`; x86 had the 4-op SIB lea variant but arm64's `lea` had only symbol (ADRP+ADD, D-AS4-3) + base+disp (ADD-imm12) тАФ NO base+index, so EVERY `p[n]` failed loud on arm64 for int AND char alike (exposed by the char value-codegen cycle's `p[n]` witnesses тАФ NOT a char limitation). Closed by: a NEW universal width-0 `EncodingSlotKind::MemOffsetZero` ("memoffset.zero") validator slot тАФ the displacement twin of `MemBaseNoScale` (asserts the GEP's vestigial MemOffset IS zero, writes no bits; an Imm12 reuse was UNSAFE тАФ bits 10..21 overlap Rm in the register-add encoding) тАФ plus an arm64 `lea` 4-op variant `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm` (fixedWord 0x8B000000), wiring baseтЖТrn / indexтЖТrm / membase.noscale / memoffset.zero. Scale=1 + disp=0 (the GEP's only emitted shape today) IS the complete realization. Pins: `Arm64Encoder.BaseIndexLeaEncodesAddRegReg` (0x8B020020 byte-pin) + `BaseIndexLeaNonZeroDispFailsLoud` (memoffset.zero red-on-disable) + `examples/c-subset/char_ptr_indexed` GAINED arm64 targets (was x86-only because of THIS gap; runtime-witnessed char `q[n]`/`p[1]` + int `p[n]` on arm64-ELF qemu тЖТ 131/99/77, byte-identical exits to x86). | (closed) | plan 13 ┬з2.4; commit 69dbf9f (#25); `D-AS4-3` (arm64 symbol lea) + `D-AS-ENCODINGSLOT-EXHAUSTIVE-WARN` (the slot-switch gate the new enumerator passed) + successor `D-AS4-ARM64-INDEXED-LEA-SCALE` |
| `D-AS4-ARM64-INDEXED-LEA-SCALE` | **тП╕ TRIGGER-NOT-FIRED тАФ RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c8, branch p14): ┬зB-surfaced as NOT a real bug, deliberately NOT built (a speculative build would violate trigger-discipline).** Empirically re-confirmed the reframe still holds: `scaleIndexToBytes` (hir_to_mir.cpp:1790/2052) pre-scales every array index to a BYTE offset at HIRтЖТMIR, so the LIR `lea` always carries scale=1; a runtime-indexed WIDE-element array (`int a[N]; a[i]`, 4-byte stride, `i` a runtime var) RUNS on arm64-ELF under qemu TODAY тАФ `examples/c-subset/array_storage_index` exit 42 (re-run 2026-06-23 on WSL qemu) тАФ with NO hardware scaled-lea; the `MemBaseNoScale` slot (fixed32.cpp:895 тЖТ `A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant`) fails any scaleтЙа1 LOUD, so a scaled stride can never silently miscompile. The hardware scaled-lea remains a pure OPT with NO consumer. **REFRAMED 2026-06-17 (array-storage cluster, Option A тАФ `D-MIR-STORAGE-ARRAY-INDEX-GEP`): now a pure CODEGEN OPTIMIZATION, no longer a correctness gap.** The original trigger below ("a scaleтЙа1 indexed GEP exists") now NEVER fires: Option A pre-scales every array index to a BYTE offset at HIRтЖТMIR, so the LIR `lea` always carries scale=1 (the `membase.noscale` guard still fails any scaleтЙа1 LOUD тАФ never a silent wrong stride). Element scaling needs NO hardware scale for CORRECTNESS (and non-pow2 strides could never be hardware-scaled anyway тАФ exactly why MIR pre-multiply is the complete solution). **New trigger:** a future address-mode-synthesis peephole that folds a `Mul-by-pow2 + Gep` into a hardware-scaled `lea` / `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm,LSL #k` is built (an OPT, not speculative). Priority: **opt, trigger-gated.** **Original trigger:** First consumer emits an indexed GEP with a NON-unit element scale (a typed-array `lea [base + index*sizeof(T)]`, scale тИИ {2,4,8}) тАФ the ML6 address-mode synthesis co-design (`lowerGep` emits scale=1 today, so this is unfired). | Add the shifted-register arm64 lea form `ADD Xd, Xn, Xm, LSL #log2(scale)` (the ADD-shifted `imm6` shift-amount field, bits 10..15) + a `membase.scale тЖТ shift-amount` encoder slot; the `membase.noscale` guard already fails scale!=1 LOUD meanwhile (never a silent wrong stride). x86's 4-op lea already encodes the SIB scale. Priority: **opt (trigger-gated)** (build only when a scale-folding peephole consumer exists). | plan 13 ┬з2.4; `D-AS4-ARM64-BASE-INDEX-LEA` (the scale=1 form it extends); `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-WIDE-IMMEDIATE` (sibling shifted-immediate precedent) |

| `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c10).** struct/union FIELDS used a LEGACY positional schema (`name:1, type:0`) so a function-pointer member `int (*f)(int);` (the SQLite-pervasive shape that derailed MOST structs тЖТ the cascade of `};`/scope-unbalance errors) was rejected at the member declarator. FIX = switch `structField`/`unionField` to declarator mode (`head:0, declarator:1`, the SHARED `declarator` rule every other position uses) so the parenDeclarator group + fnSuffix derive the fn-ptr field type for free. FIX = change `castTypeRef`'s `{repeat StarOp}` тЖТ `{repeat pointerLayer}` (`pointerLayer` = `StarOp {repeat ptrQualifier}`, the SAME generic layer every declarator position uses; `ptrQualifier` = const/volatile/restrict). The resolver's co-located cast/typeRef arm counts each `pointerLayer` child as ONE pointer level for `ptrDepth` (alongside the still-supported bare-`StarOp` token form `typeRef`/`sizeofType` keep) and STRIPS the layer's ptrQualifiers тАФ a cast yields an RVALUE with NO top-level cv (C 6.5.4): `(int * const)p` and `(int *)p` intern the SAME `Ptr<int>`, and a post-star VOLATILE is dropped (NO `VolatileQual` on the cast POINTER). тШЕ KEY CORRECTNESS POINT: the c27 PRE-stars-volatile POINTEE path (`volatile u32 *`тЖТ`Ptr<VolatileQual(u32)>`, the head-volatile-before-the-first-star arm) is SEPARATE and UNBROKEN тАФ the star-run boundary for the `baseIsVolatile` scan is now the first `pointerLayer` child, so a post-star volatile inside a layer can NEVER reach the base-volatile wrap (the declaration path's east `* volatile`тЖТ`VolatileQual(Ptr<тАж>)` object-volatile is a DIFFERENT engine, `declaratorDeclaredType`, and stays). AGNOSTIC: reuses the generic `pointerLayer`/`ptrQualifier` rules + `cfg.pointerToken`/`DeclaratorConfig.pointerLayerRule` (no lang/arch/format/keyword hardcode). | `D-CSUBSET-LABEL-BUDGET-CLIFF` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c31).** sqlite3.c:213922 тАФ a C label before a LARGE statement: `json_parse_restart:` preceding a ~900-case JSON `switch` failed P0001 "got ':'". ROOT: `declOrExprStmt` (`alt:[labelStmt,identVarDecl,exprStmt]`, speculative lookahead 256 тЗТ a `lookahead*16` = 4096-token probe budget) speculatively parses `labelStmt = Identifier Colon statement` to COMPLETION; a labeled statement >4096 tokens exhausts the probe budget тЖТ rollback тЖТ exprStmt тЖТ P0001 at the `:` (confirmed empirically: a small labeled switch parses, a ~900-case one does not). | тЬЕ FIX = a NEW general parser facet `commitAfterPrefix` (a PEG "cut"), AGNOSTIC (names no token/rule/keyword/language). `labelStmt` KEEPS its `Identifier Colon statement` shape (so a label stays correct in EVERY context, incl. a braceless control-flow body тАФ `if(x) L: s;` тЗТ `s` IS the if's body). The facet: once a candidate's FIXED leading token-prefix (`predictivePrefixLen` тАФ here `Identifier Colon` + the first `statement` token) is consumed in a speculative probe WITHOUT failure, the parser COMMITS (`probe.commit()`) and hands the STILL-OPEN frame to the non-speculative dispatch loop тАФ the rest of the rule parses with NO budget (exactly the unique-production direct-descent's "open frame, let the outer loop drive it"). SOUND because after `Identifier Colon` no other `declOrExprStmt` alt can match (neither identVarDecl nor exprStmt has Colon in 2nd position), so committing discards nothing; a non-label `foo + bar;` fails at the `Colon` slot BEFORE the prefix is consumed, so the cut never fires (falls through to exprStmt). Wiring: `CompiledRule::commitAfterPrefix` (bool) тЖР loader parses an optional boolean `commitAfterPrefix` on the shape body (fail-loud if non-boolean; mutually-exclusive with `commitRequiresTypeName`); `GrammarSchema::commitAfterPrefix(RuleId)` accessor; the cut block inside `trySpeculativeBranch`'s probe loop after the post-`stepOnce` diag/desync rechecks; `SpeculationProbe::startPos()` accessor. Only `labelStmt` carries the flag (`"commitAfterPrefix": true`). NO HIR/MIR/verifier/lowering change (`git diff --stat`: 6 files тАФ parser.cpp, compiled_shape.hpp, grammar_schema.{hpp,cpp}, grammar_schema_json.cpp, c-subset.lang.json). тШЕ BRACELESS-BODY CORRECTNESS (the case that doomed any flatten-labelStmt alternative) VERIFIED at runtime: `if(x) L: g=42;` exits 5 for x=0 (the labeled `g=42` is the if's body and is NOT executed) and 42 for x=1. WITNESS: 2 HIR pins (`LabelBeforeOversizeStatementParsesPastProbeBudget` тАФ a label before a ~5500-token block parses clean, red-on-disable тЗТ P0001; `LabelAsBracelessIfBodyStaysInsideTheIf` тАФ the silent-miscompile guard: the if's then-branch IS the LabelStmt with the assignment UNDER it) + runtime corpus `label_before_switch_goto` (a `goto` INTO a label before a multi-case switch, fold-resistant via feed() + 3 goto passes тЖТ exit 42; 4 targets pe/elf/qemu-arm64/macho + release arm). 452тЖТ453 ctest; anchor guard OK. | (closed тАФ the `commitAfterPrefix` PEG-cut facet IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c31; cited in `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`labelStmt` `commitAfterPrefix`), `src/core/types/compiled_shape.hpp` + `src/core/types/grammar_schema.hpp` + `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (the facet + loader), `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` (the cut); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; sibling facet to the FC2 `commitRequiresTypeName` type-name commit guard; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |

(1) CONFIG: a NEW OPTIONAL declarator role `prototypeParamScopeRule: "paramList"` (beside `fnSuffixParamsRule`; a language OMITTING it = prior behavior тАФ toy/tsql declare no `declarators` block). (2) STRUCT+LOADER: `DeclaratorConfig::prototypeParamScopeRule` (+ name field) loaded via the same `readOptionalRuleRole` discipline + added to the closed-keys set (15тЖТ16). (3) SCOPE-OPEN: Pass-1 opens a THROWAWAY scope for any node of that rule UNLESS it is a function DEFINITION's OWN param list тАФ detected by the SHARED config-driven shape tests (`directDeclaratorIsNameBearing` тАФ the suffix sits on a NAME-bearing direct declarator, NOT a fn-pointer whose base is a `parenDeclarator`) AND `declNodeIsFunctionDefinition` (the enclosing decl's kindByChild resolves to Function тАФ a body present). So: a fn-ptr member/typedef/param тЗТ base is a parenDeclarator тЗТ ISOLATE; a bare prototype тЗТ name-bearing but no body тЗТ ISOLATE (its name re-homes to file scope via the separate `isProto` path); a definition тЗТ name-bearing + body тЗТ do NOT open (params bind into the topLevelDecl scope, body-visible). (4) тШЕ 3-PASS CONSISTENCY: the whole "does this node open a child scope (and which)" decision is FACTORED into ONE shared predicate `nodeOpensChildScope` (folding the c25 `scopeByRule`+`isDefinitionAtNode` gate AND the c32 prototype case) used by ALL THREE passes тАФ `pass1Node` (pushes), `resolveDeclTypes` + the `pass2` driver (re-derive by anchor lookup) тАФ so a param can never bind in one scope yet be looked up in another. The fn-ptr's TYPE (FnSig param types via `collectParamTypes`, which walks the tree not the name symbols) is UNCHANGED тАФ only NAME scoping changes. | (closed тАФ the per-declarator prototype scope + the shared `nodeOpensChildScope` predicate IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c32; cited in `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`declarators.prototypeParamScopeRule`) + `src/core/types/semantic_config.hpp` (`DeclaratorConfig::prototypeParamScopeRule`) + `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (loader) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`nodeOpensChildScope` / `isPrototypeParamScopeNode` / `directDeclaratorIsNameBearing` / `declNodeIsFunctionDefinition`); witness = 9 semantic pins (`FnPtrStructMembersSharedParamNameNoRedecl`, `FnPtrTypedefsSharedParamNameNoRedecl`, `FnPtrParamsSharedParamNameNoRedecl`, `FnPtrParamNameDoesNotLeakToEnclosingScope` (collision-form leak) + `FnPtrParamNameDoesNotResolveOutsideDeclarator` (the DECISIVE resolve-form leak guard тАФ an outer use of a fn-ptr-typedef param name is S_UndeclaredIdentifier; distinguishes a leaked-but-non-colliding param the type-only collision pin cannot), `NestedFnPtrParamsSharedNamesNoRedecl`, `DefinitionParamsRemainBodyVisible`, `PlainPrototypesSharedParamNameClean`, `DuplicateParamNameInSingleFnPtrStillCollides` тАФ 7 red-on-disable, the 2 PRESERVE pins green both ways) + runtime corpus `fnptr_struct_dispatch` (a struct of fn-ptr members with SHARED param names, assigned + called through тЖТ exit 42; 4 targets pe/elf/qemu-arm64/macho + release). 453тЖТ454 ctest; reuses the c10 `hasFnSuffixOnName` / `isDefinitionAtNode` machinery; a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |
| `D-CSUBSET-ARRAY-SIZE-INFERENCE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c34).** sqlite3.c:506 `SQLITE_API const char sqlite3_version[] = SQLITE_VERSION;` (i.e. `const char x[] = "3.x.y";`) тАФ an array declared with an EMPTY bound `[]` AND an initializer was rejected `error[S000B] S_NonConstantArrayLength` (the resolver returned an incomplete array ONLY in a flexible-array-member context; a `[]` VARIABLE with a missing length always erred). C 6.7.9p22: such an array infers its length from the initializer тАФ `char x[] = "abc"` тЖТ `char[4]` ("abc"+NUL), `int a[] = {1,2,3}` тЖТ `int[3]`. | тЬЕ FIX (config-driven, REUSE; one completion site so all downstream agree). (1) The array-suffix `[]` (absent length) already yields an INCOMPLETE array when "missing-length-is-OK" (`allowFlexibleArray`); REUSE that path for a VARIABLE by RELAXING the flag at the Pass-1.5 var/global declared-type site (`resolveDeclTypes`, semantic_analyzer.cpp) to `decl.allowFlexibleArray || <this declarator has an initializer>` тАФ so a `[]`-with-init resolves to `T[]` (incomplete) instead of erroring, while a `[]` with NO init keeps the original flag (false for a var/global rule) тЗТ `S_NonConstantArrayLength` still fires (never a silently-sized array). The group recursion into a nested declarator still passes false (a nested fn-ptr param's `[]` errors). (2) A NEW helper `completeIncompleteArrayFromInit` (Pass-1.5, the SINGLE completion site тЖТ sizeof/layout/element-access/BOTH HIR paths observe the SAME sized array) completes the incomplete array: a STRING-literal init тЖТ N = decoded-bytes + 1 (the NUL), counted via the SHARED `decodeAdjacentStringBodies` chokepoint (escapes count once тАФ `"\x41\x42"` тЖТ 2+NUL=3; adjacent concat `"ab" "cd"` тЖТ 5); a BRACE init тЖТ N = the count of TOP-LEVEL `initElement` children of the brace list. The element type is recovered from the incomplete array's `operands()[0]`, so a malformed escape leaves it incomplete (no guessed size; the HIR tier fails loud on the bad literal). NO HIR/MIR change тАФ the existing explicit-size lowering (`char x[4]="abc"`, `int a[3]={тАж}`) applies once the type is completed to the same sized array (the HIR var/global path reads the symbol's resolved `.type`). AGNOSTIC: two new by-NAME index rules (`braceInitList`/`initElement`, resolved like `stringLiteralExprRule`) + the existing `stringLiteralExprRule`/body-token; no lang/arch/format/keyword hardcode. PRESERVE-verified: explicit `[N]` unchanged (completion is a no-op on an already-sized array тАФ `char x[8]="abc"` stays `[8]`, not re-derived to 4); over-long explicit `char x[2]="abc"` keeps its current S0003; a bare `[]` no-init stays incomplete/error. тШЕ AUDIT-CAUGHT REGRESSION (fixed before commit): an EMPTY brace `int a[] = {}` (top-level count 0) returned the incomplete array with NO diagnostic тАФ and the unguarded HIR/MIR tier LOOPED on the -1 sentinel length (a compiler HANG; a fail-loud VIOLATION the relaxation introduced тАФ pre-c34 it was a clean S_NonConstantArrayLength). FIX: `completeIncompleteArrayFromInit`'s un-sizable returns (empty brace; OR no string/brace initializer found at all) now call `failUnsized()` тЖТ emit S_ArrayLengthOutOfRange (an inferred 0/undeterminable length = the non-positive `int a[0]` case) + return InvalidType, matching the no-init clean-error path (no hang). WITNESS: 6 semantic pins (`ArraySizeInferredFromStringInit` Array len 4 + NOT incomplete; `ArraySizeInferredFromBraceInit` Array<I32,3>; `ArraySizeInferredFromInitLocal`; `ExplicitArraySizeUnchangedByInference`; `EmptyArrayNoInitNotSized` тАФ no-init S_NonConstantArrayLength; тШЕ `ArraySizeInferenceEmptyBraceFailsLoud` тАФ empty-brace `{}` тЖТ S_ArrayLengthOutOfRange, NOT a hang) + runtime corpus `array_size_inference` (an inferred `char s[]="DSS"`, `int a[]={2,7,11,22}`, and the sqlite frontier shape `const char ver[]="9"` read across a non-inlined call тЖТ exit 42; 4 targets pe/elf/qemu-arm64/macho + release arm). 455тЖТ456 ctest; anchor guard OK. тШЕ NOTE (pre-existing, NOT c34): `sizeof(a[0])` mis-resolves to `sizeof(a)` for ANY array (reproduces with explicit `int a[5]`) тАФ independent of inference; size correctness was proven via an out-of-the-explicit-bound read (`int a[]={0,0,0,0,42}; a[4]`тЖТ42) + `sizeof(x)`тЖТ4 for the string. | (closed тАФ the relaxed-flag REUSE + the single `completeIncompleteArrayFromInit` site IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c34; cited in `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the relaxed `allowIncomplete` at the Pass-1.5 var site + `completeIncompleteArrayFromInit` + the `braceInitList`/`initElement` index rules); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier (sqlite3_version line 506); reuses the FAM incomplete-array path (`D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR` c10) + the adjacent-string decoder (`D-CSUBSET-ADJACENT-STRING-CONCAT` c20); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |
| `D-CSUBSET-UNARY-PLUS` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c12).** The unary `+` operator (`int x = +1;`, C 6.5.3.3) was rejected (P0009) тАФ only unary `-` was in the prefix op-group; SQLite uses `+` as a no-op sign/grouping marker. | тЬЕ Grammar = add `"+"` to the prefix operator-group + a `PlusOp тЖТ Pos` `unaryOps` row. CSTтЖТHIR's `combineUnaryOp` lowers `+x` to its OPERAND DIRECTLY (identity тАФ there is no `Pos`/`Identity` HirOpKind; the integer promotion is already realized by the lazy-consumer model, sub-int values live promoted in 32-bit regs), gated on an ARITHMETIC (or Enum) operand тАФ a pointer/struct operand fails LOUD (`H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind` "operand of unary '+' must have arithmetic type"; STRICTER than `-`, which has no such gate). The semantic typer's `combineUnary` makes `Pos` operand-preserving (explicit, mirroring PreInc). `#if +1` works for free (`pp_if_eval` already special-cased a `Pos` target as identity). GRAMMAR + thin HIR, AGNOSTIC (config target name `Pos`, no hardcoded `+`). NOTE: making `+` a prefix op made `(a) + b` record a type-name candidate (the cast-vs-binary ambiguity) exactly like the pre-existing `(q) - x` тАФ the `ParserCastExpr` pin was updated to match. WITNESS: corpus `unary_plus` (`+1`, `+(x+40)`, `+ +x` тЖТ exit 42; pe-native + elf/macho/arm64 + release arm). | p18 Cluster G c12; cited in `examples/c-subset/unary_plus/` + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (prefix group + `Pos` unaryOps row) + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`combineUnaryOp`). |

WITNESS: corpus `pp_if_char` (`#if 'A' == 65` TRUE + `#if 'A' == '\301'` FALSE-on-ASCII octal-escape guard тЖТ exit 42; pe-native + elf/macho/arm64 + release arm) + the octal arm proven by `'\101'`==65 in normal code. | p18 Cluster G c12; cited in `examples/c-subset/pp_if_char/` + `src/analysis/preprocess/pp_if_eval.cpp` (`parsePrimary` char primary) + `src/core/types/char_decode.hpp` (octal escape arm). |

| `D-CSUBSET-DIVISION-CORPUS-MATERIALIZE-BUG` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-04** (cycle 10r BUG FIX). Root cause identified + fixed: NOT a materialize-pass bug. The cycle-10q compound encoding packed `[REX.W 0x99, REX.W 0xF7 /7]` as a single opcode's `opcode` array. The x86-variable encoder auto-emits ONE REX prefix at instruction start computed from operand high bits (e.g., 0x41 = REX.B for R14). Per x86 decode the LAST REX before the opcode wins тАФ the embedded second literal 0x48 (REX.W only) superseded the auto-REX, losing REX.B. IDIV operand decoded as RSI (without REX.B) instead of R14 тЖТ divide-by-zero trap. Cycle 10r SPLIT the compound into separate `cqo`/`idiv_op`/`xor_rdx_zero`/`div_op` opcodes (each a single instruction with its own auto-REX). | (closed) | plan 12 ML5 (asm encoder) + ML6 (regalloc); paired with D-CSUBSET-DIVISION-OP-CODEGEN closure |

RED-on-disable DEMONSTRATED + restored: commenting out the `implicitClobbersCrossedBy` consultation lands covering vregs on rdx(ord 2)/rax(ord 0) at EVERY sweep point. Runtime corpus witness: `examples/c-subset/division/` gained the shift_ops-style `pressured()` arm (10 locals + q/x/n live across a runtime 100/7); with the exclusion disabled the BASELINE arm's alloca-ADDRESS vreg (&a9) lands on RDX, CDQ zeroes it, the post-div reload null-derefs тЖТ deterministic STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION 0xC0000005 (empirically verified, 3 identical runs; the optimized arm folds the call chain to a constant тАФ the baseline arm is the witness, exactly the shift_ops discipline); GREEN exit-14 witnessed on PE native + WSL x86_64-ELF + qemu arm64-ELF (arm64 native SDIV = no implicit registers, regression arm). **Original finding**: Cycle 10r (2026-06-04, deferred тАФ non-blocking after 10r corpus closure): the regalloc unit pin `LirRegAlloc.DivisorVregExcludesImplicitClobberSet` is unpressured тАФ the linear-scan picks R14 for the divisor naturally (because R3..R13 are also free), so the pin passes EVEN WITH the implicit-clobber-exclusion DISABLED. The R14 byte-pin tests (`IDivOpR14EmitsREX_B_49_F7_FE` + analogs for R8/R15) + the end-to-end corpus `division/` returning 14 jointly attest the mechanism at the byte + binary tiers; the pressured-allocator pin would add an additional unit-tier strength but is no longer load-bearing for the close. **Partial sibling closure 2026-06-11 (sweep-c1 CRITICAL fix): the SHIFT-RESULT exclusion now has exactly the pressured pin this row prescribes тАФ `PressuredShiftResultExcludesImplicitInputAndClobberSet` sweeps 10..16 live values; with the result-def exclusion disabled, EVERY sweep point lands the shl result on rcx (ordinal 1) тЖТ RED (demonstrated + restored). The DIV-DIVISOR pressured pin (this row's original subject) remains open.** | Build a pressured synthetic LIR: 13+ live values across the compound ops, exhausting all GPRs except RDX/RAX, FORCING the allocator's hand toward those forbidden regs. With exclusion enabled: divisor spills OR picks a callee-saved reg. With exclusion disabled: divisor lands in RDX/RAX, pin RED. (The shift-result sibling pin in tests/lir/test_lir_regalloc.cpp is the template тАФ same sweep recipe over a `a/b` div under pressure.) | tests/lir/test_lir_regalloc.cpp |

Pins: `TypedefNameStarDeclaresPointerLocal` / `TypedefNameStarCommitsDeclarationStatement` (semantic + parser tiers), `TypedefFnPtrAliasDeclaresSameInternedType`; corpus `typedef_decl_position` (4-target runtime 42 тАФ `myint x` + `MyP *pp` declaration-position witnesses) + `typedef_struct_body` (tagged + anonymous bodies, runtime 42) + `fnptr_declare` (`H h = fp;`). **The REMAINDER lives in `D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-FIELD-EXTERN-POSITION` (below): structField/unionField/externDecl kept the legacy `typeRef` spine, so typedef-named FIELDS and EXTERNS (+ declarator-shaped externs) still do not parse тАФ explicitly NOT closed here.** тАФ *Original row (audit trail):* **OPEN (created 2026-06-11, audit-residue sweep c2, `D-AUDIT-BOOKKEEPING-TRUTH` (e)) тАФ promoted from the `D-CSUBSET-COMPOUND-LITERAL-TYPEDEF` closed row's buried residue (c).** RE-VERIFIED by CLI probe this sweep: `typedef struct P { int a; } MyP;` does NOT parse (`P0001` expected Identifier got `{` at 1:18 тАФ typedefDecl rides `typeRefAllowingStruct`, which admits the struct KEYWORD reference but not a struct BODY in typedef position) and `MyP p;` does NOT parse in DECLARATION position (`P0009` at the `;` тАФ varDeclHead/typeBase lack the bare-Identifier base that `castTypeRef` gained in FC2/FC3.5; typedef names work in CAST and COMPOUND-LITERAL position only). So a typedef'd struct can be named in expressions but never declared as a variable's type тАФ the declaration grammar is the gap, the binder sketch machinery (FC2) already resolves typedef names. | (closed at the declaration-statement/param/typedef-head scope; field/extern remainder = `D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-FIELD-EXTERN-POSITION`) | promoted from `D-CSUBSET-COMPOUND-LITERAL-TYPEDEF` residue (c); plan-23 ┬з0.1 FC4 row; `D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-FIELD-EXTERN-POSITION` |
| `D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-22 (FC-deferral close-out loop cycle 3, branch p14).** A C function PROTOTYPE (`int f(int);`) is now a function DECLARATION that MERGES with a later definition into ONE symbol тАФ forward/mutual recursion works (the registry trigger). **As-built (plan-locked GO-WITH-FIXES, 4 MF folded; delegated build + 3 independent verification passes):** the 3-pass pre-order already pre-binds all symbols (forward CALLS already worked); the merge hooks the Pass-1 bind-collision site with a 4-CASE direction table keyed on proto-vs-def (`SymbolRecord.isProtoDeclaration` set SYNTACTICALLY at Pass 1 via a shared `hasFnSuffixOnName` тАФ a fn-suffix on the name's direct declarator, distinguishing a proto from a fn-pointer): **protoтЖТdef** = the DEFINITION wins the binding (`injectBinding`), proto absorbed; **defтЖТproto / protoтЖТproto / externтЖТproto** = redundant decl, keep prior, absorb the new (its name binds to its OWN record so the HIR skip + compat compare both work тАФ a deliberate, documented deviation); **defтЖТdef / externтЖТdef** = the existing `S_RedeclaredSymbol` (two bodies / scope-out). Pass 1.5 UPGRADES a proto's kind VariableтЖТFunction (callable) instead of `S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator`. A post-Pass-1.5 sweep compares each merged pair's resolved FnSig TypeId (interner equality = STRUCTURAL: return type + params + count + cc + variadic) тЖТ NEW `S_IncompatibleRedeclaration` (0xE022) on mismatch. The HIR lowerer emits NO node for an `isProtoDeclaration || isAbsorbedProto` declarator (no spurious FnSig-typed Global тАФ the pre-change silent path). **Fail-loud (all verified):** incompatible sig тЖТ S0022; a proto declared-but-called-but-never-defined тЖТ loud `H0009` unbound Ref at HIRтЖТMIR (NOT a silent FnSig-global read тАФ the make-or-break the plan-lock caught); two real defs / extern+def тЖТ S0002. AGNOSTIC (merge reads DeclarationKind/effectiveKind/directRule/fnSuffixRule from config, no identity branch). **тШЕ Found+fixed a LATENT pre-existing issue:** 10 MIR/LIR variadic tests declared the callee as a BARE prototype `int sink(int n, ...);` and leaned on the now-removed spurious-FnSig-global behavior (the call resolved through a data global despite an unchecked S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator) тАФ corrected to DEFINE the callee (assertions unchanged). **Witnesses:** corpus `fn_prototype` (mutual recursion `is_even`/`is_odd` via forward proto, value-divergent тЖТ exit 42) on x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [macho macos leg]; `fn_prototype_incompat_error` (expectDiagnostics S_IncompatibleRedeclaration 6:5); 5 semantic unit pins (protoтЖТdef merge / idempotence / defтЖТproto / incompatible-loud / forward-call) DEMONSTRATED red-on-disable (merge off тЖТ 3 tests + corpus fail). 364/364 ctest; independent plan-lock + pr-review (empirical, runtime-witnessed the use-resolution ordering) + my own diff/gate/CLI review тАФ all CLEAN. **RESIDUALS (fail-loud, named exclusions тАФ NOT silent):** (1) `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE` (below) тАФ a block-scope proto (C 6.7.6.3, `int g(int);` inside a function) SHADOWS the file-scope function rather than referring to it, so a block-proto + file-def fails loud (H0009) instead of resolving to the file def; (2) `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE` (below) тАФ `extern int f(int); ...def` stays S_RedeclaredSymbol (the externтЖТExternFunction machine differs; bare-proto+def is the C-idiomatic forward decl and the deliverable). тАФ ORIGINAL DEFERRAL (created 2026-06-11, FC4 c1 stage 2a): the FC4 declarator grammar makes a C function PROTOTYPE (`int f(int);` тАФ declaration-without-definition, at file or block scope) PARSE for the first time (head + declarator-with-fnSuffix + `;`), but declaration-merging (a later definition completing the prototype) and prototype-only emission are NOT implemented тАФ `extern` declarations carry the cross-unit-declaration role today. The semantic tier fails LOUD: a Variable-kind declarator whose folded type is a bare `FnSig` emits `S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator` ("function prototype declarations are not supported here; use 'extern'") rather than silently minting an FnSig-typed data global (which would miscompile at MIR). Fn-POINTER objects (`int (*fp)(int)`) are unaffected (type = Ptr<FnSig>, fully supported). | (closed) | (closed) | FC4 stage 2b registry sweep; `semantic_analyzer.cpp` Pass-1 4-case merge + Pass-1.5 proto upgrade + compat sweep; `cst_to_hir.cpp` proto-node skip; `S_IncompatibleRedeclaration` 0xE022; plan 23 FC4 |
| `D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (robustness sweep, user-authorized тАФ the user's authorization fired the "robustness sweep" trigger; branch feature/0-0-2-p14, 393/393 ctest MSVC Release + MinGW).** As-built: a block-scope function prototype now RE-HOMES onto the file scope (new `fileScopeOf()` helper in `semantic_analyzer.cpp`; Pass-1 `if(isProto) bindScope=fileScopeOf(...)`) so the existing FC fn-prototype 4-case merge (`D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` тЬЕ) resolves it against the file definition (C 6.2.2p4 тАФ a no-linkage block decl of a function refers to the external); `+ warnIfUnused = decl.warnIfUnused && !isProto` (a re-homed proto is never an unused-variable). | `D-CSUBSET-TENTATIVE-DEFINITION-MERGE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c33).** sqlite3.c:16250 then 24217 тАФ `SQLITE_PRIVATE u32 sqlite3WhereTrace;` (a file-scope TENTATIVE definition, no initializer) then `SQLITE_PRIVATE u32 sqlite3WhereTrace = 0;` (the real definition) collided `error[S0002] S_RedeclaredSymbol` (also `sqlite3_temp_directory`/`sqlite3_data_directory`). C 6.9.2: a file-scope object declared with NO initializer and NO `extern` is a TENTATIVE definition тАФ any number of tentatives + at most ONE real (initialized) definition of the same name MERGE into ONE object. ROOT: only an `extern`/proto NON-DEFINING declaration was treated as mergeable (`D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE` тЬЕ, via `nonDefiningDeclaration` + `mergeOrCollideRedeclaration`); a bare tentative was minted as a full DEFINITION, so the merge's `bothDefinitions` arm fired тЖТ S0002. тЬЕ FIX (the non-defining axis EXTENDED, AGNOSTIC тАФ config roles only, no lang/arch/format/keyword hardcode): the Pass-1 variable bind computes `isTentativeDefinition` = (effectiveKind Variable) тИз ┬мisProto тИз ┬м`nonDefiningDeclaration` тИз a declarator-LIST row (`declaratorListChild` тАФ EXCLUDES single-`declaratorChild` PARAMETER rows, so a prototype param sharing a name never merges) тИз bindScope == `fileScopeOf(current)` (FILE-scope only тАФ a block-scope `int x; int x;` and a struct/union MEMBER dup, bound in a body scope, STAY S0002) тИз the init-declarator carries NO initializer (a NEW structural `declaratorHasInitializer` helper тАФ the init-declarator's lone non-declarator visible-Internal child, mirroring the HIR global-init lowering so the two can't drift). It folds into the merge's non-defining flag (`newNonDef = isProto || isExtern || isTentativeDefinition`) AND is RECORDED on a NEW `SymbolRecord.isTentativeDefinition` (so a LATER redeclaration sees THIS symbol as `priorNonDef` and merges). The 4-case `mergeOrCollideRedeclaration` table then handles every shape WITHOUT a new state: tentativeтЖТdef = the def wins the binding + its init; tentativeтЖФtentative / defтЖТtentative = keep the survivor (one surviving object); defтЖТdef (BOTH initialized) = S0002 STAYS. A TYPE CONFLICT (`int g; int* g = 0;`) fails loud `S_IncompatibleRedeclaration` via the EXISTING post-1.5 merged-decl type-compat sweep (interner-TypeId identity тЖТ target-aware: `int g; long g = 5;` MERGES under LLP64 where `long`тЙб`int` but conflicts under LP64/ELF where `long` is 64-bit тАФ VERIFIED both targets). NO HIR/MIR change тАФ the surviving (non-absorbed) symbol lowers to ONE global with the right init via the existing `isAbsorbedProto` skip + the `.bss` tentative-zero-init path. тШЕ Two existing tests UPDATED to match conformant C (real GCC accepts both): `test_declarator_engine` `SameNameTwiceInOneListRedeclares`тЖТ`тАжTentativeDefinitionsMerge` (`base x, x;` тАФ two same-list tentatives тАФ now MERGES, was asserted-collide); the FFI `DuplicateExternRedeclarationRejectedByFrontend` STAYS green (the param-exclusion preserves its prototype-param collision). WITNESS: 7 semantic pins (tentative+def / two-tentatives / static-tentative+def MERGE; тШЕ PRESERVE two-real-defs collide + block-scope dup collide + type-conflict fails-loud; extern+def still merges) + runtime corpus `tentative_definition` (tentative+def + def+tentative + two-tentatives, each read across a NON-INLINED call тЖТ exit 42; 4 targets pe/elf/qemu-arm64/macho + release). 454тЖТ455 ctest. тАФ *Original OPEN row (audit trail):* **OPEN тАФ normal (created 2026-06-24, robustness sweep, branch feature/0-0-2-p14).** Two NON-extern OBJECT declarations of the same name at file scope тАФ `int g; int g;` or `int g; int g = 5;` (C 6.9.2 TENTATIVE definitions: a file-scope object declaration with no initializer and no `extern` is a tentative definition that coalesces with a later real or tentative definition into a single object) тАФ still collide LOUD `S_RedeclaredSymbol`. Only an `extern` NON-DEFINING declaration merges with a definition (`D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE` тЬЕ, via the `nonDefiningDeclaration` flag); a bare tentative definition is NOT marked non-defining, so the shared `mergeOrCollideRedeclaration()` collide-arm fires. Rare, FAIL-LOUD (S0002), NOT a silent miscompile. | (closed) | (closed) | p19 Cluster G c33; cited in `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`isTentativeDefinition` Pass-1 fold + `declaratorHasInitializer` + `mergeOrCollideRedeclaration` `priorNonDef`) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_model.hpp` (`SymbolRecord.isTentativeDefinition`) + `examples/c-subset/tentative_definition/`; GENERALIZES `D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE` (the non-defining/extern axis) + `D-CSUBSET-FN-PROTOTYPE` (the 4-case merge table); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green. |

The 4 GATED sites are init(├Ч2)/call-arg/return; this surfaced a SEPARATE pre-existing strictness asymmetry тЖТ new `D-SEMANTIC-ASSIGN-STMT-ASSIGNABILITY-BYPASS` (below). тАФ ORIGINAL DEFERRAL (created 2026-06-18, FC8 cycle-5 investigation, branch p10): A cross-signedness implicit conversion in an ASSIGNMENT CONTEXT тАФ `int x = u;`, `x = u;`, `return i+u;` (int-returning fn), `f(u)` (int param) where one side is signed and the other unsigned тАФ is REJECTED `S_TypeMismatch` (S0003); a few return forms (unsigned literal, intтЖТunsigned) surface `S_ReturnTypeMismatch` (S0008). **This is INTENTIONAL, UNIFORM, and already DOCUMENTED:** `isAssignable` (`type_rules.hpp` lines 83-86) deliberately makes cross-signedness (and intтЖФfloat) NOT assignable тАФ *"languages requiring them widen their config rather than getting silent C-style implicit conversions."* It is NOT a return-specific bug: init / assignment / call-arg / return ALL reject identically through the one `isAssignable` rule (the originating report's "return inconsistent with assignment" premise was DISPROVEN тАФ `int x = u;` rejects too, same S0003). **But** C 6.3.1.3 / 6.5.16.1 MANDATE an implicit signedтЖФunsigned assignment conversion (value-preserving in-range, modular out-of-range), so for the full-C arc + SQLite (which mixes `int`/`unsigned`/`size_t` freely) c-subset MUST admit it тАФ via the SAME config-gated opt-in as Bool/Char/Enum, default-strict for non-C schemas. | (closed) | **Closing work (mirrors `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-INT-CONVERSION` / `D-CSUBSET-CHAR-INT-WIDENING` EXACTLY):** a new `intCrossSignednessConverts` gate in `semantic_config.hpp` (c-subset ON; default false) + an `isAssignable` cross-signedness arm (admit signedтЖФunsigned within the int ranks, both widening AND narrowing per C) threaded to the 4 semantic callers (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` 2792/2840 init, 3161 call-arg, 3988 return) + HIR `coerce()` materializing the intтЖФunsigned Cast (a width-exact reinterpret/mov тАФ no value change at equal width) + strict red-on-disable tests + a runtime corpus (`return i+u` exit-code witness). | plan 23 FC; | `D-SEMANTIC-ASSIGN-STMT-ASSIGNABILITY-BYPASS` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (robustness sweep, user-authorized тАФ the user's authorization fired the "robustness sweep" trigger; branch feature/0-0-2-p14, 393/393 ctest MSVC Release + MinGW).** As-built: the assign-STATEMENT arm (`assignByRule`) now routes through the SAME gated `isAssignable()` chokepoint the 4 init/call/return sites use (same conversion gates + null-pointer admission, S_TypeMismatch positioned at the RHS), gated to PLAIN assign (`target=="Assign" && compoundBase.empty()`, so compound `+=` is EXCLUDED as the arithmetic-conversion path). So `x = f;` now rejects like `int x = f;`, and cross-sign at an assignment statement is consistently GATED. Corpus `assign_stmt_assignability_error` + 4 pins, red-on-disable; blast-radius probe = zero over-rejection. **REGRESSION FIX 2026-06-24 (this cycle, branch feature/0-0-2-p14, 394/394 MSVC Release + MinGW + Debug):** routing the assign-stmt RHS through `isAssignable` exposed a PRE-EXISTING GAP in that chokepoint тАФ it had NO function-to-pointer decay arm, so a bare function NAME assigned / initialized / passed to a matching function pointer (`fp = add` тАФ the most common fn-ptr idiom: vtables / callbacks / dispatch tables) was wrongly rejected S0003, while `fp = &add` (already a pointer) passed (the asymmetry that isolated it; dss-state 90тЖТ88, `fn_fnptr_call` + `fn_fnptr_deref_call` regressed). FIXED by adding the C 6.3.2.1p4 function-to-pointer decay arm to the SHARED `isAssignable` chokepoint (`type_rules.hpp`, mirroring the array-to-pointer decay; ungated structural-decay family per `D-LANG-STRUCTURAL-DECAY-OPT-OUT`; pinned to the SAME interned FnSig so an incompatible-signature assignment stays a loud mismatch) тАФ one arm clears assignment + initializer + call-argument at once (also clears `fn_fnptr_callback`, the related call-arg gap), with NO HIR `coerce()` change (a bare function Ref already lowers to its ADDRESS at MIR time, which is why `fp = add` compiled AND ran pre-sweep тАФ only the new SEMANTIC check rejected it). Corpus `fnptr_bare_decay` (exit 42; bare assign + init + callback; fold-resistant self-recursive `apply`; baseline + full-release-like + shipped-release) + semantic pin `BareFunctionNameDecaysToPointerInEveryPosition` (3 positions + the incompatible-signature negative), BOTH red-on-disable DEMONSTRATED (arm disabled тЖТ the pin fails on all 3 positions AND the example fails to build). Closes the bare-decay coverage gap the suite lacked (the whole prior fnptr corpus used `&fn`). тАФ *Original OPEN row (audit trail):* **OPEN тАФ normal (created 2026-06-22, FC-deferral close-out cycle 1 self-audit, branch p14).** The bare assignment STATEMENT (`x = e;`) runs NO semantic assignability check тАФ `subtreeType(Assign)` returns the LHS type without comparing operands, and the pass-2 assign arm checks only `S_ConstViolation`. Type compatibility is enforced only DOWNSTREAM: HIR `coerce()` materializes a width-exact Cast for any arith-coreтЖФarith-core pair, and the HIR verifier catches truly-incompatible (non-arith) shapes. CONSEQUENCE тАФ a strictness ASYMMETRY between the assignment statement and the `isAssignable`-checked init/call-arg/return sites: `int x = f;` (init, intтЖРfloat) REJECTS S0003, but `x = f;` (assignment stmt) is SILENTLY ACCEPTED (coerce truncates floatтЖТint тАФ a correct C VALUE, but the c-subset "no silent C-style implicit conversion" strictness is bypassed); likewise a non-C schema with `intCrossSignednessConverts` OFF rejects cross-sign at init/call/return yet accepts it at an assignment statement. NOT a miscompile (coerce's conversions are correct; non-arith mismatches still fail loud via the verifier as `H_VerifierFailure`) тАФ a DIAGNOSTIC-QUALITY + consistency gap. PRE-EXISTING (independent of the cross-sign gate); empirically confirmed via CLI (`int x=f;`тЖТS0003 exit 1; `x=f;`тЖТexit 0). | Trigger: the robustness sweep / first user-visible inconsistency (assignment-stmt vs init for the same incompatible pair). | **Closing work:** route the assignment-statement RHS through the SAME gated `isAssignable` check as the 4 init/call/return sites (emit `S_TypeMismatch` on failure), so `x = f;` rejects like `int x = f;` AND cross-sign at assignment-stmt becomes consistently GATED (not gate-independent coerce); blast-radius check for existing assignment-stmt tests (intтЖФfloat forms currently passing to the verifier); a red-on-disable pin per direction. | `semantic_analyzer.cpp` assign pass-2 arm (~2992-3042) + `subtreeType` Assign (~3843); `type_rules.hpp` isAssignable; `cst_to_hir.cpp` coerce |
| `D-PARSE-SCHEMA-NESTED-NULLABLE-FOLLOW` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-17 (struct-head cycle тАФ the schema-compiler root fix for `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION`).** AltChoice `expectedSet` was computed EAGERLY at build time, but `repeat` uses a tie-the-knot: the loop-entry slot is reserved as a placeholder `End` (EMPTY expectedSet), the loop BODY is built against it, THEN it is overwritten with the real AltChoice тАФ so an `optional`/`alt` inside the body whose fall-through `cont` is that loop entry captured the placeholder's EMPTY set, permanently missing the loop's exit FOLLOW. Concretely the enum `enumerator (Comma enumerator?)*` before `}`: the trailing `{optional enumerator}` never learned `}` could follow тЖТ under a SPECULATIVE probe (`topLevelCompositeSpec`) the body parse hit `P_NoAlternativeMatched` (nullableTail=false, `}` not in the optional's expectedSet) тЖТ rolled back to the ref form тЖТ `{тАж}` mis-parsed. FIX: new monotone fixed-point pass `recomputeAltExpectedSets` re-unions every AltChoice position's `expectedSet = тИк branches[i].expectedSet` until stable, sequenced AFTER `detectAmbiguousAlternatives` + BEFORE `computeFollowSets` (+ `Position::setExpectedSet`). Blast radius = EVERY grammar's expectedSets, but it only WIDENS to the correct union тАФ 318/318 across toy/tsql/c-subset. RED-on-disable pinned by `HirLoweringCSubset.D5_5_EnumTrailingCommaParses` (top-level `enum E {тАж,};` тАФ VERIFIED red when the orchestration call is toggled off, restored). **SCOPE:** fixes the TOP-LEVEL speculative surface (the NAMED `topLevelCompositeSpec`); the typedef-position surface (INLINE alts in `typedefHead`) stays a SEPARATE pre-existing gap тЖТ `D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-ENUM-TRAILING-COMMA`. | (closed) | `grammar_schema_json.cpp recomputeAltExpectedSets` + `compiled_shape.hpp setExpectedSet`; parent `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION` |

| `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-18 (FC8 cycle 2 "bitfields", branch p10).** C bit-fields end-to-end (`struct/union { T x : W; }`), named + ANONYMOUS (`int : 3;`) + ZERO-WIDTH (`int : 0;`), signed + unsigned, read + write (incl. compound-assign / `++` via the read+write intercepts). **Type:** the per-field bit-width rides the interned Struct/Union `scalars` pool ((width+1) encoding; EMPTY scalars when no bitfield тЖТ every pre-bitfield composite interns bit-identically) тАФ the width is part of type identity, so `computeLayout` (TypeId-only) packs without a semantic-model dep. **Layout:** `BitFieldStrategy` (`.target.json` `aggregateLayout.bitFieldStrategy`, default None=fail-loud; `gnu_packed` on both targets) + a GNU/SysV little-endian LSB-first packing algorithm in `type_layout.cpp` (a field that would straddle its type's unit boundary starts the next aligned unit; zero-width forces a unit break; `StructLayout.bitFields[]` carries per-field {unitBytes,bitOffset,bitWidth}). **Codegen:** `hir_to_mir.cpp` member-access READ = Load unit тЖТ shift+mask (signed: sign-extend via Shl+AShr); WRITE = read-modify-write (Load тЖТ clear тЖТ OR shifted value тЖТ Store) тАФ reuses existing MIR Shl/LShr/AShr/And/Or, NO new substrate. **Fail-loud:** non-integer base (`S_BitFieldNonIntegerType`), width negative/>type-bits/zero-on-named (`S_BitFieldWidthOutOfRange`), anonymous-non-bitfield `int;` (`S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing`). **Witnesses:** corpus `bitfields` (round-trip + packing + cross-unit + signed sign-extension + neighbour-preservation + overflow-truncation) x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (native WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [arm64-Mach-O runtime witness CONFIRMED on the macos-latest leg тАФ green continuously since 2026-06-18, latest run 28150522650]; layout bit-offset pins + semantic fail-loud pins + a host-independent LIR structural pin, all red-on-disable. **Scope (all fail-loud, never silent):** the ABI-EXACT per-platform layout (MS straddling, Apple specifics) is trigger-gated тЖТ `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT` (the ┬зB decision, user-approved 2026-06-18); enum-typed bit-fields тЖТ `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-BITFIELD`. **REVIEW-CAUGHT (independent review) & folded to fail-loud the same cycle:** (a) aggregate INITIALIZATION of a bit-field struct (`struct S f={1,2};`/designated/global) would write each field full-width тЖТ clobber co-resident bit-fields тЖТ now rejected at MIR + the asm static-data backstop тЖТ `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-INIT` (field-by-field assignment is the supported path); (b) a >32-bit-base (`long long`) bit-field needs 64-bit unit codegen (incomplete `mov`/ALU encodings) тЖТ rejected at semantic for a clean diagnostic (the `constInt`тЖТ`constIntOfType` fix already made it fail-loud not silent) тЖТ `D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-WIDE-UNIT` (тЬЕ CLOSED 2026-06-18 тАФ the real gap was wide-const materialization, not 64-bit ALU). (struct S){тАж} : (struct S){тАж}`) or comma/`SeqExpr` rvalue used BY VALUE (arg/return/assign/member) is a DIFFERENT carrier than the compound-literal slot: each control-flow arm must be materialized into a COMMON result slot under the branch (control-flow-dependent), not initialized once in place. ADJUDICATION: mir_to_lir LOCAL FOLD chosen over a LIR peephole pass** тАФ (1) the SymbolId is directly readable at MIR tier (`mir.globalAddrSymbol`; a LIR pass would re-derive it from the lea's operand via the heavyweight rewrite machinery), (2) single-use analysis is well-defined on MIR SSA (post-regalloc LIR reuses registers), (3) the direct-call SymbolRef fold in `lowerCall` is the established in-file precedent. Mechanism: a per-function MIR value-use census (`computeValueUses` тАФ instOperands + Phi `phiIncomings`, which live OUTSIDE instOperands; missing them would under-count and fold away a lea a phi still needs) + ONE predicate `globalAddrRiprelFoldsIntoLoad` (use count == 1 AND the user is a Load whose sole operand is the value AND the load's result-class load mnemonic declares a 1-operand SymbolRef encoding variant at the load's effective width) consulted by BOTH fold sites via the `foldedGlobalAddrs_` set тАФ `lowerGlobalAddr` skips the lea, `lowerLoad` emits the [SymbolRef] form directly; a site disagreement surfaces as a loud undefined-vreg failure, never a silent wrong address. CAPABILITY-DRIVEN: fires for x86 movsd_load/movss_load (the FC2/FC3.5-c2 riprel variants' FIRST pipeline consumers тАФ F2/F3 0F 10 05 rel32); does NOT fire for x86 GPR `load` (no [symbol] variant) or arm64 `fldur` (arm64's lea is the 2-word ADRP+ADD macro тАФ a symbol-relative LDR fold is a DIFFERENT encoding shape, honestly out of scope, pinned by `Arm64GlobalAddrLoadKeepsLeaPlusBaseFormLoad`). Pins: `FullPipelineDoublePlusRodataConstFoldsToRipRelMovsd` (riprel ModR/M mod=00 rm=101 present + lea ABSENT + reloc preserved; RED-on-disable demonstrated + restored) + `FullPipelineRodataConstTwoLoadsKeepsLeaPlusBaseForm` (NOT-single-use keeps the pair, no riprel form). | `D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-10** (FC3 c1, V2-4.X тАФ the prescribed `dataModel` axis LANDED, the "SemanticConfig axis" arm of the closing-work fork, plan-lock-refined to put the FIELD on the FORMAT schema [the OS lives in the format: peтЖТLLP64, elf/machoтЖТLP64, wasm/spirvтЖТILP32 declared-only/fail-loud-if-selected; x86_64 serves BOTH Windows and Linux so a target-level field was unrepresentable]): `ObjectFormatSchema.dataModel` (REQUIRED, closed enum, loader+validate reject missing/unknown) тЖТ resolved at `buildCuMir` тЖТ threaded into `analyze()` (defaulted-LP64 param = the identity model; the driver always passes `format.dataModel()`) and carried on `SemanticModel` to HIR. `builtinTypes`/`typeSpecifiers` rows carry `coreByDataModel` overrides (`long`тЖТI64, LLP64тЖТI32; `unsigned long`тЖТU64/LLP64тЖТU32); the literal ladder resolves type NAMES through the same table (LLP64 long=I32 falls out free); **the descriptor surface closed too**: `signatureByDataModel` per-symbol overrides (Model-3 library-map shape) on the 6 width-divergent symbols (fseek/ftell/atol/strtol/strtoul/labs тАФ LLP64 i32/u32 forms; every declared override must parse, unknown keys reject). WITNESS: `datamodel_long_width` (LP64 runtime, 2┬│тБ░+2┬│тБ░=2┬│┬╣ proves 64-bit long, WSL+qemu exit 42) + `datamodel_long_width_llp64` (the SAME `long` vocabulary `expectDiagnostics`-REJECTS S_TypeMismatch@21:14 under LLP64 on the PE arm тАФ a DEFINED-behavior runtime-divergent witness is impossible-by-construction until c2's 32-bit ALU [signed overflow=UB, unsigned-32=gated]; the runtime divergent witness rides c2). Red-on-disable: pe64 dataModel flipped LLP64тЖТLP64 тЖТ the llp64 example RED. ORIGINAL text below. тАФ First time a c-subset corpus row needs `long` to bind to I32 (LLP64 / Win64 ABI) instead of I64 (LP64 / SysV ABI). Today `c-subset.lang.json` binds `long тЖТ I64` unconditionally тАФ that's correct for LP64 Linux/macOS, silently wrong for Win64 LLP64 (Windows `long`=I32). Today's corpus runs Win64 only because `pe64-x86_64-windows-exec` is the sole `targets[*].spec`, but the `long`тЖТI64 binding bakes a data-model decision into the source-language config in a way that violates the agnosticism rule (lang config should not assume ABI). **SCOPE EXTENSION (2026-06-09, the `#include puts` 3-OS string-FFI capstone тАФ STILL OPEN):** this anchor now ALSO covers the **shipped-lib descriptor surface**. When the 15 per-platform descriptors were collapsed into 5 neutral `shippedLibs/<h>.json` (Model 3 тАФ see `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-PLATFORM-SELECT` тЬЕ dissolved), the **6 ABI-WIDTH-DIVERGENT symbols** тАФ stdio `fseek`/`ftell` + stdlib `atol`/`strtol`/`strtoul`/`labs` (the ones whose signatures involve `long`/`size_t`) тАФ were MERGED to the **LP64 (i64/u64)** form in the neutral signatures. That is CORRECT for the runnable linux/macos targets (LP64), but the **Windows LLP64 form for these 6 (where `long` = i32) is now LATENTLY DEFERRED** тАФ confirmed UNEXERCISED by any corpus (no shipped-`fseek`/`strtol`/etc. call compiles to a Windows target today; the running `puts`/`abs` corpora touch no width-divergent symbol). The proper fix is the SAME as the `long`-keyword case: an abstract `long`/`size_t` resolved per-target (the descriptor signatures would spell `long`/`size_t` abstractly, the data-model axis below would bind them per active target). Do NOT file a duplicate anchor тАФ this is one and the same "config baked an ABI/width decision" gap, now spanning both the c-subset `long` keyword AND the neutral shipped descriptors. | Extend the `SemanticConfig` schema with a `dataModel` axis (e.g., `"ILP32"` / `"LP64"` / `"LLP64"`) that `builtinTypes[*].core` can vary on; OR add an ABI-conditioning layer in the HIRтЖТMIR lowering that overrides core types per active target. Closes the silent-mismatch when the same c-subset source compiles to two different ABIs. Pairs with the multi-target capstone (D-CAP-MARKER-MULTI-TARGET-E2E-PIN). | plan 22 (alias arc); pairs with D-CSUBSET-LONG-PRIMITIVE (which made this decision provisional) |
| `D-OPT-IDENTITYREBUILD-CALLER-WRAP-REQUIRED` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-04** (cycle 10h post-fold) тАФ `identityRebuild` test helper signature refactored from `void identityRebuild(Mir const& src, Mir& out)` to `[[nodiscard]] bool identityRebuild(...)`. The void+ASSERT_NO_FATAL_FAILURE shape required caller discipline; a caller writing `identityRebuild(src, dst);` without the wrap would silently allow tests to proceed with a half-built `dst`. `[[nodiscard]]` triggers an unused-result warning at the missing-check call site, converting the silent-failure trap into a compile-time gate. | Refactor + ADD_FAILURE on the carve-out arm (vs the prior ASSERT_EQ which required void return); both existing test bodies updated to `ASSERT_TRUE(identityRebuild(...))`. | tests/opt/test_mir_rebuild_helper.cpp; same compile-time-enforcement pattern as `[[nodiscard]] Result<T>` patterns elsewhere in the codebase |
| `D-TARGET-ENCODING-WIDTH-GUARD` | **REWRITTEN to post-FC3.5 truth 2026-06-11 (audit-residue sweep c2, `D-AUDIT-BOOKKEEPING-TRUTH` (b) тАФ the original text below had become pre-FC3 fiction cited by LIVE diagnostics).** The mechanism the original row asked for SHIPPED: FC3 c2 added the variant-guard WIDTH key (`guard.width`тИИ{32,64}, loader-validated, + the ONE shared `variantMatchesInst` matcher + the `kLirInstFlagWidth32` carrier) and `trunc`'s FIRST encoding (mov r32,r32); FC3.5 c2 put F32 on the SAME width key (F3-prefix SSE / arm64 S-forms at width 32 vs F2/D-forms at 64) with SOURCE-width keying for conversions (the fpcvt/fp_to_si widthOverride). What the anchor now MARKS тАФ and what its live citations mean тАФ is the residual width WALLS: any width with NO encoded variant fails LOUD instead of first-match-winning a wrong form. Citations: `requireEncodedFloatWidth` (mir_to_lir.cpp ~:648) walls F16/F128 scalar floats; the FPR literal-promotion guard (~:1350) cites it for non-F64 inline-literal widths (F32 literals ride the rodata path); sub-native INTEGER widths (8/16/128-bit ALU; the I8/I16 sext variants) wall at `requireNativeIntWidth` / the SExt source gate тАФ that successor work lives at `D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS`. | Encode the missing width variants WHEN a consumer arrives (F16/F128: no C producer today; I8/I16: the `D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS` triggers) тАФ the guard.width axis makes each a declaration-only addition. **ORIGINAL pre-FC3 text** (history): "First time a target needs distinct encodings for different operand WIDTHS (not just kinds like reg/imm/mem) тАФ e.g., sext I8тЖТI64 vs I32тЖТI64 are different x86 opcodes (`0F BE` vs `63`). Today's variant-guard only supports `operandKinds` discrimination; same trap exists for the existing zext (BYTEтЖТr64 single-variant) and trunc (no encoding) mnemonics. / Extend the variant `guard` schema with an `operandWidths` array discriminator + teach the encoder to dispatch on the source operand's width-of-record. Then add the missing sext variants (I8тЖТI16, I8тЖТI32, I8тЖТI64, I16тЖТI32, I16тЖТI64) + the trunc encoding (mov r32, r32 zeroes upper 32). Pre-fold the same trap masks any I8/I16 widening / narrowing as silent miscompiles." | plan 13 ┬з2.4 (encoding shapes); pairs with D-CSUBSET-LONG-PRIMITIVE (which closed only the I32тЖТI64 variant); integer-width successor: `D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS` |

| `D-FC6-SIZEOF-CONST-CONTEXT` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-15 (FC6-deferrals cycle).** `sizeof` now const-folds in const-expression contexts via a symmetric `resolveTypeSize`/`resolveSizeof` hook on BOTH const-eval engines, sharing the FC6 `computeLayout` kernel: **GLOBAL initializers** (`int g = sizeof(T)`, both type+value forms) fold via the HIR const-eval `EvalEnvironment::resolveTypeSize` + `HirKind::SizeOf` case (wired at the MIR-globals site); **ARRAY-dimension TYPE form** (`int a[sizeof(T)]`) folds via the CST const-eval `CstEvalEnvironment::resolveSizeof`, dispatched by `cfg.sizeofRule` BEFORE the wrapper-peel (the M1 hazard тАФ else `sizeof x` would fold to x's VALUE). The earlier "architectural tension" was DISPROVEN: semantic analysis re-runs per-(CU├Чtarget) into its own private `TypeLattice`, so a target-dependent array-dim type is sound. String-value rodata (`char* p="abc"; *p`) loads via the existing rodata producer + byte load. Indexed char access (`p[n]`, x86 SIB byte forms) works on x86; arm64 indexed is blocked by a PRE-EXISTING arm64 `lea` (symbol-only) gap affecting int+char alike тАФ NOT a char limitation. Pins: 5 LIR width flags (red-on-disable demonstrated) + 2 asm byte-word pins (exact bytes, both targets) + `char_value` (cross-target, exit 132, sign-correct) + `char_ptr_indexed` (x86 indexed, exit 131). | `D-FC7-SYSV-STRUCT-RETURN-IN-REGS` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-16 (FC7 C1c, ┬зB Option A, commit 1016d47).** A struct/union RETURNED by value now works across ALL SysV return classes. **┬зB (user) = Option A: a new MIR `ReturnPiece` opcode** (the caller-side mirror of `Arg`) for the multi-register capture тАФ over (B) a side-table + post-regalloc memory synthesis or (C) sret-everything (ABI-non-conformant for тЙд16B, rejected). **As-built:** (1) **callee** loads the eightbyte pieces and emits a MULTI-operand `Return` (maxOp bumped to N); the lir_callconv materialization moves each piece тЖТ `returnReg[class][per-class cursor]`. (2) **caller** (`emitStructReturningCall`): the struct Call's result IS piece 0 (returnReg ordinal 0 of its class) and each further eightbyte is a `ReturnPiece(call, ordinal)` read; all pieces store into the result slot `R` (a frame Alloca), and `R`'s address is the call's value (consumed by every aggregate-by-address consumer via the new `lowerLvalueAddress(Call)` arm). (3) **sret** (>16B MEMORY): a hidden result-pointer `Arg` (GPR ordinal 0, shifting the real params via the shared `ArgOrdinalCounter`) the callee copies the result through (`lowerByteWiseCopy`) + returns; no new opcode. (4) **MF-1 cycle-break** (BUILT тАФ the arg path only REJECTS cycles, D-ML7-2.3): `emitParallelRegMoves` breaks a return-register 2-swap with a caller-saved scratch (тЙе3-cycle = AAPCS64 HFA, fails loud тЖТ C3). **Latent-crash fix:** `lowerFunction` copied the `interner.fnParams` SPAN into an owning vector тАФ the sret/by-value `interner.pointer(тАж)` interning can REALLOCATE the pool and dangle a retained span (an interner-state-dependent "TypeId out of range"; also hardened the C1a/C1b struct-arg path). Verifier relaxed: a struct-returning func's Return may carry the first-class aggregate VALUE, N register pieces (I64/F64), or an sret Ptr. Runtime-witnessed x86_64-ELF exit 42 (reg-pair rax:rdx + sret + mixed {double;long} xmm0:rax, debug AND release). Pins: MF-2 callconv swap unit pin (pre-colored {rdx,rax}, value-correct sim) + 4 MIR pins (multi-Return shape, sret hidden-ptr, mixed-class split, call-once SF-2) + `struct_return_sysv` corpus. Reachable via the typedef-name return form (`struct Tag` top-level return type = the pre-FC4 grammar residue `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION`). | plan 23 ┬з3.1 FC7; commit 1016d47; `mir_opcode.hpp` ReturnPiece + `hir_to_mir.cpp` emitStructReturningCall/lowerStructReturn + `lir_callconv.cpp` emitParallelRegMoves; `D-FC7-SYSV-STRUCT-ARG-MULTIREG` (shared substrate); parent `D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN` (C2/C3 remain) |

POINTER indexing (`p[i]`, 2-op `[ptr,idx]`) works; storage-array indexing never did. Closing work: lower the 3-op array-index GEP тАФ the vestigial `0` makes it equivalent to base+index, so collapse to the 2-op base+index form + apply the ELEMENT SCALE (x86 SIB scale / arm64 LSL), coordinating with `D-AS4-ARM64-INDEXED-LEA-SCALE` for scaleтЙа1. Until then a struct with an array field COPIES correctly (byte-wise, `D-FC7-AGGREGATE-COPY-MEMCPY`) but its elements can't be read (MIR-tier pin only: `StructWithArrayFieldCopyIsByteWise`). | `D-LIR-INT-MEMORY-WIDTH-EXACT` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-17 (array-storage cluster, branch p9, user ┬зB "all widths, one cycle").** DISCOVERED by the array work's self-audit: integer memory access was 8-byte-wide for every int except `char` (`memAccessWidthFlags` returned width-default; the x86/arm64 schemas had only width-64 + width-8 mem variants), so a 64-bit load/store of a PACKED sub-8-byte element (array element or struct field) overran тАФ `array_storage_index` CRASHED (ACCESS_VIOLATION) under the REAL release pipeline; FC7 struct-field writes were latently affected too. Also fixed the latent `lowerByteWiseCopy` I32-chunk overrun (it was silently a width-64 access). Witness tiers: int(32) RUNTIME-witnessed (`array_storage_index` exit 42, x86-PE/ELF + arm64 qemu, under the REAL release pipeline) + char(8) by the pre-existing `char_ptr_indexed`; I8/U8/Bool(8) + I16/U16(16) are BYTE-PINNED (`WidthAxisX86/Arm64.Int32And16MemFormsEmit*`) + fail-loud-safe тАФ their VALUE codegen (Trunc/SExt/ZExt to/from sub-32) is the SEPARATE `D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS` gap, so they cannot be runtime-exercised yet (no miscompile тАФ they fail loud at value codegen). Loader pin: `WidthAxisLoader.GuardWidthSixteenAcceptedOutOfVocabularyRejected`. | `D-CSUBSET-INDEX-INTEGER-PROMOTION` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-22 (FC-deferral close-out loop cycle 4, branch p14).** A `char` subscript of a WIDE-element array/pointer (`int a[N]; char c; a[c]`) now integer-PROMOTES to `int` (C 6.3.1.1 / 6.5.2.1) BEFORE the index arithmetic. **As-built (small, front-end, the BEST-long-term placement тАФ not a MIR band-aid):** the CSTтЖТHIR `Index` lowering (`cst_to_hir.cpp` ~1862) coerces a sub-int index via the SAME `integerPromotedType`+`coerce` the binary-op path uses (config-driven C 6.3.1; gated on `arith_.has_value()` so block-less toy/tsql are byte-identical) тАФ so the HIR index node is already `int` and `scaleIndexToBytes` sees a promoted index. **`builder.makeIndex` has EXACTLY ONE call site** in all of `src/` (independently verified), so EVERY subscript тАФ rvalue load, lvalue store, compound-assign, `&a[i]`, `a[i]++` тАФ funnels through the one promoted chokepoint (no ┬зA.5 multi-site miss). The crux is OVERFLOW: an un-promoted `(char)100 * 4` = 400 wraps mod 256 тЖТ reads/writes the WRONG element; promotion computes 400 at int width. Signedness preserved (signed `char`тЖТSExt, unsignedтЖТZExt; a negative index sign-extends). int/long index untouched (`integerPromotedType` returns the same TypeId тЖТ no coerce). **Witnesses:** corpus `index_integer_promotion` exit 42 тАФ char load AND char-indexed STORE both overflow-sensitive (`c=100`/`d=120`, value-divergent) + signed-char тАФ on x86_64-PE (ctest) + x86_64-ELF (WSL) + arm64-ELF (qemu) [macho macos leg]; MIR pin `CharIndexIntegerPromotesToI32BeforeStrideMul` (the stride `Mul` is I32-typed, its lhs is the SExt/ZExt promotion not the raw char Arg) DEMONSTRATED red-on-disable (promotion off тЖТ inst-count 8тЖТ7, Mul Char-typed, example walls/overflows). Independent review VERIFIED-CLEAN. 365/365 ctest. **SHORT residual (orthogonal, fail-loud, NOT this anchor):** a `short` index promotes CORRECTLY (the cycle emits the SExt) but the I16 SExt/Trunc ENCODING is unrealized at the backend тЖТ loud `L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode` on x86 + arm64 тАФ the separate sub-native I16-codegen gap `D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS`/`D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS`, NOT the promotion. тАФ ORIGINAL: First need a `char`/`short`-typed subscript of a WIDE-element array/pointer (`int* a; a[c]` where `c` is `char`) to compile. DISCOVERED 2026-06-17 (array-storage cluster re-audit, NIT). Today FAIL-LOUD (NOT a miscompile тАФ and strictly better than the pre-cycle SILENT scale-1 form): `scaleIndexToBytes` types the scaling `Mul(idx, stride)` at the index's OWN type (`hir.typeId(kids[1])`), so a `char` subscript makes a `Char`-typed Mul that walls at the Char-ALU gap (`D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS`). Per C 6.3.1.1 the subscript should integer-PROMOTE to `int` before the index arithmetic тАФ a pre-existing FRONT-END promotion gap the scaling faithfully reflects. `char`-index-of-`char`-array (stride 1, no Mul) and `int`-index both work. | (closed) | `cst_to_hir.cpp` Index lowering (integerPromotedType+coerce); `D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS`/`D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS` (the orthogonal I16-codegen gap blocking the short end-to-end); `D-MIR-STORAGE-ARRAY-INDEX-GEP` (the cycle that surfaced it) |
| `D-CSUBSET-WIDE-HEX-OCTAL-ESCAPE-VALUE` | тП│ **trigger-gated** тАФ first corpus that needs a `\x` hex / `\ooo` octal escape to name a raw CODE-UNIT VALUE inside a wide/UTF literal тАФ e.g. `u"\xFFFF"` тЖТ one 0xFFFF `char16_t` unit, `u8"\xFF"` тЖТ one 0xFF `char8_t` unit, or `U"\x1F600"` тЖТ one 0x1F600 `char32_t` unit. | **Behavior is now FAIL-LOUD (Cycle C, `\u`/`\U` + surrogate arc), NO LONGER a silent miscompile.** Cycle C found that `\x`/octal in a wide/UTF literal SILENTLY collapsed the escape bytes through the UTF-8 re-decoder тАФ `u"\xC3\xA9"` became ONE wrong 0x00E9 unit instead of two intended units. So the wide path now REJECTS any consumed `\x`/octal escape: strings тЖТ `H_WideByteEscapeUnsupported` (`cst_to_hir.cpp lowerStringLiteral`, keyed on `EscapeDecodeOutcome::usedByteEscape` + a wide opener), chars тЖТ `WideCharError::ByteEscapeInWide` тЖТ the same code (`decodeWideCharCodepoint`). The DEFERRED FEATURE is assembling the escape VALUE directly as one code unit of the element (C 6.4.4.4 / 6.4.5: the byte/hex value IS the unit тАФ unbounded hex digits for `\x`, must fit the element width), bypassing the UTF-8 round-trip. Closing work: thread the escape VALUES (not the decoded bytes) so a wide element consumes them as code-unit values; the NARROW `"тАж"`/`'тАж'` path stays byte-producing (correct, UNCHANGED). Use `\u`/`\U` for a code POINT until then. | `char_decode.hpp` (`EscapeDecodeOutcome.usedByteEscape`); `wide_string_encode.hpp` (`decodeWideCharCodepoint` `ByteEscapeInWide`); `cst_to_hir.cpp` (`lowerStringLiteral` FF3 guard); `semantic_analyzer.cpp` (leave-untyped mirror); `H_WideByteEscapeUnsupported`; examples `wide_string_hex_escape_error`; plan 23 (full-C wide/UTF literal arc, Cycle C) |
