| `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-PLATFORM-SELECT` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-09 тАФ DISSOLVED by Model 3** (the `#include puts("hello")` 3-OS string-FFI capstone cycle). The per-platform-dir SELECTION problem this anchor tracked no longer exists: the 15 per-platform `shippedLibs/<os-arch>/<h>.json` descriptors were COLLAPSED into 5 **NEUTRAL** `shippedLibs/<h>.json`, each carrying platform-neutral signatures + a per-format **`library` MAP** `{pe,elf,macho}`; `shippedLibDirs` became the single neutral root `["shippedLibs"]`. The map is threaded target-AGNOSTICALLY through the front-end (descriptorтЖТ`ShippedExternSymbol`тЖТ`HirExternRecord`) and resolved to ONE library string per-target LATE, at `compile_pipeline.cpp` (via `objectFormatKindName(format.kind())`; missing key тЖТ `externLibraryByFormat` fallback; unknown key тЖТ fail loud) тАФ so there is no longer a *directory* to pick by active target, and the front-end stays target-blind. This is **dissolution, NOT the registry's hinted per-dir platform-predicate remediation** (that fix is no longer needed тАФ the platform axis moved INTO the neutral descriptor as the format-keyed library map). Cite: the Model-3 ┬зB decision (plan-00 ┬з0.2). тАФ ORIGINAL trigger/context retained below. FF11 (2026-06-05, cycle 18+; was STILL OPEN after v0.0.2 V2-2 2026-06-06): the angle-form `#include <h>` system search path (`SemanticConfig.shippedLibDirs`) lists its platform EXPLICITLY (c-subset ships `["shippedLibs/windows-x86_64"]`, the only dir). Trigger = the FIRST time a SECOND platform's shipped-lib descriptors land (e.g. a `shippedLibs/linux-x86_64/` with a libc-flavored `stdio.json` descriptor) AND a single c-subset source must compile to BOTH тАФ the resolver would then need to PICK the dir matching the active target (`ObjectFormatKind` / OS / arch) instead of searching every declared dir. Today: `applySystemDirs` (program.cpp) resolves EVERY `shippedLibDirs` entry and the resolver searches all of them, so a Windows + Linux shipped pair would both be on the path (first-match wins тЖТ wrong-platform descriptor could shadow). Priority: **normal** (the single-dir path is correct for the Win64-only corpus). UPDATE (2026-06-08, shippedLibs build-out): the `linux-x86_64` + `macos-arm64` descriptor sets NOW EXIST (stdio/stdlib/string/ctype/math each, all decode-validated by `AllShippedDescriptorsDecode`) тАФ so the trigger's "a SECOND platform's descriptors land" half HAS fired. But they are STAGED data only: c-subset `shippedLibDirs` still lists ONLY `["shippedLibs/windows-x86_64"]`, so nothing is on the path to shadow and no source compiles to a non-Windows target yet. The REMAINING (unfired) trigger half is purely the resolver needing to SELECT the dir by active target. NOTE: V2-2 (2026-06-06) replaced the c-subset-source `stdio.h` with the neutral JSON descriptor (`D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` тЬЕ); the platform-select mechanism is unchanged. | Add a per-`shippedLibDirs`-entry platform predicate (e.g. `{ "dir": "shippedLibs/linux-x86_64", "format": "elf", "os": "linux" }`) OR derive the platform subdir from the active target spec at `applySystemDirs` time; the resolver then searches only the matching dir(s). Keep it config-driven (the language declares the platform axis; the engine selects) тАФ NO `if (format == pe)` in the resolver. Pin with two shipped dirs (win + linux) each holding a same-named descriptor with a DISCRIMINATING extern, compiled to each target, asserting the right one resolved. | plan 11 FFI (FF11 angle-include); pairs with D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH (both are "config baked an ABI/platform decision" anchors); D-CAP-MARKER-MULTI-TARGET-E2E-PIN; D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC (the descriptor that replaced the `.h`) |
| `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE` | A shipped-lib descriptor (`shippedLibs/<platform>/*.json`, V2-2 2026-06-06) needs a VARIADIC function (e.g. `printf`) OR a signature form the shared `parseTypeFromText` codec does not yet accept. Today the hir-text `fn(...) -> ...` type grammar parses the param list + a trailing `cc` but has NO variadic marker, while the `FnSig` `isVariadic` scalar EXISTS in the IR but is not spellable in type-text тАФ so a descriptor cannot express `printf`'s `(ptr<char>, ...)`. Priority: **normal** (the shipped surface [stdio/stdlib/string/ctype/math, broadened 2026-06-08] is ALL non-variadic BY DESIGN тАФ `printf`/`fprintf`/`scanf`/`sscanf`/`snprintf` and the rest of the variadic family are deliberately EXCLUDED from the descriptors and documented as such in `shippedLibs/README.md`; a variadic-needing descriptor signature fails LOUD via `F_ShippedLibUnsupportedType` [0x501C] тАФ never silent). | Extend the hir-text `fn` type production (`parseType` in `hir_text.cpp`) + the shared `parseTypeFromText` to parse a variadic marker (e.g. `fn(ptr<char>, ...) -> i32`) into the `FnSig` `isVariadic` scalar; then a descriptor can express `printf`. Pin: a `stdio.json` `printf` row round-trips through `parseTypeFromText` with `isVariadic` set, and the symbol injects + resolves end-to-end. | `D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-DESCRIPTOR-AGNOSTIC` (the consumer); `docs/ir-type-text.md` (the `fn`-type grammar); `D-LANG-VARIADIC`; do NOT cite in `src/` (future-work pin only) |
| `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** тАФ format-driven extern-call dispatch (the fix for a CONFIRMED latent x86_64-ELF FFI miscompile). The plan-lock proved byte-level that the ELF linker points an extern's `symbolVa` at its PLT STUB (code: `jmp [GOT]`), yet MIRтЖТLIR unconditionally emitted `call_indirect_via_extern` (`FF 15` = `call [rip+disp32]`), dereferencing the stub's bytes as a function pointer тЖТ SIGSEGV. The extern-call shape is a property of the OBJECT FORMAT, not the CPU target (x86_64-PE vs x86_64-ELF need OPPOSITE opcodes). Fix: new closed enum `ExternCallDispatch` (`core/types/object_format_kind.hpp`) + a per-format `externCallDispatch` field on `ObjectFormatSchema` (`indirect-slot` = PE IAT / Mach-O __got, deref a pointer slot via `call_indirect_via_extern`; `direct-plt` = ELF, plain direct `call`/BL to the linker-synthesized PLT stub). `lowerToLir`/`lowerCall` selects the opcode from the active format's value as DATA тАФ ZERO `if (arch/format == тАж)`; ELF needs NO new codegen opcode (reuses the existing `call`/BLтЖТPLT-stub). The 4 live exec formats declare it (pe64 + macho64-x86_64 = `indirect-slot`; elf64-x86_64 + elf64-aarch64 = `direct-plt`). NOT validate-required (an exec format built for a non-FFI purpose may omit it); the real fail-loud is at LOWERING тАФ a module with extern imports under a nullopt-dispatch format тЖТ `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing`; an unknown value тЖТ load fail-loud. Pins: `MirToLir.{ExternCallEmitsCallIndirectViaExternOpcode, ExternCallOnDirectPltFormatEmitsPlainCall, ExternImportsWithNoDispatchFailLoud, IndirectSlotWithoutOpcodeFailsLoud}` + the `extern_call_elf` runtime corpus (x86_64-ELF `abs` FFI тЖТ exit 42; RED-on-disable by flipping the ELF format to `indirect-slot`). | (closed) | plan 14 linker + plan 11 FFI |
| `D-FFI-PE-IMPORT-THUNK` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-05 (c112) тАФ the pe64 leg's LAST run-green blocker: sqlite3.exe RUNS on Windows (`--version` 3.54.0 + full CRUD `SELECT sum`тЖТ62), so all 3 legs (win pe64 + linux x86_64 + arm64-qemu) now run real SQL.** The follow-up to `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH`: PE was the sole `indirect-slot` format, so `symbolVa[extern]` named the raw `.idata` IAT *data* slot. A DIRECT extern call worked (`call [IATslot]` derefs the slot), but an ADDRESS-TAKEN import (sqlite os_win.c `aSyscall[]` = a static-init array of `(SYSCALL)Win32Func` called indirectly) got the slot ADDRESS as a value тЖТ `call *reg` jumped INTO `.idata` and executed the pointer bytes as code тЖТ 0xC0000005 (no output). ELF/Mach-O ran the identical construct green because a `direct-plt` format points the extern's VA at a callable STUB (ELF PLT / Mach-O `__stubs`). FIX (user-chosen A; ┬зB GO-WITH-FIXES design-audit, all 7 fixes folded): the PE walker synthesizes one `FF 25 disp32` import THUNK per extern at the tail of `.text` (`jmp *[IAT slot]` тАФ the PE analog of Mach-O `__stubs`; `peThunkSizeFor`/`emitX86_64PeThunk`/`emitPeThunk` MIRROR `emitMachoStub`, machine-dispatched on 0x8664 with a fail-loud default), sets `symbolVa[extern]` = the THUNK VA (a `.text` code address, not the IAT slot), and flips `pe64-x86_64-windows-exec` `externCallDispatch` `indirect-slot`тЖТ`direct-plt` so every extern reference (call OR address-taken) is a callable address тАФ PE now matches ELF/Mach-O (NO `if(format==)`; thunk block reserved before `.text` is sized, emitted after IAT VAs known; shared `applyExecRelocations` UNCHANGED). Pins: `PeExecFormatJson.ShippedPeExecIsDirectPlt` (host-independent format pin, mirrors `MachOArm64Exit.ShippedX86DarwinExecIsDirectPlt`) + `PeExecWriter.AddressTakenImportResolvesToTextThunkNotIdataSlot` (host-independent byte-scan: one `FF 25` thunk in `.text`, the extern call resolves to it, the thunk jumps into `.idata`; RED-on-disable=revertтЖТresolves to `.idata`) + `examples/c-subset/addr_import` 3-leg runtime corpus (volatile-load fold-proof; PE 0xC0000005тЖТexit 42; elf/arm64 already green). | (closed тАФ sqlite3.exe run-green on Windows) | `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH` (parent dispatch enum); `D-LK6-8` (ELF PLT) + macho `__stubs` (mirror precedents); `D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS` (c111, prior pe64 blocker); plan 14 linker |
| `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-FFI-ISEL` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** тАФ the END-TO-END ARM64-ELF runnable FFI call-WITH-RESULT path is complete. **The gap was the `lea`/GlobalAddr lowering, NOT a "reg-move-class op"** (the D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH cycle's first guess) тАФ empirically re-confirmed this cycle: with the arm64 `lea` opcode disabled, BOTH the MIRтЖТLIR fixture AND the `extern_call_elf` arm64 compile fail with the EXACT diagnostic `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing: target 'arm64' declares no 'lea' opcode (required for lowering MIR GlobalAddr)` тАФ the raw `lowerToLir` lowers a `Call`'s callee `GlobalAddr` via `lowerGlobalAddr`тЖТ`lea` (the peephole that folds the symbol into the call's SymbolRef is an OPTIMIZED-path thing, absent in the raw lowering). The D-AS4-3 ARM64 `lea` (ADRP+ADD, commit 92386d3) supplied the missing opcode; this cycle PROVES the FFI path: (1) the MIRтЖТLIR pin `MirToLir.Arm64ExternCallWithResultLowersUnderDirectPlt` (the dropped pin, reconstructed тАФ lowers clean, 2 direct calls, 0 indirect; RED-on-disable demonstrated above), (2) the runnable corpus `examples/c-subset/extern_call_elf` gained an `arm64:elf64-aarch64-linux-exec` arm (compiles host-independently тЖТ valid AArch64 ELF; RUNS on the native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` CI leg + qemu via the cross-arch gate тЖТ exit 42). The ELF-aarch64 PLT/GOT (`emitArm64PltStub`, D-LK6-8 тЬЕ) + the format-driven `direct-plt` dispatch (D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH тЬЕ) complete the chain. **тЪая╕П CORRECTION (2026-06-08, same-day next cycle): the "тЖТ exit 42" RUNTIME claim above was an OVER-CLAIM** (exactly the runtime risk that cycle's own self-audit flagged). The compile-to-valid-AArch64-ELF half was real, but the binary SIGSEGV'd at runtime (CI exit 139; locally reproduced under qemu once the file's execute bit was set). Root cause = a SEPARATE codegen bug, NOT the FFI path: non-leaf ARM64 functions did not spill the link register x30, so `main`'s epilogue `ret` returned to a clobbered LR (an infinite self-loop overrunning the stack). The FFI dispatch itself was always correct тАФ `abs(7-49)` returned 42 in x0. The corpus GENUINELY runs to exit 42 only after `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER` (below) landed the x30 spill. | (closed тАФ runtime corrected by the NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER fix) | plan 14 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64 family); D-AS4-3 (the lea that closed the compile path); D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH + D-LK6-8 (the PLT/dispatch chain); D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER (the x30 fix that made it actually run) |
| `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** тАФ non-leaf ARM64 functions now spill + reload the link register x30. **This is the ACTUAL root cause of the arm64-FFI SIGSEGV (CI exit 139)** that `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-FFI-ISEL`'s "тЖТ exit 42" over-claimed away (the prior cycle's self-audit had flagged exactly this runtime risk). A calling convention that carries the return address in a LINK REGISTER (AAPCS64 x30) rather than pushing it on the stack (x86_64 `call`; callPushBytes=8) makes x30 live across the whole body of a non-leaf function тАФ every `bl`/`call` clobbers it. The frame logic spilled the `calleeSaved` set (x19тАУx29) but NOT x30 (deliberately the `linkRegister`, NOT in `calleeSaved`, so it stays un-allocatable), so `main`'s epilogue `ret` returned to the clobbered LR (the address after the last call) тЖТ an infinite self-loop whose repeated epilogue `add sp` walked SP off the stack into a guard page тЖТ SIGSEGV. **The FFI dispatch was always correct** тАФ `abs(7-49)` returned 42 in x0; the PLT stub (ADRP x16/LDR x17/BR x17) + the GLOB_DAT relocation both worked. **Fix** (`src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp::materializeOneFunc`, agnostic + config-driven): when `hasCalls && cc.linkRegister.has_value()`, fold the link register into `savedRegs` so the existing prologue/epilogue machinery spills it at entry + reloads it before `ret`. ZERO `if(arch/format==тАж)` тАФ x86_64 (no `linkRegister`) takes the no-op path; the schema's long-declared `linkRegister` field (whose `target_schema.hpp` comment PROMISED "ML7 lowering checks linkRegister.has_value()") is finally consumed. **Pins**: `LirCallconv.NonLeafAarch64FunctionSpillsLinkRegister` (non-leaf SAVES x30 + leaf does NOT; RED-on-disable: revert the fold тЖТ x30 absent тЖТ fail) + `LirCallconv.NonLeafX8664FunctionHasNoLinkRegisterToSpill` (agnosticism control: x86_64 cc has no linkRegister тЖТ no-op path) + the `extern_call_elf` arm64 runtime corpus (now genuinely exit 42 under qemu + native-arm64; was SIGSEGV тЖТ CI red тАФ so red-on-disable by construction). **+ HOST-INDEPENDENT BYTE-PIN (2026-06-08, same-day hardening)**: `LirCallconv.NonLeafAarch64FramePrologueEpilogueByteExact` drives the real production tail (legalizeTwoAddress тЖТ materializeCallingConvention тЖТ assemble) and byte-pins the ASSEMBLED non-leaf prologue+epilogue EXACT тАФ incl. `stur x30,[sp,#16]` (f80103fe) at prologue offset 12 + `ldur x30,[sp,#16]` (f84103fe) reloaded before `ret` тАФ against hand-verified ARM-ARM literals, plus a leaf-control (g spills x28/x29 but no `stur x30`). RED-on-disable DEMONSTRATED (disable the fold тЖТ frame 0x20тЖТ0x10 тЖТ both byte windows diverge). Closes the gap where the COMPOSED frame's byte-correctness was witnessed ONLY by the native-arm64 runtime leg (the per-instruction arm64 byte-pins prove instructions in isolation, never the composed frame). The THREE layers тАФ structural (savedRegs, every leg) + this byte-pin (assembled bytes, every leg incl. Windows MSVC) + runtime corpus (exit 42, native-arm64 leg) тАФ are complementary; none replaces another. 207/207 (no frame-size regression тАФ x86_64 has no linkRegister so its frames are byte-identical; no arm64 non-leaf frame-size pin existed pre-fix). | (closed) | plan 14 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64 family); `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-FFI-ISEL` (the over-claim this corrected); `D-LK10-ENTRY-ML7-FRAME-BIAS-UNIFY` (the frame substrate it extends); `target_schema.hpp` linkRegister field |
| `D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** тАФ AArch64 intra-function branch displacement resolution. The cycle-13.5 substrate (`BlockRelPatchKind { X86Rel32, Arm64Imm19, Arm64Imm26 }` on `BlockRelPatch`) left the two ARM64 arms as a fail-loud stub (`A_NoEncodingShapeWalker`); this cycle implements the real resolver in `src/asm/asm.cpp`. The displacement is PC-relative TO THE INSTRUCTION ITSELF (`disp = (target - patchOffset) >> 2`, NO +4 bias тАФ unlike x86 rel32's relative-to-after-the-disp) and SCALED by 4 (branch targets are word-aligned); Imm19 (B.cond) occupies bits 5..23, Imm26 (B) bits 0..25. The resolver READ-MODIFY-WRITES only that bit-field (a `writeU32LEAt` would clobber the opcode/cond bits), masks the two's-complement displacement to the field width BEFORE the shift (negative-disp sign bits cannot bleed past the field), asserts 4-byte alignment (`A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant` on a non-multiple delta тАФ never a silent low-bit drop), and signed-range-checks against the field width (`A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange`; >reach тЖТ `D-CSUBSET-LONG-BRANCH` thunk is the future generalization). The fixed32 encoder selects `Arm64Imm19`/`Arm64Imm26` purely from `wire.slotKind` (config-driven; the new `EncodingSlotKind::Imm19={5,19}` added in lockstep) and queues a `BlockRelPatch` into `blockPatches`. ZERO `if(arch/format==тАж)` тАФ the dispatch is on config-selected patch-kind DATA, symmetric with the x86 `X86Rel32` arm. **Pins**: `LirCallconv.Arm64ControlFlowSkeletonEncodesCmpBcondBWithResolvedOffsets` тАФ a 6-word byte-exact pin through the real `assemble()` path covering forward Imm19 (+2), forward Imm26 (+3), and the **negative Imm26 back-edge** (тИТ4 тЖТ `0x17FFFFFC`, two's-complement); RED-on-disable DEMONSTRATED (remove the `>>2` scaling тЖТ `0x14000003`тЖТ`0x1400000C` + `0x17FFFFFC`тЖТ`0x17FFFFF0`). **Runtime**: the `arm64_control_flow` while-loop corpus (exit 42) executes the resolved branches end-to-end тАФ PROVEN LOCALLY under qemu-aarch64 (exit 42 ├Ч3); the native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` CI leg (real arm64 hardware) CONFIRMED it GREEN тАФ run 27170873148, 2026-06-08 (┬зA.5 cross-target runtime closure SATISFIED). | (closed тАФ runtime CONFIRMED green on the native ubuntu-24.04-arm CI leg, run 27170873148) | plan 13 ┬зAS3 (block-rel substrate, cycle-13.5); plan 14 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-CONTROL-FLOW); `D-CSUBSET-LONG-BRANCH` (the >reach thunk, still open) |
| `D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** тАФ AArch64's condition-code table + the generic cond-placement knobs. `arm64.target.json` now declares a `condCodeEncoding` table (10 entries: eq:0 ne:1 slt:11 sle:13 sgt:12 sge:10 ult:3 ule:9 ugt:8 uge:2) consumed by BOTH `jcc` (B.cond, the cond nibble at bits 0..3) AND `setcc` (CSET). The cycle generalized `condCodeFromPayload` with two NEW template fields тАФ `condBitPos` (uint8, default 0) + `condInvert` (bool, default false) тАФ so the SAME generic encoder line (`words[0] |= (condNib & 0x0F) << condBitPos`) serves x86 (the defaults тЖТ byte-identical to pre-knob) AND ARM64 B.cond (condBitPos=0) AND ARM64 CSET (condBitPos=12, condInvert=true: `CSET Xd,cond` = `CSINC Xd,XZR,XZR,invert(cond)`, the AArch64 cond-inversion `cond ^ 1`). ZERO `if(arch==тАж)` тАФ the bit position + inversion are config DATA. Fail-loud: a variant declaring `condCodeFromPayload` on a schema with no `condCodeEncoding` table errors. **Pins**: `Arm64Encoder.CsetEncodesInvertedCondAtBits12` (cset x0,gt=`0x9A9FD7E0` GT 0xCтЖТ0xD; cset x0,eq=`0x9A9F17E0` EQ 0x0тЖТ0x1 тАФ two conditions defeat a "constant nibble" bug; RED-on-disable DEMONSTRATED: disable `condNib ^= 1u` тЖТ 0xD7тЖТ0xC7) + `Arm64Encoder.ZextEncodesOrrW` (zext x0,w1=`0x2A0103E0`) + the B.cond cond-at-bit-0 path pinned via the skeleton's `b.sgt`=`0x5400004C`. **Runtime**: the `arm64_control_flow` corpus exercises cmp+jcc end-to-end (PROVEN LOCALLY under qemu, exit 42; native-arm64 CI CONFIRMED green, run 27170873148). Note: lowerCondBr FUSES the loop's ICmp into cmp+B.cond, so the separately-lowered setcc(CSET)/zext are emitted-but-dead in that corpus тАФ their CORRECTNESS is pinned host-independently by the byte-tests, not the corpus. | (closed тАФ runtime CONFIRMED green on the native ubuntu-24.04-arm CI leg, run 27170873148) | plan 13 ┬зAS3 (cond-code table, cycle-13.5); pairs with `D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26` |
| `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-CONTROL-FLOW` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-08** тАФ the CONTROL-FLOW half of plan-14's combined `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-CONTROL-FLOW + NON-LEAF` row (the NON-LEAF/x30 half closed 2026-06-08 by `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER`). A c-subset function with `if`/`while`/loops now compiles + runs on ARM64: `cmp` (SUBS XZR,Rn,Rm тАФ flags), `jcc` (B.cond imm19 тЖТ ifTrue + B imm26 тЖТ ifFalse, a target-blind 2-WORD MACRO via `fixedWords`+`wordIndex`), `jmp` (B imm26), `setcc` (CSET), `zext` (ORR Wd,WZR,Wm), and `alloca` (virtual тЖТ callconv materializes a frame-relative `lea` = ADD Xd,sp,#imm12) all land. The block-relative branch offsets are filled by the `D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26` resolver; the cond codes by `D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64`. **Proof**: the `examples/c-subset/arm64_control_flow/` runtime corpus (`while (n<42) n=n+1; return n;` тЖТ exit 42) тАФ a genuine cross-target differential (x86_64 + arm64 arms, both must exit 42), PROVEN LOCALLY under qemu-aarch64 (exit 42 ├Ч3 for both the while-loop AND a fused-if `max(a,b)`), plus the host-independent byte-pins (skeleton 6-word + cset/zext + frame-lea). ┬зA.5 closure SATISFIED: the native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` CI leg CONFIRMED green тАФ run 27170873148, 2026-06-08. | (closed тАФ runtime CONFIRMED green on the native ubuntu-24.04-arm CI leg, run 27170873148) | plan 14 line 324 (the combined row); `D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26` + `D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64` (the encoding substrates it consumes); `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-NONLEAF-LINK-REGISTER` (the NON-LEAF sibling half) |
| `D-OUTPUT-EXEC-BIT` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (deferral loop iteration 1, branch feature/0-0-2-p16).** `writeImage` now sets the POSIX execute bits (owner/group/others, `std::filesystem::permissions(..., perm_options::add)`) on an executable-flavor output; `compile_pipeline::linkAndWrite` decides executability CONFIG-DRIVENLY via `ObjectFormatSchema::isImageFlavor()` (no arch/format identity branch тАФ `writeImage` switches on a `bool executable = false`, staying format-blind). A rare chmod failure is a fail-loud WARNING `K_ImageExecBitFailed` (0x8014), NOT a write failure: the bytes are valid, the exec bit a best-effort convenience. No-op on Windows (PE ignores Unix modes). Pins: `LinkWriter.ExecutableOutputCarriesExecuteBitPosix` (#ifndef _WIN32 тАФ red-on-disable BOTH ways: `true`тЖТ`+x` present, `false`тЖТabsent; the POSIX-CI-leg witness) + `LinkWriter.ExecutableWriteSucceedsEveryPlatform` (host-independent smoke, runs on the MSVC gate). `run_binary.hpp`'s test chmod kept as a now-no-op safety net (by its own design). тАФ *Original row (audit trail):* The compiler writes the output executable (`src/link/writer.cpp`, the `--output` path) at the host umask default тАФ mode 0644 on POSIX, NO execute bit. A user running the produced binary directly (`dss-code-prime --output foo; ./foo`) hits "Permission denied"/ENOEXEC (qemu's `prepare_binprm` rejects a file lacking `mode & 0111`; the kernel's execve too). NOT currently blocking: the examples/integrated test runners `chmod +x` the spawned binary before `posix_spawn` (`tests/test_support/run_binary.hpp`), so the corpus runs green on every CI leg. Discovered 2026-06-08 while root-causing the arm64-FFI failure тАФ the 0644 mode masked the real x30 SIGSEGV under a *manual* `qemu-aarch64 <file>` invocation (ENOEXEC fired before any guest code ran). Trigger = the first non-test consumer running a `--output` binary directly, OR a decision to ship runnable-out-of-the-box output. Priority: **normal**. | When the output object-format is EXECUTABLE (`format.type == "exec"` тАФ config-driven, read from the active format, NOT an arch/format identity branch), set the execute bits on the written file via `std::filesystem::permissions(path, perms::owner_exec \| perms::group_exec \| perms::others_exec, perm_options::add)` (portable; effectively a no-op on Windows where PE ignores Unix modes). Pin: write an exec-format output, assert the file carries the execute bit on POSIX. | plan 14 linker (`writer.cpp`); pairs with `run_binary.hpp` (which chmods for tests today) |

Const-ness is threaded sourceтЖТcodegen via a dedicated `MutabilityAttr` HIR side-table (`src/hir/attributes/mutability_attr.hpp` тЖТ `HirMutabilityMap` populated in `cst_to_hir` from `SymbolRecord.isConst` тЖТ read at `hir_to_mir` тЖТ `MirGlobal.isConst` + `globalIsConst()` accessor; preserved across DCE / rebuild / merge). The shared `exec_data_section.hpp` helper was generalized `buildExecRodata`тЖТ`buildExecDataSection(kind,тАж)` (kind-parameterized: Rodata/Data/Bss; Bss = empty bytes + spanSize). All THREE exec writers emit `.data` (writable, on-disk bytes) + `.bss` (NOBITS / zero-fill): ELF (`.data`+`.bss` in a SEPARATE R+W PT_LOAD #2; bss extends p_memsz not p_filesz тАФ both static + dynamic arms), PE (new `.data`/`.bss` section headers; bss SizeOfRawData=0; Characteristics incl. MEM_WRITE read from the schema row), Mach-O (new `__DATA` segment rw- after `__DATA_CONST`; `__data` S_REGULAR + `__bss` S_ZEROFILL last; flags read from schema). Section flags/types come from the format JSON rows (zero identity branches). All FOUR exec formats declare `data`+`bss` rows + `supportedDataSections`. RUNTIME-PROVEN locally: corpus `examples/c-subset/global_write` (mutableтЖТ.data + tentativeтЖТ.bss, both written-then-read) тЖТ **exit 42** on PE (Windows host, was SIGSEGV 139 before), ELF x86_64 (WSL), arm64-ELF (qemu-aarch64); macho arm64 structurally verified (CI-only тАФ Apple Silicon leg runs it). Pins: `tests/asm/test_asm_substrate.cpp` `AsmDataSection.*` (section selection) + `tests/link/test_{pe,elf_exec}_writer.cpp` writable-bit writer pins (the former Rejects* tests flipped to Accepts*). | (closed) | plan 14 LK4 / LK1; supersedes the writable-`.data`/`.bss` "STILL DEFERRED" notes on `D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS` + `D-LK1-MACHO-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS`; closes `D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-BSS-EMIT`; runtime-init globals stay fail-loud (`D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-RUNTIME-INIT`, out of scope) |

| `D-LK1-ELF-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC` | First ELF ET_EXEC module whose `AssembledData` rodata item carries its OWN relocations (a dataтЖТdata reference тАФ e.g. a vtable / const pointer table / 8-byte rodata pointer slot). Priority: **normal** (fail-loud today; no producer emits dataтЖТdata rodata relocations yet тАФ the PE walker's analogous `D-LK4-RODATA-WALKER-RELOC-BASE-OFFSET` path is the precedent to mirror). | Patch each rodata-item relocation into `rodataBytes` at the item's section-relative offset + the reloc's intra-item offset (the def's VA already lives in `symbolVa`), mirroring the PE walker's data-item-relocation arm (`pe.cpp` "Apply DATA-ITEM relocations into the .rdata bytes"). | plan 14 LK1 (ELF exec data sections); mirrors PE `D-LK4-RODATA-WALKER-RELOC-BASE-OFFSET` |
| `D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS` | ЁЯЯб **RODATA PATH CLOSED (rodata-only scope)** тАФ the ELF ET_EXEC writer (`src/link/format/elf.cpp`) now emits a loadable `.rodata` section from `module.dataItems` (SHF_ALLOC, folded into the single R+X `.text` PT_LOAD via `shFlagsToPFlags`; data-symbol VAs added to the static `symbolVa` map so codeтЖТrodata relocations resolve through `applyExecRelocations`). Both `elf64-x86_64-linux-exec` + `elf64-aarch64-linux-exec` declare `supportedDataSections: ["rodata"]` + a `.rodata` row (one shared elf.cpp code path; arch differs only via config). Byte-pinned host-independently (`ElfExecWriter.Rodata*` in `test_elf_exec_writer.cpp`, incl. the PT_LOAD-extension RED-on-disable). **RUNTIME-PROVEN LOCALLY** (2026-06-09): the `global_int` ELF arms (`int answer=42; return answer;`) compile + RUN тЖТ **exit 42 on BOTH x86_64-ELF (WSL native) AND arm64-ELF (qemu-aarch64)**; `strace` confirms `exit_group(42)`, and inspection confirms the global's 42 is read from `.rodata` (`lea [rip+disp]` тЖТ 0x401048 тЖТ load) under a single R+X PT_LOAD whose `p_filesz` covers the rodata extent. **CI RE-CONFIRMED 2026-06-09** (commit `8040410`, run **27187309300**): the `linux-gcc-release` (x86_64) + **`linux-arm64-gcc-release` (native `ubuntu-24.04-arm` hardware)** + `linux-clang-asan` + `macos-clang-release` legs all GREEN тАФ `global_int` RAN тЖТ exit 42 on real arm64 + x86_64. (The `windows-msvc-release` leg failed at the **Setup-CMake** step = a CI-infra flake, NOT a code failure тАФ redundant with the local Win-MSVC 212/212; re-triggered.) The rodata-path runtime is now witnessed BOTH locally (WSL+qemu) AND on the authoritative native CI legs. **DYNAMIC-PATH ARM ALSO CLOSED (2026-06-09, the `#include puts` string-FFI capstone):** the `.rodata` emission was originally wired into the STATIC `encodeElfExec` writer ONLY; the capstone extended it to `encodeElfExecDynamic` (the dynamic-linked ELF exec path, which every libc-importing program now takes тАФ string-literal args reach a DYNAMIC extern, and exit routes through libc) via the SAME shared `buildExecRodata`/`addDataSymbolVas` helper тАФ byte-IDENTICAL when no rodata. So a string-literal pointer arg to a dynamic FFI call (`puts("hello")`) now lands its data in `.rodata` on the dynamic path too; runtime-proven by the `shipped_include_puts` corpus тЖТ stdout `"hello\n"` + exit 42 on linux-x86_64 (WSL) AND linux-arm64 (qemu). **WRITABLE PATH CLOSED 2026-06-19** (by `D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER-MUTABLE-GLOBAL`): writable `.data` + zero-fill `.bss` now emit into a SEPARATE 2nd R+W PT_LOAD (W^X; bss extends p_memsz not p_filesz), on BOTH the static + dynamic ELF arms тАФ runtime-proven (`global_write` exit 42 x86_64-ELF WSL + arm64-ELF qemu). **STILL DEFERRED:** the dataтЖТdata dataItem-reloc path (`D-LK1-ELF-RODATA-DATAITEM-RELOC`). Historical context тАФ the original blocker: the ELF ET_EXEC writer emitted only `.text`/`.symtab`/`.strtab`/`.shstrtab` тАФ NO data section. So a module-level data global (`int g = 42;`) could not reach an ELF executable. This is the blocker for a RUNNABLE ARM64 `lea` corpus: the D-AS4-3 ARM64 `lea` (ADRP+ADD) encoder is built + proven by byte-pins (`Arm64Encoder.Lea*`) + the linker reloc-formula tests (`test_aarch64_reloc_formulas.cpp`), but an end-to-end `static int g=42; int *p=&g; return *p;` corpus that exercises leaтЖТloadтЖТexit-42 needs `g` in a loadable data section (and a 2nd R+W PT_LOAD), which the static ET_EXEC path lacks. Same gap affects x86_64-ELF data globals (the format-dispatch cycle's `extern_call_elf` corpus avoided strings for exactly this reason). NOTE (2026-06-08): the FFI direct-call path that REQUIRES the lea to COMPILE now runs end-to-end (`extern_call_elf` arm64 arm тЖТ `D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-FFI-ISEL` тЬЕ CLOSED) тАФ but that exercises the lea on the COMPILE path only (the lea materializes `&abs` yet the direct BL targets the symbol via the PLT, so the lea's runtime RESULT is dead there). OBSERVING the lea's runtime VALUE still needs either this data-global deref path OR an indirect call through a function POINTER (`D-ML7-2.4`, a separate isel gap). **UPDATE 2026-06-12 (FC4 c2): the fn-pointer option LANDED тАФ `D-ML7-2.4` closed; the `fnptr_call`/`fnptr_callback` arm64-ELF arms BLR through an ADRP+ADD-materialized `&helper` at RUNTIME (qemu exit 42), so the arm64 lea's runtime VALUE is now observed end-to-end.** Priority: **normal** (the lea ENCODER, the user-directed deliverable, is complete + strongly pinned; only the lea's runtime-VALUE observation is gated). | Extend the ELF ET_EXEC writer to lay out a `.rodata`/`.data` section with the module's `AssembledData` bytes, assign each data symbol a VA, add a 2nd page-aligned PT_LOAD (R[+W]), and let the existing relocation applier patch codeтЖТdata references. Then reuse `global_int`-style sources (`int g=42; return g;`) with an `arm64:elf64-aarch64-linux-exec` target arm under the qemu/native cross-arch gate тЖТ exit 42 proves leaтЖТload end-to-end. | plan 14 LK1 (ELF exec data sections); pairs with `D-AS4-3` (the lea encoder it would make runnable) + the `D-LK4-RODATA-PRODUCER-*` family (the constant-encoding walker) |

| `D-LK-RODATA-HELPER-PE-PARITY` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (deferral loop, branch feature/0-0-2-p16) тАФ ALREADY-SATISFIED (stale-open row), verified this cycle.** PE was retrofitted onto the shared helper by the **D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER** cycle: `pe.cpp` calls `buildExecDataSection` (660-677) + `addDataSymbolVas` (921-938) for ALL three section kinds тАФ NO inline rodataтЖТsymbolVa loop remains (the premise of this row is stale). **M1** (anonymous-`SymbolId{}` skip) is handled by `addDataSymbolVas` and chokepoint-tested via `test_elf_exec_writer.cpp:2107` (PE inherits it by-construction through the shared chokepoint). **H1** (over-aligned item) is **STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE** for PE, not merely masked: the `Alignment` type CAPS at 256 bytes (`alignment.hpp` `fromBytes`/`of<N>` reject > 256) while PE `sectionAlignment` is тЙе page (4096), so a section's `maxAlign` can NEVER exceed its alignment тАФ a `checkMaxAlign` fail-loud guard would be untestable dead code (no constructible over-aligned item). Verified the live code state directly; no honest code or test to add. тАФ *Original row (audit trail):* PE (`src/link/format/pe.cpp`) is NOT yet converted to the shared `src/link/format/exec_data_section.hpp` helper (`buildExecRodata` + `addDataSymbolVas`) тАФ elf.cpp + macho.cpp are. Plan-lock chose to DEFER PE (Option b): PE is the Windows-critical-path runtime and its latent H1 (max-item-align) is MASKED by page-aligned `.rdata`, and its M1 (anonymous-symbol-skip) is dormant тАФ so retrofitting it carries Windows-runtime risk for no current correctness gain. Priority: **normal** (DRY/maintainability + closes PE's latent H1/M1; not a correctness bug today). | Retrofit `pe.cpp`'s inline rodataтЖТsymbolVa loop onto `buildExecRodata`/`addDataSymbolVas` (this ALSO fixes PE's latent H1 over-aligned-item + M1 anonymous-item gaps). Trigger: the next PE-touching cycle, OR a producer ever emits an anonymous / over-aligned data item (which would surface PE's H1/M1 as a real bug). | `D-LK6-AARCH64-ADDABSLO12-HIGH-VA` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-09 тАФ RUNTIME-PROVEN on Apple Silicon** (run **27192415923**, same witness as `D-LK1-MACHO-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS`: the `macos-clang-release` leg ran `global_int` macos-arm64 тЖТ ctest **#184 Passed** тЖТ exit 42, which means the high-VA ADD_ABS_LO12_NC reloc RESOLVED at runtime тАФ else exit тЙа 42). Cited at `exec_reloc_apply.hpp` `Aarch64AddAbsLo12`. The ADD_ABS_LO12_NC ("No Check") reloc applier carried an ELF-centric `S+A тЙд UINT32_MAX` upper bound that WRONGLY rejected valid Mach-O PIE VAs (which sit above the 4 GiB `__PAGEZERO`, e.g. 0x1'0000'4040) тАФ it blocked the macOS-arm64 data-global access path (`global_int` тЖТ `__const`). Root cause: `(S+A) & 0xFFF` is a magnitude-INDEPENDENT low-12 extraction (the uint32 cast cannot perturb the low 12 bits), and the address magnitude is range-checked by the PAIRED ADRP companion (`adr_prel_pg_hi21`, signed-21-bit page delta) тАФ so the ADD itself needs no upper bound. A code-correctness relaxation, byte-pinned host-independently by `Aarch64AddAbsLo12.ResolvesSplusAAboveUint32Max` (`test_aarch64_reloc_formulas.cpp`) + `MachOArm64Exit.MachoArm64ConstAdrpAddLeaHighVaResolvesLowTwelve` (`test_macho_arm64_exit.cpp`), with RED-on-disable demonstrated (restore the `> UINT32_MAX` clause тЖТ the high-VA pins fail). The relaxation is confirmed NO-OP for ELF/PE (their VAs are < UINT32_MAX) by the `linux-gcc-release` + `linux-clang-asan` + `linux-arm64-gcc-release` + `windows-msvc-release` legs of run 27192415923 all staying GREEN. | (closed) тАФ dropped the `S+A > UINT32_MAX` clause; kept `S+A тЙе 0` (a negative VA is nonsensical). RUNTIME witness = run 27192415923 `macos-clang-release` #184 (`global_int` macos-arm64 тЖТ exit 42). | plan 14 LK6; macOS data-section cycle; runtime witness shared with `D-LK1-MACHO-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS` |
| `D-MERGE-CONTEXT-PREFIX-CLOBBER` | First time `mergeWithTargetContext` clobbers a prefix from a sibling import. | Per-prefix-source side-table (the `D-MERGE-CONTEXT-PREFIX-SIDE-TABLE` companion). | plan 11 FF / merge policy |
| `D-MERGE-CONTEXT-PREFIX-SIDE-TABLE` | Companion to D-MERGE-CONTEXT-PREFIX-CLOBBER тАФ same closure cycle. | (paired with above) | plan 11 FF / merge |
| `D-MERGE-DEDUP-PREFIX-COLLISION` | First time two distinct prefix sources dedup to the same key but produce different values. | Fail-loud on collision with both source citations. | plan 11 FF / merge |
| `D-MERGE-POLICY-IDEMPOTENCY` | First time the merge policy needs idempotency (re-running the merge on already-merged input changes the output). | Define an `isMerged()` predicate + skip-when-already-merged guard. | plan 11 FF / merge |
| `D-MERGE-SCRATCH-FRESH` | First time the merge's scratch context is silently reused across calls. | Reset / fresh-construct the scratch on each merge entry. | plan 11 FF / merge |
| `D-FFI-ANGLE-INCLUDE-LINE-SCOPED-HEADER` | A UX pass on include diagnostics, OR a header name legitimately needing multi-token content. Priority: **normal** (fail-loud today тАФ a malformed `#include <foo` is reported, see `tests/tokenizer/test_tokenizer.cpp::FF11MalformedAngleIncludeNoCloserFailsLoud`; only the diagnostic flavor + consumption scope are deferred polish). | A malformed `#include <foo` (no closing `>`) currently runs the coalescing `header-body` mode to EOF and reports the generic `P_UnterminatedString` (matching `string`-body semantics тАФ `header-body` mirrors the `string` body: coalesced `defaultToken`, `endsAt: ">"`, no `popAtNewline`). The long-term polish is a header-specific unterminated flavor (a new `UnterminatedFlavor::Header` тЖТ a dedicated `P_*` code) PLUS line-scoped termination (stop the header body at the newline, like `popAtNewline`) so the error is header-specific AND the rest of the file is not consumed past the directive line. Do NOT re-engineer this cycle (scope creep) тАФ the current behavior is consistent established fail-loud, NOT a regression. | FF11 angle-include (`c-subset.lang.json` `include-directive`/`header-body` modes); pairs with the general `popAtNewline` capability; do NOT cite in `src/` (future-work pin only) |
| `D-FFI-CROSS-HEADER-ODR-FIDELITY` | Full C ODR fidelity becomes a goal, OR a corpus surfaces two included headers declaring the same name as DISTINCT symbols. Priority: **normal** (rare тАФ the common case of two headers declaring the SAME `extern` is correctly idempotent). | FF11 goal-2 flags a PROGRAM `extern` conflicting with an INCLUDED header's decl (`S_RedeclaredSymbol`), but NOT the header-vs-header case: two included headers each declaring the same name as DISTINCT symbols currently last-writer-wins silently (idempotent cross-tree `injectBinding` visibility тАФ deliberately not flagged, correct for FF11's program-vs-header scope). Closing work = extend the cross-tree redeclaration check to also flag header-vs-header same-name conflicts (distinct symbols), leaving the program-vs-header path + the same-symbol idempotent case unchanged. | FF11 goal-2 (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` cross-tree redeclaration loop); surfaced by the cycle-21 self-audit; do NOT cite in `src/` (future-work pin only) |
| `D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32` | The first ILP32 (32-bit-pointer) target lands (e.g. i386 / armv7 / wasm32). Today EVERY DSS exec target is 64-bit (x86_64-PE/ELF, arm64-ELF/Mach-O) and LP64 + LLP64 agree on pointer width (8 bytes), so the pointer-width + greatest-width typedefs in the neutral `stdint.json`/`stddef.json` (`intptr_t`/`uintptr_t`/`intmax_t`/`uintmax_t`/`size_t`/`ptrdiff_t`) are correctly i64/u64. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated тАФ the 64-bit-only values are correct for every current target; first surfaced by the p18 SQLite-readiness Cluster G headers cycle as the FIRST consumer of the descriptor typedef path). | When an ILP32 target is added, the pointer-width typedefs (`intptr_t`/`uintptr_t`/`size_t`/`ptrdiff_t`) must become 4-byte for THAT target while staying 8-byte on the 64-bit ones. A single literal hir-text typedef can't express "pointer-width" across both ABIs, so the close is a per-target width resolution: the descriptor declares the typedef as a width-CLASS the semantic phase resolves against the active target's `dataModel`/pointer width (NOT an `if (target == i386)` in the engine тАФ the descriptor/loader declares the width axis, the engine reads it). Pin: an ILP32 target compiles a program asserting `sizeof(intptr_t)==4` while a 64-bit target sees 8. | cited in `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/{stdint,stddef}.json` ($comment); pairs with `D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH` + `D-FFI-WCHAR-WIDTH` (both "a descriptor baked an ABI/platform width") + `D-FFI-STDINT-LIMIT-MACROS` (the width-dependent *_MAX inherit this); SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18) |
| `D-FFI-STDINT-LIMIT-MACROS` | A program references a `<stdint.h>` limit macro тАФ `INT8_MAX`/`INT64_MIN`/`UINT32_MAX`/`SIZE_MAX`/`INTPTR_MAX`/etc. Today the neutral `stdint.json` ships ONLY the typedefs (no `constants` surface), so a direct limit-macro reference fails loud as an undefined identifier (never silent). Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated тАФ SQLite's amalgamation mostly self-defines these via `#if !defined(...)`, but a direct reference would need them; first noted p18 Cluster G). | Add a `constants` surface to `stdint.json` carrying the *_MAX/*_MIN integer macros (INT8_MIN=-128 тАж INT64_MAX=9223372036854775807, the UINT*_MAX, plus SIZE_MAX/PTRDIFF_MIN/PTRDIFF_MAX/INTPTR_*/INTMAX_*), each integer-typed (rides as i64/u64 via the descriptor `constants` mechanism). The pointer-width + greatest-width limits (SIZE_MAX/INTPTR_MAX/INTMAX_MAX) are 64-bit-target values that inherit the ILP32 deferral [[D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32]]. Pin: a program using `INT32_MAX` compiles and the value is byte-exact. | cited in `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/stdint.json` ($comment); pairs with `D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32` (width-dependent limits) + the descriptor `constants` mechanism (FC14 item-1, cf. `limits.json`); SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18) |
| `D-FFI-WCHAR-WIDTH` | A program needs `wchar_t` at its platform-correct width тАФ 16-bit on Windows (PE), 32-bit on POSIX/Linux+macOS (ELF/Mach-O). The neutral `stddef.json` ships `wchar_t = i32` (the Linux+macOS width); on a PE/Windows target the conforming C width is 16-bit. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated тАФ SQLite is UTF-8/`char` internally and uses no `wchar_t`, so the i32 default is correct for the Linux+macOS deliverables and no current corpus exercises it; first noted p18 Cluster G). | Make `wchar_t`'s width per-target (2 bytes on PE/Windows, 4 on ELF/Mach-O) via the SAME per-target width resolution as [[D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32]] (a `dataModel`/format-keyed typedef value the semantic phase resolves against the active target тАФ NOT an `if (format == pe)` in the engine). Pin: a Windows-target program sees `sizeof(wchar_t)==2`, a Linux-target one sees `4`. | cited in `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/stddef.json` ($comment); pairs with `D-FFI-STDINT-PTR-WIDTH-ILP32` + `D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH`; SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18) |
| `D-FFI-OFFSETOF-MACRO` | A program references `offsetof(type, member)` from `<stddef.h>`. Today `stddef.json` ships `size_t`/`ptrdiff_t`/`wchar_t`/`NULL` but NOT `offsetof` (a function-like macro тАФ no descriptor surface for it yet), so an `offsetof` reference fails loud. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated тАФ SQLite uses `offsetof` in a few spots, so this may fire during the SQLite compile; first noted p18 Cluster G). | `offsetof` is the function-like macro `((size_t)&((type*)0)->member)`. The cleanest agnostic home is a `__builtin_offsetof`-style intrinsic the semantic phase recognizes and const-folds to the member's layout byte-offset via the EXISTING FC6 aggregate-layout machinery (exact, no UB null-pointer arithmetic) тАФ preferred over (b) shipping a textual function-like macro in the descriptor (which needs a function-like-macro surface the descriptor schema lacks today). Pin: `offsetof(struct S, b)` const-folds to the correct byte offset for a known-layout struct. | cited in `src/dss-config/shippedLibs/stddef.json` ($comment); pairs with the FC6 aggregate-layout offset computation + the descriptor `typedefs`/`constants` mechanism; SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18) |
| `D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MISCOMPILE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 Cluster G c2 тАФ ROOT CAUSE = INLINING a variadic callee).** A variadic function SILENTLY MISCOMPILED under the RELEASE optimizer pipeline. Minimal repro (`examples`-shaped): `static int vsum(int n, ...){ va_list ap; int acc=0,i; va_start(ap,n); for(i=0;i<n;i=i+1) acc=acc+va_arg(ap,int); va_end(ap); return acc; } int main(void){ return vsum(3,10,13,19); }` тЖТ **baseline (debug pipeline) exit 42 (CORRECT); release (`--config=release`) exit 0 (WRONG)** on x86_64-PE (reproduced HEAD 0e3a550, `build/bin/dss/Release`). **Priority: HIGH тАФ correctness-critical, silent miscompile.** BLOCKS SQLite-green RELEASE: SQLite is variadic-heavy (`sqlite3_mprintf`/`sqlite3_snprintf`/`sqlite3VXPrintf`/its test harnesses). ROOT of the blind spot: FC12 shipped the variadic feature BASELINE-ONLY (its corpus carried no `{"shippedPipeline":"release"}` arm), so the optimizer├Ч`va_list` composition was NEVER witnessed тАФ exactly the masked-effectiveness trap the dss-cycle `release`-arm corpus rule exists to prevent. | тЬЕ (CLOSED) ROOT CAUSE = the INLINER inlined the static single-call variadic callee into `main`; the spliced `va_start` leaves (`VaRegSaveAreaAddr`/`VaOverflowArgAreaAddr`/`VaHomeArgAreaAddr`) bound to MAIN's frame (which never spilled the varargs) тЖТ `ap` read garbage. FIX = `inlineLegalityGate` (`src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp`) refuses any callee whose body contains one of those three va_start leaves тАФ the universal "a variadic fn that calls va_start is not inlinable" rule; agnostic (MIR opcodes only), sibling of the `RecvByValueStackParam` refusal. PINS (both red-on-disable): `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::VariadicCalleeCallingVaStartIsNotInlined` (callsInlined==0) + corpus `examples/c-subset/varargs_inline_release` (release arm exit 42, PE-runnable). тШЕ Closing this surfaced 2 MORE variadic-release optimizer bugs the FC12 BASELINE-ONLY corpus masked (a CLUSTER, NOT a single bug) тЖТ [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MEM2REG-PHI]] + [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-ARGINDEX]]; the broad "add a `release` arm to EVERY variadic example" closure follows each fix (this cycle adds only `varargs_inline_release` тАФ the other arms stay off until their bug lands). | CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 c2); cited in `src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp` (gate refusal) + `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/varargs_inline_release/`; pairs with `D-OPT7-INLINE-FRAME-SENSITIVE-INTRINSIC` (the hypothetical-intrinsic sibling тАФ va_start in DSS is these dedicated MIR leaves, not an IntrinsicCall) + `D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-VARIADIC-SIGNATURE` + the FC12 variadic cycle + the dss-cycle `release`-arm corpus discipline |
| `D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MEM2REG-PHI` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 Cluster G c3) тАФ was the variadic SYMPTOM of a GENERAL Mem2Reg bug; fixed together as [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]].** Compiling `examples/c-subset/varargs_win64_sum` under RELEASE aborted FAIL-LOUD `Mem2Reg fatal: phi ... has no reaching value from predecessor`. тШЕ The c3 PROBE OVERTURNED the scope: it is NOT Win64/variadic-specific тАФ the trigger is the `int iv = va_arg(ap,int); double dv = va_arg(ap,double);` LOOP-BODY-LOCALS, and a minimal NON-variadic `int f(int n){int t=0,i=0;while(i<n){int iv=i*2;t=t+iv;i=i+1;}return t;}` reproduces the IDENTICAL fatal. The variadic example was just the FIRST to exercise it under release (FC12 shipped the variadic corpus baseline-only). | тЬЕ (CLOSED) FIXED by [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]] (semi-pruned SSA тАФ see that row for the mechanism). `varargs_win64_sum` gained a `{"shippedPipeline":"release"}` arm (exit 39, was Mem2Reg-fatal); the general witness is `examples/c-subset/loop_body_local`. | CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 c3); subsumed by [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]]; pairs with [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-MISCOMPILE]] (the inlining sibling) + [[D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-ARGINDEX]] (the still-open 3rd cluster member) |
| `D-OPT-MEM2REG-DEAD-PHI-PRUNE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 Cluster G c3 тАФ the COMPLETE fix; HIGH, GENERAL Mem2Reg correctness bug).** Under the RELEASE pipeline Mem2Reg ABORTED FAIL-LOUD (`phi at block ... has no reaching value from predecessor`) whenever a promoted alloca got a DEAD phi at an IDF block where it was not LIVE but its incoming was undefined. TWO classes: (1) a loop-BODY-LOCAL (`while(){ int x=expr; use(x); }`, dead at the header) тАФ the first-found symptom [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]]; (2) the SUBTLER NESTED-LOOP case тАФ an inner counter `j` re-initialized each OUTER iteration is LIVE across the inner back-edge yet DEAD at the OUTER header. Class (2) (and a single loop whose body contains an `if`-merge) crashes with ZERO body-locals тЖТ an ENORMOUS class of ordinary C (nested loops saturate SQLite). The c3 pr-review's RUNTIME battery caught (2) after the initial semi-pruned commit (ecf40c3) only handled (1) тАФ the green-but-incomplete trap; upgraded to the complete fix the same cycle. | тЬЕ (CLOSED) FIX = FULLY-PRUNED SSA (Choi/Cytron/Sarkar) in `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp`: place a phi for alloca A at IDF block S ONLY IF A is LIVE-IN at S (a backward live-in dataflow: `live_in[B]=use[B] тИк (live_out[B]\def[B])`, use=upward-exposed Load, def=Store, alloca-site NOT a def). Strictly stronger than semi-pruned (upward-exposure): a name DEAD at one IDF block but live elsewhere (the nested counter) is pruned THERE specifically. PROVABLY SAFE: every KEPT phi is at a block where A is live тЖТ a needed merge is never pruned (no miscompile, verified by a base==release battery: diamond/induction/nested/triple-nested/body-local-live-across all correct on both pipelines). PRESERVES fail-loud: a genuine uninitialized read IS live-in at its use тЖТ phi kept тЖТ still aborts (verified). PINS (red-on-disable): `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp::{LoopBodyLocalAllocaNeedsNoPhi, NestedLoopInnerCounterNoOuterHeaderPhi}` + corpus `examples/c-subset/{loop_body_local (exit 20), nested_loop_release (exit 4)}` release arms. Agnostic (MIR/SSA only). | CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 c3); cited in `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp` (the live-in gate) + `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/nested_loop_release/`; subsumes [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-LOOP-BODY-LOCAL-DEAD-PHI]] (class 1); pairs with the Choi/Cytron/Sarkar pruned-SSA literature |
| `D-OPT-MEM2REG-CONDITIONAL-INIT-UNDEF` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (post-sqlite C23+perf arc, cycle 1) тАФ the RELEASE-pipeline Mem2Reg abort on benign CONDITIONAL-INIT; the blocker to `--config=release` sqlite.** Root: Mem2Reg's rename walk `std::abort()`'d whenever a promoted alloca had NO reaching value at a phi-incoming edge (`mem2reg.cpp:627`) or a Load (`:599`) тАФ a LIVE-but-conditionally-defined local (`int x; if(c) x=1; return x+c;`) or a dead-path uninit read. Both are VALID C (gcc compiles sqlite, full of them); p18 [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-DEAD-PHI-PRUNE]] deliberately "preserved the fatal" (its comment), which was WRONG тАФ a compiler must compile valid C. DISCOVERED by the T1.0 perf-profiling probe: sqlite-green was only ever verified in the DEFAULT (debug) config, which runs no Mem2Reg; `--config=release` aborted at the first sqlite conditional-init (block 1492). FIX (agnostic, MIR/SSA only): materialize undef-as-zero for the missing incoming тАФ a new `ReachingValue::Kind::ZeroConst` (value = element TypeId.v), ONE `Const 0` per element type emitted in the ENTRY block (dominates every edge тЖТ verifier-legal incoming everywhere; entry is `selectBlocks[0]` so it precedes any Load resolve). GPR-Const-zeroable element types stay PROMOTED (the common case, no perf loss); FPR (float тАФ no float-immediate) + aggregates have no scalar zero тЖТ the alloca is DE-PROMOTED (left as memory = what debug does; detected PRE-walk via `liveIn[entry]` = read-before-write), a narrow perf loss pinned [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-FPR-CONDITIONAL-INIT-RODATA-ZERO]]. Uninit-read DIAGNOSTIC responsibility moves to a frontend warning [[D-SEMANTIC-MAYBE-UNINITIALIZED-WARN]]. Independent design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (caught the float-`Const`-poison blocker тЖТ the de-promotion; resetPerFunction clears + fail-loud-on-miss тАФ all folded). WITNESSES: 3 red-on-disable units (`ConditionallyInitializedLiveAllocaZeroFillsUndefEdge` [phi zero-fill + MirVerifier dominance] / `...LoadZeroFillsUninitPath` / `...FloatAllocaIsDepromoted`) + 2 release-arm corpus (`examples/c-subset/cond_init_release`тЖТ2, `cond_init_float_release`тЖТ1) + the 2-TU sqlite `--config=release` compile clears the Mem2Reg wall + the minimal repro `int f(int c){int x;if(c)x=1;return x+c;}`+`main(argc)`тЖТexit 2. | тЬЕ (CLOSED) | post-sqlite C23+perf arc cycle 1 (2026-07-07); `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp` + `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/{cond_init_release,cond_init_float_release}`; discovered by T1.0 profiling; pairs with [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-DEAD-PHI-PRUNE]] (complements the p18 dead-phi prune) |
| `D-OPT-MEM2REG-FPR-CONDITIONAL-INIT-RODATA-ZERO` | тП│ **GATED (perf; opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-CONDITIONAL-INIT-UNDEF]]).** A CONDITIONALLY-INITIALIZED FLOAT/aggregate local is DE-PROMOTED (left as memory) at the missing-incoming site because a float has no directly-lowerable zero `Const` (register machines have no float-immediate; DSS routes float constants through rodata `GlobalAddr+Load`) and aggregates have no scalar zero. CORRECT but a minor perf loss (those allocas stay in memory under release; rare тАФ most floats are init-before-use). Trigger: a profile shows conditionally-initialized float promotion matters. Resolution: mirror the HIRтЖТMIR float-literal promotion (`hir_to_mir.cpp:559-578`) тАФ mint an anonymous rodata `MirGlobal` holding the zero bit-pattern + `GlobalAddr`+`Load` in the entry block тАФ so FPR conditional-init allocas promote too; fail-loud-in-place for F16/F128 (no encodings). | (gated тАФ perf refinement; de-promotion is correct today) | post-sqlite C23+perf arc; `src/opt/passes/mem2reg.cpp` (the de-promotion loop) + the `hir_to_mir.cpp` float-promotion precedent; plan 12 MIR/LIR optimizer |
| `D-SEMANTIC-MAYBE-UNINITIALIZED-WARN` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-OPT-MEM2REG-CONDITIONAL-INIT-UNDEF]]).** Removing the Mem2Reg abort on conditional-init (a compiler must compile valid C) dropped the incidental "genuine uninitialized read" signal it provided. Its correct home is a FRONTEND/semantic WARNING (gcc/Clang's `-Wmaybe-uninitialized`), not a hard optimizer abort тАФ a promoted automatic scalar Load not dominated by any Store, no address taken. Trigger: a maybe-uninitialized diagnostic is prioritized (or a corpus wants it). Resolution: a semantic-tier maybe-uninitialized warning. NOT a blocker тАФ silently materializing undef-as-zero is conforming (LLVM's mem2reg does the same; the diagnostic is a frontend concern). | (gated тАФ a diagnostic-quality improvement, non-blocking) | post-sqlite C23+perf arc; `src/semantic/` (frontend); the mem2reg.cpp comment records the move |
| `D-OPT1-VERIFY-FREQUENCY-CONFIG` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (post-sqlite perf arc) тАФ config-driven optimizer verify frequency (the LLVM/GCC posture).** `optimize()` ran a full-module `MirVerifier` after EVERY pass under ALL build modes (`D-OPT1-VERIFY-AFTER-EVERY-PASS`) тАФ on sqlite that is ~11s ├Ч (9 passes ├Ч up-to-4 iterations ├Ч 3 CU-runs) тЙИ 10тАУ20 min of pure re-verification (surfaced by the T1.0/perf profiling of the >30-min release compile). FIX: a config-driven `OptPipeline::verifyEveryPass` (default TRUE = the DEVELOPER posture, LLVM `-verify-each` / GCC `--enable-checking=yes`, which pinpoints the offending pass); `release.pipeline.json` sets FALSE тЖТ verify ONCE after the whole pipeline before codegen (the PRODUCTION posture тАФ trust tested passes, verify at the boundary). Agnostic (a pipeline-config bool, NOT a build-mode branch in the engine); overridable per-pipeline (flip releaseтЖТverify-each on demand to hunt a release-only miscompile). Also split `opt-verify` out as its own `PhaseTimers` phase + added env-gated `DSS_OPT_TRACE` per-pass / per-function optimizer instrumentation (kept тАФ zero-cost when off). Windows ctest 397/397 (opt+examples+pipeline); the `ShippedReleasePipelineCompilesBothProbeShapes` test's explicit final-verify still green (result.ok now implies FINAL-module cleanliness). The RELEASE-optimize perf ROOT (every pass rebuilds the module + recomputes all analyses from scratch тАФ no caching, no change-tracking) is the [[project_v0_0_2_post_sqlite_c23_perf_roadmap_2026_07_07]] LLVM-style arc (analysis caching + in-place passes). тШЕ **EXTENDED 2026-07-07 to MARKER MAINTENANCE:** `rederiveStructCfMarkers(mir)` (whole-module preds + per-fn RPO + dom + derivation, ~13s/call on sqlite) ran after EVERY SimplifyCfg + EVERY Inlining (8├Ч/compile = 127s measured тАФ ~91% of Inlining's and ~78% of SimplifyCfg's cost). Markers feed ONLY the verifier (grep-verified: no optimization decision and no codegen reads them; pass rebuilds copy them through) тЖТ `runSimplifyCfg`/`runInlining` gained `bool maintainMarkers = true` (dev/test posture byte-identical), optimize() threads `verifyEveryPass` into it and, under the release posture, re-stamps ONCE from the FINAL CFG before the final verify. Inlining 62тЖТ7.3s (тИТ88%), SimplifyCfg 64тЖТ14.4s (тИТ78%); compile 5m54тЖТ4m24. WITNESS: `test_optimizer.cpp::ReleasePostureRederivesMarkersOnceAfterPipeline` (canonicality-by-idempotence over a fold shape whose stale IfThen marker provably differs тАФ dropping the final re-stamp reds it); 3-leg 566/566. | тЬЕ (CLOSED) | post-sqlite perf arc (2026-07-07); `src/opt/optimizer.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/opt/optimizer_json.cpp` + `src/dss-config/pipelines/release.pipeline.json` + `src/core/substrate/phase_timers.{hpp,cpp}`; plan 22 optimizer / the LLVM-style optimizer perf arc |
| `D-OPT-CSE-ANALYSIS-HOIST` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (post-sqlite perf arc, LLVM-style Phase 1) тАФ CSE was the single biggest release-optimizer pass on sqlite (118s of a 7m39s optimize); now ~56s.** Root: `mirBuildPredecessors(mir)` (WHOLE-MODULE) was recomputed (a) per function in `CsePolicy::analyze` (cse.cpp:158; runCse calls it per function тЖТ O(numFunctions ├Ч moduleSize)) AND (b) per Load-CSE query inside `mirRegionBetween` (mir_alias.hpp), whose intersection loop ALSO scanned all module blocks per query. | `D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS` | тЬЕ **CARRIER CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T3 full-C23 groundwork) тАФ the type-system CARRIER for C11/C23 `alignas` on a struct/union MEMBER; a PRECISE MIRROR of the c107 `fieldOffsets` channel ([[D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-UNION-OVERLAY]]).** A struct may carry a per-field EXPLICIT alignment override span `CompositeFields.fieldAligns` (bytes, power of two; `0` = no override тЖТ natural alignment). ALL-fields-or-EMPTY (a partial set is a caller bug, fail loud at `completeComposite`); enters `contentDeclSiteKey` GUARDED on non-empty so an align-free struct hashes BYTE-IDENTICALLY to today (ZERO TypeId churn тАФ every existing composite keeps its exact declSiteKey) while `struct{alignas(16) int x;}` is a DISTINCT TypeId from `struct{int x;}`. `computeLayout`'s ordinary (non-bitfield) struct arm folds the override into each field's natural alignment with MAX semantics (a member alignas RAISES, never LOWERS) тАФ applied to both the ordinary field path AND the flexible-array-member sub-case (`alignas(16) int fam[];`); `Alignment::fromBytes` fail-louds (nulloptтЖТno layout) on a stored non-pow2/>256 value (an upstream bug). Threaded end-to-end exactly like offsets: the new 5-arg `structType` overload + `completeComposite` param + `hasExplicitAligns`/`explicitFieldAlign` accessors (type_interner.hpp/type_lattice.cpp) тЖТ reintern carry (type_reintern.cpp, so a reinterned member-aligned struct keeps its TypeId) тЖТ HIR type-text round-trip via a NEW per-field `~<align>` marker (non-colliding with the offset `@<off>`; the two channels are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE тАФ `completeComposite` fail-louds if BOTH aligns and offsets are set, since explicit offsets override alignment wholesale). MIR text was NOT touched (mirrors c107 EXACTLY тАФ MIR text never round-tripped offsets either; a member-aligned struct is not MIR-text-reachable in the carrier tier). WITNESSES: `TypeInterner.MemberAlignsEnterContentIdentityZeroChurn` (empty-span == 2-arg TypeId; aligns={16} distinct) + `TypeLayout.{MemberAlignasRaisesStructAlignAndSize, MemberAlignasNeverLowersBelowNatural, MemberAlignasRaisesFollowingFieldOffset}` + `TypeReintern.MemberAlignsSurviveReintern` + `HirText.MemberAlignsRoundTrip` + `ParseTypeFromText.MemberAlignsParseAndForkIdentity` (all red-on-disable). Full existing type-interner/layout/reintern/hir-text suites + the 573-case Debug ctest stay green (the zero-churn proof). тЬЕ FOLLOW-UP LANDED 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]] (the `alignas`/`_Alignas` PARSING + SEMANTICS frontend that POPULATES `fieldAligns` from a declared member `alignas(N)`/`alignas(T)`). The frontend cycle ALSO extended the carrier's `computeLayout` UNION arm to fold `explicitFieldAlign` (the struct arm already did; the union arm was a carrier gap тАФ a `union { alignas(16) char c; int i; }` now correctly aligns to 16). | тЬЕ (CARRIER CLOSED; frontend LANDED via [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]]) | T3 full-C23 groundwork (2026-07-07); `src/core/types/type_lattice/{type_interner.hpp,type_lattice.cpp,type_layout.cpp,type_reintern.cpp}` + `src/hir/hir_text.cpp` + `tests/core/type_lattice/{test_type_interner,test_type_reintern}.cpp` + `tests/core/test_type_layout.cpp` + `tests/hir/test_hir_text.cpp`; a precise mirror of [[D-FFI-DESCRIPTOR-UNION-OVERLAY]] (c107); plan-23 full-C23 alignas |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T3 full-C23) тАФ the `_Alignas`/`alignas` alignment-specifier FRONTEND + SEMANTICS (C11/C23 ┬з6.7.5), targeting the [[D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS]] carrier.** Config-driven vocabulary (2 keywords `_Alignas`/`alignas` + `alignasSpec` [`kw ( alignasArg )`] + `alignasArg` [SPECULATIVE `castTypeRef` (type form) \| `assignmentExpr` (value form), with the shared `commitRequiresTypeName` binder triage sizeof/va_arg use]) admitted in the variable specifier alts (`singleDeclSpecifier`/`localDeclSpecifier`) + a NEW `structMemberDeclSpecifiers` prefix on `structField`/`unionField` (declared as their `specifierPrefix` so decl_prefix_strip drops it before the head:0/declaratorList:1 count тАФ zero index churn) + `semantics.alignas {specRule, argChild, typeFormRule}`. SEMANTIC path (`semantic_analyzer.cpp`, Pass-1 declarator-mode, mirroring the bit-field-width plumbing): `evalOneAlignasSpec` reads the operand тАФ TYPE form тЖТ `_Alignof(T)` via `computeLayout(...)->align`; VALUE form тЖТ `constIntExpr` (the SAME evaluator static_assert/array-dims use, so `alignas(16)`/`alignas(2*8)`/`alignas(ENUM_VAL)` fold) тАФ validates (power-of-two тЖТ S_AlignasNotPowerOfTwo; >256 тЖТ S_AlignasExceedsMax [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-EXCEEDS-MAX]]; < the declared type's natural align тЖТ S_AlignasWeakerThanNatural, 6.7.5p4; non-constant тЖТ S_AlignasNonConstant; `alignas(0)` = NO-OP per 6.7.5p3), and STORES: `SymbolRecord.explicitAlignment` (a NEW field) for a VARIABLE (deliberately UNCONSUMED by variable codegen тАФ a SEPARATE deferred task), OR the composite's `fieldAligns` span (gathered at Pass-1 completion exactly like `fieldBitWidths`, all-fields-or-EMPTY for zero churn) for a MEMBER тЖТ `computeLayout` raises the layout END-TO-END. CONTEXT checks (6.7.5p2, S_AlignasInvalidContext): a FUNCTION (definition/prototype) or a TYPEDEF (kind Type) declaration, or a BIT-FIELD member. All 5 codes UNSUPPRESSABLE (constraint-violation-with-silent-failure-risk, the static_assert precedent). AUDIT-caught fix folded: the TYPE-form branch resolves the type BEFORE the layout-params check so a value optimistically parsed as a type-name (`alignas(nc)`) fails loud S_AlignasNonConstant even in a layout-free analysis, NEVER silent. Agnostic (config-keyed RuleIds; toy/tsql never see it). WITNESSES: 16 semantic unit tests (`SemanticAnalyzerCSubset.Alignas*` тАФ parse both spellings/forms, variable store, member+union layout end-to-end via `_Alignof`/`sizeof`, each constraint red-on-disable) + 5 diagnostic-corpus goldens (`alignas_{not_power_of_two,exceeds_max,on_function,on_bitfield,nonconstant}`) + a pe64 e2e probe (`struct S{alignas(16) char c;}` тЖТ `_Alignof`==16, `sizeof`==16; `struct T{char c; alignas(8) int i;}` тЖТ offsetof(i)==8). тП│ DEFERRED (pinned): [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-VARIABLE-CODEGEN]] (thread `explicitAlignment` to globals/locals emit) + [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-TYPEDEF-PARAM-PARSE]] (a `typedef alignas(тАж)` / `f(alignas(тАж) int)` is a fail-loud PARSE error, not the S_AlignasInvalidContext semantic code тАФ the dedicated `typedefDecl`/`param` rules don't admit a strippable mid/prefix alignas without extra grammar surface). | тЬЕ (CLOSED) | T3 full-C23 (2026-07-07); `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_analyzer.cpp,semantic_model.hpp}` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp,type_lattice/type_layout.cpp}` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (linkage-ignore) + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + tests + corpus; targets the [[D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS]] carrier; plan-23 full-C23 |
