Unsuppressable. WITNESS: `SemanticAnalyzerCSubset.AlignasExceedsMaxFailsLoud` + corpus `alignas_exceeds_max` (both assert S002D AND zero S002C). | тЬЕ (CLOSED) | folded into [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]] (2026-07-07); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` + `src/core/types/{parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/core/types/alignment.hpp` (the 256 cap) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-VARIABLE-CODEGEN` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T3 full-C23) тАФ thread the stored `SymbolRecord.explicitAlignment` into GLOBAL + LOCAL variable codegen.** GLOBALS: a NEW declaration-keyed HIR side-map `HirAlignmentMap`/`AlignmentAttr` (a PRECISE mirror of the mutability side-table) is populated in CSTтЖТHIR (`recordAlignment`, sparse тАФ only a real >0 override) from the bound symbol's `explicitAlignment` at every Global + local VarDecl lowering site; HIRтЖТMIR stamps it onto a NEW `MirGlobal.alignment` (via a new `addGlobal(..., alignmentBytes)` param + `Mir::globalAlignmentBytes`), and the assembler's `lowerMirGlobalsToDataItems` raises the emitted data item's alignment to `max(natural, explicit)` at ALL SIX `.alignment=` arms (a plain `alignas(32) int g;` is a SCALAR/zero-init тЖТ the primitive+scalar arms, NOT just the aggregate `lay->align` ones). LOCALS: the effective alignment `max(naturalAlign(type), alignas)` rides a NEW MIR `MirInst.payload2` channel set at `allocaForLocal` (the primary `payload` is the aggregate byte size); MIRтЖТLIR aggregates the per-function MAX into `MirToLirResult.funcLocalAlignments` (SymbolId-keyed тЖТ survives the LIR rebuilds, mirrors `SehFuncletParent`), threaded through the pipeline to `materializeCallingConvention` тЖТ `computeFrameLayout`. THE FRAME FIX (2 mechanisms, both gated on `maxLocalAlign>0` so every other frame is BYTE-IDENTICAL): (1) `localAreaAlignPad` тАФ pads the local-area base up to the local's alignment when the raw base (outgoing+saved+spill) doesn't already satisfy it (an ODD outgoing-arg count leaves the x86 base тЙб8 mod 16); folded into `localAreaOffset()` + `rawPreShadow`; (2) the LEAF-frame RSP bias тАФ a leaf function with an over-aligned local takes the SAME `alignedSizeWithBias(callPushBytes)` path a non-leaf uses, so its post-prologue RSP is stack-aligned (a plain `alignUp` leaves RSP тЙбcallPushBytes on an x86 return-address-push ABI тЖТ the local `callPushBytes` bytes off; on an LR ABI callPushBytes==0 so the forms coincide тАФ no arm64 change). The alignment ceiling is `cc.stackAlignment` (16 every ABI), NOT slotWidth (=8 on arm64, which would wrongly reject a 16-aligned local there). тШЕ AUDIT-CAUGHT during my own runtime verification: the effective-alignment channel + `MirGlobal.alignment` are DROPPED by every MIR rebuild that copies insts/globals тАФ the fix required preserving `payload2` in `MirFunctionRebuilder::emitInstruction` + `mir_merge` + BOTH inliner clone arms, AND `globalAlignmentBytes` in `cloneGlobalsOrCarveOut`/`cloneGlobalsVerbatim`/`dce`/`mir_merge` (a partial thread silently under-aligned in the release/inlined path тАФ the PE global witness was a COINCIDENTAL pass until the rebuild-preservation was complete; ELF `.bss` readelf proved 0xтАжb8 [8-aligned] тЖТ 0xтАжc0 [32-aligned] after the fix). WITNESSES (all UNFORGEABLE runtime + red-on-disable): `examples/c-subset/alignas_local_aligned` (a NON-LEAF/leaf `alignas(16)` local with an ODD 9-arg stack-overflow callee тЖТ `&buf&15==0` тЖТ 42, release arm all 4 targets; RED-on-disable тЖТ 1 confirmed by disabling the pad+bias) + `alignas_global_over16` (`alignas(32) int g;` тЖТ `&g&31==0` тЖТ 42; ELF `.bss` VA readelf-verified 32-aligned; strace `exit_group(42)` on x86_64+arm64; native 42 on PE) + `alignas_local_overaligned_error` (`alignas(32)` local тЖТ the gate). Verified byte-identical existing frames (the zero-blast-radius proof = full ctest green). тШЕ FOLLOW-UP CORRECTION 2026-07-07 (independent audit тАФ PER-ALLOCA arm64 gap): the base-only `localAreaAlignPad` above aligns the local-area BASE but each alloca then sat at `base + ╬г allocaSlotCount*slotSize` with NO per-alloca re-alignment. On arm64 `slotWidth = max(GPR,FPR) = 8` while `stackAlignment = 16`, so a NON-FIRST `alignas(16)` local (e.g. `char pad; alignas(16) T x;`) landed at `base + 8` тЖТ only 8-aligned (x86_64 is safe: slotWidth=16). FIX (the full per-alloca channel, gated so тЙдslotWidth allocas are a no-op тЗТ x86_64 + arm64-тЙд8 frames BYTE-IDENTICAL): the per-alloca effective alignment (already on `MirInst.payload2`) is harvested at MIRтЖТLIR into `FuncLocalAlignment.perAllocaAlignBytes` (scan order, one entry per alloca, alongside the per-function MAX) тЖТ threaded SymbolId-keyed through `LirFuncLocalAlignment` тЖТ `materializeCallingConvention` тЖТ `materializeOneFunc` тЖТ `computeFrameLayout`; each alloca's offset is `alignUp(off, itsOwnAlign)` at BOTH the `allocaSlotOffsets` precompute AND the materialize placement (identically, so the c69 `lea_frame_slot` consistency assert holds), and `computeFrameLayout.localAreaSize` walks the SAME per-alloca-aligned progression so the reserved area never under-sizes. A length guard (per-alloca list vs the function's LIR alloca count) fails loud on any MIR-harvest/LIR-scan-order divergence. The B3 `maxLocalAlign > stackAlignment` fail-loud gate stays. WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/alignas_two_locals_over16` (`char pad; alignas(16) T x,y;` both address-taken тЖТ `(&x|&y)&15==0` тЖТ 42, release-arm ALL 4 targets incl. arm64/qemu тАФ where #2 manifests) + `MirVerifier` now rejects a non-pow2 Alloca payload2 ([[I_AllocaAlignmentNotPowerOfTwo]]). тП│ DEFERRED (pinned): [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-OVERALIGNED-STACK-LOCAL]] (a >stack-alignment local fails loud тАФ no dynamic RSP realignment). | тЬЕ (CLOSED) | T3 full-C23 (2026-07-07); `src/hir/attributes/alignment_attr.hpp` (new) + `src/hir/hir_attrs.hpp` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/mir/{mir.{hpp,cpp},mir_node.hpp}` + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/mir/merge/{mir_merge,synth_seh_funclets}.cpp` + `src/opt/passes/{mir_rebuild_helper,dce,inlining}.cpp` + `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/lir/lir_callconv.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/asm/asm.cpp` + `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp` + 3 examples; mirrors the mutability side-table + `SehFuncletParent` SymbolId-keyed metadata |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-OVERALIGNED-STACK-LOCAL` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-VARIABLE-CODEGEN]]).** A stack LOCAL whose effective alignment EXCEEDS the CC's stack alignment (`cc.stackAlignment`, 16 on every shipped ABI) тАФ an `alignas(32)`/`alignas(64)` local, OR a member-alignas struct with align>16 used as a local тАФ needs a DYNAMICALLY realigned stack pointer (an AND-mask of RSP + a frame pointer to still find spills at fixed offsets), which the static frame layout does not build. `computeFrameLayout` FAILS LOUD with `L_OverAlignedStackLocal` (0xB00C, new), the message reporting the COMPUTED bound (agnostic тАФ never an arch name). Never a silent under-align (the very miscompile the parent cycle fixes). A тЙд stack-alignment local (e.g. `alignas(16)`) is HONORED via the local-area pad + the leaf RSP bias тАФ only past that bound does this gate fire. TRIGGER: a real consumer needs an over-stack-aligned local (a cache-line-aligned / AVX-512 stack buffer). Resolution: dynamic SP realignment (`and rsp, -N`) + a frame-pointer-relative addressing mode for spills/locals in the affected function. WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/alignas_local_overaligned_error` (`alignas(32) int loc;` тЖТ the gate; the callconv-tier diagnostic has no source span so it resolves to 1:1). | (gated тАФ no shipped consumer needs an over-stack-aligned local; sqlite uses zero) | opened by the alignas-variable-codegen cycle; `src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp` (`computeFrameLayout`) + `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-REGISTER-CONTEXT` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]]).** C11/C23 ┬з6.7.5p2 makes an `alignas` on an object declared with the `register` storage-class specifier a constraint violation (`register alignas(16) int x;` тЖТ should be `S_AlignasInvalidContext`). The frontend's context check (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` ~3644-3673) rejects a FUNCTION/TYPEDEF/bit-field alignas but does NOT reject a `register` one, so the alignment is currently HONORED. This is HARMLESS тАФ `register` is an IGNORED storage specifier in c-subset (it carries no linkage; `c-subset.lang.json` lists it in the ignored-kinds), so `register alignas(16) int x;` VALIDLY applies the 16-byte alignment; it is a conformance NIT (an accepted constraint violation), NEVER a miscompile or a silent under-align. Rejecting it needs a NET-NEW mechanism: `register` is not tracked as a first-class storage flag on `SymbolRecord`/`DeclarationKind` (only `static`'s linkage/storage-duration axis is folded from the specifier prefix). Resolution: add a config-driven `register`-storage detection (a `RegisterKeyword` token scan of the specifier prefix, mirroring `firstAlignasSpecInPrefix`, likely surfaced as a `SymbolRecord` storage-class bit) + gate the alignas context check on it. TRIGGER: a real consumer / the C23 conformance corpus requires the `register alignas` rejection (rather than the standards-lenient acceptance). | (gated тАФ `register` is ignored, so the alignment is validly applied; no shipped consumer needs the reject, and it is a conformance nit not a miscompile) | opened by the alignas frontend cycle; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the context check) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_model.hpp` (a storage-class bit) + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (RegisterKeyword) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS-TYPEDEF-PARAM-PARSE` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by [[D-CSUBSET-ALIGNAS]]).** C11/C23 ┬з6.7.5p2 makes an `alignas` on a TYPEDEF or a function PARAMETER a constraint violation. The frontend emits `S_AlignasInvalidContext` for the contexts the shared declaration-specifier prefix reaches (function/prototype declaration, bit-field member) тАФ but `typedef alignas(16) int T;` and `void f(alignas(16) int x)` are dedicated `typedefDecl`/`param` grammar rules that do NOT admit the alignas prefix, so they are rejected as FAIL-LOUD PARSE errors (P0009), not the specific semantic code. Still a LOUD rejection (never silently accepted тАФ both are constraint violations no real code writes). The semantic typedef-context check IS wired (a Type-kind declaration carrying the prefix тЖТ S_AlignasInvalidContext) and would fire the moment the grammar admits the prefix. Cleanly admitting it needs either strip support for a MID-sequence prefix (typedef: the alignas follows `typedef`, not at child-0) or a new per-rule context flag + a position-0 optional on `param` (a variable-vs-parameter discriminator the shared declarator-mode path lacks тАФ keying on the rule NAME would break source-agnosticism). TRIGGER: a real consumer / the C23 corpus writes a typedef-or-parameter alignas and needs the exact `S_AlignasInvalidContext` (rather than a parse error). Resolution: add the alignas admission + a config-driven context discriminator; the semantic reject is already in place. | (gated тАФ no shipped consumer writes a typedef/parameter alignas; the parse-error rejection is loud) | opened by the alignas frontend cycle; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`typedefDecl`/`param` rules) + the existing semantic context check in `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-PACKED` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-08 (T3 full-C23 / FC16) тАФ C/C23 struct/union `packed` attribute SEMANTICS (`__attribute__((packed))` / `[[gnu::packed]]` / `[[packed]]` / `__packed__`), a PRECISE MIRROR of the just-landed [[D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS]] channel.** `packed` removes ALL inter-field padding and sets the aggregate's natural alignment to 1; a member `alignas` still RAISES per-field via the unchanged MAX-fold (alignas wins per-field тАФ `alignas(1)` INSIDE a packed struct is legal via a `naturalBaseline=1` thread into `resolveAlignasOverride`, while `alignas(1) double` OUTSIDE still fails 6.7.5p4). CODEGEN needed ZERO changes (arm64 uses only unaligned-tolerant LDUR/STUR, x86 plain mov тАФ an independent audit confirmed). A per-COMPOSITE `CompositeFields.packed` bool + `isPacked` accessor (type_interner.hpp/type_lattice.cpp); `completeComposite` gains a DELIBERATELY NON-DEFAULTED `bool packed` param (F2 hardening: a forgotten packed FAILS TO COMPILE тАФ the reintern channel is the ABI-critical one, type_reintern.cpp carries `src.isPacked` else a cross-CU round-trip silently unpacks); enters `contentDeclSiteKey` GUARDED-ON-TRUE (an unpacked composite hashes byte-identically тЖТ ZERO TypeId churn) so a packed `S` is a DISTINCT TypeId from a padded `S`; `computeLayout`'s struct AND union arms feed `Alignment::of<1>()` as the per-field baseline (F5 belt: `packed && anyBitfield тЖТ nullopt` at BOTH arms, the backstop behind the semantic front door). Grammar: a plain trailing `{ "optional": "compositeAttrList" }` on structSpec/unionSpec (the feared ambiguity тАФ `stdAttr`'s `[[` colliding at 1 token with a following array `[` тАФ never materialized, so the plain optional shipped and works: green + `PackedStructFollowedByDeclaratorParses` passes) тАФ TRAILING so it never shifts the `name:1` tag index (the after-keyword position is deferred, see below). Config-driven: `semantics.packed {listRule, attributeNames, strictAttrRule}` (nothing hardcodes "packed"). Semantic scan (`scanCompositePacked`) extracts each attr identifier (dunder-normalized via the shared `stripDunder`, promoted to `core/types/attribute_naming.hpp` so the preprocessor + this scan share ONE normalizer), threads packed to BOTH completion sites. New `S_UnknownTypeAttribute=0xE031` (a GNU `__attribute__((pakced))` typo fails loud тАФ H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier precedent; an unknown C23 `[[тАж]]` is standard-ignorable тАФ [[deprecated]] precedent) + `S_PackedBitfieldUnsupported=0xE032` (UNSUPPRESSABLE тАФ a packed bit-field ships wrong padded bytes if silenced). hir_text round-trips a `packed` marker (emit + parse via forwardComposite+completeComposite). WITNESSES: `tests/core/test_type_layout.cpp` (7 hermetic pins тАФ plain packed, packed+alignas-member-still-raises, packed union, array-element stride, nested packed, packed+bitfield nullopt belt) + NEW `tests/core/test_packed_abi_conformance.cpp` (native gcc/clang/cl cross-check тАФ 6-struct battery, sizeof+offsetof byte-identical) + `TypeReintern.PackedFlagSurvivesReintern` (F2) + `ParseTypeFromText.PackedParseAndForkIdentity` + `HirText.PackedFlagRoundTrip` + 11 semantic tests (GNU+C23 spellings end-to-end via `_Static_assert(sizeof/_Alignof)`, alignas-wins, alignas(1)-legal-in-packed, packed union, following-declarator, packed+bitfield fail-loud, typo fail-loud, unknown-[[тАж]] ignored) + `examples/c-subset/packed_struct` (a MISALIGNED multi-byte field read+written at runtime тЖТ 42, release arm all 4 targets). | тЬЕ (CLOSED) | T3/FC16 (2026-07-08); `src/core/types/type_lattice/{type_interner.hpp,type_lattice.cpp,type_layout.cpp,type_reintern.cpp}` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp,attribute_naming.hpp[new]}` + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` + `src/analysis/preprocess/pp_if_eval.cpp` + `src/hir/hir_text.cpp` + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + tests + example; a precise mirror of [[D-CSUBSET-MEMBER-ALIGNAS]]; plan-23 FC16 |
| `D-CSUBSET-PACKED-BITFIELD-INTERACTION` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-PACKED]]).** A `packed` struct/union that ALSO contains a bit-field member тАФ bit-granular packed packing (each bit-field packed to bit-precision, no unit-alignment padding) is a DISTINCT algorithm from the byte-level packed layout. Combining the two is UNSUPPORTED and fails loud TWO ways: the SEMANTIC front door `S_PackedBitfieldUnsupported` (0xE032, UNSUPPRESSABLE тАФ emitted at completion, and the composite is completed UNPACKED so only the one error fires, no cascade) + the layout `computeLayout` nullopt BELT at both the struct and union bit-field arms (`packed && anyBitfield тЖТ nullopt`, the reliable backstop for a packed+bitfield type constructed DIRECTLY via the interner / reinterned, bypassing the semantic scan). NEVER a silent NON-packed (padded) layout. TRIGGER: a real consumer needs a packed bit-field struct with GNU bit-granular semantics. Resolution: a bit-granular packed bit-field packer arm in `type_layout.cpp` (a new strategy or a packed-aware GnuPacked variant). WITNESS: `TypeLayout.PackedPlusBitfieldFailsLoud` (belt nullopt) + `SemanticAnalyzerCSubset.PackedBitfieldFailsLoud` (front door). | (gated тАФ no shipped consumer needs a packed bit-field; sqlite uses zero) | opened by the packed cycle; `src/core/types/type_lattice/type_layout.cpp` (the F5 belt) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the front door) + `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.hpp` (S_PackedBitfieldUnsupported) |
| `D-CSUBSET-PACKED-AFTER-KEYWORD-POSITION` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-PACKED]] тАФ the SUFFIX-ONLY fallback).** GCC honors `packed` in TWO positions: the SUFFIX `struct S {тАж} __attribute__((packed))` (LANDED) and the AFTER-KEYWORD `struct __attribute__((packed)) S {тАж}` (this gap). The suffix form keeps the tag at the positional `name:1` index; the after-keyword form shifts the Identifier to index 2, so honoring it requires locating the composite tag BY KIND (a direct-child Identifier scan) instead of `name:1` тАФ a NET-NEW `NameMatchMode` threaded through MULTIPLE positional readers (the semantic analyzer's dual definition-name sites AND the parser's binder-sketch `resolveNameNode_` mirror + the schema decode + validation), a genuine multi-cycle refactor with silent-miscompile risk (a mis-scoped scan would treat the tagged struct as anonymous, silently dropping the tag). The after-keyword form currently parses as an ANONYMOUS struct specifier (the grammar admits `compositeAttrList` only as a TRAILING element, so the attribute is consumed as its trailing list) and the tag `S {тАж}` is then mis-read as a malformed function definition тЖТ it stays a LOUD SEMANTIC rejection (S_InvalidFunctionDeclarator; the anonymous variant тЖТ S_UnknownType), fail-loud, never a silent drop. TRIGGER: a real consumer / the C23 corpus writes the after-keyword form and needs it honored. Resolution: a `firstDirectIdentifier` NameMatchMode (or equivalent) applied uniformly to the composite tag lookup + an after-keyword `compositeAttrList` slot. | (gated тАФ the suffix form is the dominant real-world idiom; sqlite uses zero packed attributes; the after-keyword form is a loud rejection) | opened by the packed cycle; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (structSpec/unionSpec after-keyword slot) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` + `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` (the tag-lookup readers) + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp}` (the new NameMatchMode) |
| `D-CSUBSET-PER-MEMBER-PACKED` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-PACKED]]).** GCC also honors `packed` on an INDIVIDUAL member (`struct S { int a; int z __attribute__((packed)); };` тАФ pack only `z`, not the whole struct), a DIFFERENT grammar slot (a member-declarator suffix / the member specifier prefix) + a DIFFERENT layout channel (a per-FIELD packed bool, like `fieldAligns`, feeding the field's baseline to 1 without packing its siblings). The whole-composite packed cycle deliberately scoped this out (the trailing composite-attribute list is a whole-composite property). Currently a per-member `__attribute__((packed))` on a struct field does NOT parse (the member grammar admits only the alignas prefix) тЖТ a loud parse error, never a silent drop. TRIGGER: a real consumer writes a per-member packed. Resolution: a per-field packed channel (`CompositeFields.fieldPacked`, mirroring `fieldAligns`) + a member-attribute grammar slot + the semantic wiring. | (gated тАФ no shipped consumer needs per-member packed; the whole-composite form covers the dominant idiom) | opened by the packed cycle; `src/core/types/type_lattice/{type_interner.hpp,type_lattice.cpp,type_layout.cpp}` + `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (member-attribute slot) + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-PACKED-ATOMIC-MEMBER` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-PACKED]]).** An `_Atomic`-qualified member inside a `packed` struct is a HAZARD: packing may leave the atomic member misaligned, and arm64's atomic instructions (LDAXR/STLXR/LDADD) TRAP (SIGBUS) on an unaligned address тАФ a runtime crash, not a silent miscompile, but one that should be REJECTED at compile time (`_Atomic` in a packed aggregate is ill-advised; GCC warns/errors). Currently MOOT: c-subset does not yet model `_Atomic` as a type qualifier (only `volatile` is materialized, as VolatileQual), so an `_Atomic` member is not expressible in a packed (or any) struct тАФ the hazard cannot arise. TRIGGER: `_Atomic` support lands (a real atomic type qualifier). Resolution: at composite completion, reject a packed struct carrying an `_Atomic` member whose packed offset is not naturally aligned (fail-loud, a new diagnostic). | (gated тАФ MOOT until `_Atomic` is modeled; today no atomic member is expressible) | opened by the packed cycle; awaits `_Atomic` support; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the composite completion check) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NORETURN` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-08 (T3 full-C23 / FC16) тАФ C11/C23 `noreturn` FUNCTION-attribute SEMANTICS (`_Noreturn` [C11 6.7.4] / `[[noreturn]]` [C23 6.7.12.7] / `__attribute__((noreturn))` / `__attribute__((__noreturn__))` / `[[gnu::noreturn]]`), a DIRECT structural mirror of the proven `wrapIfProvablyInfinite`.** MECHANISM (Approach A): a function symbol declared noreturn carries `SymbolRecord.isNoreturn`; at HIR lowering a DIRECT call to such a function is wrapped `Block{ ExprStmt(call), Unreachable }` (Block + terminator leaf both `Synthetic`) EXACTLY like a provably-infinite loop тАФ so a noreturn-terminated path structurally terminates. ZERO changes to hir_verifier / hir_to_mir / prune_unreachable (all already generic: `pathTerminates` recurses to a Block's last child; the following statement lowers into a dead pruned block via the existing open-block-has-terminator guard). Config-driven vocabulary `semantics.noreturn {keywordToken:"NoreturnKeyword", attributeNames:["noreturn"]}` + a bare `_Noreturn` keyword joined into `singleDeclSpecifier`/`localDeclSpecifier` (FIRST disjoint тЖТ no speculation). DETECTION `specifierPrefixNamesNoreturn` (a bounded DFS over the STRIPPED specifier prefix) matches BOTH forms тАФ the `_Noreturn` KEYWORD TOKEN **and** an attribute IDENTIFIER (dunder-normalized via the shared `stripDunder`, so `__noreturn__`/`[[gnu::noreturn]]` match); a scan of identifiers ONLY (the `scanCompositePacked` precedent) would MISS the bare keyword. Stored gated on `isFnSig` (per-declarator, the `explicitAlignment` pattern), OR-MERGED across a proto/def pair in the post-1.5 `mergedFnDecls` sweep (a call resolves to the SURVIVOR=definition; OR-ing both covers either merge order). SHIPPED externs (no user prototype) carry it on the descriptor: `stdlib.json` marks `abort`/`exit` `noreturn:true` тЖТ `ShippedSymbol.noreturn` тЖТ the injected record. тЪая╕П F1 (THE miscompile guard): `isDirectNoreturnCall` inspects the lowered Call's CALLEE CHILD directly (`children().front()` must be `HirKind::Ref` whose record is noreturn), NEVER `firstNameToken` тАФ a noreturn function is ADDRESS-TAKEABLE, so `(cond ? die : other)(1)` is legal C whose ternary callee lowers to a NON-Ref node тЖТ NOT wrapped (else `other`'s return path is elided = MISCOMPILE); a fn-POINTER object `fp(1)` тЖТ Ref(fp), isNoreturn==false тЖТ NOT wrapped. LINKAGE seam (audit fix, DEVIATION from the design's `{binding:global}`): the GNU `__attribute__((noreturn))` shares the `attrSpec` rule with HONORED linkage attrs (weak/visibility) so its subtree can't be ignored wholesale on `topLevelDecl`; a `{binding:global}` no-op linkageSpecifiers entry was REJECTED because it CLOBBERS a co-present `static`/`weak` last-wins (`static __attribute__((noreturn))` тАФ legal, common тАФ silently loses internal linkage = an order-dependent miscompile). Instead a NEW identifier-granularity `DeclarationRule.linkageSpecifierIgnoredNames` (completing the ignoredKinds/ignoredRules trio) skips `noreturn` by NAME (dunder-normalized, covers `__noreturn__`) WITHOUT a linkage effect; any OTHER unknown identifier still fails loud H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier. The bare `_Noreturn` keyword rides `linkageSpecifierIgnoredKinds` (both varDecl + topLevelDecl); `[[noreturn]]` rides `stdAttr` тИИ ignoredRules (unchanged). ZERO NEW DIAGNOSTIC CODES (reuse H_VerifierFailure / H_UnreachableCode / H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier). WITNESSES: `tests/hir/test_hir_lowering_c_subset.cpp` тАФ `NoreturnCallTailWrapsAndVerifies` (red-on-disable: revert тЖТ H_VerifierFailure=1), `NoreturnAllFourSpellingsCompileClean` (all 4 spellings clean + zero H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier), `NoreturnIndirectCalleeIsNotWrapped` (F1: BOTH the ternary AND fn-ptr callee still fire H_VerifierFailure=1 тАФ the un-elided-return-path witness); `tests/analysis/semantic/test_semantic_analyzer_c_subset.cpp` `NoreturnProtoMergesIntoDefinition` (proto-only `_Noreturn`, def doesn't тЖТ survivor.isNoreturn==true, red-on-disable for the OR-merge); `examples/c-subset/noreturn_function` (proto/def OR-merge + `die` tail-calls the shipped noreturn `exit`; a non-void `compute` whose only non-return path is `die(42)` тЖТ main returns compute(0) тЖТ exit 42, **release arm** [`shippedPipeline:release`] all 4 targets тАФ the FIRST optimizer-visible reachable Unreachable NOT behind an infinite loop). тП│ DEFERRED (all fail-loud/SAFE, NOT miscompiles): [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN-VALUE-POSITION]] (noreturn call in value / for-init-update / cast/comma-wrapped position тАФ only the `wrapBare` statement chokepoint wraps, so such a rare form keeps a spurious H_VerifierFailure, un-relaxed) + [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN-NON-FUNCTION-OBJECT]] (`_Noreturn` on a non-function object тАФ 6.7.4 constraint; today INERT via the `isFnSig` store-gate, a fail-loud reject deferred). NAMED inline (no src citation, safe): noreturn on a function-POINTER TYPE (`_Noreturn`-qualified fn-ptr тАФ not detected, safe miss); `_Noreturn` on `main` (inert тАФ main already terminates); a noreturn function that RETURNS (UB, undiagnosed тАФ C 6.7.4p2); the `extern _Noreturn` storage-mixed form (extern is a distinct keyword тАФ the prefix scan still finds `_Noreturn`, so this actually works, but a dedicated witness is deferred); `<stdnoreturn.h>`'s bare `noreturn`/`_Noreturn` macro surface (not shipped as a header). | тЬЕ (CLOSED) | T3/FC16 (2026-07-08); `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_analyzer.cpp,semantic_model.hpp}` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `src/ffi/shipped_lib_descriptor.{hpp,cpp}` + `src/dss-config/{sources/c-subset.lang.json,shippedLibs/stdlib.json}` + tests + `examples/c-subset/noreturn_function`; a direct mirror of `wrapIfProvablyInfinite` (D-HIR-INFINITE-LOOP-NOT-TERMINATING); plan-23 FC16 |
| `D-CSUBSET-NORETURN-VALUE-POSITION` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN]]).** The noreturn-call HIR wrap fires ONLY at the `lowerStmtExprCore(wrapBare=true)` STATEMENT chokepoint (block-item / bare if/while/for/label arm). A noreturn call appearing in a NON-statement position тАФ a for-INIT/UPDATE clause (`wrapBare=false`, deliberately untouched), or nested inside a value expression / cast / comma operator (`(void)die(1)`, `x, die(1)`) тАФ is NOT wrapped, so a non-void function whose ONLY terminating path routes through such a form keeps a SPURIOUS H_VerifierFailure. FAIL-LOUD (a loud over-rejection), NEVER a silent miscompile тАФ and vanishingly rare (a noreturn call is idiomatically a bare statement). TRIGGER: a real consumer terminates a function via a noreturn call in value/for-clause position. Resolution: extend the wrap to the value-expression lowering (an ExprStmt-free Unreachable emission) + the for-clause caller, with the SAME F1 direct-Ref-callee guard. | (gated тАФ no shipped consumer needs a value-position noreturn call; the bare-statement form covers the idiom) | opened by the noreturn cycle; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`lowerStmtExprCore` / `lowerForClause`) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NORETURN-NON-FUNCTION-OBJECT` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-08 by [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN]]).** C11 6.7.4p2 constrains `_Noreturn` to FUNCTION declarations; `_Noreturn int x;` (a non-function object) is a constraint violation. The noreturn detection STORE is gated on `isFnSig`, so today such a spelling is silently INERT (the flag is simply not stored тАФ the object is unaffected, no miscompile). A fail-loud reject (a dedicated diagnostic at the `_Noreturn`-on-non-function site) is deferred: it needs the detection scan to run UNCONDITIONALLY (not only when a FnSig is present) + a context check, mirroring the alignas `S_AlignasInvalidContext` context gate. NEVER a miscompile (an inert attribute). TRIGGER: a real consumer / the C23 conformance corpus requires the rejection. Resolution: emit the reject when `declHasNoreturn && !isFnSig` (a new unsuppressable code, the alignas-context precedent). | (gated тАФ `_Noreturn` on a non-function is inert, not a miscompile; no shipped consumer writes it) | opened by the noreturn cycle; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the per-declarator store gate) |

ROOT CAUSE: `lowerTypeDecl` (`cst_to_hir.cpp`) resolved the TypeDecl's type off the descriptor's `nameChild`, but for an ANON composite `vis[nameChild]` is the BODY node (which carries no symbol тАФ the tag symbol + type are bound on the SPECIFIER node itself by the Pass-1.5 stamp `nodeToType[specNode]=compositeTy`). The name-child probe left the type Invalid тЖТ the verifier fired. FIX: after the name-child probe, fall back to `model.typeAt(node)` (the type the analyzer already stamped on this specifier node) when the type is still invalid тАФ a HIR-lowering stamp, NOT a semantic gap (the side-table was already correct) and NOT dropping the TypeDecl; keeps named+anon symmetric and also covers anonymous struct/union top-level defs. WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/anon_enum_file_scope_arraydim` (`enum{V=16}; int arr[V]; return sizeof(arr)` тЖТ 64 on all 4 targets; red-on-disable: revert the fallback тЖТ H0001). 3-leg gate + sqlite re-probe green. | тЬЕ (CLOSED) | user-queued cycle (2026-07-07); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`lowerTypeDecl`) |
| `D-OPT-DOMTREE-SCRATCH-REUSE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (post-sqlite perf arc) тАФ the reusable dominator-computation scratch: THE biggest single win of the arc (compile 4m24тЖТ2m00; the whole arc >30minтЖТ2m00).** Sub-profiling (env `DSS_OPT_TRACE` Cse/Licm sub-timers) proved `computeMirDomTree` was ~95% of BOTH passes' analyze (~94s/compile): per CALL (per function!) it re-copied the ENTIRE whole-module predecessor map into `predSlots` (~90k inner-vector allocs + all module edges) + allocated 5 more whole-module-sized arrays + ran an O(module) conversion тАФ for ONE function's CHK walk (the fixpoint itself is function-local-linear). Threaded: runCse + runLicm + rederiveStructCfMarkers (one scratch per pass call; `auto const&` bindings). MEASURED: Cse dom 14.9sтЖТ**26ms**/iter (pass 18.3тЖТ2.98s), Licm dom 14.3sтЖТ**8ms** (pass 18.9тЖТ4.3s), rederive 12тЖТ2.1s/call, optimize 3m41тЖТ1m35. ROOT: minimal (un-pruned) SSA placed a DEAD phi for the block-local at the loop HEADER (the body-Store's iterated dominance frontier reaches the header via the back-edge); that phi's entry-predecessor incoming was genuinely undefined (the slot was never stored before the loop) тЖТ the rename walk's empty-stack guard aborted. PROVABLY SAFE: a global alloca's placement is BYTE-IDENTICAL to before (induction vars / diamond joins unchanged), so the fix ONLY removes the spurious dead phis. CRUCIALLY PRESERVES fail-loud: a genuine uninitialized read (`int x; if(c) x=1; use(x)`) IS upward-exposed at the use тЖТ stays global тЖТ still gets its phi тЖТ still aborts on the undefined path (verified: that case still fatals). PINS: `tests/opt/test_mem2reg.cpp::LoopBodyLocalAllocaNeedsNoPhi` (induction var=1 phi, body-local=0 phi; red-on-disable = the pass aborts) + corpus `examples/c-subset/loop_body_local` (release arm exit 20, PE-runnable) + the `varargs_win64_sum` release arm (exit 39). | `D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-ARGINDEX` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-25 (p18 Cluster G c4) тАФ ROOT CAUSE = DCE; GENERAL (not variadic), the LAST cluster member.** Compiling `examples/c-subset/varargs_overflow_fixed_stack` under RELEASE aborted FAIL-LOUD `I_ArgIndexOutOfRange: argIndex 6 >= physical-arg count 2`. тШЕ The c4 probe BISECTED it to **Dce** (without `Dce` it compiles) and re-scoped it GENERAL: `pick(a,b,c,d,e,f,g,...)` uses only `a` + `g`, so DCE elided the 5 unused param `Arg`s тЖТ only 2 `Arg`s survived, but the still-live `Arg(6)` (g, the 7th int тЖТ SysV stack) kept its physical ordinal 6 тЖТ the MirVerifier's FC7 count-based bound (`argIndex < count(Arg)`, valid only while every Arg survives) spuriously rejected it. The codegen was always correct (ordinals preserved); only the verifier over-rejected. | тЬЕ (CLOSED) FIX = treat `Arg` as an ABI-parameter ROOT in DCE (`isSideEffectRoot` in `src/opt/passes/dce.cpp` returns true for `MirOpcode::Arg`): a parameter is part of the fixed ABI signature, never dead code (LLVM models params as non-instruction `Argument` roots) тАФ so DCE never elides an unused param's `Arg`, holding `count(Arg)` == the physical-arg count the verifier bounds against. | `D-CSUBSET-DESCRIPTOR-TYPEDEF-POINTER-DECL` | тШЕ **FIRED тАФ discovered 2026-06-25 (p18 SQLite-readiness Cluster G c1, while strengthening the `shipped_int_headers` corpus with a NULL witness).** A DESCRIPTOR-injected typedef name (from a shipped-header JSON descriptor тАФ `int32_t`/`uint8_t`/`size_t`/тАж) used in POINTER-DECLARATOR position (`int32_t* p` / `size_t *p`) is NOT recognized as a type-name by the PARSER, so `int32_t * p` parses as a MULTIPLICATION expression (`int32_t` ├Ч `p`) тЖТ fails loud `S_UndeclaredIdentifier` (p) + `S_TypeMismatch` (initializer). ROOT: descriptor typedefs are injected SEMANTICALLY (post-parse, into cuRoot by `semantic_analyzer`), so the parser's `T * x` declaration-vs-multiply disambiguation never learns them. CONTRAST (all verified this cycle): a USER `typedef int myint; myint* p` WORKS (the parser sees the typedef decl тЖТ type-name visible); a BUILTIN `int* p` WORKS (keyword); and descriptor typedefs WORK in the UNAMBIGUOUS positions тАФ scalar decl (`int32_t r`) + cast (`(int32_t)n`) тАФ which need no parser type-name table. Only the pointer-declarator ambiguity exposes it. **FAILS LOUD (never a silent miscompile).** Priority: **normal тАФ SQLite blast-radius UNMEASURED** (SQLite mostly uses its OWN amalgamation typedefs [`u8`/`u32`/`sqlite3_int64`, parser-visible тЖТ fine], but a direct `size_t *`/`uint8_t *` on a stdint/stddef typedef would mis-parse; re-probe sqlite3.c for descriptor-typedef pointer usage to set the real priority). | Make descriptor typedef-names parser-visible BEFORE the `T * x` disambiguation. Two designs (likely a ┬зB fork): **(a)** pre-scan the source's `#include <...>` set тЖТ resolve descriptors тЖТ SEED the parser's type-name table before parsing (today includes resolve POST-parse in `import_resolver`, so this needs include-set-at-parse-time тАФ the classic C lexer-hack ordering); **(b)** make the declaration-vs-multiply disambiguation SEMANTIC тАФ speculatively parse both, resolve by whether `T` names a type in the semantic scope (incl. descriptor injections). Pin: `#include <stdint.h>\nint32_t g=42; int main(){ int32_t* p=&g; return (int)*p; }` compiles + exits 42, RED-on-disable. | discovered by p18 Cluster G c1 (`examples/c-subset/shipped_int_headers/main.c` references it in a comment тАФ examples/ is NOT anchor-guard-scanned, so informational); pairs with the FC14 descriptor typedef-injection path (`semantic_analyzer.cpp` inject) + the c-subset declaration-vs-expression speculative parse + `D-PARSE-IDENTIFIER-KIND-HARDCODED-VS-CONFIG`; SQLite-readiness Cluster G (p18); do NOT cite in `src/` yet (discovered-gap pin) |

(2) The recursion refusal is now SCC-based: a NEW agnostic substrate `src/opt/analysis/call_graph_scc.{hpp,cpp}` (`computeCallGraphSccs(Mir const&)` тЖТ `funcId.v тЖТ sccId`, iterative Tarjan over the self-contained direct call graph) lets the gate refuse ALL recursive-cycle inlining via `funcToScc.at(caller.v) == funcToScc.at(callee.v)` тАФ which SUBSUMES self-recursion (a self-call is the singleton-SCC case, caller==callee тЖТ same id) AND generalizes to MUTUAL recursion (`fтЖТgтЖТf` share a multi-member SCC). The anchor stays OPEN for the STILL-DEFERRED relaxations: **(a) depth-bounded RECURSIVE inlining** тАФ allow BOUNDED recursion (a depth/unroll policy), ADDITIVE on top of the SCC refusal; each bounded form needs its OWN miscompile pin (a wrongly-unrolled recursion = miscompile). **(b) the SOPHISTICATED cost model тАФ PARTIALLY CLOSED cycle 28:** the conservative SIZE/cost bloat bound LANDED тАФ `OptPipeline.inlineThreshold` (default 50, max `kMaxInlineThreshold`, config-driven via the pipeline JSON's optional `inlineThreshold`, loader-validated `[1, kMaxInlineThreshold]` тЖТ `X_PipelineMalformed`, threaded `runInlining(...,inlineThreshold)` тЖТ `inlineLegalityGate`) so the gate now REFUSES a callee whose instruction-count (summed across all blocks during the body scan) EXCEEDS the threshold (`if (instCount > inlineThreshold) return std::nullopt;` тАФ `>` not `>=`, so an at-threshold callee inlines), and **`Inlining` now SHIPS in `release.pipeline.json`** (at index 1, after Identity, before ConstFold, with `inlineThreshold: 50`) bounded by that size cap. Pinned GREEN by `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::{InlineCostModelRefusesLargeCallee, InlineCostModelBoundaryIsExclusiveUpper, InlineCostModelZeroThresholdRefusesAll}` (a real >threshold Add-chain callee refused while a small one inlines; the EXACT-threshold/over-by-one boundary; the threshold-0 fail-safe refusing all тАФ all RED-on-disable when the cost gate is removed, and the boundary arm RED if `>` is flipped to `>=`) + `tests/opt/test_pipeline_loader.cpp::{InlineThresholdZeroRejects, InlineThresholdOverflowRejects, InlineThresholdNonIntegerRejects, InlineThresholdDefaultsWhenAbsent, InlineThresholdInRange, ShippedReleaseLoadsAllPasses (9-pass exact list + index pin), ShippedReleaseContainsInlining}`. (The pre-cycle-28 bound "(ii) `Inlining` is NOT in release.pipeline.json" is NO LONGER TRUE тАФ inlining ships, and the SIZE threshold is now the load-bearing bloat bound in its place.) **(c)** tightening the conservative address-escape refusal once an escape-analysis substrate can prove a taken address never reaches an indirect call. **(d) IntrinsicCall inlining тАФ тЬЕ CLOSED cycle 29 (2026-06-05):** the gate's `IntrinsicCall` refusal was REMOVED тАФ a callee whose body contains an `IntrinsicCall` (single-block OR multi-block) is now ADMITTED. Because the `tryRewrite` hook (1 Call тЖТ 1 value, mid-block-fill) structurally cannot create blocks, a function with any multi-block target is rebuilt by a new `MultiBlockInliner` (`src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp`): per original caller block kept as a branch target (`blockMap_`); the call-site block SPLIT at the Call (a fresh CONTINUATION block holds the after-Call insts); the callee CFG CLONED in RPO (fresh block+inst ids, shared calleeтЖТcaller `local` map); each cloned `Return` rewritten to `Br(continuation)` collecting (value, cloned-pred) edges; a RETURN-MERGE PHI in the continuation joins them (elided to the single value for a 1-return callee; void callee тЖТ no Phi). StructCfMarker RE-DERIVED to `Linear` for every cloned + continuation block (SimplifyCfg cycle-9 precedent) тАФ a clone of the callee's `EntryBlock`/`ExitBlock` would violate the verifier's entry/exit rules, and transplanting If/Loop marker COUNTS would break caller marker-parity; `Linear` is parity-neutral. Caller `Phi` incomings redirect `pred` through `blockExitMap_` (the last continuation of a split chain). **Proven**: runtime corpus `examples/c-subset/multiblock_inline/` (`pick(x){if(x>0)return 7;return 9;}`; `main` = `pick(1)+pick(0)` = 16) with an `optimizedPipelines:["Inlining"]` arm == baseline 16; **REGRESSION-PROVEN RED** тАФ mis-wiring the merge to always pick the first return edge makes the optimized arm fail to compile (the then-clone value doesn't dominate the elseтЖТcontinuation path тЖТ MirVerifier rejects via verify-after-every-pass тЖТ differential ASSERT fires). MIR-tier pins in `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp`: `MultiBlockLeafCalleeIsInlined` (Call gone + caller block count +4 [3 clones + 1 cont] + exactly 1 merge Phi + merge-Phi incomings CORRECTLY valueтЖФpred paired [`instBlock(value)==pred` for the 7-from-then + 9-from-else edges; a swapped incoming тЖТ RED, catching a mis-merge the MIR tier alone would miss] + verifier-clean, RED-on-disable) + (at cycle-2 time) `MultiBlockNonLeafCalleeIsNotInlined` (scope boundary тАФ **SUPERSEDED in cycle 27**: that scope boundary was the multi-block leaf restriction, which `D-OPT7-INLINE-LEGALITY-GATE` cycle 27 LIFTED; the test was flipped to `MultiBlockNonLeafCalleeIsInlined`, and a multi-block non-leaf callee now inlines). **Robustness gate (review fold 2026-06-05):** `inlineLegalityGate` ALSO refuses a leaf callee with NO RETURNING PATH (no `Return` in any block; every path ends in `Unreachable` тАФ e.g. `int f(){ while(1){} }`). Without the refusal the splice routes each callee `Return` to a continuation block; a never-returning callee leaves that continuation predecessor-less тЖТ the MirVerifier (run after every pass) rejects the module тЖТ an otherwise-valid program becomes a build error UNDER inlining. Refusing keeps the Call out-of-line so the program compiles (conservative, never a miscompile). Pinned by `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp::NonReturningLeafCalleeIsNotInlined` (multi-block entryтЖТ`Unreachable` leaf тЖТ callsInlined==0, Call survives, module still verifies; RED-on-disable: removing `if (!hasReturn) return nullopt;` admits it тЖТ the splice's `mapCallerValue` fail-loud aborts the build). | (closed) | plan 22 ┬з2.9 OPT7; cited in `src/opt/passes/inlining.{hpp,cpp}` + `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/multiblock_inline/`; pairs with `D-OPT7-MULTIBLOCK-SPLICE-PHI` (callee-Phi deferral) + `D-OPT7-INLINE-LEGALITY-GATE` |
| `D-OPT7-MULTIBLOCK-SPLICE-PHI` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-13** (OPT7 callee-Phi cycle тАФ ┬зB design brief signed off, then built to the bar). `inlineLegalityGate`'s callee-`Phi` refusal is DROPPED (TOTAL тАФ no shape gate replaces it); `MultiBlockInliner` now CLONES callee Phis via a DEFERRED-incoming-flush, a sibling of the return-merge `returnEdges` with a DISJOINT map (they compose without interference): a placeholder `dst_.addPhi(instType)` is emitted in the RPO clone loop (recorded into `deferredCalleePhis` + `local`, so later insts referencing the phi resolve), and AFTER the whole clone loop each incoming is flushed тАФ the VALUE remapped via the shared `local` map (`mapCalleeOperand`), the PRED via the `calleeBlockMap` (callee-blockтЖТcaller-block clone), with a fail-loud `std::abort` on a pred-not-in-clone-map (structurally unreachable тАФ every callee block is pre-created in phase 1). The deferral is LOAD-BEARING for a LOOP-phi: the back-edge incoming VALUE is defined later in RPO (in the latch), so an inline flush would hit `mapCalleeOperand`'s def-before-use abort тАФ flushing after the loop (when `local` is complete) is what makes it resolve. The `emitCalleeInst` Phi arm is KEPT as a defensive fail-loud guard (a Phi is handled in the clone loop and never reaches it; rationale comment corrected). Correctness comes from the remap, NOT from restricting shape тАФ handles any incoming count / multiple phis / Arg-or-instruction incomings / loop back-edges uniformly. Runtime witness: `examples/c-subset/phi_inline/` (`pick(0)` тЖТ exit 9, the `["Inlining"]`-arm corpus). ctest 281тЖТ282. Independent review = CLEARS-THE-BAR (no FOLD-NOW); the verifier-blind sole-guard test was folded from the review's NIT-1 (also subsuming NIT-2's Arg-incoming coverage). *(Original status, preserved:)* **ЁЯФУ USER-AUTHORIZED 2026-06-13 тАФ the OPT7 hard-stop (`G-406`) is OPENED for this anchor (the user explicitly authorized addressing it: best-long-term + no-workaround + 100% source/target/linker agnostic). It REMAINS a SUPERVISED cycle: per the OPT7 discipline, the closing cycle must open with a ┬зB DESIGN BRIEF for the user's sign-off BEFORE building (the Phi-clone-with-deferred-incoming-flush design needs review). Schedule after the pre-FC5 HIR mini-arc.** OPT7 cycle 2 (2026-06-05): the multi-block splice (`D-OPT7-MULTIBLOCK-SPLICE`) admits a multi-block LEAF callee but `inlineLegalityGate` STILL REFUSES any callee containing a `Phi` in ANY block (`tests`-covered via the leaf+Phi gate). This is a CONSERVATIVE refusal (sound тАФ nothing miscompiles, just a missed inline), but the gap is **FRONTEND-REACHABLE**, NOT a post-optimization edge case: the c-subset frontend emits a Phi-bearing multi-block LEAF callee DIRECTLY, because `hir_to_mir` lowers the ternary `?:` AND the short-circuit `&&` / `||` operators to a MIR `Phi` at the merge тАФ independent of Mem2Reg. So a leaf such as `int f(int x){ return x>0 ? 7 : 9; }` (and likewise any `&&`/`||` returning a value) is a frontend-producible multi-block-leaf-with-Phi that cycle 2 refuses today. (A post-Mem2Reg promoted-slot join is one MORE way to reach a callee Phi, but the ternary/short-circuit frontend path is the common, optimization-independent one.) Trigger = the cycle that admits a callee Phi. Closing work = clone the callee Phi with its incomings REMAPPED through the callee-blockтЖТcaller-block clone map, deferring the incoming flush to a phase-3 pass (the `MirFunctionRebuilder` phase-3 / `Mem2Reg::finalizePhiIncomings` deferral discipline) so an incoming whose value/pred is emitted later in the clone resolves correctly. | `D-OPT7-CROSSCU-LTO-SINGLE-OPTIMIZE` | Cycle 26 (2026-06-05, D-OPT7-1): the N>1 whole-program path now optimizes EACH CU's MIR in `buildCuMir` AND optimizes the MERGED module once in `Program::compileOneTarget` (`optimizeModule(merged->mir, ...)`) тАФ a DOUBLE-OPT. This is correctness-neutral (a cross-CU call's per-CU inline is a no-op since the callee is an unresolvable extern per-CU; the merged inline does the work) but does redundant optimization work for multi-CU builds. A true LTO shape would SKIP the per-CU optimize for multi-CU builds and optimize the whole-program merged module ONCE. Not done now because `buildCuMir` is the SHARED build half used by the N==1 path too (which must still optimize per-CU since it never merges), so skipping requires a build-half variant or a "defer optimize" flag threaded through `buildCuMir`. Priority: **normal** (efficiency-only тАФ the redundant per-CU pass changes no output; the merged optimize is the load-bearing one for cross-CU inlining). | Thread a "skip per-CU optimize" signal into `buildCuMir` for the N>1 path (e.g. a `CompileOptions` field or a separate build-half entry) so multi-CU builds optimize ONLY the merged module; pin with a metrics/effectiveness check that the per-CU optimize is skipped while the merged optimize still inlines the cross-CU call. | plan 22 ┬з2.9 OPT7 (D-OPT7-1); cited in `src/program/program.cpp` (the merged-optimize site); pairs with `D-OPT7-CROSSCU-MIR-MERGE` |

| `D-MERGE-UNREACHABLE-BLOCK` | Cycle 25 Stage B (2026-06-05): `mergeCuMirs` (`src/mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp`, `FunctionCloner::clone`) clones each surviving function's REACHABLE CFG тАФ it pre-creates every natural block then fills in RPO from the entry (a valid def-before-use order for SSA). A pre-created block NOT reached by RPO (genuinely unreachable from the entry) is left unfilled, which the `MirBuilder`'s every-block-must-be-filled invariant would abort on at `finish()`; the cloner instead FAILS LOUD first (named abort) with attribution. This is correct for the merge's real inputs because the **c-subset frontend does not emit entry-unreachable blocks** (every block it lowers is reachable from the function entry; DCE, when the active pipeline runs it, additionally removes any dead block), and the hand-built tests are likewise all-reachable тАФ so the unfilled-block case never arises today and the limitation is unobservable. The reachable-only assumption is NOT load-bearing for soundness, though: the **fail-loud abort is the actual backstop** тАФ if a future input ever carried an unreachable block, the merge aborts with attribution rather than emitting a malformed function, so it is never a silent miscompile. Trigger = the first merge input carrying an unreachable block (e.g. a future frontend that emits an entry-unreachable block, or a debug/identity pipeline that runs no DCE on input that happens to contain one). Priority: **normal** (fail-loud today; never a silent miscompile). | Either (a) run DCE / dead-block elimination on each CU's MIR BEFORE the merge so only reachable blocks remain, OR (b) extend `FunctionCloner` to clone unreachable blocks too (fill them after the RPO pass in natural order; their intra-block SSA is self-contained, and cross-block operands into a dead block can't be live, so a second natural-order fill pass over the unfilled set suffices). Pin with a hand-built callee carrying a provably-unreachable block тЖТ the merged function preserves it (option b) or the merge runs post-DCE (option a). | plan 22 ┬з2.9 OPT7 (cross-CU MIR merge); cited in `src/mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp`; pairs with `D-OPT7-CROSSCU-MIR-MERGE` |

| `D-MIR-UNREACHABLE-PRUNE-NORMALIZE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-13 (MIR unreachable-prune cycle).** The ┬зB-decided (Design 1) MANDATORY, unconditional MIR normalize that drops every block unreachable-from-entry, REUSING the shared `MirFunctionRebuilder`/`MirRebuildPolicy` substrate (policy: `selectBlocks` = RPO-reachable from entry, `acceptPhiIncoming` = pred-in-blockMap тАФ DCE's exact shape) + a new `cloneGlobalsVerbatim` (NO carve-out тАФ it must process every module; func-ids are stable since the prune drops BLOCKS only, so `initFunc` is tag-re-stamped `MirFuncId{old.v, newTag}`, never ordinal-remapped) + `rederiveStructCfMarkers`. Wired at the TOP of `optimizeModule` (compile_pipeline.cpp ~74), the universal post-lowering chokepoint that runs on every CU BEFORE `mergeCuMirs` AND on the merged module, BEFORE `opt::optimize`'s verify-after-every-pass тАФ NOT a `PassId` (a pipeline config must never omit it); idempotent. This is the single BY-CONSTRUCTION chokepoint that owns the orphan-block class for ALL constructs (if / loop / switch / &&-\|\| / dead-code) and the FUTURE goto (FC5). ┬зB alternatives REJECTED: Design 2 (lazy per-construct) = multi-site (the do-while-break sibling was already missed once that way); Design 3 (verifier exempts `Unreachable`-terminated blocks) = weakens a load-bearing invariant. Closes `D-HIR-SEALED-JOIN-UNREACHABLE-BLOCK`; unblocks plan-23 FC5. 272/272 ctest; 8 strict pins (incl. globals-not-carved-out + phi-pred-drop + red-on-disable) + 2 runnable 4-target corpus examples. | (closed тАФ the prune IS the closing mechanism) | src/opt/passes/prune_unreachable.{hpp,cpp}; `cloneGlobalsVerbatim` in mir_rebuild_helper.{hpp,cpp}; wired in src/program/compile_pipeline.cpp `optimizeModule`; closes `D-HIR-SEALED-JOIN-UNREACHABLE-BLOCK`; unblocks plan-23 FC5 |
| `D-HIR-LOOP-BODY-ONLY-RETURN-DOUBLE-ATTACH` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-13 (pre-FC5 deferral mini-arc).** Root cause was NOT loop-specific: `maybeAppendImplicitReturnZero` (src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp) re-wrapped the body's ALREADY-PARENTED children via `makeBlock(oldKids+ret)`, and `makeBlock`тЖТ`addParent` aborts on a re-parented child (HIR is immutable) тАФ so ANY non-empty non-terminating `main` crashed (the infinite-loop shapes AND `int main(){int x;x=1;}` are instances; the empty `int main(){}` survived only because oldKids was empty). FIX: immutability-safe nest тАФ wrap the (unparented) body block + the synthetic return in a fresh outer block (`makeBlock({body,ret})`), never re-parenting children; the `addParent` guard is untouched. Infinite-loop mains now compile + run (the dead `return 0` is dropped by the MIR unreachable-prune); 274/274 ctest; corpus `infinite_loop_return_main` (exit 5) + `for_inf_return_main` (exit 9) + 2 HIR pins; red-on-disable demonstrated. The SEPARATE non-`main` infinite-loop over-rejection is `D-HIR-INFINITE-LOOP-NOT-TERMINATING` (next in the mini-arc). *(Original status, preserved:)* **OPEN (surfaced 2026-06-13 by the MIR unreachable-prune cycle's test-shape exploration тАФ PRE-EXISTING; the prune diff touches NO src/hir/ or src/mir/lowering loop builder). Priority: normal.** A loop whose body is ONLY a return/seal (`while(1){return X;}`, `do{return X;}while(c);`, `for(;;){return X;}`) trips a HirBuilder fatal `addParent: HirNodeId=N already has a parent (double-attach)` during HIR construction тАФ the tree-shaped-HIR structural guard fires on a VALID-C shape. Orthogonal to the MIR prune (upstream, in HIR build); the prune's loop-orphan coverage is instead via `for(i=0;i<10;i=i+1){return 11;}` (update-block orphan). | Root-cause the double-attach in HirBuilder for a loop body that seals immediately (a node re-parented between the body slot and a synthesized continue/exit edge); pin with the three loop-only-return shapes compiling + running. | hir_to_mir.cpp loop lowering; HirBuilder `addParent` guard; surfaced by the prune cycle; future-work pin (do NOT cite in src) |
| `D-HIR-DEAD-CODE-AFTER-RETURN-REJECTED` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-13 (pre-FC5 deferral mini-arc).** Relaxed the HIR verifier's dead-code-after-terminator rejection: `checkBlockTermination` now emits a NEW `H_UnreachableCode` (0xF00D) WARNING instead of the `H_VerifierFailure` ERROR, so `verify()` passes and `return X; <dead>;` COMPILES тАФ the dead statement reaches MIR, lowers via the Block-lowering fresh-dead-block, and is dropped by the unreachable-prune (runtime = the reachable `return X`). ISO-C-aligned (warn, don't reject). Scope: ONLY `checkBlockTermination`; the non-void-fall-through `H_VerifierFailure` ERROR (`checkReturnCompleteness`) is UNTOUCHED (still rejects). The new code is suppressible (not in the unsuppressable set). 281/281 ctest; corpus `dead_code_after_return` (exit 42) + 5 HIR warning pins; red-on-disable pinned. *(Original status, preserved:)* **OPEN (surfaced 2026-06-13 by the MIR unreachable-prune cycle тАФ PRE-EXISTING). Priority: normal (matters for the full-C / FC arc).** Statements after a `return` in the same block (`return 42; return 7;`) are REJECTED by the HIR verifier (`H0003`, "statement is unreachable") BEFORE MIR тАФ so the MIR prune is not their guard. This is stricter than ISO C (which permits unreachable code, typically with a warning); a real full-C program with dead-code-after-terminator would be rejected. | For the FC arc: relax the HIR verifier to ACCEPT (optionally warn on) unreachable-after-terminator statements, letting the MIR prune drop the resulting dead blocks; pin with `return X; <dead>;` compiling + running X. | HIR verifier `H0003`; `D-MIR-UNREACHABLE-PRUNE-NORMALIZE` is the downstream dead-block owner once HIR admits it; plan-23 full-C arc; future-work pin (do NOT cite in src) |
| `D-HIR-INFINITE-LOOP-NOT-TERMINATING` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-13 (pre-FC5 deferral mini-arc).** Implemented the documented design: `lowerWhile`/`lowerFor` (cst_to_hir.cpp) now wrap a PROVABLY-INFINITE loop (const-truthy/absent condition via the shared `evalCstConstInt` const-eval, AND no `break` reachable in this loop's own frame тАФ a frame-respecting body scan that does NOT descend into nested loops/switches) as `Block{ loop, Unreachable }`, so `pathTerminates` (recursing to the Block's last child) recognizes the function terminates. `int f(){while(1){return 5;}}` now compiles + runs (exit 5); `for(;;)`/`do-while(1)` likewise. NEGATIVE-pinned (no false positive = no miscompile): a breakable `while(1){if(x)break;}`, `while(0)`, `while(x)`, and a nested-inner-break are NOT wrapped. A statement-position `Unreachable` gained MIR lowering (`addUnreachable`), and MIR Block-lowering opens a fresh dead block for any sibling after a mid-block seal (dropped by the MIR unreachable-prune) тАФ which INCIDENTALLY fixed a pre-existing latent crash of `<both-arms-return-if> <sibling>` (`int f(){if(x){return 1;}else{return 2;} return 99;}`), now pinned (corpus `nonmain_if_both_ret_dead_tail`). `pathTerminates`/the verifier/H0003/the inliner UNTOUCHED. 280/280 ctest; red-on-disable pinned. *(Original status, preserved:)* **OPEN (surfaced 2026-06-13 by the pre-FC5 deferral mini-arc тАФ PRE-EXISTING). Priority: normal (next in the mini-arc, before FC5).** `pathTerminates` (the HIR verifier-substrate SSOT, src/hir/hir_verifier.hpp) is deliberately CONSERVATIVE тАФ it does NOT count a loop body as terminating; the doc says lowering should append an `Unreachable` after a PROVABLY-INFINITE loop to make the structural answer match the dynamic one, but that step was NEVER implemented (no Unreachable-synthesis in cst_to_hir.cpp). Consequence: a non-`main` function whose only exit is inside a provably-infinite loop (`int f(){ while(1){ return 5; } }`, `for(;;){...}`) is OVER-REJECTED by the non-void-return-completeness check (pathTerminates(body)==false). Valid ISO C. (`main` is unaffected as of 2026-06-13: the implicit-return-0 nest gives it a terminating return; this anchor is the NON-`main` case.) Sibling of `D-HIR-DEAD-CODE-AFTER-RETURN-REJECTED` (both are pathTerminates/termination over-rejections of valid C). | Implement the documented design: detect a provably-infinite loop (constant-truthy or absent condition AND no `break`/`goto` that can exit this loop's frame) in cst_to_hir lowering and append a synthetic `Unreachable` after it, so `pathTerminates` recognizes the function terminates (the MIR unreachable-prune drops the resulting dead block). Pin: `int f(){while(1){return 5;}}` called from main тЖТ exit 5; a breakable loop still requires a real return after it. | src/hir/hir_verifier.hpp `pathTerminates` doc (the unimplemented append-`Unreachable` contract); src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp loop lowering; the non-void-completeness check in hir_verifier.cpp; sibling `D-HIR-DEAD-CODE-AFTER-RETURN-REJECTED`; downstream owner `D-MIR-UNREACHABLE-PRUNE-NORMALIZE`; future-work pin (do NOT cite in src) |
| `D-PP-FUNCTION-LIKE-MACRO` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-19 (FC13 cycle 2 тАФ function-like macros).** The config-selected C preprocessor now implements function-like `#define F(a,b) ...` definitions AND invocations over the common direct-invocation subset of ┬з6.10.3 (full hide-set deferred тЖТ `D-PP-MACRO-HIDESET-PRECISE`). `MacroExpander::handleDefine` parses the parameter list (comma-separated `Word` params, adjacency `(`-after-name detection on raw spans, empty `()` тЗТ zero params); the redefinition-compatibility check compares kind + params + replacement text (`sameDefinition`). The cycle-1 token-by-token `expandInto` was reworked into a STREAM expander `expand(std::span<Token const>, painting, depth)` (object- AND function-like uniformly): a function-like name is an invocation ONLY if the next significant token is the configured `(` (else emitted verbatim, ┬з6.10.3p10); `collectArgs` collects paren-balance-tracked arguments (depth via the configured open/close paren; a depth-1 comma separates, the depth-0 close ends; trivia-trimmed); each argument is PRE-EXPANDED (`expand` in the current paint context, the invoked macro not yet painted, ┬з6.10.3.1); `substitute` maps param-name tokens тЖТ pre-expanded arg tokens; the result is RESCANNED with the macro painted blue (┬з6.10.3.4 self-reference freeze). Zero-PARAM `M()` is normalized to zero args at the call site. FAIL-LOUD: arity mismatch + unterminated invocation (`P_PreprocessorMacroArgument`, new 0x0017); duplicate parameter + malformed param list (`P_PreprocessorDirective`); VARIADIC `#define V(...)` (`P_PreprocessorUnsupported` тЖТ `D-PP-VARIADIC-MACRO`, deferred). AGNOSTIC: the close-paren is config (`preprocess.functionLikeCloseToken`, mirroring the opener тАФ `)` lexes as core `Punctuation`, so it MUST come from config). The cycle-1 `FunctionLikeMacroDefinitionFailsLoud` test was FLIPPED to `FunctionLikeMacroSimpleInvocationExpands`; 12 new function-like unit tests (nested, arg-is-macro pre-expansion, arity, verbatim-name, mixing, recursive-freeze, empty/zero-paren, variadic, dup-param, unterminated) + runnable 4-target corpus `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_function_macros` (exit 42). 331 ctest; object-macro tests + corpus unregressed; RED-on-disable demonstrated (neutering `substitute`). *(Original status, preserved:)* FC13 cycle 1 (2026-06-19): the config-selected C preprocessor (`src/analysis/preprocess/`) implements OBJECT-like `#define`/`#undef` only. A FUNCTION-like macro DEFINITION (`#define F(x) ...` - a `(` immediately adjacent to the macro name) is recognized and FAILS LOUD (`P_PreprocessorUnsupported`, `MacroExpander::handleDefine`), never silently passed through as object-like (which would mis-expand). Priority: **normal (cycle 2)** - the function-like macro feature (parameter capture, argument substitution; the `#`/`##` operators are SEPARATE = FC15) is the next preprocessor cycle. | Build function-like macro definition + invocation: parse the parameter list, capture call-site arguments (paren-balanced), substitute parameters into the replacement, then rescan. Pin: `#define F(x) ((x)+1)` + `F(2)` expands to `((2)+1)`; arity mismatch fails loud. Closing test in `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` (today `FunctionLikeMacroDefinitionFailsLoud` pins the fail-loud guard - flip it to expand-correctly when the feature lands). | plan 23 FC13; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp`; successor cycle 2 |
| `D-PP-MACRO-HIDESET-PRECISE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-19 (FC13 cycle 4 тАФ the precise per-token hide set).** The recursion-scoped blue-PAINT set was REPLACED by Prosser's precise per-token hide set (the standard realization of C ┬з6.10.3.4) тАФ within FC13's no-`#`/`##` scope this now realizes the FULL ┬з6.10.3.4, not merely the cycle-2/3-softened "common direct-invocation subset of ┬з6.10.3". `Token` is a 16B trivially-copyable POD that cannot carry a set, so the expansion WORKING set is a wrapper `struct ExpToken { Token tok; HideSet hide; }` where `HideSet = shared_ptr<set<string> const>` (IMMUTABLE-once-built тЖТ copies are a refcount bump, every token of one replacement shares the SAME set object; a null pointer is the canonical EMPTY hide set, so the parser-bound body lifts in with `nullptr` and pays no allocation; `std::set` makes the hot intersection/union a linear merge). `run` lifts `vector<Token>` тЖТ `vector<ExpToken>` (empty hide sets), runs `expand`, drops hide sets back to `vector<Token>`. `expand` is now a SPLICE-RESCAN cursor (no longer isolated recursion): on each expansion it splices the produced tokens тАФ each carrying its computed hide set тАФ back over the consumed `[i,past)` region of the working set and rescans from the splice point, so a function-like name and a `(` that become adjacent only ACROSS the replacement/parent boundary RE-PAIR. A token (name M) expands iff `M тИЙ hideset(T)`. OBJECT-like M: replacement tokens carry `hideset(T) тИк {M}`. FUNCTION-like M (next significant token is the configured `(`, matching `)` exists): `HS' = (hideset(name) тИй hideset(closeParen)) тИк {M}` (the Prosser intersection тАФ `collectArgs` surfaces the close paren's hide set; an argument's own accreted hide set is UNIONED with `HS'`, never dropped). The cycle-2/3 cross-boundary witnesses were FLIPPED: `#define A(x) x`+`#define F(x) ((x)+100)`+`A(F)(3)` тЖТ `((3)+100)` (was the literal `F(3)`); `#define NAME SQ`+`#define SQ(x) ((x)*(x))`+`NAME(4)` тЖТ `((4)*(4))` (was a frozen bare `SQ`). PRESERVED: direct object/function self-reference freeze (`#define X X`тЖТ`X`; `#define F(x) F(x)`+`F(1)`тЖТ`F(1)`), mutual recursion termination (`PтЖФQ`+`P(1)`), nested/argument-pre-expansion, variadic, object тАФ all unchanged. The `depth` backstop is RETAINED as defense-in-depth but now bounds NESTING (argument pre-expansion) rather than chain length: a finite object CHAIN (`M0`тЖТтАжтЖТ`M300`тЖТ0) now expands ITERATIVELY in one frame to `0` (the cycle-2/3 `DeepMacroExpansionFailsLoudNotSilent` premise was a recursive-engine artifact тАФ FLIPPED to `DeepFiniteMacroChainExpandsToValue`), while a pathological 256-deep-nested argument (`F(F(тАжF(0)тАж))`) still FAILS LOUD (`DeepNestedMacroArgumentFailsLoudNotSilent`). AGNOSTIC: the new machinery operates on `Token`/`SchemaTokenId` + macro NAMES only; `(`/`)`/`,`/`...`/`__VA_ARGS__` remain config-driven (no new hard-coded lexeme/arch/format string). +6 net preprocessor unit tests (2 cross-boundary FLIPs, 4 preserved-freeze) + reframed deep-chain (1тЖТ2) + runnable 4-target corpus `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_hideset` (object-macro-names-function-macro + function-returns-function-name, runtime-seeded via `ident`, exit 42). 333 ctest; all object/function/variadic preprocessor tests + corpora unregressed; RED-on-disable demonstrated (reverting object-like to isolated recursion mis-freezes `NAME(4)` тЖТ bare `SQ`, `test_preprocessor.cpp` `HideSetObjectMacroNamingFunctionMacroExpands` fails). *(Original status, preserved:)* FC13 cycle 2 (2026-06-19): the function-like macro rescan uses a recursive blue-PAINT set (`expand`'s `painting` keyed on the macro NAME) тАФ an APPROXIMATION of the precise per-token ┬з6.10.3.4 hide-set. It is correct for the common DIRECT-invocation forms (a name immediately or near-adjacently invoked, self-reference freeze, nested/argument-as-macro pre-expansion тАФ all pinned), but does NOT expand cases where a function-like name and its `(` are produced across SEPARATE expansion streams: `#define A(x) x` + `#define F(x) ((x)+100)` + `A(F)(3)` тЖТ the `A(F)` yields a bare `F`, and the trailing `(3)` is NOT re-paired with it, so the result is the literal `F(3)`; likewise `#define NAME SQ` + `#define SQ(x) ((x)*(x))` + `NAME(4)` freezes to bare `SQ` (the object expansion of `NAME` does not re-scan to collect `(4)`). These residual cases are a COMPLETENESS gap, NOT a silent miscompile: each FAILS LOUD downstream at the PARSER (verified: `got F` / `got SQ` тАФ a bare function-like name with a stray `(тАж)` is a syntax error), never producing wrong code. `A(F)(3)` is the constructible loud-failure witness. Co-designed with FC15's `#`/`##` token-marker cluster (the precise per-token hide-set is the same machinery that tracks `#`/`##` placemarkers). Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** тАФ closes with FC15, or sooner if a real corpus needs cross-stream re-pairing. | Replace the name-keyed blue-paint with a precise per-token hide-set (each replacement token carries the set of macro names disabled for it, per ┬з6.10.3.4): a token's hide-set is the intersection of the invoked-macro's hide-set with the next-token's, plus the invoked macro; re-pairing of a function-like name with a `(` produced by a SEPARATE expansion then follows. Pin: `A(F)(3)` тЖТ `((3)+100)`; `NAME(4)` тЖТ `((4)*(4))`. Implement alongside the FC15 `#`/`##` placemarker tokens. | plan 23 FC13/FC15; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` `expand`/`painting`; witness `A(F)(3)` (today fails loud `got F` at the parser); FC15-area cluster (precise hide-set co-designed with `#`/`##`) |
| `D-PP-VARIADIC-MACRO` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-19 (FC13 cycle 3 тАФ variadic `__VA_ARGS__` macros).** The config-selected C preprocessor now implements PLAIN variadic function-like macros over the C99/C23 `__VA_ARGS__` subset (NO `#`/`##`, so NO GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` comma-elision тАФ that stays FC15). `MacroDef` gained `bool isVariadic`; `parseParamList` ACCEPTS a trailing `...` (the configured `variadicMarkerToken` KIND) as the LAST parameter-list element (`(...)` or `(a, b, ...)`) тАФ setting `isVariadic`, replacing the cycle-2 `P_PreprocessorUnsupported` fail-loud тАФ and FAILS LOUD (`P_PreprocessorDirective`) when `...` is NOT last (`(a, ..., b)` / a stray token after it). The catch-all identifier is CONFIG-DRIVEN: new `PreprocessConfig::variadicArgsName` (c-subset `"__VA_ARGS__"`, matched by TEXT in the replacement like the directive words; loader OPTIONAL + non-empty-string-validated, `C_InvalidPreprocess`). Invocation of `F(named..., ...)`: the first `params.size()` args bind to the named params; the REST form `__VA_ARGS__` as ONE comma-joined run that PRESERVES the ORIGINAL source separator commas (captured by `collectArgs` into a `separators` vector), each trailing arg PRE-EXPANDED like a named arg (C 6.10.3.1). ARITY floor = the NAMED count (`args.size() < params.size()` тЖТ too-few fail-loud, `P_PreprocessorMacroArgument`); the variadic portion MAY be EMPTY (C23 тАФ `__VA_ARGS__` then substitutes to nothing). `substitute` replaces a replacement `Word` whose text == `variadicArgsName` IN A VARIADIC macro with the `__VA_ARGS__` run; `__VA_ARGS__` in a NON-variadic (object- or function-like) macro is REJECTED at DEFINITION (`P_PreprocessorDirective`, C 6.10.3p5). AGNOSTIC: `__VA_ARGS__` appears as a STRING nowhere in `src/*.{cpp,hpp}` (only in comments + the c-subset config JSON) тАФ the engine reads `cfg().variadicArgsName`; a red-on-disable rebind test (`variadicArgsName` тЖТ `__REST__`) proves it. The cycle-2 `VariadicMacroDefinitionFailsLoud` test was FLIPPED to `VariadicMacroNamedPlusVaArgsExpands`; +7 new variadic unit tests (named+__VA_ARGS__, zero-named, empty-C23, trailing-arg-pre-expansion, `...`-not-last, too-few-args, `__VA_ARGS__`-in-non-variadic) + a config-driven `VaArgsNameIsConfigDrivenNotHardcoded` pin + runnable 4-target corpus `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_variadic_macros` (runtime-seeded via `ident`, exit 42). All function/object preprocessor corpora + tests unregressed; RED-on-disable demonstrated (neutering the `__VA_ARGS__` arm in `substitute`). *(Original status, preserved:)* FC13 cycle 2 (2026-06-19): the function-like macro engine (`src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp`, `MacroExpander::parseParamList`) RECOGNISES a VARIADIC parameter list (`#define V(...) ...` тАФ a `...` ellipsis lexeme in parameter position) and FAILS LOUD (`P_PreprocessorUnsupported`), never silently accepting it as a named parameter (which would mis-substitute) and never dropping it. Variadic macros need the `__VA_ARGS__` substitution mechanism + the `,##__VA_ARGS__` GNU comma-elision, which pair naturally with the `#`/`##` operators тАФ both are FC15-area, so this is parked with that cluster. Priority: **trigger-gated** тАФ closes when FC15 (`#`/`##`/predefined + variadic) lands, or sooner if a real corpus needs `__VA_ARGS__`. | Implement `...` in the parameter list + the `__VA_ARGS__` identifier in the replacement (collect the trailing arguments incl. their separating commas into one token run; substitute at `__VA_ARGS__`), plus the GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` empty-elision once `##` exists. Pin: `#define LOG(fmt, ...) printf(fmt, __VA_ARGS__)` expands a call; the zero-trailing-arg case elides the comma. | `D-PP-INCLUDE-SPLICE-POSITION-ATTRIBUTION` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-19 (FC13 cycle-1 review fold тАФ Fix 2).** Resolved via option (a): `makeRemap` now redirects BOTH header- AND main-origin diagnostics off the synth buffer onto their real origin buffer (the main-origin early-return was removed), and the line-map's per-segment `originStart` (already built across splice boundaries by `copyVerbatim`/`appendWithContinuationSplice`) shifts a post-`#include` main-file synth offset back to its ORIGINAL main.c offset -- so a main-file error after a leading quote-`#include` now reports the real main.c line, not a splice-shifted synth line. The missing pieces were: (1) `PreprocessResult::originBuffers` (the distinct origin buffers the line-map references, ex-synth) surfaced as `CompilationUnit::auxiliaryBuffers()`, and (2) registering them with the diagnostic `BufferRegistry` at every render site (`program.cpp runCusToTargets` + the `test_diagnostic_corpus` harness) so a remapped diagnostic resolves instead of rendering `--> <unknown-buffer:N>`. PINNED: `test_preprocessor.cpp::IncludeSpliceDiagnosticsRenderToRealFilesAndLines` formats a real TU (leading `#include` whose header errors + a main error on line 2) through `DiagnosticReporter::formatAll` over the assembled registry and asserts `bad.h:1:` + `main.c:2:` + NO `<unknown-buffer`; RED-on-disable demonstrated (starving `originBuffers` -> both render `<unknown-buffer>`). No residual: header AND main both attribute to the correct file:line. *(Original status, preserved:)* FC13 cycle 1 (2026-06-19): the preprocessor line-map remaps a HEADER-origin diagnostic to the header file, but a MAIN-file-origin diagnostic is intentionally LEFT on the synthesized buffer (`PreprocessResult::makeRemap` skips the main-source origin). For a no-include TU the synth buffer is byte-identical to the main file so line:col matches; for a TU with a LEADING quote-`#include` the spliced header text SHIFTS subsequent main-file content, so a main-file diagnostic renders at the SYNTH line:col, not the original main.c line:col. | (closed by Fix 2 тАФ option (a) implemented) | plan 23 FC13; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` `makeRemap`/`LineMap`/`originBuffers`; `CompilationUnit::auxiliaryBuffers`; `program.cpp` registry; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |

| `D-PP-CONDITIONAL-COMPILATION` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-22 (Item 2 / FC14 тАФ C conditional compilation).** `#if`/`#ifdef`/`#ifndef`/`#elif`/`#else`/`#endif` + the `defined` operator in the config-selected C preprocessor (`MacroExpander`). A single-pass CONDITION STACK (`CondFrame{enclosingActive, anyBranchTaken, thisBranchActive, seenElse}`): the 6 conditional directives are dispatched UNCONDITIONALLY (always balance-track nesting), while `#define`/`#undef`/`#include` + the unsupported-directive diagnostic + body-token emission are gated on `stackActive()` (a dead `#if 0` group is elided тАФ only nesting is tracked, no errors, C 6.10.1); `seenElse` fail-loud on `#elif`/`#else` after `#else`; unterminated-at-EOF fail-loud. Short-circuit `&&`/`||` + ternary fold only the live arm (C 6.6 unevaluated operands not folded тЖТ `0 && (1/0)` is safe); div0/shift/overflow refuse via `EvalOptions` тЖТ `P_Preprocessor*`. 20 strict unit pins (precedence `1+2*3==7` vs `==9`, `defined` paren+no-paren, dead-`#elif` operand-not-evaluated, `#elif`/`#else`-after-`#else`, config-rebind agnosticism) + runnable `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_conditionals` (exit 42, 4 targets + release arm).
