| `D-PP-CONDITIONAL-INCLUDE-ORDERING` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-26 (p18 Cluster G c17).** Both symptoms fixed: a quote-`#include` guarded by a dead `#if`/`#ifdef` is no longer resolved (P0016), and an illegal char (`$`/`@`/`` ` ``) in a dead branch no longer reports (P000E) while a LIVE one STILL fails loud. **тШЕ The originally-planned Option A (a pre-scan dead-region oracle) was IMPLEMENTED then CAUGHT by the in-cycle review as a CRITICAL SILENT MISCOMPILE: the SynthBuilder pre-scan cannot see predefined (or header-supplied) macros, so a predefined-macro-guarded LIVE branch (`#if __STDC__`) was folded to 0 -> recorded DEAD -> a genuinely-live illegal char consumed before the parser (e.g. on a `#define` line) was SILENTLY suppressed (reproduced end-to-end via the CLI: `#if __STDC__\n#define M $\n#endif` compiled with zero diagnostics).** ┬зB (user, 2026-06-26): **Option 1 тАФ authoritative dead-regions.** The P000E suppression is now driven by the AUTHORITATIVE `MacroExpander` pass's liveness (`MacroExpander::deadRanges()`, recorded in `run()` from the full `table_`+`predefined_` state) тАФ the SAME liveness the compiler emits/skips tokens on тАФ so the illegal-char oracle can NEVER diverge from the real branch decision (the silent class is gone by construction). The P0016 include gate stays in the conditional-aware `SynthBuilder` (it must precede the macro pass), gated on `includeResolvable()`; its pre-scan dead-region machinery was REMOVED (now dead code). Plan-locked GO-WITH-FIXES (3 blocking folded: byte-interval recorder spec, per-directive re-sync, the proven-RED predefined test + false-green guard). Pins: the c17 `Preprocessor.*` block in `test_preprocessor.cpp` (`DeadBranch*`, `LiveBranch*`, `ActiveIllegalChar*` incl. `#define`-line/`#`-stringize/uninvoked-body, `PredefinedMacroGuardedLiveIllegalCharStillErrors` + false-green guard, `LiveOuterDeadInnerNest*`, `UnterminatedDeadBranch*`, `DeadRegionCloseUsesConfigEndifWord*`, FIX-3 function-like + `UnevaluableGuard*`) + runnable corpus `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_dead_branch_include` (predefined-guard live arm; exit 42, 4 targets + release). A residual conservative LOUD edge (predefined-VALUE-position guards in the include gate) is pinned separately as `D-PP-PRESCAN-PREDEFINED-VALUE-INCLUDE-GATE`. тАФ ORIGINAL TRIGGER-FIRED note (2026-06-26): SQLite is the first real input to hit it: `#if SQLITE_OS_WIN`тЖТ`#include "windows.h"` resolves on linux/macos (P0016 ├Ч5) AND a TCL gen-script in `#if 0` (sqlite3.c:32562) trips the illegal-char `$` (P000E ├Ч10). ┬зB DECISION (user, 2026-06-26): **Option A тАФ a conditional pre-scan** (evaluate the `#if`/`#ifdef`/`#endif` structure right after tokenization + STRIP dead-group tokens BEFORE SynthBuilder splices quote-includes AND before illegal-char diagnostics finalize; share the conditional eval with the existing MacroExpander pass тАФ DRY). Chosen over Option B (fold include-splice into the MacroExpander + separately defer the tokenizer illegal-char) for the single-mechanism cleanliness. тАФ ORIGINAL deferral below. FC14 named exclusion (2026-06-22): a quote-`#include` GUARDED by a conditional is spliced by `SynthBuilder` BEFORE the `MacroExpander` conditional pass runs, so `#if 0\n#include "x.h"\n#endif` still RESOLVES x.h (its spliced CONTENT is then correctly elided by the conditional pass, but x.h's resolution side-effects тАФ recursive includes, a missing-file `P_PreprocessorIncludeError` тАФ already happened). NOT a silent miscompile: the elision is correct, and a missing dead-branch include errors LOUDLY (over-approximation, never a silent mis-include). The same class covers an illegal char (`@`) inside a dead branch (the single global tokenize precedes elision тЖТ a `P_IllegalChar`, loud). Priority: **normal** (rare тАФ conditionally-guarded includes; the common case works). | Reorder so conditional elision precedes quote-include splicing тАФ i.e. run a conditional/`#if` pre-scan that strips dead groups from the token stream BEFORE `SynthBuilder` resolves quote includes (or fold include resolution INTO the conditional-aware macro pass). Pin: `#if 0\n#include "does_not_exist.h"\n#endif\nint x;` compiles clean (no include error). | plan 23 FC14; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`SynthBuilder` splice vs the conditional pass ordering) |
| `D-PP-PRESCAN-PREDEFINED-VALUE-INCLUDE-GATE` | NEW (2026-06-26, p18 Cluster G c17): the `SynthBuilder` quote-`#include` gate (`includeResolvable`) evaluates `#if`/`#elif` guards with a pre-scan that sees only this file's live `#define`s + `defined()`/`#ifdef` (which already AGREE with the macro pass тАФ both treat predefined macros as not-`defined`). It does NOT materialize a predefined macro used in VALUE position (`#if __STDC_VERSION__ >= 201112L`), folding it to 0. So a LIVE such branch's quote-`#include` is conservatively SKIPPED (тЖТ loud missing-symbol downstream) and an angle-`#include`'s descriptor MACRO surface is not spliced (тЖТ loud undefined-macro). **NEVER silent** (the c17 illegal-char oracle is authoritative via `MacroExpander::deadRanges()`; only the include gate is affected, whose every failure mode is loud). Rare тАФ predefined-VALUE-guarded includes (version checks usually guard ANGLE includes, which the import resolver handles, not project QUOTE includes); NOT hit by sqlite3.c (its conditional includes use `#ifdef SQLITE_OS_WIN` / `#if 0`, which evaluate correctly). The user-approved "seed predefined macros into the pre-scan" deferred here per the c17 plan-lock (loud-only + rare тЖТ disproportionate to build the predefined value-materialization machinery now). Priority: **normal**. | Materialize predefined CONSTANT macro values in the pre-scan `#if` evaluation via the existing `PpProductText` product-text convention (`sbExpand`/`sbEvalIfOperand` emit a predefined name's value tokens with spans in the product region; `productCb` returns the product). Pin: `#if __STDC_VERSION__ >= 201112L\n#include "missing_live.h"\n#endif` тЖТ P_PreprocessorIncludeError (the live include IS resolved) + converse `#if __STDC_VERSION__ < 0` skips. | plan 25 c17; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`SynthBuilder::sbEvalIfOperand`/`sbExpand`/`includeResolvable`); `src/analysis/preprocess/pp_if_eval.hpp` (`PpProductText`) |
| `D-PP-LINE-COMMENT-BEFORE-DIRECTIVE` | тЬЕ CLOSED 2026-06-26 (c22) тАФ ROOT CAUSE: the `//` token's `stringStyle.endsAt:"\n"` CONSUMED the terminating newline as a comment-body token, so a `code // comment` line's only newline vanished тЖТ a following `#` directive lost its line boundary тЖТ `firstOnLine` judged it not-first-on-line тЖТ leaked to the parser (P0001). FIX: a new GENERIC `StringStyle.endsAtExclusive` (config-driven, agnostic) тАФ the `//` mode ends AT but EXCLUDES the `\n`, which survives as a Newline token; handled at BOTH endsAt-consume sites (per-codepoint + coalesced). The c19 `firstOnLine` hypothesis was correctly DISPROVEN тАФ the real gate was the TOKENIZER, not firstOnLine. Indep review+audit VERIFIED-CLEAN (adversarial: corpus exit 42 WITH the fix, directive-leak + NO binary WITHOUT); 445/445. (EOF line comment w/o trailing newline still fails loud P_UnterminatedComment тАФ pre-existing, intended, C-conforming.) [orig NEW c18, refined c19] A `//` line comment AFTER code on a line, IMMEDIATELY followed by a `#` directive on the next line, makes the preprocessor FAIL to recognize the directive: it leaks to the parser тЖТ `D0004 <malformed include>` + `P0001 expected 'IncludeKeyword' got 'define'`, then a full cascade. Precise repro (CLI-confirmed): `int a; // c\n#define Z 1\nint b=Z;` тЖТ P0001. A `//` comment ALONE on its line before a directive (`// c\n#define Z 1`) WORKS; a trailing `//` comment ON the directive line (`#define Z 1 // c`) WORKS; a `/* */` comment before a directive WORKS тАФ only `<code> // comment` then a directive breaks. NOT silent (loud parse error). Likely root (confirm at fix time): `MacroExpander::firstOnLine` + the `//`-comment token swallowing its trailing newline, so the `#` after `code // comment` scans back through the comment (trivia, not-newline) to the code `;` and is judged not-first-on-line тЖТ not stripped as a directive. PRE-EXISTING (FC14-era `firstOnLine`; NOT introduced by c17/c18 тАФ neither touched directive recognition). NOT on the SQLite path (sqlite3.c uses `/* */`, so the c18 re-probe did not hit it тАФ IncludeKeyword count 0). Priority: **normal** (rare pattern; loud, not silent; NOT on the SQLite path). тШЕ **c19 UPDATE (2026-06-26): the `firstOnLine` hypothesis was TESTED and DISPROVEN** тАФ adding a LineComment line-boundary check to `MacroExpander::firstOnLine` did NOT fix it (a clean rebuild still failed `int a; // c\n#define Z 1`). The `#` IS first-on-line, so `firstOnLine` is not the gate. Variant probes narrowed it: `int a; // c\n#define` FAILS, but `int a;\n// c\n#define` (comment on its own line) and `int a;\n\n#define` (blank line) both WORK тАФ so the trigger is specifically a `//` comment SHARING A LINE WITH CODE, before a directive. The real mechanism is a preprocess-tier `//`-comment interaction not yet root-caused (`ppLexemes` at the preprocess tier shows `//` lexed as `/`+chars, but the full CLI pipeline handles `//` elsewhere тАФ so the dump is misleading; needs a parse-tier token dump of the failing vs passing case). DEEPER than a one-liner тАФ its own focused investigation cycle. | Root-cause the `code // comment`-then-directive desync (dump the synth + parse token streams for `int a; // c\n#define Z 1` vs `int a;\n// c\n#define Z 1`); fix at the real gate. Pin: `int a; // c\n#define Z 1\nint b=Z;` compiles clean AND `b` expands to `1`. | plan 25 c18/c19; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` + the `//`-comment tokenizer mode |

| `D-LK-MACHO-BSS-ONLY-LINKEDIT-STRUCTURAL-PIN` | NEW (2026-06-26, p18 Cluster G c19). Defense-in-depth so a future `linkeditFileOff` regression is caught even when the mac╨ЮS leg is unavailable. The fix itself IS verified (binary inspection: `__LINKEDIT` 0x0тЖТ0xc000) + the corpus caught the bug on c18's mac╨ЮS leg. NOT a silent miscompile тАФ fail-loud. | `D-PP-VARIADIC-GNU-COMMA-ELISION` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-24 (cycle d тАФ FC15 paste residuals).** The GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` idiom is now CONFIG-GATED (`preprocess.variadicCommaElision`, c-subset opts in): in Phase A's `isVaArgsName` arm, when the `##` immediately precedes `__VA_ARGS__` AND the prior `items` token is the config-declared arg-separator, an EMPTY `__VA_ARGS__` drops both the separator and the `##` (`LOG("x")`тЖТ`f("x")`), a NON-empty one keeps the separator and emits the pre-expanded args with no paste (`LOG("x",1)`тЖТ`f("x",1)`). NOT a silent miscompile (a stray trailing comma is a parse-visible artifact, not wrong code). Pin: `#define LOG(fmt, ...) f(fmt, ## __VA_ARGS__)` + `LOG("x")` тЖТ `f("x")` (no trailing comma); `LOG("x", 1)` тЖТ `f("x", 1)`. | plan 23 FC15b; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` `substitute`/`collapsePastes`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-PP-PRAGMA-OPERATOR-FORM` | FC15c named exclusion (2026-06-24, `#pragma` + `__has_include` + `__has_c_attribute`): the `_Pragma("...")` OPERATOR form (C 6.10.9) is NOT implemented. NOT a silent miscompile тАФ a `_Pragma(...)` token sequence today expands as an ordinary identifier + call shape (or fails loud at the parser), never altering codegen. Pin: `_Pragma("GCC optimize(\"O2\")")` in a replacement list expands to nothing (no error, no stray tokens). | plan 23 FC15c; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`expand`); `src/core/types/preprocess_config.hpp`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |

The standard pragmas `#pragma STDC FP_CONTRACT`/`FENV_ACCESS`/`CX_LIMITED_RANGE` (C 6.10.6p2) and the ubiquitous `#pragma once` (header-guard idiom) currently have NO effect тАФ `#pragma once` does not dedup a doubly-included header (the `#include` machinery's existing canonical-path cycle guard catches a CYCLE, but a non-cyclic double-include is re-spliced). NOT a silent miscompile тАФ dropping a pragma is standard-permitted, and the FP/FENV pragmas only relax codegen latitude DSS does not currently take. Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** (closes when a real corpus needs `#pragma once` dedup or an `STDC` pragma's codegen effect). | Give the recognized pragmas effect: parse the pragma payload (after the consume scan), dispatch on a config-declared recognized-pragma set. `#pragma once` records the current file's canonical path in a once-set the `SynthBuilder` consults to SKIP a second splice; the `STDC` pragmas set a per-region float-eval/fenv flag the lowering honors. Pin: a header with `#pragma once` included twice is spliced ONCE; `#pragma STDC FP_CONTRACT OFF` disables FMA contraction in the following region. | plan 23 FC15c; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`SynthBuilder` splice + `handleDirective` pragma arm); `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |

A `#line` directive would OVERRIDE that presumed line number + file name for the tokens that follow, but until it lands `__LINE__`/`__FILE__` always report the PHYSICAL source line/name. NOT a silent miscompile тАФ a `#line` directive currently falls through to the generic `P_PreprocessorUnsupported` fail-loud arm in `handleDirective` (an unsupported directive, never silently ignored); and `__LINE__`/`__FILE__` reporting the physical line/name (when no `#line` is in play, the universal case) is CORRECT, so this is a fail-SOFT gap that only bites a TU that actually uses `#line` (rare in hand-written C; common in generated code / `yacc`/`lex` output). The line-map already gives the physical origin; `#line` layers a logical override on top. Pin: `#line 100 "gen.c"` then `__LINE__` on the next physical line тЖТ 100 (not the physical line), and `__FILE__` тЖТ `"gen.c"`. | plan 23 FC15b; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`handleDirective`, `materializePredefined`); `src/core/types/preprocess_config.hpp`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-INCDEC-CONST-LVALUE` | **OPEN тАФ pre-existing gap, fail-SOFT, normal (Cluster F item F1, 2026-06-24).** A `const`-qualified lvalue used as the operand of `++`/`--` (`const int x; x++;`) is NOT rejected тАФ `classifyLvalue` / `simpleLvalue` do not model `const` today, so an `x++` on a `const` object lowers as an ordinary modifiable-lvalue read-modify-write. NOT introduced by F1: the SAME gap exists on plain assignment (`const int x; x = 5;`) and compound-assign (`x += 1;`), all of which route through the same `const`-unaware lvalue classification. F1's ++/-- lvalue guard DOES reject a manifest RVALUE operand (`5++`/`++5` тЖТ `S_IncDecNeedsModifiableLvalue`), but a `const` *lvalue* is addressable, so the shape guard admits it. Not a silent miscompile of VALUE (the increment is arithmetically correct); it is a missing CONSTRAINT diagnostic (C 6.5.2.4p1 / 6.5.16p2 require a MODIFIABLE lvalue). Requires the type lattice to carry a const-qualified marker the classifier can read. Pin: `const int x = 0; x++;` and `const int y = 0; y = 1;` both fail loud. | plan 23 Cluster F (F1); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`classifyLvalue`/`classifyIncDecLvalue`/`simpleLvalue` + the assignment path); `tests/hir/test_hir_lowering_c_subset.cpp` |
| `D-PP-VA-OPT` | FC15-paste-residuals deferral (2026-06-24, cycle d): the C23 `__VA_OPT__(content)` construct (C23 6.10.3.1) is NOT implemented. It is a DISTINCT, more general mechanism than the GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` comma-elision THIS cycle delivered: `__VA_OPT__(content)` emits `content` (an arbitrary balanced-paren token sequence) iff `__VA_ARGS__` expands to a non-empty list, and elides the whole `__VA_OPT__(...)` otherwise тАФ it requires recognizing `__VA_OPT__` + balanced-paren body extraction + conditional emission, none of which the placemarker/comma-elision machinery does. PRE-EXISTING gap (not introduced this cycle): a `__VA_OPT__` token in a replacement is left as an ORDINARY identifier (so a user writing it gets the literal `__VA_OPT__ ( тАж )` tokens through, a parse-visible wrong result, NOT a silent miscompile and NOT a fail-loud). Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** (closes when a real corpus / SQLite needs `__VA_OPT__`; the GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` idiom тАФ far more common in existing C тАФ is already covered). | Add a config `vaOptOperator` word (matched by TEXT like `defined`/`__has_include`); in `substitute`, recognize `__VA_OPT__ ( <balanced> )`, and emit the `<balanced>` content (pre-expanded) iff `rawVaArgs` is non-empty, else nothing. Pin: `#define F(...) g(__VA_OPT__(0,) __VA_ARGS__)` + `F()` тЖТ `g()`, `F(1)` тЖТ `g(0, 1)`. | plan 23 FC15; `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`substitute`); `src/core/types/preprocess_config.hpp`; `tests/analysis/preprocess/test_preprocessor.cpp` |
| `D-CSUBSET-FLOAT-NEG-ENCODING` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c78; ┬зB USER folded into c78) тАФ fneg realized capability-dispatched: arm64 native FNEG (0x1E614000/0x1E214000); x86 `xorpd/xorps xmm,[rip+16B-signmask]` (a minted 16-byte rodata sign-mask + the RipRelDisp32 slot). Objdump-confirmed 16-aligned masks on all 3 legs INCL. pe64 (4096 PE section + per-item 16-offset; the USER's PE-align concern refuted); signed-zero IEEE-correct (-я╝И+0.0)=-0.0). Witness `examples/c-subset/float_negate`=42 (RED-ON-DISABLE тЖТ A_NoEncodingDeclared). Was a Cluster-F F3 deferral (2026-06-24, cycle 7): float unary minus `-x` AND a negative float LITERAL (`-42.0`) lower to the MIR `fneg` op, which has NO encoding declared тАФ `A_NoEncodingDeclared` (x86) / `L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing` (arm64). FAIL-LOUD (the function is dropped with a positioned diagnostic; NEVER a silent miscompile). NOT a simple mirror of fadd/fmul/fsub: x86 float negate is not a 2-reg ALU op тАФ it is `XORPS xmm, signmask` (needs a `0x8000тАж0` sign-bit mask constant in rodata) OR `0.0 - x` (an FSUB from a materialized zero); arm64 FNEG IS a single 1-source FP instruction (`FNEG Dd, Dn` = 0x1E614000 / S-form 0x1E214000). Surfaced when the F3 `float_mul_sub` corpus used `-42.0` literals (the corpus was rewritten to avoid it via `(a-b)-(b-a)`). Priority: **normal / trigger-gated** (closes when a real corpus / SQLite needs float negation тАФ common in real C). | x86: add an `fneg` encoding (an XORPS-with-rodata-signmask sequence, OR lower MIR FNeg тЖТ `0.0 - x` FSUB at MIRтЖТLIR тАФ the simpler, arguably-better long-term path since it reuses the now-encoded fsub). arm64: add an `fneg` opcode row (FNEG single-source, mirror the `fmov`/`fpcvt` 1-source fixed32 shape). Pin: `double f(double x){return -x;}` compiles + `-x` is runtime-correct on both targets; byte-pin the encoding. | plan 23 Cluster F; `src/dss-config/targets/{x86_64,arm64}.target.json`; possibly `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` (FNegтЖТFSub lowering) |

---

## Allowlist (code-internal pins, NOT deferred work)

Anchor-shaped strings in `src/` that are **not** deferred-work markers and
should be **ignored** by the CI guard. Add a row when an in-code constant
or diagnostic-message identifier matches the `D-*` regex but isn't a
deferred-work anchor.

| Pattern | Reason |
|---|---|
| `D-MERGE-GLOBAL-INITFUNC` | Cycle 25 Stage B code-internal INVARIANT pin (`src/mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp`), NOT deferred work. Cited in the fail-loud abort that fires if a merged global's `initFunc` MirFuncId was not cloned into the merged module (`plan.funcMerged` miss). The invariant always holds for valid input тАФ a global's module-init function is a defined function in the same CU, which the merge always clones (an init function is never a cross-CU weak loser). The abort is defense-in-depth: a breach means the planning pass dropped a function a global still references (a structural contradiction), so failing loud beats emitting a global whose initializer points at a stale/absent func. No closing work тАФ it is a permanent guard, like the other `mergeFatal` invariant aborts. |
| `D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c9, branch p14 тАФ USER OPTED IN, overriding the named-exclusion).** GNU computed goto (`&&label` label-address + `goto *p` indirect branch) implemented END-TO-END, correct under the full release pipeline. ┬зB-signed-off (full-mechanism-correct-under-opt + synthetic-per-block-symbol reloc); independently plan-locked (MF-1 CFG-correctness verified by construction; 8 opt passes cleared) + independently pr-reviewed. AS-BUILT: grammar (a dedicated `labelAddressExpr` operand rule `[AndAndOp, Identifier]` тАФ NOT a prefix-group `&&`, because the parser rejects `&<undeclared-id>`; a 2-alt `gotoStmt`) + HIR `LabelAddressOf`/`IndirectGotoStmt` + MIR `IndirectBr` terminator (variadic successors = ALL address-taken blocks, like Switch) + `BlockAddress` value op (side-effecting тЖТ DCE-stable; `isBlockAddressTaken` DERIVED from BlockAddress ops, drift-proof) + the clone arms at mir_merge + mir_rebuild_helper + a SimplifyCfg guard (don't fold/merge an address-taken TARGET block) + Inlining refusal + LIR/asm (x86 NEW `jmp FF /4` / arm64 NEW `BR Xn` 0xD61F0000; BlockAddressтЖТlea/adrp+add to a synthetic per-block symbol) + a NEW format-neutral linker interior-block-symbol VA (`interior_block_symbol_va.hpp`, ELF/PE/Mach-O, `SymbolKind::BlockLocal`). Witnessed: `examples/c-subset/computed_goto` (threaded interpreter exit 17) + `computed_goto_inline_host` (exit 11) on x86-PE + WSL arm64-qemu, under BOTH baseline AND release pipelines; red-on-disable pins (IndirectBr-successors-truncated, SimplifyCfg-address-taken-fold, inline-into-host-routing). 369тЖТ370 ctest. **The pr-review caught a reachable silent miscompile the plan-lock + build BOTH missed**: the `MultiBlockInliner`'s hand-rolled caller-host emit (a 3rd clone family) mis-copied a BlockAddress payload + aborted on IndirectBr when inlining INTO a computed-goto host тАФ folded by routing computed-goto hosts to the single-block rebuilder (correct remap), red-on-disable demonstrated. **NAMED FOLLOW-UP (open):** `D-CG-INLINE-MULTIBLOCK-INTO-COMPUTED-GOTO-HOST`. | (closed тАФ c9 branch p14) | grammar + HIR + MIR + opt + lir + asm + link (interior_block_symbol_va.hpp) / plan-23 full-C arc |
| `D-CG-INLINE-MULTIBLOCK-INTO-COMPUTED-GOTO-HOST` | **OPEN, OPT, trigger-gated (FC-deferral close-out c9, 2026-06-23).** A function that ITSELF contains a computed goto (`BlockAddress`/`IndirectBr`) is currently routed through the SINGLE-block `MirFunctionRebuilder` inliner (which remaps both correctly), NEVER the `MultiBlockInliner` тАФ so a MULTI-block callee is NOT inlined into a computed-goto host (single-block callees still are). Correct + fail-safe (no miscompile); the only cost is a missed optimization. Closing work (only if a real perf consumer needs it): add `BlockAddress` (block-id payload remap via `blockMap_`) + `IndirectBr` (successor remap) arms to `MultiBlockInliner::emitCallerInst`/`emitTerminator` (mirroring the rebuild-helper arms), + a test inlining a multi-block helper INTO a computed-goto host under the release pipeline. Trigger: a benchmark showing the missed inline matters. | Add the 2 caller-host emit arms to MultiBlockInliner + a red-on-disable test. Priority: **opt (trigger-gated)**. | `src/opt/passes/inlining.cpp` MultiBlockInliner (the `functionHasComputedGoto` routing guard) + `D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO` |
| `D-CSUBSET-LABEL-BEFORE-CASE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-23 (FC-deferral close-out c10, branch p14, 371 ctest + corpus `label_before_case` exit 25 PE/ELF/qemu baseline+release).** AS-BUILT = Option A (user-chosen тАФ label NESTS over case, C-faithful): a new `caseStmt = [caseLabel, statement]` grammar form added to the `statement` alt + `switchBodyItem` made **speculative** (plan-lock CRITICAL catch: a non-speculative alt would trip the loader's `C_AmbiguousAlternatives` gate at CONFIG LOAD once {Case,Default} enters FIRST(statement) тАФ c-subset would not load AT ALL) + a `caseStmtRule` config pointer (AGNOSTIC; toy/tsql don't declare it тЖТ `.valid()`==false тЖТ the new arms no-op, exactly like `caseLabelRule`). `lowerSwitch` peels a (multi-)label-wrapped `caseStmt` into arms via an iterative chain (handles `foo: case 1: case 2: stmt`), producing HIR **identical** to the long-supported `case 1: foo: stmt` тАФ the label STAYS a real `LabelStmt` node, so the CST pre-scan + `goto`-INTO-switch resolution + duplicate/undefined-label checks all work UNCHANGED. A `caseStmt` reached as a non-direct-switch-item fails loud via NEW `S_CaseLabelNotInSwitch` (0xE023). Pins (all red-on-disable): HIR-equivalence (`foo: case 1: S` тЙб `case 1: foo: S`), bare-case-stays-flat (Finding 2), outside-switch guard; corpus `goto`s INTO the switch at the label тЖТ exit 25 baseline+release. Residual: [[D-CSUBSET-CASE-LABEL-IN-NESTED-BLOCK]]. ORIGINALLY: NAMED LIMITATION (FC5, 2026-06-15) тАФ a goto-label immediately preceding a `case`/`default` label (`switch(x){ foo: case 1: тАж }`) is legal-but-rare ISO C that FC5 did NOT parse: `labelStmt`'s body is a `statement`, while `caseLabel` is reachable only via `switchBodyItem`, so `foo: case 1:` is a clean fail-loud parse error (P0001 "expected EndStatement", no binary тАФ NEVER a silent mis-parse). All OTHER label positions (before any ordinary statement, forward/backward goto, goto into/out of loops, label on an empty statement, goto-over-initialization) are fully supported. Priority: **normal** (rare form; fails loud). | The clean fix is the case-labels-as-statements switch-grammar refactor тАФ model `case N : stmt` / `default : stmt` as `statement` forms (so labels nest uniformly: `foo: case 1: stmt` = `label(foo, case(1, stmt))`) and re-point `lowerSwitch` at the nested shape. That is its own piece of work (touches the switch lowering's flat case-item consumption), disproportionate to bolt onto FC5 тАФ hence the named blocker. Pin: `switch(x){ foo: case 1: return 1; }` parses + runs. | plan-23 full-C arc (FC5); c-subset `labelStmt`/`switchBodyItem`/`caseLabel`/`caseStmt`/`caseStmtRule`; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` lowerSwitch + the lowerStmt CaseStmt guard |
| `D-CSUBSET-CASE-LABEL-IN-NESTED-BLOCK` | **OPEN тАФ NAMED LIMITATION, fail-loud, rare (FC-deferral close-out c10, 2026-06-23).** A `case`/`default` label nested inside an INNER block of a switch arm (`switch(x){ case 1: { foo: case 2: y(); } }`) is not associated with the switch by the dss flat-switch model тАФ `lowerSwitch` groups only DIRECT switch-body items, so a case in a nested block reaches `lowerStmt` and fails loud with `S_CaseLabelNotInSwitch` (C 6.8.1). Priority: **normal** (rare; fails loud). | Closing it = lowerSwitch recursing into nested blocks to harvest their case labels (Duff's-device territory) тАФ a switch-lowering generalization, only if a real consumer needs it. | `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-27 (p19 Cluster G c27) тАФ model A: `volatile` as a TYPE QUALIFIER.** тШЕ ┬зB (user, 2026-06-27): **Design B** тАФ a qualified `TypeId` (`TypeKind::VolatileQual`, the registry's anticipated "model A") тАФ chosen OVER a Ptr-pointee bit. Accesses set `MirInstFlags::Volatile` via a new `volatileFlagForType` ORed with the c21 `volatileFlagFor` at EVERY access form. c21's per-symbol `SymbolRecord.isVolatile` RETIRED тЖТ migrated to the type (fixed a c21 double-count: `volatile int *p` wrongly marked the pointer OBJECT volatile). The two `S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported` (0xE025) reject sites DELETED + retired from kUnsuppressable (enum kept for ordinal stability). тШЕтШЕ The independent post-build AUDIT CAUGHT a silent-miscompile the local Windows suite missed: member/index/by-value access of a volatile CONTAINER (`volatile struct S *p; p->a` тАФ the sqlite Kahan-summation `volatile SumCtx*` + WAL `volatile WalCkptInfo*` shapes) dropped the flag тАФ a REGRESSION from c21's loud reject to silently-wrong. FIXED by type-propagation: a member/index access of a volatile container yields a volatile-qualified RESULT type, covering nested chains + complex-lvalue compound-assign (`pSum->rErr += r`) by-construction, + explicit by-value-arg/struct-return/brace-init threading; 10 per-form red-on-disable container pins. A reintern silent-drop (transparent `kind()` would reintern `volatile T` as `T` cross-CU) also caught+fixed. WITNESS: 29 MIR volatile pins (red-on-disable, exact-count) + runtime `volatile_pointee_cse` (CSE folds iff the flag is missing: exit 3 vs 2, WSL-verified) + `volatile_pointee`/`volatile_pointee_member` (the c21 reject examples INVERTED to positives); 449тЖТ450. тШЕ LESSON: a multi-site volatile contract needs EVERY container-access form tested тАФ the c21 lesson recurred (the local Windows suite was green-but-wrong; only the WSL runtime pin + the audit's per-form MIR probes caught the container hole). [orig:] **OPEN тАФ NAMED LIMITATION, fail-loud, trigger-gated (c21, D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-QUALIFIER, 2026-06-26).** c21 shipped `volatile` via **model B** (per-symbol/member `SymbolRecord.isVolatile` тЖТ `HirVolatileMap` on the access node тЖТ `MirInstFlags::Volatile` on every user Load/Store; the optimizer's DCE/CSE/Mem2Reg/LICM already honor the flag). Model B handles the OBJECT / struct-MEMBER / pointer-OBJECT (`int * volatile p`) forms тАФ everything the SQLite frontier (`volatile int isInterrupted;`) needs. It CANNOT express a pointer-to-volatile **POINTEE** (`volatile int *p; *p`): there the volatility rides the DEREFERENCED access, which requires type-level cv-qualification (model A тАФ a cv-qualified `TypeId` threaded through the interner + type-equality + every TypeId consumer + the optimizer's type inspection, a large core change). Building model A NOW, before a re-probe proves SQLite needs pointer-to-volatile, would be a speculative over-build, so the pointee form is REJECTED LOUD (`S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported`, 0xE025, unsuppressable) at BOTH type-name sites тАФ the per-declarator typing arm (head `volatile` + a declarator star) AND the co-located pointer arm (head `volatile` before the first star) тАФ making the fail-loud COMPLETE BY CONSTRUCTION (no pointer-to-volatile-pointee TYPE can be built тЗТ no `Deref` can silently drop the flag, the silent miscompile this gate forbids). | **Trigger:** a SQLite re-probe (or any real consumer) shows a genuine need for pointer-to-volatile-pointee. **Closing work (model A):** add cv-qualification to the type lattice (a const/volatile-qualified `TypeId`), thread it through type-equality + every TypeId consumer + the optimizer's type-inspection, and lower a volatile-pointee `Deref` to a flagged Load; then DELETE the two `S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported` reject sites (and the diagnostic + its kUnsuppressable membership). Pin: `volatile int *p; int a = *p; int b = *p;` keeps BOTH loads under the release pipeline (red-on-disable, exit-code witness mirroring `volatile_member`). | `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-QUALIFIER` (the c21 model-B parent); `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.hpp` `S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported`; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the two reject arms); plan 23 full-C arc |

The 3 `XxxSpecifierBody` rules RETIRED (folded). FULL struct+union+enum this cycle. **NOTE (behavior delta, intentional + still-loud):** a bare `struct S;` (no body, no object) PREVIOUSLY did not parse (a parse error); it now PARSES as a tag REFERENCE and fails loud with `S_UnknownType` (undefined tag) + `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` at HIR lowering тАФ still loud, never silent; minting an incomplete tag from `struct S;` stays the separate deferral `D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION`. FIX (GRAMMAR-mostly, REUSE c25): make `varDecl`'s `initDeclaratorList` OPTIONAL in BOTH branches (the storage-class-led `localDeclSpecifiers declHead тАж` and the keyword/qualifier-led `kwDeclHead тАж`) тАФ a local `struct S { тАж };` routes through `kwDeclHead` (Struct/Union/EnumKeyword-led тЖТ the unified c25 `structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec` DEFINES the type via `definesWhenChild`, minting the tag into the ENCLOSING BLOCK scope), takes NO declarator, ends at `EndStatement`. `identVarDecl`'s list is DELIBERATELY left REQUIRED (a bare typedef-name `T;` must stay an expression statement, NOT a no-declarator decl тАФ the struct/union/enum definition is keyword-led so only `varDecl` needs the optional list). BLOCK-SCOPING is automatic: the `structSpec` define machinery binds the tag into `current` (the enclosing scope = the BLOCK scope during a body walk) via the c24 nominal decl-site identity, so a local `struct S { тАж }` does NOT leak to an outer/sibling scope and a same-name outer `struct S` with a different layout stays DISTINCT. SEMANTIC: NO new code needed тАФ the existing Pass-1 `structSpec` define path mints + interns the type in the block scope. CSTтЖТHIR: the ONE C++ change тАФ `lowerVarLikeInto` gains the LOCAL twin of `lowerTopLevelInto`'s no-object branch: a LIST-mode (non-single) declaration with an EMPTY declarator list + `requireNamedDeclarators` either (a) DEFINES a composite (`findCompositeSpecifierIn` hits a body-present spec) тЗТ emit nothing (the type was already minted/interned semantically тАФ a later `struct S v;` resolves through it; a body TypeDecl carries no MIR effect), or (b) introduces NO composite (`int;`) тЗТ FAIL LOUD `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` (C 6.7p2) тАФ the SAME diagnostic + tier as the top-level `int ;`. The guard is gated on an EMPTY list so an ABSTRACT declarator (`int *;`, list non-empty) is NOT double-reported (the semantic `requireNamedDeclarators` arm already rejects it once). FAIL-LOUD preserved: `int;` loud (declares nothing); a ref to an undefined tag still `S_UnknownType`; no silent accept. WITNESS: corpus `local_struct_def_stmt` (the walMergesort shape тАФ a block-scoped `struct Sublist { int nList; int aList; };` no-declarator definition used to compute a return value across a non-inlined call тЖТ exit 42, with a file-scope same-name 1-field `struct Sublist` proving decl-site distinctness; pe-native + elf/macho/arm64 + a `{shippedPipeline:release}` arm) + 7 semantic pins (`C28LocalStructDefineNodeShapeAndType` asserting the varDecl/structSpec/structBody node shape + NO initDeclaratorList + the 2-field Struct type + the in-block ref resolving to the SAME TypeId; union+enum local defines; `C28LocalStructDoesNotLeakToOuterScope` block-scope non-leak тЖТ outer ref S_UnknownType; `C28InnerStructShadowsOuterDistinctType` nominal distinctness; `int;` semantically-clean; `int *;` single-diagnostic; ordinary-local-decls-unaffected) + 3 HIR pins (`LocalStructDefinitionLowersClean`; define+declare+ref lower clean; `LocalDeclaresNothingFailsLoud`); 451тЖТ454 ctest (1 new corpus + the new pins). | (closed тАФ the grammar tweak + the lowerVarLikeInto twin IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c28; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`varDecl` optional `initDeclaratorList` in both branches); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`lowerVarLikeInto` no-object local twin); `D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER` (c25 тАФ the `definesWhenChild` machinery it reuses); `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION` (the top-level no-declarator path this mirrors); `D-CSUBSET-SELF-REFERENTIAL-STRUCT` (c24 nominal decl-site identity тЖТ the block-scope distinctness); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-27 (p18 Cluster G c26).** ROOT (the c26 frontier): `((uid_t(*)(void))aSyscall[21].pCurrent)` (sqlite3.c:40989, the `osGeteuid` define) failed P9006/P0009 тАФ a cast to an ABSTRACT FUNCTION-POINTER type `(T (*)(params))expr` was unsupported: `castTypeRef` admitted only `{repeat StarOp}` after the base, not the parenthesized abstract declarator `(*)(params)`. FIX = REUSE the existing declarator machinery, no new mechanism: `castTypeRef` gains an OPTIONAL `abstractDirectDeclarator` tail (the abstract twin of `directDeclarator`, sharing `parenDeclarator`/`fnSuffix`/`arrayDeclSuffix` verbatim); the semantic resolver folds it via the SHARED `directDeclaredType` engine тАФ factored out of `declaratorDeclaredType` (the part after its pointer-layer loop) so the abstract type-name fold and the declaration-position fold can NEVER drift тАФ yielding `Ptr<Fn(params)->base>` (the SAME type params get). Covers cast + sizeof + compound-literal + va_arg (all share `castTypeRef`). The abstract twin's base EXCLUDES `Identifier` (C 6.7.7 direct-abstract-declarator has no identifier production) тАФ THE CRUX: a bare-identifier base would make `(c * c)` (a multiplication, sqlite/zext `(c*c)*(c*c)`) speculatively COMMIT as a bogus cast (the type child gains >1 leaf тЖТ commit-triage rule-1 'not a lone identifier' fires). AGNOSTIC: config grammar + the generic declarator engine; the optional `DeclaratorConfig.directAbstractRule` is nullopt for toy/tsql (zero change). FAIL-LOUD: a NAMED type-name declarator (a name nested in parens, `(int (x))expr`) is rejected with `S_TypeNameDeclaratorNotAbstract` (0xE027) тАФ never silently parsed as the bare base; an unknown base type still `S_UnknownType` (the declarator fold never masks a missing base). тШЕ ┬зB FORK (reported + resolved empirically): approach B (reuse the full `directDeclarator`, Identifier included) was tried FIRST and REGRESSED `(c * c)` тЖТ bogus cast тЖТ S_UnknownType (caught by the zext_u32_to_u64 corpus); approach A (the dedicated Identifier-excluding twin) is FORCED + correct. WITNESS: corpus `abstract_fnptr_cast` (a `void*` slot cast to `(int(*)(int))` and CALLED тАФ the osGeteuid shape тАФ exit 42, pe-native + elf/macho/arm64 + a `{shippedPipeline:release}` arm) + 8 parser pins (exact `abstractDirectDeclarator` nesting for cast/sizeof/compound/va_arg + `(c*c)`-stays-multiplication regression + base+stars no-tail regression) + 5 semantic pins (cast types `Ptr<Fn(void)->int>` / `Ptr<Fn(int)->int>` exact shape, named-cast fail-loud, unknown-base S_UnknownType, sizeof-resolves-clean); 448тЖТ449 ctest. NOTE: `sizeof(fn-ptr-type)` as an ARRAY DIMENSION does not fold (stays `S_NonConstantArrayLength`) тАФ a PRE-EXISTING limit that equally affects `sizeof(int*)` as an array dim (orthogonal to c26, fails loud not silent). | (closed тАФ the reuse IS the fix) | p18 Cluster G c26; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`castTypeRef` tail + `abstractDirectDeclarator` rule + `declarators.directAbstractRule`); `src/core/types/semantic_config.hpp` (`DeclaratorConfig.directAbstractRule`); `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (load the optional role + closed-key); `src/core/types/declarator_walk.hpp` (`declaratorNameNode` abstract-twin arm); `src/core/types/parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` (`S_TypeNameDeclaratorNotAbstract` 0xE027); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`directDeclaredType` extraction + the type-name fold + named-reject); `D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MEMBER-DECLARATOR`/`D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR` (the declarator engine it reuses); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-ARRAY-TYPE-NAME` | **OPEN тАФ NAMED LIMITATION, fail-loud, off the SQLite frontier (surfaced p18 Cluster G c26, 2026-06-27).** The BARE-array abstract type-name `(int[10])` / `sizeof(int[10])` (a type-name whose abstract declarator is a top-level `[N]` array suffix) is NOT supported: c26's `abstractDirectDeclarator` deliberately EXCLUDES `arrayDeclSuffix` from its BASE alt because `(b[0])` тАФ a pervasive array-INDEX value expression тАФ would otherwise speculatively COMMIT as a type-name `b[0]` (base `b` + array suffix; the type child gains >1 leaf тЖТ commit-triage rule-1 'not a lone identifier' fires before the binder-sketch value check), regressing `sizeof(b) / sizeof(b[0])` (the element-count idiom тАФ the `sizeof_value_array_dim` corpus). C's direct-abstract-declarator DOES allow a bare `[N]` base, but it collides with the index expression under the speculative type-vs-value triage. The `(*)[N]` array-OF-pointer form DOES work (its base is `parenDeclarator`; the `[N]` rides the suffix repeat). FAIL-LOUD: `(int[10])x` is a parse error / `sizeof(int[10])` resolves the bare `int` (no array) тАФ never a silent miscompile. OFF the c26 fn-ptr frontier (sqlite3.c:40989 is the `(uid_t(*)(void))` fn-ptr cast); `sizeof` of a known array OBJECT (`sizeof(arr)`) is the value form and works. Priority: **normal.** | **Trigger:** a real consumer needs a bare-array abstract type-name (`sizeof(int[N])` / a `(int[N]){...}` compound literal). **Closing work:** teach the speculative type-vs-value commit triage (parser `typeNameCommitApproved_`) that when the type child's BASE is a lone Identifier (not a keyword) followed by an array/other abstract suffix, it must still consult the binder sketch (rules 2/3 тАФ a known VALUE rolls back) instead of blindly committing via rule-1; THEN re-add `arrayDeclSuffix` to `abstractDirectDeclarator`'s base alt. Pin: `sizeof(int[4])` folds to 16 AND `sizeof(b)/sizeof(b[0])` stays the value form (both green). | `D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME` (the c26 parent тАФ its grammar comment carries the exclusion rationale); `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`abstractDirectDeclarator` base alt); `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` (`typeNameCommitApproved_` rule-1); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17) тАФ C23 ┬з6.7.2.5 `typeof`/`typeof_unqual`, the type-query type-specifiers.** `typeof(x)`=x's type (quals kept); `typeof_unqual(x)`=x's type top-level-qual-stripped; `typeof(T)`/`typeof_unqual(T)`=type-name forms. A PURE semantic-tier type resolver тАФ **ZERO HIR/MIR/LIR/codegen change** (resolves to a TypeId inside `resolveTypeNodeImpl`, reusing the sizeof/_Alignof machinery; consumed downstream exactly like a typedef). Config-driven grammar: `typeof`/`typeof_unqual` keywords + `typeofSpecifier={alt:[typeofType,typeofValue],speculative}` (`typeofType` uses `castTypeRef` + `commitRequiresTypeName{requireKnownType}` [mirrors `alignasTypeName` тАФ so `typeof(ENUM_CONST)` resolves as a VALUE, not mis-committed as a type], `typeofValue` uses `expression`) appended to the 7 type-specifier alt-lists (brand-new disjoint tokens тЖТ no FIRST-set collision, existing dispatch byte-identical); config fields `typeofTypeRule`/`typeofValueRule`/`typeofOperandChild`/`typeofStripQualifiersToken`. `typeof_unqual`=`stripVolatile` (only VolatileQual is interned; const is not). тШЕ **The design-audit caught a CRITICAL silent-miscompile the design MISSED: `resolveTypeNodeImpl`'s coarse volatile/const decl-head scans (`subtreeContainsToken`/`declaratorObjectIsConst`) descended INTO the typeof operand and re-qualified the result AFTER the arm stripped it тЖТ `typeof_unqual(volatile T)` was silently INERT (strip is its ONLY observable effect). FIX: a config-driven `opaqueRules` set (the typeof rule ids) makes the operand OPAQUE to both scans тАФ code-audit CONFIRMED provably inert for every NON-typeof declaration on this shared decl-head path.** Fail-loud: `S_TypeofBitfieldOperand`=0xE036 (bit-field operand, C 6.7.3.6p3, unsuppressable); reuses `S_UnknownType` (bad type-name) / existing expr codes / `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` (bare `typeof(x);`). тЪа KNOWN GAP (LOW, code-audit): a `typeof(const int) v;` scalar LOSES its const-flag (the const-scan opacity hides the operand тЖТ `v=5;` not rejected) тАФ the SAFE direction of the const-model tradeoff (the alternative leaks const onto pointer objects тЖТ spurious `S_ConstViolation`), and const isn't interned at all тЖТ belongs to the deferred [[D-CSUBSET-POINTEE-CONST-ENFORCEMENT]] class; a missing DIAGNOSTIC (correct runtime value), NOT a miscompile. Independent design-audit (GO-WITH-FIXES тЖТ the scan-opacity fix) + code-audit (VERIFIED-CLEAN). 3-leg ctest **609/609** + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч 3 legs. WITNESSES: `examples/c-subset/typeof_basic` (RUNTIME wrap-at-256 via a `typeof(base)` u8 object тЖТ 42, release arm) + `TypeofUnqualStripsVolatile` (red-on-disable for the scan-opacity fix тАФ preservation passes either way, only strip catches the bug) + `TypeofEnumConstantOperandResolvesAsValue` (polarity pin) + 7 semantic tests + a bare-`typeof(x);` HIR test + 3 diagnostic goldens. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; plan-00 stepper T3.2; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the arm + the `opaqueRules` shared-scan fix); opens [[D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF-GNU-SPELLING]]; const gap тЖТ [[D-CSUBSET-POINTEE-CONST-ENFORCEMENT]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF-GNU-SPELLING` | тП│ **TRIGGER-GATED (opened 2026-07-09 with the FC17 `typeof`/`typeof_unqual` feature, `D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF`).** The GNU pre-standard spellings `__typeof__` / `__typeof` (and `__typeof_unqual__`) are NOT recognized тАФ only the C23 `typeof` / `typeof_unqual` (┬з6.7.2.5) keywords are. `__typeof__` behaves EXACTLY like the qualifier-preserving `typeof` (no strip); it is common in glibc system headers but has ZERO uses in the vendored SQLite amalgamation (which is C89-portable). FAIL-LOUD, never a silent miscompile: an `__typeof__(x)` today lexes `__typeof__` as an ordinary identifier тЖТ a parse/semantic error, never a wrong type. **Trigger:** a real consumer (a glibc-style header, or a user program) uses `__typeof__`/`__typeof`. | **Closing work:** add 2тАУ3 more keyword rows to `c-subset.lang.json` (`__typeof__`/`__typeof` тЖТ `TypeofKeyword`; optionally `__typeof_unqual__` тЖТ `TypeofUnqualKeyword`) тАФ the grammar's inline keyword-alt at `typeofType`/`typeofValue` child 0 already accepts a set, so the ONLY change is more `{ "word": тАж, "kind": "TypeofKeyword" }` rows (they route through the identical arm тАФ qualifier-preserving). No semantic/config change. Pin: `__typeof__(x)` resolves identically to `typeof(x)`. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | `D-CSUBSET-TYPEOF` (the parent FC17 feature тАФ the C23 `typeof`/`typeof_unqual` type-specifier, shipped 2026-07-09); `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (keyword table + `typeofType`/`typeofValue`); plan 23 full-C arc (FC17) |
| `D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17) тАФ C23 ┬з6.7.1 `constexpr`, the OBJECT storage-class (scalar arithmetic + null-pointer objects; NOT functions тАФ C23 has no constexpr functions).** THE EMPIRICAL DELTA vs `const`: `const int x = argc;` compiles clean (const-ness is initializer-blind; the fold is lazy) while `constexpr int x = argc;` fails AT ITS OWN DECLARATION (S_ConstexprNonConstantInitializer). **ZERO codegen** тАФ a validated constexpr object lowers byte-identically to a const object with a foldable init (`hir_to_mir` folds global inits via `evaluateConstant` regardless of const-ness). Config-driven: `constexpr` keyword + `singleDeclSpecifier`/`localDeclSpecifier` alt appends (forDecl reuses the local prefix тЖТ `for (constexpr int i = 0;;)` parses, VALID C23 6.8.5p3) + `semantics.constexpr{keywordToken}` тЖТ `SemanticConfig::constexprKeywordToken`; linkage rides config too тАФ C23 6.2.2p3 file-scope INTERNAL linkage via `topLevelDecl.linkageSpecifiers["constexpr"]={binding:local}` (composes idempotently with `static`; NOT the noreturn no-op-clobber hazard) + `varDecl.linkageSpecifierIgnoredKinds += ConstexprKeyword` (block scope carries no linkage). Pass 1 mints `SymbolRecord::isConstexpr` (implies isConst) via `specifierPrefixHasConstexpr` (the noreturn-scan mirror); Pass 2's `validateConstexprDeclarator` enforces 6.7.1 тАФ тШЕ **F1 (audit): the hook is HOISTED ABOVE the per-declarator loop's `initDeclaratorRule` gate** (collectDeclarators admits BARE declarator carriers тАФ a post-gate hook would silently skip the missing-init AND accept `constexpr int f(void){}` with wrong internal linkage). Initializer folding via the NEW float-capable `constExprValue` (allowFloat + `floatLiteralTokens`) sharing ONE extracted `buildConstEvalEnv` (6 resolvers, 3 audit-pinned invariants: the scope-gate, the dynamic/static scope-capture asymmetry, caller-owned token sets) with `constIntExpr` тАФ broad semantic/hir/mir/preprocess/parser/integrated suites prove the extraction behavior-preserving. тШЕ **F2 (audit; also closed 2 PRE-EXISTING static_assert gaps): config-driven char/bool leaf arms in the SHARED CST evaluator** тАФ a fixed-value keyword arm (`literalTypes` `value:` rows via `CstEvalContext::fixedValueTokens`, integer-valued cores ONLY so the NullptrT `nullptr` row NEVER folds as an integer) + a narrow-char arm ([charStart, charBody] shape via the SHARED `decodeCharLiteralBody`; wide/UTF openers stay loud тАФ their element core is format-keyed/semantic-stamped) тЖТ `_Static_assert('a'==97,"")` and `_Static_assert(true,"")` FAILED S0029 at HEAD (empirically confirmed) and now PASS. F3 no-leak walls pinned (`_Static_assert(1.5>1.0)` still S0029; `int a[1.5+1.5]` still S000B; `int a[nullptr]` still S000B). 5 unsuppressable diagnostics: S_ConstexprNonConstantInitializer=0xE037 / S_ConstexprMissingInitializer=0xE038 (per-declarator) / S_ConstexprUnsupportedType=0xE039 / S_ConstexprFunctionNotSupported=0xE03A (proto + definition) / S_ConstexprInvalidQualifier=0xE03B (volatile OBJECT; volatile POINTEE + east-volatile pinned both ways). Enum-typed constexpr ADMITTED (F5, enumerators fold via resolveSymbolValue). WITNESSES: `examples/c-subset/constexpr_array_dim` (exit 42, 4 targets + release arm; sizeof(data)==20 proves N sized the TYPE; float/nullptr/char/bool arms + argc-seeded runtime fill) + the delta pin (`ConstexprDeltaVsConst`) + 13 semantic tests + the MIR linkage pin (`FileScopeConstexprGetsInternalLinkage`, bare/static/const contrast) + 6 diagnostic goldens (zero drift in existing goldens). Deferrals opened: [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-AGGREGATE-TYPE]], [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-POINTER-CAST-NULL]]. F7 nit (doc): a no-declarator `constexpr struct S{int x;};` silently ignores the specifier (GCC-warning parity, no declarator to validate). | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; plan-00 stepper T3.2; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_model.hpp,semantic_analyzer.cpp}` (mint + validate + buildConstEvalEnv/constExprValue) + `src/hir/cst_const_eval.{hpp,cpp}` (fixed-value/float/char leaf arms); [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]] (the null-pointer-constant machinery it rides); [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-CONSTEXPR-FOLD]] (nullptr==nullptr ICE folding тАФ still open, unchanged) |
| `D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-AGGREGATE-TYPE` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]]).** A `constexpr` object of ARRAY / STRUCT / UNION type (`constexpr int a[3] = {1,2,3};`, `constexpr struct S s = тАж;`, **incl. `constexpr char s[] = "hi";` тАФ a UNIFORM boundary, the char-array-from-string-literal form is deliberately NOT carved out** [Fork C]) is rejected loud with S_ConstexprUnsupportedType (0xE039), NEVER silently degraded to plain const. | `D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR-EXACT-REPRESENTABILITY` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]], code-audit MEDIUM-1).** C23 6.7.1p10 requires a constexpr initializer's value be EXACTLY representable in the declared type тАФ `constexpr int x = 1.5;`, `constexpr unsigned u = -1;`, `constexpr short s = 100000;` are constraint violations (GCC/Clang: тАЬinitializer not representableтАЭ). DSS currently ACCEPTS them (the fold succeeds; the stored value is byte-identical to the plain-`const` equivalent тАФ a silent ACCEPT of invalid C23, NOT a miscompile). Pinned by the accepting-pin `ConstexprExactRepresentabilityCurrentlyUnenforced` (flips red when this closes тЖТ update deliberately). **Trigger:** conformance sweep (FC18) or a real TU depends on the reject. | Add an exact-representability check to `validateConstexprDeclarator`'s arithmetic arm: compare the folded `HirLiteralValue` against the declared type's range/kind (fractional-to-int, out-of-range, negative-to-unsigned тЖТ a new S_Constexpr code or 0xE037 with a distinct message). Priority: **normal**. | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]] (code-audit); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`validateConstexprDeclarator`) |
| `D-CSUBSET-CHAR-HIGHBYTE-ICE-SIGNEDNESS` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by the [[D-CSUBSET-CONSTEXPR]] code-audit, MEDIUM-2; the underlying class is PRE-EXISTING from the wide/UTF cycle B).** The new shared-evaluator char leaf arm folds a high-byte narrow char constant `'├┐'` to +255 in ICE contexts (faithful to the cycle-B value tier тАФ the SAME shared `decodeCharLiteralBody`), but (a) all four DSS reference targetsтАЩ compilers use SIGNED char (`'├┐' == -1`) тЖТ `int a['├┐' < 0 ? 1 : 2]` sizes differently under DSS than GCC/Clang (char signedness is implementation-defined тАФ a LEGAL choice, but a silent parity divergence where pre-fold behavior was a LOUD non-fold), and (b) DSSтАЩs own runtime materializes CharтЖТint as SExt тЖТ a POSSIBLE fold(+255)-vs-runtime(тИТ1) disagreement on the same comparison тАФ UNPROBED (the audit was read-only). 0тАУ127 bodies (all real-world usage incl. sqlite) have ZERO divergence. **Trigger:** a runtime-vs-fold probe confirms a disagreement, or a real program hits the boundary. | Probe `'├┐'` fold-vs-runtime; then either pin the unsigned-code-unit model end-to-end (incl. the SExt path) or reject high-byte narrow char constants in ICE contexts loud (the narrow sibling of [[D-CSUBSET-WIDE-HEX-OCTAL-ESCAPE-VALUE]]).
