| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17, the LAST FC17 item) тАФ C23 ┬з6.7.13 standard-attribute SEMANTICS: `[[maybe_unused]]`/GNU `unused` (suppress S_UnusedVariable), `[[deprecated["msg"]]]`/GNU (warn per use site), `[[nodiscard["msg"]]]`/GNU `warn_unused_result` (warn on a discarded call result), + the unknown-`[[...]]` policy.** ZERO codegen тАФ pure semantic-tier warnings + one grammar statement rule (see [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT]]). Config = a TABLE with a CLOSED VERB SET (`semantics.attributeSemantics{attrSpecRule, stdAttrRule, bareStatementRule, effects:[{names, effect}]}`, effect тИИ {suppressUnused, warnOnUse, warnOnDiscard, none}; loader validates the verb closed-set тЖТ C_InvalidSemantics on a typo) + `semantics.nodiscard{discardStatementRule, expressionRule}`. Scan: `extractOneAttrClause` (last-::-segment name, dunder-normalized via the shared `stripDunder`; optional string message via the SHARED `decodeAdjacentStringBodies` chokepoint) + `scanAttributeSemantics` (bounded descent that STOPS AT each attrSpec/stdAttr node; a stdAttr's Internal children are one clause each). Facts fold ONCE per declaration (the alignas/noreturn shared-prefix precedent, Pass-1.5 ~semantic_analyzer.cpp:4260) тЖТ applied to EVERY named declarator (`SymbolRecord.isMaybeUnused/isDeprecated+deprecatedMessage/isNodiscard+nodiscardMessage`); deprecated/nodiscard OR-merge across the proto/def pair in the post-1.5 `mergedFnDecls` sweep (messages first-non-empty-wins; isMaybeUnused deliberately NOT merged тАФ consulted only at the declarator's own D8 check). CONSUMERS: D8 skips `isMaybeUnused`; the Pass-2 reference-resolution chokepoint warns S_DeprecatedSymbolUsed per use (incl. a call's callee тАФ the operand-rule row); checkCall's direct-symbol tail warns S_NodiscardResultDiscarded. тШЕ **F1 (design-audit): the nodiscard discard check is TWO-hop** тАФ the expression engine MATERIALIZES an `expression` node between the call and its statement, so fire iff `rule(parent(call))==expressionRule && rule(grandparent)==exprStmtRule` (both config rule ids; a one-hop parent==exprStmt check would NEVER fire тАФ the audit caught exactly this). By construction `(void)f();` suppresses (castExpr interposes) and `x=f()`/`g(f())`/`return f()` no-fire тАФ pinned red-on-disable (`NodiscardVoidCastSuppresses`). тШЕ **F4: the effects table carries `none` rows for KNOWN vocabulary** (fallthrough/likely/unlikely/reproducible/unsequenced + noreturn [the FC16 scan owns it] + packed [the composite scan owns it]) so `[[noreturn]] int f(void);` NEVER false-fires the unknown warning (pinned: `KnownC23NoOpAttributesDontWarn`). UNKNOWN-name policy: the C23 `[[...]]` form warns **S_UnknownAttribute=0xE03C, SUPPRESSIBLE** (C23 forbids fatal unknown standard attrs тАФ the WG21 P2552 posture; hasErrors stays false, pinned + `--suppress=0xE03C` probe-verified); the GNU form keeps its pre-existing loud gates (file-scope H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier pinned STILL firing for `__attribute__((frobnicate))`; block scope = [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-GNU-BLOCK-SCOPE-UNKNOWN-NAME]]). All 3 new codes (0xE03C/S_DeprecatedSymbolUsed=0xE03D/S_NodiscardResultDiscarded=0xE03E) are WARNINGS, deliberately NOT in unsuppressable_codes (the S_UnknownTypeAttribute posture тАФ comment added there). REQUIRED linkage seam: `topLevelDecl.linkageSpecifierIgnoredNames` ["noreturn"] += ["deprecated","maybe_unused","unused","nodiscard","warn_unused_result"] тАФ WITHOUT it every file-scope GNU spelling hard-fails H000C (probe-confirmed at the pre-change HEAD); by-name skip has NO linkage effect so a co-present `static` keeps internal linkage (MIR pin `GnuDeprecatedDoesNotClobberStaticLinkage`); string args (`deprecated("m")`) independently skipped by the string-token guard. WITNESSES: 17 semantic tests (suppress+control/GNU-unused/multi-declarator/per-site├Ч2-spans/message/proto-OR-merge red-on-disable/object-use/discard/void-cast F1 pin/consumed-positions/message/GNU-warn_unused_result+merge/unknown-suppressible/F4/fallthrough├Ч2-spellings/bare-stmt-unknown) + 3 parser wrapper pins + 3 HIR tests (7 GNU spellings clean / the frobnicate H000C boundary / fallthrough-lowers-to-Skip) + the MIR static pin + 4 diagnostic goldens (attr_maybe_unused_suppressed / attr_deprecated_two_sites / attr_nodiscard_discard_pair / attr_unknown_std) + `examples/c-subset/switch_fallthrough_attribute` (exit 11 = 1+10 through the marked fall-through, 4 targets + full-release-like arm; empirically 11 on pe64 debug AND release). Deferrals opened: [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-DEPRECATED-TYPES]], [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-PARAM-POSITION]], [[D-CSUBSET-NODISCARD-INDIRECT-DISCARD-CONTEXT]], [[D-CSUBSET-NODISCARD-INDIRECT-CALLEE]], [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-GNU-BLOCK-SCOPE-UNKNOWN-NAME]], [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-TYPE-POSITION]]. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17 (LAST item); plan-00 stepper T3.2; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (attributeSemantics + nodiscard blocks + linkage names + grammar) + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_model.hpp,semantic_analyzer.cpp}` (scan + mint + merge + D8 + deprecated + nodiscard + bare-stmt arm); [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT]] (the grammar sibling); [[D-CSUBSET-NORETURN]] (the scan/merge precedents) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-STATEMENT` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17, with [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]) тАФ the C23 ┬з6.8.1 bare attribute-declaration STATEMENT (`[[fallthrough]];` / `__attribute__((fallthrough));`), pre-FC17 a P0009 parse error.** Grammar: `attributeDeclaration = [compositeAttrList, EndStatement]` (REUSES packed's `compositeAttrList` verbatim тАФ audit-verified side-effect-free: `scanCompositePacked` runs only from structSpec/unionSpec) + `declOrAttrStmt = {alt:[varDecl, attributeDeclaration], speculative, lookahead 256}` REPLACING `varDecl` in the `statement` alt (the ONLY varDecl-as-alt-branch site; a direct statement-alt addition trips C_AmbiguousAlternatives тАФ varDecl's FIRST already carries BracketOpen/AttributeKeyword via the localDeclSpecifiers attr alts). HOT PATH (design-audit confirmed + suite-timed): every non-attribute statement lead has varDecl as the SOLE FIRST-survivor тЖТ the parser's unique-production DIRECT DESCENT (no probe, no checkpoint); only `[[`/`__attribute__`-led statements speculate (varDecl probes first, rolls back on the immediate `;`). тШЕ **F2 (design-audit): the wrapper MATERIALIZES a CST node** (named alt rules do тАФ the declOrExprStmt golden precedent) тАФ OWNED deliberately: the `tests/corpus/c-subset/mini_calc.c.tree` golden regenerated (NET delta = exactly 14 `rule:declOrAttrStmt` insertions, one per statement-position varDecl тАФ verified by a diff-counter script; the design's "2 goldens" hint was stale, exactly ONE c-subset corpus .tree exists), NO other golden drifted (hir-text/diag-corpus/toy/tsql all green untouched), + the wrapper-shape pin `StatementVarDeclRidesTheDeclOrAttrStmtWrapper` (statement>declOrAttrStmt>varDecl). Consumers transparent BY MECHANISM: HIR's unmapped-statement `soleMeaningfulChild` PassThrough peels the wrapper (both branches); semantic passes are rule-KEYED full-tree walks. Lowering: `attributeDeclaration тЖТ hirKind Skip` (the emptyStmt precedent тАФ an empty Block, ZERO C++); Pass-2's `pass2Post` arm runs the attribute scan on the bare statement for the unknown-name warning only (facts discarded тАФ no symbol). DSS emits no fallthrough-placement diagnostic to suppress, so `[[fallthrough]]` is semantics-neutral by construction (design F7); file-scope `[[x]];` stays a parse error (F7). WITNESSES: the runnable example `switch_fallthrough_attribute` (exit 11 iff the fall-through happens; debug + full-release-like) + parser pins (attributeDeclaration under declOrAttrStmt; attributed decl still commits varDecl) + `FallthroughStatementLowersToSkip` (HIR) + `FallthroughStatementParsesInSwitch`/`GnuFallthroughSpellingParses` (semantic) + `UnknownBareStatementAttrWarnsSuppressibly`. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (attributeDeclaration + declOrAttrStmt + the statement alt swap + the hirLowering Skip row тАФ config-only for the lowering); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the pass2Post bare-statement arm); `tests/corpus/c-subset/mini_calc.c.tree` (the F2 regen); [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-DEPRECATED-TYPES` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]], design F3).** `[[deprecated]]` on a TYPE тАФ a struct/union/enum TAG (`[[deprecated]] struct S {тАж};`) or a typedef тАФ is silently INERT: tags mint via `definesWhenChild` and their uses resolve via TYPE resolution (resolveTypeNodeImpl / the isTagReference arm), NOT the Pass-2 ordinary-namespace reference chokepoint the use-warning lives at; typedefDecl carries no specifier prefix at all. The declaration PARSES clean and the program compiles тАФ a missing WARNING on advice-tier semantics, never wrong bytes (honest-named silent-inert, the audit-sanctioned boundary). ADJACENT (code-audit M1): the unknown-attr warning ALSO misses DECLARATOR-LESS declarations (`[[frobnicate]] struct S {тАж};` тАФ the scan mint site sits inside the declarator-carrier branch, so a no-init-declarator-list decl is never scanned; GCC warns) тАФ the same conservative-miss class, closes with this row. A `[[deprecated]] struct S {тАж} v;` (WITH a declarator) DOES warn on uses of `v` (the object carries the flag per C23 6.7.13 appertainment). **Trigger:** a real TU deprecates a type and needs the use-sites flagged. | Thread the facts onto the TAG/typedef symbol (the fieldChildren/definesWhenChild mint path + typedefDecl) and emit at the TYPE-resolution use sites (resolveTypeNodeImpl's tag/alias arms) тАФ a distinct chokepoint from the ordinary-namespace one this cycle shipped. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the tag mint + type-resolution arms) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-PARAM-POSITION` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]], design F5).** A PARAMETER-position attribute (`void f([[maybe_unused]] int p)`) is a PARSE ERROR today тАФ the `param`/`declHeadForParam` grammar has no attribute surface. LOUD, never silent; and there is NO semantic hole behind it: params never warn unused in DSS (the `param` row does not opt into warnIfUnused тАФ pinned by `UnusedParamDoesNotWarn`), so the attribute would be a no-op even if parsed. **Trigger:** a real TU writes param-position attributes (common in C++-leaning codebases; zero uses in sqlite). | Add an optional attribute prefix to `declHeadForParam` (FIRST {AttributeKeyword, BracketOpen} disjoint from every param head lead тЖТ non-speculative) + strip it from the positional count (the specifierPrefix precedent); the semantics stay a no-op until params gain an unused warning. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (param/declHeadForParam) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NODISCARD-INDIRECT-DISCARD-CONTEXT` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]], the F1 boundary).** The nodiscard discard check is TWO-hop-EXACT (parent==expression, grandparent==exprStmt), so a discard reached through an INTERPOSED wrapper is MISSED: the parenthesized `(f());`, the comma operand `f(), g();`, the FOR-clause expression `for(;;f())` (code-audit M2) тАФ each discards the result without warning (GCC/Clang warn on both). A WARNING-only miss on advice-tier semantics тАФ never wrong bytes; the dominant bare `f();` form (the idiom) fires correctly, and the miss direction is conservative (no false positives). **Trigger:** conformance sweep (FC18) or a real TU depends on the wrapped-discard warning. | Generalize the discard context to a bounded parent-chain walk that skips discard-TRANSPARENT wrappers (parenExpr, the comma binaryExpr's LHS operand position) before the exprStmt test тАФ each skipped rule config-declared (a `discardTransparentRules` list), never engine-hardcoded. Priority: **normal** (trigger-gated). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the checkCall two-hop check) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NODISCARD-INDIRECT-CALLEE` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]).** The nodiscard check lives in checkCall's DIRECT-symbol tail тАФ a call through a FUNCTION POINTER (`fp();` where `int (*fp)(void)` points at a nodiscard function) or an expression callee (`(c ? f : g)();`) never consults the callee symbol's flag (the pointer OBJECT carries no nodiscard; the ternary has no single symbol). The isDirectNoreturnCall scope precedent тАФ same boundary, same conservative (no-false-positive) direction; a WARNING-only miss. C23 attaches nodiscard to the FUNCTION (and in C++ to types тАФ nodiscard TYPES are a distinct, larger feature). **Trigger:** a real TU discards through a fn-pointer and needs the warning. | Either thread nodiscard onto the function TYPE (an interner attribute тАФ heavy) or flag the fn-pointer OBJECT when its initializer is a nodiscard function (a symbol-level approximation). Priority: **low** (advice-tier, rare form). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (checkCall) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-GNU-BLOCK-SCOPE-UNKNOWN-NAME` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; the underlying behavior is PRE-EXISTING, FC4-era).** An UNKNOWN GNU attribute at BLOCK scope (`__attribute__((frobnicate)) int x = 1;` inside a function) is silently ignored WHOLESALE тАФ varDecl's `linkageSpecifierIgnoredRules` includes `attrSpec` (a block-scope decl carries no linkage, so the linkage scan skips the whole subtree), and the semantic attribute scan deliberately keeps unknown-name warnings to the C23 `[[...]]` form only (the GNU form's typo protection is the LINKAGE tier's, which doesn't run at block scope). File scope stays loud (H_UnknownLinkageSpecifier тАФ pinned). A missing WARNING (GCC warns `unknown attribute`), never wrong bytes. **Trigger:** conformance sweep or a user hits a silent block-scope GNU typo. | Emit a suppressible S_UnknownAttribute for an unmatched GNU clause when `emitUnknown` тАФ needs a decision on the vocabulary boundary first (GNU attribute space is vast: aligned/cleanup/format/тАж would all need `none` rows or a GNU-specific known-list before the warning is signal, not noise). Priority: **low**. | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`scanAttributeSemantics`'s fromStdForm gate) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-TYPE-POSITION` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; the underlying behavior is PRE-EXISTING).** A TYPE-position attribute тАФ after the type specifier (`int [[deprecated]] y;`), on a declarator (`int x [[deprecated]];`), or the after-keyword composite form (`struct [[gnu::packed]] S {тАж}` тАФ the separately-anchored [[D-CSUBSET-PACKED-AFTER-KEYWORD-POSITION]]) тАФ is a PARSE ERROR: the grammar admits attributes in the declaration-specifier PREFIX and the trailing composite slot only. LOUD (a positioned P-code), never a silent drop. C23 6.7.3.1 appertainment for these positions differs (type vs declarator vs tag) тАФ admitting them without the appertainment split would attach the wrong meaning. **Trigger:** a real TU writes type/declarator-position attributes. | Add the positions to the grammar WITH per-position appertainment routing (declarator-position тЖТ the declarator's symbol; type-position тЖТ the type, which needs [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-DEPRECATED-TYPES]] first). Priority: **low** (rare positions; the prefix form covers the idiom). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-ATTRIBUTE-SEMANTICS]]; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` |
| `D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c35) тАФ OPAQUE / forward-declared (incomplete) struct types, via REUSE of the c24 incomplete-composite machinery (no new type state).** ON the SQLite path тАФ opaque handles `sqlite3_stmt` / `sqlite3_blob` / тАж are exactly this pattern (`typedef struct sqlite3_stmt sqlite3_stmt;` at sqlite3.c:4649, used ONLY as `sqlite3_stmt *`); the prior "off the SQLite path" framing was wrong (sqlite3_stmt is a direct counterexample). ROOT (the c25-noted gap): a body-ABSENT named composite specifier never MINTED a tag тАФ c24 forward-mints only when a body opens an inner scope (`rec.structScope.valid()`), so an opaque-tag reference MISSED тЖТ `S_UnknownType`, and a bare `struct S;` was body-absent тЖТ `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` at HIR. FIX (3 edits, config-driven, agnostic): (1) the `resolveTypeNodeImpl` isTagReference arm, on a tag MISS, FORWARD-MINTS an INCOMPLETE composite (`forwardComposite(kind,name,declSiteKey)`) and binds it into the Tag namespace of the nearest enclosing namespace scope (`floatToNamespaceScope`) тАФ covers the opaque typedef base (`typedef struct S S;`), the opaque pointer base (`struct S *p`), and the bare `struct S;` head; the Tag binding deduplicates so repeat references resolve to ONE TypeId; driven by a NEW config field `references[].compositeKind` (struct/union mint, ENUM keeps the fail-loud miss тАФ value-typed, no incomplete representation). (2) the HIR declares-nothing sites (top-level + local) gain a `findForwardCompositeSpecifierIn` sibling: a body-absent NAMED composite specifier (a forward declaration) emits NOTHING instead of `S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing` (the tag was minted semantically); `int ;` / `int;` (no tag) stays loud. тШЕ EDIT 2 (tag-merge relaxation for forward-then-define) proved UNNECESSARY тАФ Pass-1's whole-tree PRE-ORDER definition-binding guarantees a same-TU definition's COMPLETE tag is bound BEFORE any reference resolves, so the isTagReference forward-mint only fires for a GENUINELY-never-defined tag; a forward-then-define (`struct S; struct S {тАж};`) resolves every reference to the definition's complete TypeId (no two-TypeId split, no collision) тАФ verified by a type-identity round-trip pin (opaque `S*` from one fn, dereferenced as the complete type elsewhere тЖТ 42). The `completeComposite` conflicting-re-completion fatal remains the backstop. тШЕтШЕ THE FAIL-LOUD AXIS IS PRESERVED (the no-silent-zero-size backstop): the c24 `computeLayout` incomplete guard (`type_layout.cpp` ~279) and its 5 loud consumers are UNTOUCHED тАФ a VALUE / by-value-member / sizeof of an incomplete still fails loud (a local `struct S v;` тЖТ MIR `allocaForLocal` H0009; a member `p->x` тЖТ S_NotAComposite; sizeof тЖТ no layout; a global `struct S g;` тЖТ the .bss data-producer fails loud; a by-value param/return тЖТ H0009). WITNESS: 6 semantic pins (the INVERTED `C35BareStructForwardDeclMintsIncompleteTag` [was `C25BareStructForwardDeclFailsLoud`, which asserted the pre-c35 S_UnknownType] + opaque-typedef-via-ptr + forward-then-define-completes-no-collision + member/sizeof-of-incomplete-fail-loud + two-definitions-still-collide) + 2 HIR forward-decl-lowers-to-nothing pins (top-level + local) + 4 MIR end-to-end pins (value/member/sizeof-of-incomplete fail loud + opaque-handle-via-ptr lowers clean) + runtime corpus `opaque_handle_ptr` (an opaque `struct Stmt;` never defined, passed by pointer through a non-inlined call, the real object a `struct Vdbe` reached via `void*` тАФ the Vdbe-behind-sqlite3_stmt* pattern тЖТ exit 42, 4 targets + release arm). 456тЖТ457 ctest. | (closed тАФ the 3 edits ARE the fix; EDIT 2 was a no-op given Pass-1 ordering) | p19 Cluster G c35; `D-CSUBSET-SELF-REFERENTIAL-STRUCT` (c24 тАФ the incomplete-composite substrate REUSED: forwardComposite/completeComposite/isIncompleteComposite + the computeLayout guard); `D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER` (c25 тАФ made `struct S;` parse as a body-absent ref); `src/core/types/semantic_config.hpp` (`ReferenceRule.compositeKind`); `src/core/types/grammar_schema_json.cpp` (load `compositeKind` on tag-ref rows); `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (6 tag-ref rows + compositeKind); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (the isTagReference forward-mint arm); `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` (`findForwardCompositeSpecifierIn` + the 2 declares-nothing sites); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-MUTABLE-POINTER-TO-CONST` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c36) тАФ object-const computed from the OUTERMOST pointer layer, not a coarse whole-decl scan.** ROOT (sqlite3.c, ~50├Ч e.g. `zFormat += 4` / `zDate++`): DSS wrongly rejected `const char *p; p += n` with `S_ConstViolation` тАФ `const char *p` is a MUTABLE pointer to const (C 6.7.3: const qualifies the POINTEE; only `char * const p` qualifies the pointer OBJECT). `rec.isConst` was set by `subtreeContainsToken(const)` over the WHOLE declaration, which saw the head/pointee const and wrongly marked the pointer object const. SAME bug c27 fixed for `volatile` тАФ the code comment even said "`isConst` keeps its scan", a deliberate c27 deferral now forced. FIX (single file, agnostic, NOT ┬зB): a new `declaratorObjectIsConst` helper (semantic_analyzer.cpp) тАФ descend the per-slot declarator to its inner declaratorRule, and if it has тЙе1 pointer layer, the object is const iff the LAST (outermost, source-order) pointer layer carries the const marker; with NO pointer layer the legacy whole-decl scan applies (scalar / typedef'd-pointer head const). Mirrors the c27 per-layer volatile walk in `declaratorDeclaredType`. Const stays a SYMBOL flag (NOT a type qualifier like c27's `VolatileQual`) because тАФ UNLIKE volatile тАФ const has no per-access codegen effect; the architect's Option-2 (a `ConstQual` lattice type) was rejected as a ~100-site surface buying nothing the helper doesn't. тШЕ CODEGEN NUANCE (the architect's "no codegen effect" claim was INCOMPLETE тАФ caught + verified): `isConst` DOES thread to `MirGlobal.isConst` тЖТ `.rodata`/`.data` global placement (asm.cpp). The fix only ever REMOVES false-const (a `const char *g` global moves .rodataтЖТ.data, the SAFE/writable direction тАФ placing a mutable pointer in read-only memory was a latent fault), NEVER the reverse; the string-literal `.rodata` override is unaffected. Sites B (anonymous field тАФ never an assignment LHS) + C (legacy positional тАФ no c-subset constMarker row reaches it) left coarse by design (documented in-code). WITNESS: 12 semantic pins (pointer-to-const param/east/multi-level/multi-declarator/const+volatile CLEAN; const-pointer / const-ptr-to-const / outer-const VIOLATE; east-const scalar still violates) + runtime corpus `const_ptr_walk` (a `const int *p` walks an array via `p++` тЖТ 42; RED-ON-DISABLE: pre-c36 `p++` was a spurious S_ConstViolation тЖТ would not compile; int ops dodge the deferred sub-32-bit ALU gap, p++ dodges the flagged p+n stride). 457тЖТ458 ctest. | (closed тАФ the helper + the Site-A swap IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c36; builds on c27 `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE` (the volatile twin + the per-layer walk) + c29 `D-CSUBSET-POST-STAR-CAST-QUALIFIER` (pointerLayer/ptrQualifier); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`declaratorObjectIsConst` + Site A); defers `D-CSUBSET-POINTEE-CONST-ENFORCEMENT` + `D-CSUBSET-GROUPED-DECLARATOR-CONST`; a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-POINTEE-CONST-ENFORCEMENT` | тП│ **DEFERRED (surfaced p19 Cluster G c36; the c36 audit broadened it).** The const-violation check (semantic_analyzer.cpp ~4404) only examines a PLAIN-IDENTIFIER LHS, so const enforcement is MISSING for every NON-plain-identifier const lvalue: (i) deref `const char *p; *p='x';` (pointee-const), (ii) member `struct S{const int v;}; s.v=2;`, (iii) array-subscript `const int a[2]; a[0]=5;`. All compile CLEAN today (a const GLOBAL-array element write would be a `.rodata` fault at runtime; a const local is silently wrong). c36 made the pointer OBJECT's const correct + did NOT touch this check (the const NAME `a`/`p`/member is correctly flagged тАФ `const int a[2]; a=b;`тЖТS0007 тАФ only the check's LHS coverage is narrow); these gaps are PRE-EXISTING, an independent axis, never a silent miscompile of c36's fix. NOT on the SQLite compile path (sqlite is const-correct). **Trigger:** a consumer that writes through a const pointer/member/element, or a desire for full const-correctness diagnostics. **Closing work:** (a) widen the const-violation check's LHS to deref/member/subscript lvalues; (b) for pointee-const specifically, model const in the type lattice (a transparent `ConstQual` mirroring/generalizing c27's `VolatileQual`) to know a deref target's const-ness тАФ a ┬зB type-system decision (separate `ConstQual` vs a unified cv-qualifier). Pins: `const char *p; *p=0;` / `const int a[2]; a[0]=5;` / `struct S{const int v;}; s.v=2;` тЖТ each emits S_ConstViolation. | (open) | p19 Cluster G c36; `D-CSUBSET-MUTABLE-POINTER-TO-CONST` (the object-const fix that scoped this out); `D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE` (c27 тАФ the VolatileQual machinery a ConstQual would mirror); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-GROUPED-DECLARATOR-CONST` | тП│ **DEFERRED (surfaced p19 Cluster G c36).** c36's `declaratorObjectIsConst` reads the outermost pointer layer that is a DIRECT child of the declarator; a GROUPED pointer declarator `char (* const p)` hides its layer inside the parenthesized group, so it falls through to the legacy whole-decl scan (STATUS QUO тАФ no regression; the coarse scan was used for ALL forms before c36). Consequence: a grouped-with-head-const form like `const char (*p)` stays an over-approximation (the head const is seen by the fallback тЖТ p wrongly marked const) тАФ IDENTICAL to pre-c36 behavior, never a silent miscompile (a LOUD false-positive S_ConstViolation on legal code, or a const global redundantly in .rodata). The common grouped case тАФ a function pointer `void (*fp)(int)` with NO const тАФ is correct (the fallback finds no const). NOT observed on the SQLite path. **Trigger:** a consumer using a grouped pointer declarator whose object-const matters. **Closing work:** descend the group (parenDeclarator) following the same recursion `directDeclaredType` uses, to find the TRUE outermost pointer layer. Pin: `const char (*p); p = 0;` тЖТ clean. | (open) | p19 Cluster G c36; `D-CSUBSET-MUTABLE-POINTER-TO-CONST` (the helper that scoped this out); plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-FUNCTION-DESIGNATOR-CAST` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c37) тАФ a function DESIGNATOR cast to a fn-ptr type.** ROOT (sqlite3.c, 29├Ч тАФ `#define SQLITE_DYNAMIC ((sqlite3_destructor_type)sqlite3RowSetClear)`, `(sqlite3_syscall_ptr)posixOpen`): casting a bare function name to a function-pointer type failed error[S0010] S_InvalidCast. C 6.3.2.1p4 + 6.3.2.3p8: a function designator decays to a pointer-to-function, and a fn-ptr may be cast to ANY fn-ptr type (cross-signature included тАФ calling THROUGH an incompatible type is UB, but the CAST is legal + value-preserving = the function's code address). ROOT: `isExplicitCastable` (type_rules.hpp) had NO arm for an `FnSig` OPERAND тЖТ `isExplicitCastable(Ptr<FnSig>, FnSig)` returned false (a function symbol's type is the raw `FnSig`; DSS decays per-consumption-site, not globally, and the CAST site lacked it тАФ the assignment/init/arg path ALREADY decays via `isAssignable`'s Ptr/FnSig arm, so `int(*p)(int)=g;` already worked). FIX (NOT ┬зB, ONE semantic arm, NO grammar/config/HIR/MIR change): add `if (tk==Ptr && ok==FnSig) return true;` to `isExplicitCastable`. The HIR `combineCast` already emits a Cast node for an FnSig operand + `mapCast(FnSig,Ptr)`тЖТBitcast over the GlobalAddr (present from c12 brace-init) тЖТ the cast lowers as a value-preserving NO-OP (the function's address); a call THROUGH the cast pointer reaches the function. тШЕ OVER-ADMISSION BOUNDARY (the audit surface, verified red-on-disable): the arm is tk==Ptr + ok==FnSig ONLY тЖТ struct/union/void OPERANDS (not FnSig) stay rejected, and a functionтЖТINTEGER target (`(long)g`, tk not Ptr) stays rejected. WITNESS: 4 semantic pins (same-sig + cross-sig fn-ptr cast ACCEPT; structтЖТfnptr + fnтЖТint still REJECT) + runtime corpus `fnptr_designator_cast` (`(fp_t)helper` then call-through тЖТ exit 42; red-on-disable: pre-c37 it would not compile). 458тЖТ459 ctest. | (closed тАФ the one cast arm IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c37; builds on c12 (the FnSigтЖТPtr mapCast Bitcast + the isAssignable Ptr/FnSig decay arm) + c26 `D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME` (the abstract fn-ptr cast TYPE); `src/analysis/semantic/type_rules.hpp` (`isExplicitCastable`); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |

ROOT SITE: `floatToNamespaceScope` (semantic_analyzer.cpp) STOPPED (`break`) at a composite-body scope (a `fieldChildren` scope), treating it as a namespace тАФ but C 6.2.1p4: a struct-body is a MEMBER namespace, NOT a tag namespace; a nested tag belongs to the nearest ENCLOSING block/file scope. FIX (NOT ┬зB; ONE line + comment; NO scope-model/HIR/MIR change): change the composite-body `break` to `scope = parent` (float a nested tag PAST a composite body, exactly as it already floats past a declarator-dominator scope) тАФ only a BLOCK or the file/CU root now stops the float. тШЕ INVARIANTS PRESERVED (architect-verified, plan-lock-confirmed): nominal identity (the c24 decl-site key is independent of the BIND scope тЖТ the by-name `struct Inner *` and the member `Outer.m` are the SAME TypeId); member composition (c25 `structScope = here`, not bindScope тЖТ `o->m.x` unbroken); pre-order forward-mint (Pass-1 binds the COMPLETE nested tag at file scope BEFORE any function-body reference resolves тЖТ no c35 forward-mint, no two-TypeId split); BLOCK-scope correctness (a block-LOCAL nested tag floats only to its BLOCK scope via the not-in-declByRule break тЖТ never leaks to file); SHADOWING (a nested tag same-name as a file-scope tag тЖТ both at file scope тЖТ S_RedeclaredSymbol, correct per C 6.7p3). WITNESS: 7 semantic pins (nested ref/value/union/deeply-nested ACCEPT; member-access REGRESSION guard; block-non-leak OVER-FLOAT guard; same-name shadowing COLLISION) + runtime corpus `nested_tag_scope_ptr` (the WalSegment shape тАФ a nested tag by-name pointer + arrow reads тЖТ exit 7; red-on-disable: pre-c38 it would not compile). 459тЖТ460 ctest. | (closed тАФ the one-line float fix IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c38; builds on c24 `D-CSUBSET-SELF-REFERENTIAL-STRUCT` (nominal decl-site identity) + c25 `D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER` (the structScope/define path) + c35 `D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION` (the forward-mint that masked the miss); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`floatToNamespaceScope`); a sqlite3.c re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |

NEGATIVES + the SDiv-not-UDiv proof, debug+release; result type ptrdiff_t in 9 contexts; NO regression to p++/p[i]/int-int/compare/deferred-p┬▒n; void*/incomplete fail loud) тАФ the 2 silent gaps it surfaced are PRE-EXISTING + outside the same-pointee scope. RESIDUALS (fail-loud, NOT sqlite, named): `(T*)0x1000` non-null int-to-pointer; the no-AddressOf decay form `arr + 3`. Was: DEFERRED (residual of c68, 2026-06-30; fail-loud, non-sqlite). An aggregate-global member that is a SYMBOL-base address constant with a (possibly non-zero) offset тАФ `&realArray[N]` (AddressOf(Index) on a REAL array global) or `&s.field` (AddressOf(MemberAccess)) тАФ fails LOUD (H_UnsupportedLoweringForKind) where gcc emits a reloc with the element/field-offset addend. c68's tryClassifyNullBaseIndexConst handles only a NULL base (тЖТ a bare integer, no reloc); a symbol base returns nullopt BY DESIGN (its value is symbol+N*stride, a reloc тАФ NOT the bare integer; must not be mis-folded, the c68 audit confirmed `&realArray[2]`тЖТfail-loud). Also an all-null `(void*)0`-ONLY aggregate or a non-null `(void*)0x1000` integer-to-pointer member (no AddressOf) still falls through. Fail-loud (safe), NOT a silent miscompile. NOT confirmed in sqlite. FIX (when needed): extend tryClassifyAsSymbolAddr / tryClassifyNullBaseIndexConst to AddressOf(Index(symbolBase,N)) / AddressOf(MemberAccess(symbolBase,field)) тЖТ {sym, N*stride or fieldOffset} (c67's encoder already threads the addend); a zero/non-null-ptr-aggregate path for the no-AddressOf cases. | (open) | p19 c68 residual; [[D-CSUBSET-AGGREGATE-GLOBAL-NONSYMBOL-PTR-MEMBER]] (c68, the null-base sibling it closed); overlaps c43 [[D-CSUBSET-ADDRESS-CONSTANT-GLOBAL-FOLD]] (the symbol-base address-fold); plan 23 full-C arc |

| `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INT-ARITHMETIC` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c41) тАФ `p ┬▒ n` (pointer ┬▒ integer) stride-scaling; a RUN-green MISCOMPILE FIX** (user: include miscompile fixes in the cycles; this CLOSES the c40-deferred p┬▒n a.k.a. `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INTEGER-ARITH-SCALING`). ROOT (the most pervasive flagged silent miscompile тАФ sqlite indexes pointers everywhere): binary `p + n` / `n + p` / `p - n` in `combineBinary` emitted a RAW Add/Sub with NO sizeof scaling (a 1-BYTE stride for ALL element types) тАФ commonArithType returns invalid for a Ptr+Int pair тЖТ the fallback typed it `Ptr<T>` (correct type) but MIR `mapBinaryOp(Add, Ptr)` emitted a raw byte add. char* (stride 1) correct; int*/struct* WRONG (off by sizeof). COMPILES (re-probe can't catch it) but miscompiles at RUN time. C 6.5.6p8: `p + n` = the n-th element address = p + n*sizeof(*p). (`n + p` was even mis-TYPED as Int.) FIX (NOT ┬зB; 3 sites; REUSES F1's scaleIndexToBytes/Gep тАФ the SAME stride machinery p[i]/p++ use): (1) cst_to_hir `combineBinary` тАФ CANONICALIZE `n + p` (swap so the Ptr operand is always kids[0]) + type the result Ptr; (2) hir_to_mir `combineBinaryOp` тАФ when opтИИ{Add,Sub} + result-kind Ptr + kids[0]-kind Ptr тЖТ scaleIndexToBytes(n, pointee) then Gep(p, byteOff); for Sub Neg(n) first тЖТ Gep(p, -(n*stride)); (3) semantic `subtreeType` combineBinary тАФ type `n + p` as Ptr (not Int). тШЕ MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE: c40's p-q fires on result-kind I64 (both-Ptr); c41 on result-kind Ptr (one-Ptr-one-int); F1's p[i]/p++ never reach combineBinaryOp (the Index path) тЖТ NO double-scaling. `n - p` (no C meaning) + void*/fn-ptr pointee тЖТ unhandled / fail-loud. WITNESS: 4 semantic pins (p+n/n+p/p-n typed Ptr; int+int unaffected) + runtime corpus `pointer_int_arith` (int* stride-4 + struct* stride-8, p+n/n+p/p-n/NEGATIVE-n тЖТ exit 42; red-on-disable: revert the Gep intercept тЖТ 1-byte stride тЖТ wrong slot тЖТ 1). The existing F1 p++/p[i] + c40 p-q MIR pins stay GREEN. 462тЖТ463 ctest. | (closed тАФ canonicalize + Gep stride scaling IS the fix) | p19 Cluster G c41; the run-green pointer-arith fix family (with c40 `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-SUBTRACTION`); reuses F1 scaleIndexToBytes/Gep; `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp`; a flagged miscompile fixed IN-loop (user); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-RUN-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-INDEX-NEGATIVE-WIDEN` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-28 (p19 Cluster G c42) тАФ a NEGATIVE array subscript `p[-n]` no longer segfaults; a RUN-green MISCOMPILE FIX** (user: include miscompile fixes in the cycles; surfaced by the c41 audit as a PRE-EXISTING crash one path over from c41's p┬▒n). ROOT (run-green-critical тАФ sqlite uses `p[-1]` lookback): the F1 Index path's Gep runtime index is a byte offset (a 32-bit `int` subscript, optionally scaled by a stride `Mul`) that reached the 64-bit `lea` / arm64-`ADD` address computation UN-widened тАФ a NEGATIVE 32-bit value (high bit set, e.g. -1*12 = 0xFFFFFFF4) was consumed with garbage upper-32-bits тЖТ a huge positive address тЖТ SIGSEGV. The SAME 32-bit-negative-zero-extend bug c41 fixed for p┬▒n, but in the Index path (`combineIndexAddr` / `scaleIndexToBytes` feed). тШЕ DEBUG-ONLY crash: in release ConstFold collapses a constant `Mul(-1,stride)` to a Const that takes the `lowerGep` constant-disp32 path (hardware sign-extends disp32) тЖТ masks it; the BASELINE/DEBUG pipeline is the witness. (`*(p-1)` worked тАФ a pointer-arith Deref, a DISTINCT path; only `p[-1]` crashed.) FIX (NOT ┬зB; ONE function `lowerGep`, LIR tier; NO HIR/MIR/grammar/config change): in the runtime-index branch (AFTER the constant-disp guard), inspect `interner.kind(mir.instType(operands[1]))` and widen a sub-64-bit index to a full 64-bit register BEFORE the lea тАФ SExt for a signed source (Char/I8/I16/I32), ZExt for unsigned (U8/U16/U32), NO widen for I64/U64/Ptr (c41's pre-widened p┬▒n offset тЖТ no double-extend) or enum/other (unchanged behaviour). The kind lists MIRROR the SExt/ZExt source-width dispatch gates in the same file тЖТ every synthesized widen is one those gates accept; the width flag is `widthFlagsForType(idxTy)` (the SOURCE width). тШЕтШЕ WHY LIR not MIR (the architect's re-architecture): a FIRST attempt widened in `scaleIndexToBytes` (MIR) and REGRESSED register pressure тАФ `deep_lvalue_chain` (150-deep, all-Const(0) indices) blew the regalloc scratch pool because a MIR SExt added an address-spanning vreg per index. The LIR fix sits AFTER the constant-disp guard, so deep_lvalue_chain's Const(0) indices take the disp path and NEVER reach the widen (no added vregs); a runtime-index widen vreg is short-lived (consumed by the next lea) + coalesceable. WITNESS: runtime corpus `index_negative` (strides 1/4/12 тАФ char*/int*/struct-Trip*; CONSTANT `p[-1]`/`p[-4]` [debug: a runtime stride Mul тЖТ the crash] AND RUNTIME `int k=-3; p[k]`; 8 sub-cases тЖТ exit 42; RED-ON-DISABLE [PROVEN]: revert the lowerGep widen + rebuild тЖТ 0xC0000005 ACCESS_VIOLATION on the first runtime-Mul subscript in debug). deep_lvalue_chain + c40 ptr_subtraction + c41 pointer_int_arith stay GREEN. 463тЖТ464 ctest. тШЕ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (87 probes, debug+release, x86): the CORE widen VERIFIED CORRECT + regression-free тАФ every stride/sign/width/expression-form/enum index reads the right element, no-regression paths (positive p[i], multi-dim, `*(p-1)`, member access) intact, and the register-pressure hypothesis CONFIRMED by a causation bisect (the debug n=11 regalloc cliff is PRE-EXISTING тАФ identical for `int` [widened] vs `long` [not] indices тЖТ the widen does not shift it). тШЕтШЕ THE AUDIT CAUGHT AN OVER-ATTESTATION (fixed before commit): the corpus claimed all-4-targets/release but arm64-RELEASE does NOT compile тАФ ConstFold folds the negative subscript to a NEGATIVE-displacement lea, which arm64 cannot encode (its `lea` has only non-negative ADD-imm12 variants тАФ a SEPARATE PRE-EXISTING gap, proven c42-independent: the constant-disp path that c42 never touches fails identically). FIXED HONESTLY: the corpus now runs BASELINE-only on all 4 targets (the bug is debug-only; the runtime variable-subscript cases exercise the widen via the register path incl. arm64 SXTW under qemu) with NO optimizedPipelines arm (a global ConstFold arm would emit the un-encodable arm64 negative-disp lea on a qemu-CI runner); the arm64 negative-disp lea is DEFERRED `D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA` (c43 тАФ run-green-critical for sqlite arm64-release). Comments corrected (the audit's minor flags): a subscript integer-PROMOTES (C 6.3.1.1) so only the I32-SExt / U32-ZExt / 64-bit-noop arms fire тАФ the narrow Char/I8/I16/U8/U16 arms are DEFENSIVE (mirror the dispatch gates), and an enum index тЖТ I32 тЖТ SExt (NOT the `default` arm the old comment claimed). | (closed тАФ the one-function LIR index widen + the honest baseline-only corpus IS the fix; arm64-release neg-disp lea split to c43) | p19 Cluster G c42; the run-green pointer-arith fix family (c40 p-q `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-SUBTRACTION` + c41 p┬▒n `D-CSUBSET-POINTER-INT-ARITHMETIC` тАФ c42 is the Index-path sibling of c41's I64 widen); reuses the SExt/ZExt LIR dispatch gates; `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` (`lowerGep` runtime-index branch); a flagged miscompile fixed IN-loop (user); plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-RUN-green |

A POSITIVE disp (тЙд4095) still takes the single-word ADD-imm12 form (guard is `< 0` ONLY); x86 keeps its single-instruction `lea [base+disp32]` (gate true there тЖТ no fold; objdump-verified). Base survives into Rn; the materialized value is a DISTINCT fresh Rm тЖТ no clobber. тШЕтШЕ THE DECISIVE CLEAR: sqlite arm64 2-TU 17 lea errors тЖТ 0, 9 aborted funcs тЖТ 0, ELF PRODUCED, no new frontier тЖТ тШЕтШЕтШЕ SQLITE EXECUTES SQL ON ARM64: the qemu-aarch64 smoke = 42 == aarch64-gcc, `--version` 3.54.0 byte-identical, JOIN/CTE/typeof all == gcc. | `D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA-NATIVE-SUB` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c94) тАФ the arm64 negative-const-disp `lea` now encodes as a NATIVE single-instruction `SUB Xd,Xn,#|disp|` (config + shared matcher + encoder), the c93 base+index FOLD is REMOVED, and the residual DEAD const materialization is GONE (sign-agnostically, for the positive member-offset `ADD` too).** c93 unblocked `p[-1]`/`z[-1]` via a B-gated LIR fold that materialized the negative disp into a fresh GPR (MOVZ/MOVK ladder) + emitted a 4-op base+index `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm` тАФ CORRECT but 5-7 arm64 instructions incl. a 2-insn DEAD const. c94 replaces it with the native SUB, mirroring the positive ADD-imm12 / shifted / MOVZ-MOVK `lea` ladder with the op-bit SET. **(1) The matcher (shared substrate, AGNOSTIC):** a NEW config-driven SIGN axis `negMemoffset` (bool, `guard.negMemoffset`) on `TargetEncodingVariant` тАФ a THIRD routing axis beside width + imm-range. `walker_util.hpp` gains `variantNegMagnitude` (the |value| of a STRICTLY-NEGATIVE value-bearing operand, `-(int64)v` so INT32_MIN widens cleanly); `variantMatchesInst` reads the negative half when `negMemoffset` is set, the non-negative half (existing `variantImmMagnitude`) otherwise тАФ the two axes PARTITION the signed value line. x86's disp32 lea slot has no immMin/immMax AND no negMemoffset тЗТ its match-any slot swallows BOTH signs in ONE `lea [base┬▒disp32]` тАФ COMPLETELY UNCHANGED (byte-pinned, see witnesses). `validate()` gains negMemoffset as a disambiguator (variants differing in negMemoffset route on disjoint sign domains, never a shadow) + a coherence reject (negMemoffset needs a `memoffset` operand). **(2) The config:** 3 arm64 `lea` negMemoffset variants тАФ single-word `SUB Xd,Xn,#|disp|` (0xD1000000, |disp|тЙд4095), shifted word-pair `[0xD1000000, 0xD1400000]` (|disp| 4096..0xFFFFFF), and MOVZ/MOVK + EXTENDED-register `SUB Xd,Xn,Xd` 3-word `[0xD2800000, 0xF2A00000, 0xCB206000]` (|disp| >16MiB, Rn WIRED to the base not baked-sp). All SUB encodings re-derived from the ARM ARM (add/subtract-immediate + extended-register) + verified vs the existing `sub` opcode's fixedWords + aarch64-gcc objdump. **(3) The encoder (fixed32.cpp):** the MemOffset arm computes `effectiveDisp` = |disp| when `selected->negMemoffset` (fail-loud on a non-negative disp on a SUB variant тАФ the sign matcher should have excluded it) and feeds the SAME Imm12 / Imm12HiLo24 / Imm32MovzMovk slots (no new EncodingSlotKind тАФ the bit-windows are identical; the SUB base word makes it a subtract). **(4) The c93 fold REMOVED:** `lowerGep`'s const-disp arm emits the plain 3-op `lea [base + MemOffset(disp)]` for EVERY disp on EVERY target; `targetHasSignedDispLea()` DELETED (unused). x86 takes its signed disp32, arm64 the native SUB тАФ target-blind. **(5) The DEAD const eliminated (sign-agnostic):** a NEW `foldedConstDisps_` set + `constDispFoldsIntoGep` predicate (mirroring the `foldedGlobalAddrs_` fold-skip precedent) тАФ a single-use integer Const whose sole user is a 2-operand const-disp Gep (the disp operand, fits int32) is NOT materialized by `lowerConst` (`lowerGep` reads it via `constIntegerValue`, never `regForValue`; a stray `regForValue` fails loud on the undefined vreg). This removed the pre-existing dead-const on BOTH the negative `SUB` AND the positive member-offset `ADD` (objdump-confirmed: `mov #0xFFF..` count 0 in the arm64 release binary; `mov #0x4` gone before the positive `add x28,x28,#0x4`). WITNESSES: 4 NEW arm64 byte-pins (`Arm64Encoder.NegativeDispLea{EncodesNativeSubImm12, AtImm12BoundaryEncodesSub, WiderThanImm12SplitsToShiftedSub, Beyond16MiBSplitsToMovzMovkSub}` тАФ exact bytes 22 60 00 D1 / 60 FC 3F D1 / 22 70 32 D1 + 42 20 40 D1 / 02 00 88 D2 + 62 24 A0 F2 + 22 60 22 CB) + 1 NEW x86 agnosticism byte-pin (`X86VariableEncoder.NegativeDispLeaSingleInstructionUnchanged` тАФ 48 8D 83 F8 FF FF FF, the c93 baseline byte-for-byte) + the `index_negative` corpus (12 native SUB sites objdumped on arm64 release, ZERO dead const, exit 42 on x86_64-linux + qemu-arm64 across debug + full-release-like + release). RED-ON-DISABLE (witnessed): strip the 3 negMemoffset variants тЖТ the negative-disp `lea` fails `A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant opcode 'lea' width 64` + `A_FunctionEncodeAborted` (the c93 fold is GONE, so no fallback тАФ native SUB or bust). 549/549 Win Debug + 549/549 WSL ctest (QEMU_LD_PREFIX). AGNOSTICISM: the matcher change is 100% config-driven (a bool guard axis + a magnitude helper), zero `if(arch)`; x86 negative disp32 is BYTE-UNCHANGED (pinned). F1 dead-const: GONE. тШЕ 2 LOW audit notes (aaddb688, no fix required): (a) `foldedConstDisps_` is WRITE-ONLY dead state тАФ the fail-loud is actually provided by `regForValue`s existing undefined-vreg check, not the set (unlike the consumed `foldedGlobalAddrs_`); remove the set or correct its comment; (b) the anchor NAME understates scope тАФ the const-disp fold-skip is GENERAL (positive member-offsets, ALL targets, ALL opt levels incl. debug), not just arm64 negative lea. | (closed) | p19 Cluster G c94; replaces [[D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA]] (c93 тАФ the fold) natively; siblings [[D-CSUBSET-INDEX-NEGATIVE-WIDEN]] (c42) + [[D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-WIDE-IMMEDIATE]] (shifted-imm precedent) + [[D-LIR-GLOBALADDR-LOAD-RIPREL-FOLD]] (the foldedGlobalAddrs_ fold-skip precedent constDispFoldsIntoGep mirrors); `src/asm/format/walker_util.hpp` (variantNegMagnitude + the sign axis) + `src/asm/format/fixed32.cpp` (effectiveDisp) + `src/core/types/target_schema.{hpp,cpp,_json.cpp}` (negMemoffset field/parse/validate) + `src/dss-config/targets/arm64.target.json` (3 lea SUB variants) + `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` (fold removed, targetHasSignedDispLea deleted, foldedConstDisps_) + `tests/asm/test_asm_{arm64,x86_variable}.cpp` + `examples/c-subset/index_negative`; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green RUN phase |

| `D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-CAST-SCOPE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c99) тАФ RESOLVED-BY-NON-REPRODUCTION + a regression pin.** The c94 audit aaddb688 saw **98├Ч `error[P9006]` in a transient sqlite-master shell.c** at `span64` (`return (*(sqlite3_uint64*)&a) - тАж`) тАФ the parser read the pointer-cast as a multiply. c99 was scoped to fix it, so I INSTRUMENTED the root cause on the CURRENT master (sqlite `a20de6d683`, span64 present at `shell.c:8048`): **it does NOT reproduce.** Empirical (both x86_64 + arm64, the c98 binaries): a LOCAL-typedef `*(myu64*)&a`, a same-TU `sqlite3_int64/uint64` span64 body, a typedef-in-a-`#include`d-header cast (== the shell.c shape), real shell.c ALONE (0 parse errors; 148 K_SymbolUndefined = the sqlite3.c API, expected), AND the real 2-TU compile all parse the cast CLEAN. WHERE the typedef-ness lives: the parser's `BinderSketch` (`src/analysis/syntactic/binder_sketch.cpp`) records `sqlite3_uint64` as a **Type** binding when it parses `typedef sqlite_uint64 sqlite3_uint64;` (sqlite3.h:308, reached via shell.c:160 `#include "sqlite3.h"`); at the cast site (`typeNameCommitApproved_`, parser.cpp:900-901) `sketch.lookup("sqlite3_uint64")==Type` COMMITS the cast тАФ the `#include`d typedef reaches the disambiguation table correctly, ~7900 lines later. The 98├Ч was a use-BEFORE-visible-typedef ORDERING ARTIFACT of a since-superseded master amalgamation (the current master puts the typedef before span64); NOT a DSS parser gap. **REGRESSION PIN:** `examples/c-subset/cast_deref_through_typedef_pointer` тАФ the exact `*(myu64*)&a` cast-deref (a type-pun round-trip тЖТ 42) on x86_64-linux + qemu-arm64; a future master ordering that regresses the in-scope cast-deref goes red. The genuine sqlite-green blocker c99 actually cleared was a SIBLING (a FAM-in-union, [[D-CSUBSET-FAM-IN-UNION-MEMBER]]), not this. | (closed тАФ non-reproducing + pinned) | p19 Cluster G c99 (instrumented, non-reproducing on `a20de6d683`); sibling that DID block: [[D-CSUBSET-FAM-IN-UNION-MEMBER]]; `examples/c-subset/cast_deref_through_typedef_pointer`; `src/analysis/syntactic/binder_sketch.cpp` + `parser.cpp:836` typeNameCommitApproved_; plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-FAM-IN-UNION-MEMBER` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c99; NOT ┬зB тАФ C99 + gcc/clang give one answer) тАФ a flexible-array-member-bearing struct is a legal DIRECT member of a UNION.** ROOT (the c99 re-probe's live blocker, the 3├Ч `error[S001D]` = `S_FlexibleArrayInAggregate` in **sqlite3.c**, NOT shell.c): sqlite's `union { SrcList sSrc; u8 srcSpace[SZ_SRCLIST_1]; } uSrc;` (sqlite3.c:111815/158387/165978 тАФ a stack-allocated slab that backs a "fake" one-entry SrcList; `SrcList` = `struct{int nSrc; u32 nAlloc; SrcItem a[FLEXARRAY];}`, a true C99 FAM struct). The FC6 embedded-FAM check (`semantic_analyzer.cpp:3600`, the composite-member composition loop) rejected a FAM-struct member of a struct OR a union тАФ but C99 ┬з6.7.2.1p18 forbids it only as a member of a STRUCTURE or an ELEMENT OF AN ARRAY, saying nothing about a union; gcc/clang ACCEPT the union form CLEANLY (verified: `-std=c11 -pedantic -Wall -Wextra` тЖТ 0 warnings). FIX (NOT ┬зB; narrow, target-agnostic): the `typeContainsFlexibleArray` branch (semantic_analyzer.cpp:3600) gates the S001D emit on `permittedAsUnionMember = (ck==Union)` тАФ a FAM-struct member of a UNION is permitted; a FAM-struct as a STRUCT member (ck Struct) STAYS fail-loud AT this branch. (The independent audit + review established that `ft` reaching this branch is ALWAYS a FAM-struct тАФ `typeContainsFlexibleArray` doesn't recurse into unions, and an array-of-FAM member is InvalidType'd UPSTREAM at applyArraySuffix ~1630/1972 тАФ so the once-proposed `&& in.kind(ft)==Struct` was a TAUTOLOGY that would additionally OVER-REJECT the p18-legal `union{union V v}` under any future recursion change; DROPPED. The gate is purely union-vs-struct.) An array-of-FAM-struct union member STAYS fail-loud upstream (still S001D тАФ p18 "element of an array"); the nested `union{union V v}` form is ACCEPTED (gcc-parity, audit-verified). Matches gcc's pedantic-warn/reject posture + DSS's pre-existing FC6 stance. LAYOUT (no change needed тАФ the real risk, verified clean): `computeLayout`'s Union arm already sizes to `max(member sizes)` and `computeLayout(FAM-struct)` already returns the non-flexible PREFIX size with `hasFlexibleArrayMember=true` (the FAM tail is 0-length for sizeof), so `sizeof(union{SrcList; u8[N]})==max(prefix,N)` тАФ matches gcc (`sizeof(SrcList)==4`, `sizeof(union)==16`). тШЕ THE RE-PROBE (2-TU sqlite, current master `a20de6d683`): the 3├Ч S001D CLEARED тЖТ **x86_64:elf64 + arm64:elf64 compile the `sqlite3` binary CLEAN (0 errors), and RUN SQL: `SELECT sumтЖТ42` + `--versionтЖТ3.54.0` on x86_64-native + qemu-arm64** тАФ the chase-master run-green legs are restored. (pe64 surfaces the NEXT frontier тАФ os_win kernel32 `localtime`/`InterlockedCompareExchange` S0001├Ч50 + `time.h` F001D + cascade, the [[D-FFI-WINDOWS-KERNEL32-FUNCTIONS]] continuation, classified not fixed = c100.) WITNESS: `examples/c-subset/fam_struct_in_union_member` (the `union{struct{int n; T a[];}; char[K];}` written+read through both members тЖТ 42 on x86_64-linux + qemu-arm64; RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the carve-out тЖТ the exact `S001D got struct Slab s;`) + 4 semantic pins (union-member accepted [genuine red-on-disable for the ck==Union gate] / struct-member rejected [genuine red-on-disable, emitted AT the branch] / array-of-FAM-in-union rejected [UPSTREAM posture guard, not a carve-out guard] / nested union-of-union accepted [gcc-parity boundary lock]) + a TypeLayout companion pin (union sizes to max-of-prefix тАФ layout unchanged by the diff, guards a silent union-sizing miscompile of the newly-reachable shape) + the runnable examples given the `release`/shippedPipeline optimizer arm. | (closed тАФ union direct-member carve-out) | p19 Cluster G c99; the sibling the c99 task was mis-scoped to ([[D-CSUBSET-TYPEDEF-CAST-SCOPE]], non-reproducing); `semantic_analyzer.cpp:3600` + `type_layout.cpp` Union arm; `examples/c-subset/fam_struct_in_union_member`; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-TEST-CONFIG-DISCOVERY-OUT-OF-TREE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-04 (p19, config-discovery hardening; user-directed after the c99 gate exposed it) тАФ shipped-config discovery now honours a `DSS_CONFIG_ROOT` env override so OUT-OF-TREE / CI builds resolve `src/dss-config/` regardless of launch cwd.** ROOT: `findShippedConfig` (config_path_walk.cpp) resolved config by walking UP from `fs::current_path()` for `<ancestor>/src/dss-config/`; fine for an IN-TREE build (ctest's test cwd is a build subdir inside the repo) but an OUT-OF-TREE build's test cwd has NO `src/dss-config` in its ancestry тЖТ loadShipped returns null тЖТ tests null-deref ("segfault at 0x48") / abort. This is what made a fresh out-of-tree WSL ctest red (~40 backend tests) тАФ MISDIAGNOSED as heap corruption until ASan/gdb/isolation/serial-loop ALL passed + memory was 30GB-free; the real cause was config-discovery (symlink + in-tree both prove it). FIX (agnostic, production-unchanged-when-unset): `findShippedConfig` consults `$DSS_CONFIG_ROOT` (a dir CONTAINING `src/dss-config`, checked BEFORE the walk, falls THROUGH on miss; the path-like-name rejection still gates `loc.name` FIRST so it is not a `../` vector) + `dss_add_test` sets `ENVIRONMENT DSS_CONFIG_ROOT=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}` on every test. Unset (production) = EXACTLY the historical cwd-walk. WITNESS: `tests/core/test_config_path_walk.cpp` тАФ EnvRootResolvesConfigOutsideCwdAncestry (RED-ON-DISABLE, verified out-of-tree: a synth target under a TEMP dir the walk can never reach resolves only via the env) + traversal-still-rejected + set-but-miss-falls-through. AUDIT SOUND (a22c1ed7 + threading sub-agent a8ac1da0: agnostic, traversal-safe, portable MSVC+GCC, no runtime env writes so getenv is race-free, regression-free). | (closed тАФ config-discovery env override) | p19 config-discovery hardening (exposed by the c99 out-of-tree gate); `src/core/types/config_path_walk.{cpp,hpp}` + `tests/CMakeLists.txt`; broader residual = [[D-TEST-OUT-OF-TREE-CWD-DEPENDENCIES]]; plan 00 test-infra |

| `D-FFI-WINDOWS-OS-WIN-FLIP` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-03 (p19 Cluster G c95) тАФ the Windows-host FLIP-AND-UNBLOCK: predefine `_WIN32` so sqlite selects its native os_win.c VFS (Fork B, user ┬зB), erase `__stdcall`, resolve `#include "windows.h"`, and ship a windows.json descriptor SKELETON тЖТ os_win.c PARSES for pe64.** Probe a1a476db found DSS predefined NO OS macro тЖТ sqlite picked os_unix on pe тЖТ the c94 F001D POSIX wall; user chose Fork B (native os_win). FIX (8 files, config/format-driven, ZERO if(arch/os/format) in engine): (1) **per-format predefined macros** тАФ `PredefinedMacroDef` gains an optional `availableObjectFormats` filter (preprocess_config.hpp + grammar_schema_json decode/fail-loud-validate via objectFormatKindFromName + the preprocessor per-format SEED), the SAME `ffi::objectFormatInAvailabilitySet` set-membership the shipped-header gate uses; `_WIN32`/`_WIN64`=1 pe-gated in c-subset.lang.json. (2) **тШЕ PREREQUISITE FIX** тАФ `MacroExpander::isDefined()` + the SynthBuilder pre-scan `definedCb` consulted ONLY the `#define` table not `predefined_`, so `#if defined(X)` was FALSE for EVERY predefined macro (value-context worked, defined()-context didn't тЖТ they DISAGREED; without this, `_WIN32` seeds but sqlite's `#if defined(_WIN32)` still picks os_unix). Local/normal builds pass because hosts happen to wrap тАФ ONLY a sanitizer sees it (why the 3-leg gate stayed green while CI reddened). AUDIT of the whole shared header found exactly 4 sites (Neg:113 + Add/Sub/Mul:149-151); everything else already safe (Div/Rem REFUSE INT64_MIN/-1 explicitly citing this UBSan leg; Shl computes via uint64; Shr/BitNot defined; casts range-guarded). FIX: `wrapNegI64/wrapAddI64/wrapSubI64/wrapMulI64` helpers тАФ evaluate over uint64 (defined for every input), value-cast back (modular, defined since C++20; the `wrapToIntTarget` precedent). POLICY (from the file's own Div-contrast rationale + gcc parity): the WRAPPED value is the correct fold for Neg/Add/Sub/Mul тАФ it is the runtime outcome on every shipped target (x86_64+arm64 wrap) AND gcc/clang's `#if` fold, so folding is behavior-preserving; Div/Rem INT64_MIN/-1 STAY refused (x86 idiv #DE TRAPS at runtime тАФ folding would hide it). SINGLE CHOKEPOINT by construction: all three walkers (const_eval.cpp, cst_const_eval.cpp, pp_if_eval.cpp) route Neg/Add/Sub/Mul through these shared helpers тАФ grep-verified zero direct signed folds outside them. A non-preprocess language with pending defines fails loud (`D_DefineRequiresPreprocess` 0xD012). FIX (multi-site rule: funnel through ONE chokepoint): NEW shared per-inst predicate `mirInstClobbersLoadPtr` (mir_alias.hpp) used by the region walker AND the cse.cpp slices тАФ Store stays PRECISE (mirMayAlias), any other op in the NEW `opcodeClobbersMemory` positive list (mir_opcode.hpp, the isCommutative convention: Store/Call/IntrinsicCall/AtomicCas/CompilerBarrier) is an OPAQUE clobber. тШЕ REVIEW-CAUGHT CORRECTION (the fold's own critical): the first draft keyed the arm on `hasSideEffects` тАФ but that is a DCE-LIVENESS flag, TRUE for every TERMINATOR (Br/CondBr/Return/тАж) + Alloca + the Va* leaves; since `blockInstAt` iteration INCLUDES the terminator and every loop body ends in one, that draft disabled ALL LICM Load-hoisting (the pre-existing `Licm.LoadHoistedAcrossDistinctPrimitiveStoreтАж` pin reds) and blocked all cross-block Load CSE. The corrected key = the dedicated memory-clobber classification, with the negative-control pin asserting a Return terminator is NOT a clobber. DESCRIPTOR: `shippedLibs/intrin.json` `availableObjectFormats:["pe"]` (a Microsoft compiler-intrinsic header; elf/macho reject fail-loud), NON-empty via the honest `size_t`тЖТu64 typedef (MSVC's real intrin.h makes size_t visible; string/stdio.json convention). тШЕ EMPIRICAL TRAP RE-CONFIRMED: a draft declared `_byteswap_*` as msvcrt `symbols` тЖТ **msvcrt.dll exports NO _byteswap_*** (objdump -p, positive control memcpy/_msize/puts) тЖТ the eager-import crashed the loader 0xC0000139 STATUS_ENTRYPOINT_NOT_FOUND end-to-end (the windows.json InterlockedCompareExchange lesson, now twice-proven). DSS's FLAT-SWITCH model (lowerSwitch + the pumpSwitch driver grouped only TOP-LEVEL switchBodyItems into disjoint CaseArms; MIR minted one block per arm) could not represent a jump target inside another arm's body тЖТ the nested CaseStmt reached the general statement dispatch (cst_to_hir.cpp:3297) and failed loud. ┬зB RESOLVED by the USER тЖТ approach (I): flatten the switch body to ONE CFG, case/default = labels at any depth. PRESERVED: label-before-case ([[D-CSUBSET-LABEL-BEFORE-CASE]]), adjacent cases, fall-through (now straight-line), breakтЖТjoin, default-anywhere; S0023 STILL fires for a case genuinely OUTSIDE any switch (switchBodyDepth_ gating). `pathTerminates(SwitchStmt)` is now strictly more accurate (models fall-through). WITNESS: corpus `switch_nested_case` (the VDBE shared-tail `case 1:{int t;тАжgoto shared;} case 2:тАж; shared:тАж` + a case inside a deeper if-block `case 3:{ if(k>0){ case 4:тАж } }`; classify(2)+classify(3)+classify(1)-8 = 32+6+12-8 = 42; RED-ON-DISABLE тЖТ S0023 on the nested case 2/case 4). тШЕтШЕ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (the c57 lesson, all 3 RUN legs): x86_64-elf native + arm64-qemu both RAN to 42, every value gcc-cross-checked; the nested-case jump-into-block (dispatch DIRECTLY to a case past a decl inside an if-block тЖТ correct value), fall-through into/out of nested blocks, 8 edge cases (default-only/no-default/single/default-not-last/enum+char values/empty-body/Duff's-device/inner-loop-break-vs-switch-break), ordinal-collision + nested-switch-isolation + discriminant-once тАФ ALL correct on both legs; no dead blocks in the final binary. тШЕ c60 clears the 7 S0023 ONLY тАФ H0002 (continue-in-switch) is PRE-EXISTING and NOT cleared (a separate later cycle; the audit proved c59 has the identical H0002). | (closed тАФ the flat-CFG switch lowering: HIR shape + oracle + driver + 2 MIR forms) | p19 Cluster G c60; ┬зB approach-I USER-chosen; the c59 re-probe's S0023 frontier; reuses the goto/label infra (makeLabelStmt + getOrCreateLabelBlock) + addSwitch; `src/hir/hir_node.hpp` + `hir.{cpp,hpp}` + `hir_text.cpp` + `hir_verifier.hpp` + `src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp`; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |

WITNESS: 6 corpora (switch_vdbe_dispatch 350-dense=sqlite's shape [.data=2800=350*8], switch_jump_table_boundaries [first/last/GAP->default/below/above/negative-min], switch_wide_bound, switch_over_255_cases 300, switch_sparse_immediate, switch_unsigned_dense) тЖТ 42 all 4 targets gcc-cross-checked. тШЕтШЕ INDEPENDENT AUDIT SOUND (the WRONG-CASE hunt, the control-flow analog of c69's wrong-slot): every discrim below-min->above-max for dense/gappy/negative/unsigned CHECKSUMMED vs gcc across 6 legs тЖТ ALL AGREE; objdump both arches = slot j->case(min+j), gaps->default, idx=discrim-min, NO off-by-one (VDBE slot0->case0 slot349->case349); Win 500/500 + WSL/qemu 315/315; red-on-disable reproduces L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc. | `D-MIR-VERIFIER-UNBOUNDED-OPERAND-SENTINEL` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c70) тАФ the MIR verifier honors the variadic-operand sentinel (`maxOperands == kMirUnboundedOperands == 0xFF`) instead of reading it as a literal max of 255.** ROOT (the c69 re-probe's FIRST error, after sqlite3.c finally compiled end-to-end): `I_VerifierFailure` "opcode switch operand count 349 outside [1, 255]" тАФ the c60 switch-flatten produces ONE MIR `Switch` opcode whose operands are `[discriminant, caseValue*N]`; sqlite's ~348-case VDBE dispatch (`sqlite3VdbeExec`) = 349 operands. `MirOpcode::Switch` (and `IndirectBr`) declare `maxOperands == kMirUnboundedOperands` (0xFF) тАФ genuinely variadic тАФ and the BUILDER (`mir.cpp` addInst) has always exempted that sentinel from the upper-bound check (`maxOperands != kMirUnboundedOperands && count > maxOperands`), so it accepted the 349-operand Switch. But `MirVerifier::checkStructuralInvariants` did a plain `operands.size() > info.maxOperands`, reading the 0xFF sentinel as a literal 255 тЖТ it REJECTED the Switch the builder had already built. FIX (1 edit, `src/mir/mir_verifier.cpp`): mirror the builder's exemption тАФ `bool const overMax = info.maxOperands != kMirUnboundedOperands && operands.size() > info.maxOperands;` тАФ so a variadic-operand opcode is not upper-bounded. WITNESS: corpus `switch_over_255_cases` (a 300-case switch тЖТ 301 operands > 255; classify(k)=1000+k for k in [0,299] else 7 тЖТ 42; RED-ON-DISABLE тЖТ I_VerifierFailure "operand count 301 outside [1, 255]"). | `D-CSUBSET-CONTINUE-SKIPS-SWITCH` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c61) тАФ `continue` inside a `switch` inside a loop targets the enclosing LOOP, not the switch (C 6.8.6.2).** ROOT (the c60 re-probe's FIRST error, H0002 H_InvalidBreak ├Ч2): sqlite `sqlite3VXPrintf` (sqlite3.c:33119/33311) тАФ a format-char loop whose body `switch`es on the conversion type, with `continue` inside the switch to advance the format string тЖТ "continue resolves to a switch; continue can only target a loop". `isBranchTargetKind` (hir_node.hpp:163) counts a SwitchStmt as a branch target, and a `continue`'s depth-0 resolution (makeContinue(0) тАФ C has no labeled continue) landed on the innermost switch тЖТ the verifier (hir_verifier.cpp:193) rejected it. PRE-EXISTING (the c60 audit proved c59 has the identical H0002; c60's switch-flatten did NOT touch the continue model тАФ it only un-masked the H0002 by clearing the S0023 wall). FIX (NOT ┬зB; C 6.8.6.2 тАФ a switch is a `break` target but TRANSPARENT to `continue`; 2 symmetric edits): (a) the verifier `checkBreakContinueScoping` drops SwitchStmt frames from a continue's `enclosingBranchTargets` list (`std::erase_if`) before the depth/range check тАФ the loops-only index space, depth 0 = innermost loop; the old "resolves to a switch" rejection is gone (a continue with NO enclosing loop now fails the range check тЖТ "no enclosing loop"); (b) the MIR ContinueStmt lowering (hir_to_mir.cpp ~6211) walks the branchStack SKIPPING switch frames (a switch frame has continueBB invalid) to the depth-th loop frame. `break` is UNCHANGED (keeps the full loops+switches stack тАФ a switch IS a break target). The verifier's loops-only view and the MIR's skip-invalid-continueBB walk drop the IDENTICAL frames (a SwitchStmt HIR node тЯ║ a MIR frame with invalid continueBB), both depth 0 = innermost loop тЖТ consistent by construction. WITNESS: corpus `continue_in_switch` (a for-loop with `case 2: continue;` inside a switch; the continue must skip the post-switch `acc += 5` тЖТ 42, a wrong target [break out of switch] would yield 47; RED-ON-DISABLE тЖТ H0002). тШЕтШЕ INDEPENDENT AUDIT all 3 RUN legs (x86_64-elf native + arm64-qemu + windows-pe, every value gcc-cross-checked): the corpus (42) + adversarial nesting [continue in while/do-while; nested switch-in-inner-loop-in-outer-loop тЖТ advances the INNER loop only; loop-in-switch-in-loop тЖТ targets the INNERMOST loop] + break-unchanged [break-in-switch=47, break-in-loop=15] + no-over-admission [continue-no-loop / bare-continue / cross-function still rejected] + arm64 objdump [the continue `b`-branches to the correct loop LATCH, not the post-switch code] тАФ ALL correct; a faithful sqlite3VXPrintf miniature ran identically on gcc/DSS-x64/DSS-arm64. The audit ALSO caught a stale c60-committed unit test (`ContinueSkippingSwitchToOuterLoopIsValid` used makeContinue(1), the old depth-1-skips-switch model) тЖТ updated to makeContinue(0) (the real-C loops-only form тАФ a better regression test). | (closed тАФ the verifier erase-switches + the MIR skip-switch-frames continue resolution) | p19 Cluster G c61; the c60 re-probe's H0002 frontier (PRE-EXISTING, un-masked by the c60 switch-flatten clearing the S0023 wall); `src/hir/hir_verifier.cpp` + `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `tests/hir/test_hir_verifier.cpp`; a sqlite re-probe frontier; plan 23 full-C arc / SQLite-green |
| `D-CSUBSET-STRING-LITERAL-ARRAY-ZERO-FILL` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c62; ┬зB Option A pad-at-producer, USER-chosen) тАФ a string literal initializing a `char[N]` array zero-fills the trailing NтИТlen bytes (C 6.7.9p14).** ROOT (the c61 re-probe's FIRST error, H_VerifierFailure ├Ч4): sqlite's date/time `aXformType[]` table (sqlite3.c:26283) has a `char zName[7]` field initialized by string literals SHORTER than 7 (`"hour"`=Array<char,5>, `"day"`=4, `"month"`=6, `"year"`=5) тЖТ ConstructAggregate child[1] type Array<char,M> тЙа field Array<char,7> (the 6-char names fit [7] exactly via sameType + pass). DSS did not implement the zero-fill (the short string's Array<char,M> never widened to Array<char,N>). The SAME gap rejected the scalar `char x[7]="hi"` with S0003. ┬зB RESOLVED by the USER тЖТ Option A (pad at the producer). FIX (3 tiers, admitтЯ║realize parity): (semantic) isAssignable gains a `charArrayFromStringLiteralInit`-gated arm admitting `char[N]<-char[M]` (NтЙеM, char both, string-literal init only) + `initIsStringLiteral` threaded through the scalar VarDecl sites; (HIR) `coerce` re-types the string Literal to Array<char,N> by minting a fresh Literal SHARING the literal-pool index (no double-decode); (MIR/asm) the global is recorded at the target Array<char,N> type + the asm string-literal arm ZERO-PADS the global's bytes to the type size N (the Option-A producer step тАФ avoids the latent OOB the design was chosen to prevent: a short M-byte global + an N-byte aggregate-copy would read adjacent rodata as padding), + `lowerAggregateCopy` gains an Array arm + `encodeAggregateValue` an Array string-char arm (const-folded aggregate). EXACT-FIT (N==M) stays via sameType; OVER-LONG (N<M, `char[3]="hello"`) stays a loud S0003; `char *p="hi"` (POINTER) UNCHANGED (3-byte rodata). WITNESS: corpus `string_array_zero_fill` (the aXformType shape + a scalar `char s[7]="hi"`; byte-checks the padding == 0 AND the string bytes тЖТ 42; RED-ON-DISABLE тЖТ H_VerifierFailure / S0003). тШЕтШЕ INDEPENDENT AUDIT all 3 RUN legs (the c57 lesson тАФ the impl could only RUN pe; the audit closed arm64): the rodata global is BYTE-IDENTICAL N-byte zero-padded on x86_64 AND arm64 (`68 69 00 00 00 00 00`, NOT a short global + OOB), the corpus + repro RAN to 42/4 on x86_64-elf + arm64-qemu (gcc-cross-checked all 4), a 0x7f-neighbor probe confirmed the padding is genuinely ZERO at runtime on arm64, admitтЯ║realize parity + over-long-S0003 + char*-unchanged + scalar+aggregate ALL correct; the pre-existing mutable-char[N]-in-rodata bug confirmed NOT sqlite-critical (all 17 sqlite file-scope char[]=string globals are const). | `D-MIR-ENTRY-BLOCK-ALLOCA-HOIST` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c69; NOT ┬зB) тАФ every body-local's storage `Alloca` is pre-emitted into the function ENTRY block (which dominates all uses), not at its decl site.** ROOT: a local DECLARED in an entry-unreachable block (the canonical case: a decl before the first `case` of a `switch`, which the c60 switch-flatten places in a predecessor-less pre-case block) had its `Alloca` emitted into that dead block; the mandatory unreachable-prune (`D-MIR-UNREACHABLE-PRUNE-NORMALIZE`) then dropped the block while a reachable case body still Load/Stored the slot тЖТ the `MirFunctionRebuilder` rewrite-map completeness abort (`D-OPT2-REWRITE-MAP-COMPLETENESS`), a SIGABRT compiler crash. FIX (`src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp`): `lowerFunction` runs a `collectLocalDecls` pre-pass that emits each local's slot into the (still-open) entry block before lowering the body; the VarDecl site reuses the pre-emitted slot. The jumped-over initializer's Store stays at the (dead) decl site. Corpus `examples/c-subset/switch_decl_before_case` (тЖТ42; RED-ON-DISABLE тЖТ SIGABRT exit 134). Companion of `D-CSUBSET-ALLOCA-ADDRESS-REMATERIALIZE` (below тАФ the hoist makes alloca addresses live from entry, which that anchor's remat keeps off the register-pressure cliff). |
| `D-AS4-ARM64-LARGE-DISP-LEA-BASE-CLOBBER` | тП│ **DEFERRED (surfaced + confirmed PRE-EXISTING by the c69 audit, 2026-06-30; NOT introduced by c69).** An arm64 `lea result,[base+disp]` with a displacement > 16 MiB (> 0xFFFFFF) lowers to a `MOVZ Xd; MOVK Xd; ADD Xd, base, Xd` macro that CLOBBERS `base` when regalloc coalesces the base vreg into the result (base == Xd == result). c69's alloca-address rematerialization ([[D-CSUBSET-ALLOCA-ADDRESS-REMATERIALIZE]]) EXPOSED it on `large_frame_beyond_16mib` (a > 16 MiB frame slot) тЖТ SIGSEGV on arm64-qemu under the optimized pipeline; FIXED for the alloca-base case by folding a constant-offset alloca Gep into one `lea [sp + slotOff + disp]` (base = sp, the reserved frame register, never the clobberable result). RESIDUAL: a NON-frame `p + hugeConst` (> 16 MiB constant added to a DERIVED pointer whose vreg coalesces with the result) still hits the general Gep lea path, which c69 does NOT touch (audit-verified: no makeMemOffset/emitInst(Lea) added there; alloca bases are routed away first). PRE-EXISTING, c69-INDEPENDENT, astronomically rare, NO sqlite relevance. FIX (when needed): a scratch register for the arm64 large-disp `lea` multi-word variant, OR a regalloc baseтЙаresult constraint for it. | (open) | discovered p19 c69 (impl + audit); [[D-CSUBSET-ALLOCA-ADDRESS-REMATERIALIZE]] (c69, whose remat exposed it) + sibling [[D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA]] (c42); `src/dss-config/targets/arm64.target.json`; plan 23 full-C arc |
| `D-CSUBSET-ALLOCA-ADDRESS-REMATERIALIZE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-06-30 (p19 Cluster G c69; NOT ┬зB) тАФ a body-local alloca's ADDRESS is rematerialized at each use (a fresh `lea_frame_slot` per use) instead of held in one entry-spanning vreg.** ROOT (un-masked by the c69 entry-block alloca-hoist above): hoisting every alloca to entry makes its address-value live from entry; reusing ONE address vreg across that range tips a register-pressure cliff (`examples/c-subset/index_negative`'s `main` takes addresses of locals тЖТ `R_SpilledDueToPressure` + `L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc` scratch-pool exhaustion at the rewrite stage). FIX (the conventional "rematerialize frame addresses" treatment; `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` + `src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp` + both target JSONs): MIRтЖТLIR emits the `alloca` op ONCE (it reserves the slot, in scan order) and, at each use of the address (`regForValue` chokepoint), a fresh `lea_frame_slot k` (k = the alloca's 0-based scan-order index, on the payload) whose result has a tiny defтЖТuse range; lir_callconv materializes each `lea_frame_slot k` into `lea [sp + offsetOf(k)]` тАФ byte-identical to the original alloca's `lea` (offset from the alloca-indexтЖТoffset map it builds in scan order, asserted congruent with the alloca arm's running offset). The new `lea_frame_slot` virtual op is DISTINCT from `alloca` so the frame-layout slot-count scan is unchanged (exactly ONE slot per original alloca). A CONSTANT-offset Gep whose base IS an alloca (`&local[constIdx]`/`&s.field`) FOLDS its displacement into ONE sp-based `lea [sp + slotOff + disp]` (via an optional ImmInt operand on `lea_frame_slot`) тАФ cheaper (one lea) AND load-bearing on arm64: without the fold, a >16 MiB `disp` in the un-folded `lea_frame_slot` base + `lea [base+disp]` pair lowers to the arm64 MOVZ/MOVK macro `ADD Xd,base,Xd` which clobbers `base` when regalloc coalesces the tiny-range remat base into the Gep result (base==Xd тЖТ SIGSEGV, `examples/c-subset/large_frame_beyond_16mib` under the full-release-like pipeline); folding makes the base `sp` (never the result), so the macro is safe. Corpora `examples/c-subset/index_negative` (тЖТ42; RED-ON-DISABLE тЖТ the scratch-pool exhaustion compile error) + `large_frame_beyond_16mib` (тЖТ42 on arm64-qemu; the const-disp fold is what keeps it green under remat). Verified RUN-green on all 3 legs (Win pe compile + WSL x86_64-elf + arm64-qemu). |
| `D-CSUBSET-CHAR-ALU-INTEGER-PROMOTION` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c71; NOT ┬зB) тАФ a SUB-INT (`char`/`signed char`/`short`) operand of a truth-value condition OR of `++`/`--` integer-PROMOTES to `int` (C 6.3.1.1) before the ICmp/Sub.** ROOT (the c70 re-probe compiled sqlite3.c END-TO-END past the VDBE switch, 12m41s тЖТ 902├Ч `error[L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode]` "ICmp/Sub operand: integer TypeKind ordinal 15 [Char] has no native-width ALU forms" = the [[D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS]] gate correctly refusing an 8-bit ALU op): a text library scans/classifies/steps `char` on nearly every line. Root-caused (delegated agent) to TWO HIR-lowering sites that build the op DIRECTLY, bypassing the C 6.3.1.1 integer promotion the ORDINARY binary combine site already applies (agent-verified: `a<b`/`c-'0'`/`*z==t` widen fine): (A) `coerceCondition` (cst_to_hir.cpp ~641) minted a SAME-TYPE (Char) zero тЖТ `Ne(char,char)` (a Char-typed ICmp) for a bare char truth value (`while(*z)`, `if(c)`, `for(;*z;)`, `?:`/`&&`/`||`); (B) `incDecArithValue` (~3975) тЖТ `incDecArithType(Char)=Char` (only Enum was remapped) тЖТ `BinaryOp Add/Sub` at opType=Char for `c++`/`c--`; (A') `combineUnaryOp`'s logical `!` (~2722) built a Char-typed `ICmpEq(operand,0)` (the pervasive `if(!*z)` scan idiom) тАФ this 3rd site was FOUND by the independent audit (my initial fix + claims covered only A+B) and FOLDED IN this cycle. FIX (NOT ┬зB тАФ the tier that owns promotion [HIR combine sites] + the correct primitive [`integerPromotedType`тЖТ`coerce`тЖТSExt/ZExt] already exist and work; the sites simply skipped them): all THREE sites promote the sub-int operand to `int` via the SAME `integerPromotedType(interner, t, *arith_)` + `coerce` chokepoint the array-index path (~3093) uses тАФ Char/signedтЖТSExt, unsigned(U8/U16)тЖТZExt via `mapCast`, automatic per kind; (A) mints the zero at the promoted type тЖТ `Ne(i32,i32)`; (B)'s EXISTING trailing `coerce(sum, lv.type)` narrows the i32 result back (Trunc) for the store, preserving 8-bit wraparound EXACTLY (C's `(char)((int)c┬▒1)`; signed char 127++тЖТтИТ128); (A') `!` promotes its operand then keeps its Bool result (Bool operand EXCLUDED тАФ its ICmpEq-0 form is already native). `arith_`-gated (block-less toy/tsql unchanged); float conds pass through (`integerPromotedType` no-ops floats тЖТ the `Ne` stays an FCmp). WITNESS: corpus `char_alu_integer_promotion` (`while(*z)` scan + plain/signed-char `c++` + signed-char 127тЖТтИТ128 wrap + `if(hi)`/`hi<0` SExt assertion тЖТ 42; EVERY observable independent of plain-char signedness [DSS types char SIGNED uniformly; gcc uses UNSIGNED char on arm64] so it matches gcc on all legs, the 2 signedness asserts use `signed char` explicitly; CONSOLIDATED into ONE function of SEQUENTIAL loops so it stays under the pre-existing x86_64 register-pressure cliff that many small inlinable static helpers would tip under -O тАФ the cliff is [[D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS]]/regalloc, NOT a c71 defect). Golden test `CharIfConditionLowersAsICmpNeTypedZero`тЖТ`CharIfConditionIntegerPromotesToI32BeforeICmpNe` (asserts I32 operands, not Char). RED-ON-DISABLE: the pre-fix compiler emits the exact ordinal-15 (Char) + ordinal-1 (signed char) ICmp/Sub errors on this corpus (WITNESSED on the WSL clone тАФ 315/316, ONLY this corpus red pre-fix). 3-leg gate: Win 501/501; WSL x86_64-elf + qemu arm64 316/316. тШЕтШЕ INDEPENDENT AUDIT (worktree, all-3-legs gcc-cross-check + the wrong-value/wrong-signedness/double-eval hunt): **SOUND** for A+B (no miscompile тАФ 31 adversarial programs, 23 gcc-cross-checked ├Ч8 legs [gcc x86 signed-char + aarch64 UNSIGNED-char, O0/O2]; objdump confirms SExt(signed)/ZExt(unsigned) both arches; single-eval confirmed; red-on-disable = the ordinal-15/-1 wall; 316/316 + 212/212 no regression; float/int/ptr/enum conds unchanged). The audit's ONE material finding = the un-covered `!` site (A' above) тЖТ FOLDED IN this cycle + re-gated (Win 501/501 + WSL/qemu 316/316, gcc-cross-check 42 both arches; `!char` is signedness-agnostic тАФ 0/1 regardless of SExt/ZExt).
