| `D-CSUBSET-UNARY-SUBINT-PROMOTION` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c72; NOT ┬зB) тАФ unary `~` (BitNot) and `-` (Neg) on a SUB-INT operand integer-PROMOTE the operand to `int` (C 6.5.3.3p3/p4) and carry the PROMOTED result type.** ROOT (the post-c71 re-probe CLEARED the 902 Char [[D-CSUBSET-CHAR-ALU-INTEGER-PROMOTION]] errors тЖТ 8 REMAINED on U8/U16 [TypeKind ordinal 6/7 = sqlite's `u8`/`u16`] at a `<deferred>` opcode): sqlite's `~u8`/`-u8` flag math. c71 generalized `combineUnaryOp`'s promotion ONLY to `!` (logical Not); `~`/`-` still built a sub-int-typed `UnaryOp` (`result=operand.type`) тЖТ the MIR Not/Neg walled at the LIR sub-native ALU gate. Binary `&`/`|`/`^`/`<<` already promote via `combineBinary` (only the UNARY operators bypassed it). This is EXACTLY the sibling the c71 independent audit flagged as latent. Probe confirmed: `~u8`тЖТord6, `-u8`тЖТord6, `~u16`тЖТord7 all error; `u8 & u8`/`| ^ <<` all OK. FIX (`src/hir/lowering/cst_to_hir.cpp` combineUnaryOp): widen the promotion condition from `Not` to `{Not,BitNot,Neg}` + coerce the operand to `integerPromotedType` (u8/u16тЖТZExt, signed char/i8тЖТSExt via `mapCast`); the KEY NEW behavior vs c71 тАФ `~`/`-` carry the PROMOTED (int) RESULT type (C 6.5.3.3 тАФ "the result has the promoted type"; threaded via a `promotedTy` local into BOTH the operand coerce and the UnaryOp result), so an assignment back to a narrow lvalue truncates via the normal coerce; `!` keeps its Bool result. Bool EXCLUDED (not gated тАФ keeps `~bool`/`!bool`); float/pointer/тЙеint operands pass through (`integerPromotedType` no-ops тЖТ `-1.5`/`~intConst` unchanged). ZERO combineBinary change. ALL 256 u8 ├Ч 9 contexts + ALL 256 signed-char ├Ч 7 contexts; objdump ZExt(u8/u16)/SExt(i8/char) both arches; the result-type change proven GENUINELY-int (unsigned-wide reads + narrow-store truncation catch a stay-as-u8 bug); red-on-disable = the ordinal 6/7/1 wall; 317/317 no regression; float/int/`!`/Bool unchanged. | `D-AS-REGALLOC-SPILL-RELOAD-SCRATCH` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c75; ┬зB USER тАФ reserve-K, cycle 1 of a 2-cycle plan [[project_v0_0_2_p19_clusterg_c75_c76_regalloc_scratch_plan_2026_07_01]]; c76 = per-point scratch SUPERSEDES this reservation) тАФ a function under GENERAL register pressure (peak simultaneous live values > allocatable GPRs) reserves K caller-saved registers per class as guaranteed spill-reload scratch.** ROOT: the post-c74 MILESTONE re-probe compiled sqlite3.c 12m57s through the WHOLE 8 MB amalgamation тЖТ `error[L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc]` (lir_rewrite.cpp:411): function 1033 (sym 9794), INSTRUMENT-confirmed `op=1 [=arg] isCall=0 nOps=0 nSpilled=0` = a spilled incoming-PARAMETER's result with an EMPTY global scratch pool (general pressure, NOT a wide call). The linear-scan allocator (lir_regalloc.cpp, Poletto-Sarkar) reserved NOTHING for reloads + `pickScratchRegs` (lir_rewrite.cpp:82) built the scratch pool GLOBALLY (allocatable тИТ regs-assigned-to-ANY-range) тЖТ a huge func assigns all 15 GPRs тЖТ empty pool тЖТ a spilled vreg's reload has no register. FIX (`buildFreeLists` + a new `computeReloadReserve`, lir_regalloc.cpp): reserve K caller-saved regs per class (held back from the free lists тЖТ never assigned тЖТ `pickScratchRegs` picks them up as scratch AUTOMATICALLY, unchanged). тШЕ K is DERIVED per-function from the max single-instruction register-reload demand (count of same-class VIRTUAL reg operands + result over NON-CALL instructions тАФ calls are the deferred [[D-AS-REGALLOC-WIDE-CALL-OPERAND-COUNT]]) тАФ NEVER a hardcoded count; x86_64 and arm64 compute their own. Caller-saved so a transient reload needs no callee-save; held back from the caller-saved END (allocator's last-choice partition) minimizing codegen impact; the existing L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc check is the LOUD backstop if K is ever under-provisioned. WITNESS: corpus `regalloc_spill_reload_scratch` (f has 15 params each in TWO inline sums тЖТ the first sum's result coexists with all 15 live params тЖТ MAXLIVE 16 > 15 GPRs тЖТ spill; main calls f with 15 args = a call that FITS тЖТ 42; arm64 ~30 GPRs never spills тЖТ trivially green + value-checked). | `D-AS-REGALLOC-ARG-REGISTER-OCCUPIED` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c75 correctness fix; NOT ┬зB тАФ a clean bug fix) тАФ the allocator + rewriter model an incoming ABI ARGUMENT REGISTER as OCCUPIED over [entry, argOp.late), so neither allocation nor spill-reload scratch reuses it while the incoming parameter is still live in it.** ROOT (the c75 [[D-AS-REGALLOC-SPILL-RELOAD-SCRATCH]] independent AUDIT caught a SILENT MISCOMPILE): a param vreg's live range starts at its `arg` op's late slot, and the `mov home, argreg` materialization is inserted POST-regalloc by callconv тАФ so [entry, argOp) has the incoming arg register appear FREE, and c75's added pressure/reservation reused it. TWO variants (both pre-c75 LOUD `L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc` failures тЖТ c75 turned silent): (V1) c75's reserve-K reservation reserved an arg register (x86_64 SysV's last caller-saved GPR r9 = the 6th int arg) as scratch тЖТ the rewriter staged a spill-reload through r9 at entry тЖТ clobbered the 6th incoming param (15-param fn тЖТ 149 not 156); (V2, PRE-EXISTING, unmasked by c75's pressure) the allocator assigned an FP arg register (xmm7 = 8th FP arg) to a non-param vreg's home тЖТ clobbered the incoming FP param (тЖТ 54 not 42). FIX (4 parts, all cc-config-driven, no arch identity, reusing existing machinery): (1) `buildFreeLists` reserves K caller-saved NON-ARG registers тАФ excludes argGprsтИкargFprsтИкindirectResultRegister; under-reserves (SAFE тАФ loud backstop) when a class has <K non-arg caller-saved (ms_x64 FPR has only xmm4/xmm5=2); (2) new `collectArgRegisterOccupied` + a covered-window `excludedScratch` augment (mirrors implicit-clobber/cross-call) тАФ a range live before an arg register's release (`r.start < releasePos`, not the arg's own vreg) excludes that ordinal from allocation + spill-evict тЖТ fixes V2; (3) `rewriteOneFunc` forbids the cc's arg registers (the FC4-c2 `forbiddenBase`) as scratch for a SPILLED `arg`-op result тЖТ fixes V1's rewriter side (arg ops are contiguous entry-block-head + operand-less, so the arg-op result store is the sole entry-region reload that can clobber a still-live incoming arg reg). ┬зB: NONE (a clean correctness fix of the ABI invariant 'incoming arg reg occupied [entry, argOp)'), orthogonal to + survives the c76 per-point supersession. (4) `rewriteOneFunc` ALSO forbids the instruction's OWN implicit registers (inputOrdinalsтИкclobberedOrdinals тАФ e.g. x86 idiv rax+rdx) as reload scratch for its operands+result тАФ the 1st RE-AUDIT caught a spilled idiv DIVISOR reloading into rax (reserve-K reserves the non-arg caller-saved rax), clobbering the dividend the idiv still needs тЖТ 121 not 160 (a SILENT miscompile; the mod/remainder path тЖТ rdx тЖТ 120); driven by the SAME per-opcode schema union collectImplicitClobberPositions already builds for the allocator's crossing-range exclusion, applied at the op where the transient reload happens (no idiv special-case). VALIDATED: the 4 arg repros + 3 corpora (arg_reg_gpr 156 / arg_reg_fp 42 / div_reload_scratch 160) RUN-correct on x86_64-elf(WSL)+pe(Win)+arm64(qemu) vs gcc O0/O2; 3-leg gate Win 508/508 + WSL/qemu 323/323. A 2nd COMPLETE RE-AUDIT (worktree agent a69337) = **SOUND** тАФ all 6 focus areas complete with RUN evidence vs gcc on 3 legs (O0/O2): prior bugs stay fixed; NO next unsafe-scratch class (variable shift `x>>cl` = rcx is COVERED by the SAME generic `info->implicitRegisters` forbidden path тАФ the shift opcodes declare rcx implicit; x86's 7 implicit-reg opcodes ALL handled, arm64 has 0; `mul` has no rdx:rax implicit; return-rax is materialized POST-rewrite; 2-address is post-rewrite; scratch is confined to one instruction's window); all 3 fix-parts RED-ON-DISABLE load-bearing (idiv off тЖТ div=121, arg off тЖТ 149/54, reservation off тЖТ loud L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc); full ctest Win 508/508 + WSL/qemu 323/323 (qemu needs QEMU_LD_PREFIX+QEMU_STACK_SIZE) + knife-edges 42; under-reserve fails LOUD never silent (Win64 FP exhaustion тЖТ L_VirtualRegInPostRegalloc, no artifact). Independently RE-FOUND the pre-existing orthogonal SysV mixed-class stack-arg miscompile [[D-FF3-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-STACK-OVERFLOW-OFFSET]] (reproduces on c74, NOT caused by c75). | (closed тАФ buildFreeLists arg-exclusion + collectArgRegisterOccupied excludedScratch + rewriter arg-op-result forbidden-scratch) | p19 Cluster G c75 correctness fix; the c75 audit's silent-miscompile finding; `src/lir/lir_regalloc.cpp` + `src/lir/lir_rewrite.cpp`; a foundational fix under BOTH reserve-K and c76 per-point; plan 12 MIR/LIR / SQLite-green |
| `D-AS-REGALLOC-FPR-SPILL-RELOAD-SCRATCH` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c76) / RE-CHARACTERIZED (agent aa0c00e5, 2026-07-01) тАФ the 'FPR spill-reload scratch collapse' was a MISDIAGNOSIS; the REAL defect is a SOUNDNESS BUG in the c76 move-cycle fix ([[D-ML7-2.3]] `L_MoveCycleUnsupported`), fixed as its correctness completion (ships WITH it, same commit).** The c76 move-cycle agent's DESIGN ┬з5 mis-read an 8-FP callee's `movaps %xmmN,%xmm14` body as an FPR scratch-collapse (the FPR analog of c75's GPR reserve-K). The deeper root-cause (agent aa0c00e5, 2069s, 3-leg) DISPROVED it: (a) c75's reserve-K / computeReloadReserve / collectArgRegisterOccupied are ALREADY class-generic and cover FPR by construction (they select cc.argFprs for an FPR-class result) тАФ NO FPR spill-reload gap exists; (b) the `xmm14` body is HARMLESS dead-param coalescing (a LIVE param always gets its own home; `direct8.c` reading all 8 FP params compiles CORRECTLY on the pristine c75 base). The REAL bug is CALLER-side, inside the move-cycle patch: at a cycle-break `emitParallelRegMoves` hands `pickScratchReg` the SHRUNKEN `moves` vector, so a register whose clean move was already EMITTED (now holding its committed argument) OR a dropped IDENTITY-move dst (`dst==src`, an incoming arg already in place) falls OUT of the exclusion тЖТ the picker returns it (e.g. xmm0) тЖТ the break `movaps scratch,cycSrc` CLOBBERS a committed argument тЖТ a SILENT SysV miscompile on a wide FP rotation whose caller sources overlap the low arg registers (rot8=1 not 42). тШЕ Without this fix the move-cycle patch converts `L_MoveCycleUnsupported` REJECTIONS into silent miscompiles тАФ the two are ONE fix, MUST ship together. FIX (`src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp` `emitParallelRegMoves`, FPRFIX_isolated.diff, APPLIED to c76; config-driven, NO arch/reg names): capture the FULL move-destination footprint ONCE, BEFORE the identity-erase, and fold it into the scratch picker's `reserved` span at the cycle-break (the scratch avoids EVERY committed/identity destination, not just pending-move regs; a caller-saved scratch of the class always remains тАФ SysV xmm8-15 vs the xmm0-7 dests; the `L_MoveCycleUnsupported` fail-loud backstop stays exact). ┬зB: NONE (one agnostic fix; the anticipated reserve-K/arg-occupancy/per-point forks all target the wrong layer тАФ the allocator/reserve layer c75 governs is fine, the bug is a missing exclusion in the post-regalloc move resolver). WITNESS: unit `CycleBreakScratchAvoidsCommittedArgDestination` (replays the emitted moves as a register machine, asserts each arg-dest ends holding its correct source token тАФ name-agnostic; red-on-disable = xmm0 clobbered) + examples `fpr_arg_cycle_scratch` / `mixed_arg_cycle_scratch` (rot8 / simultaneous-GPR+FPR-cycle = 42 on 3 legs vs gcc O0/O2; red-on-disable тЖТ 1 on SysV); arg_reg_fp + all c75 witnesses green; Win 510/510. | (IN-SCOPE c76 тЖТ CLOSED at commit; the move-cycle-fix scratch-picker soundness completion, NOT an FPR spill-reload bug) | p19 c76; RE-CHARACTERIZED from the move-cycle agent's ┬з5 misdiagnosis by agent aa0c00e5; the correctness completion of the move-cycle fix [[D-ML7-2.3]]; `src/lir/lir_callconv.cpp` (emitParallelRegMoves); plan 12 MIR/LIR / SQLite-green |
| `D-FF3-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-STACK-OVERFLOW-OFFSET` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-01 (p19 Cluster G c76; USER Option 2) тАФ FIXED: the CALLEE-side incoming overflow-arg offset now follows a monotonic class-SHARED byte cursor (`incomingStackArgByteOffset` in lir_callconv materializeOneFunc) mirroring the caller's overflowIdx, so a GPR-overflow and an FP-overflow arg occupy DISTINCT source-order slots (repro=73 not 64); slot-aligned CCs (Win64) get a congruence assertion. Audit SOUND (515/515 3 legs, gcc-cross-checked; red-on-disable тЖТ 64). тШЕ AUDIT CORRECTION: NOT SysV-only тАФ arm64/AAPCS64 ALSO collides when BOTH classes overflow deeply (>8 GPR + >8 FPR args; the original 7int+9double repro only overflowed FP on arm64 so it masked this); the class-agnostic fix handles both (p05 red-on-disable тЖТ arm64=52). Was a SILENT MISCOMPILE (user-reported 2026-07-01; PRE-EXISTING тАФ reproduces on c74 440b74a AND current build-rel [CONFIRMED: repro тЖТ 64 not 73], independent of the c75 regalloc arc) тАФ x86-64 SysV (elf64): when a function has BOTH integer/GPR-class params overflowing past the 6 GPR arg registers AND float/FP-class params overflowing past the 8 FP arg registers, the CALLEE reads the overflowed GPR stack arg and the overflowed FP stack arg from the SAME stack offset instead of consecutive slots.** ROOT (disasm-confirmed by the reporter): `int probe(int i1..i7, double d0..d8)` (7 ints + 9 doubles) тАФ the callee emits `mov 0x88(%rsp),%r9` (reads i7) AND `movsd 0x88(%rsp),%xmm4` (reads d8), BOTH at 0x88(%rsp); the caller wrote i7тЖТ[rsp+0] and d8тЖТ[rsp+8], so the FP overflow arg reads the INT overflow arg's slot тЖТ garbage double. The independent per-class overflow counters (GPR-overflow-index, FP-overflow-index) must EACH advance the SHARED incoming-stack cursor, not each restart at 0. WITNESS (CONFIRMED on build-rel): the repro тЖТ **DSS x86-elf SysV = 64; gcc -O0/-O2 = 73; arm64 AAPCS64 = 73; Win64 ms_x64 = 73** (only SysV wrong тАФ AAPCS64 + ms_x64 slot-align, so no collision). FIX (config-driven per the SysV ABI's declared stack-overflow discipline тАФ a per-classтЖТshared-cursor rule, NOT an `if (arch==тАж)` identity branch): the incoming-stack-arg OFFSET assignment тАФ callee-side param stack-slot layout (`src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` ArgOrdinalCounter / stack-overflow handling + `src/lir/lowering/lir_callconv.cpp` recv_by_value_stack_param) and/or caller-side computeMaxOutgoingStackArgs. | (OPEN тАФ a full /dss-cycle: root-cause, config-driven fix, gcc-cross-check 3 legs, a c-subset example witness [mixed int+FP overflow, red-on-disable тЖТ SysVтЙаgcc]) | user-reported + added-as-a-cycle 2026-07-01 (loop's current item NOT stopped); a real silent miscompile but NOT a sqlite-green COMPILE blocker (mixed 7+GPR & 9+FP overflow is rare тАФ sqlite is int/pointer-heavy with few doubles); a potential sqlite RUN-green concern IF a sqlite fn has such a signature (in-scope the moment sqlite RUN hits it per [[feedback_address_any_sqlite_green_blocker_2026_06_30]]); priority HIGH, queued as a dedicated cycle AFTER the current c75-regalloc + func-2088-wide-call arc; `src/mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `src/lir/lowering/lir_callconv.cpp`; the FF3 per-target ABI arc / plan 12 MIR-LIR |
| `D-OPT-LIR-ARG-REGISTER-CLASS-MISMATCH-FAILLOUD` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-07 by the [[D-OPT-RELEASE-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-REG-ARG-DROP]] fix; audit F8).** The mixed-class miscompile's disassembly was `cvttss2si %xmm15` тАФ the x86 encoder SILENTLY accepted a GPR register ordinal (r15) placed into an XMM operand field, so a wrong-register-class operand assembled to nonsense instead of failing loud. A LIR/encoder register-CLASS consistency check (an FPR-class LIR operand must encode through an XMM field, a GPR-class through a GP field, else fail loud) would have made THIS bug тАФ and every future wrong-class-operand bug тАФ loud at first occurrence rather than a silent runtime miscompile. Deferred (not on the critical path now the root is fixed): it is a broad encoder-tier backstop touching the x86/arm64 encoders + regalloc's class tracking, its own cycle. TRIGGER: the next wrong-register-class operand bug (a class-confusion in regalloc/lowering) OR a general encoder-hardening pass. | (gated тАФ a defense-in-depth backstop; the arg-position fix removed the one known trigger) | opened by the T2 mixed-class fix (2026-07-07); `src/lir`/`src/asm` x86_64+arm64 encoders + regalloc register-class tracking |

SIGNATURE-INDEPENDENT: `main(void)` ignores the two registers (C-legal) тАФ the whole corpus is unaffected (Win 545тЖТ546 green incl. every main(void) example). Mach-O declares NO block тАФ LC_MAIN entry is CALLED by dyld with argc/argv ALREADY in the argument registers (pass-through is the correct mechanism; untestable locally, no darwin run leg); pe64 declares NO block тАФ the Windows OS entry receives NO C argument vector at all (msvcrt `__getmainargs` out-parameter route = [[D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS]], the Windows-leg cycle; pe main(void) unchanged-green meanwhile). envp NOT materialized (c-subset main is `(int,char**)`; libc `environ` serves programs that need it). | `D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS` | тШЕ USER ┬зB DECISION (2026-07-04, c111): chose the **synthesized pre-main INIT FUNCTION** hosting (option B тАФ a compiler-generated function in ordinary MIR/LIR where stack locals + calls are FREE via the normal callconv pass, NOT hand-built trampoline RSP math) with the **msvcrt `__wgetmainargs`** CRT route (dumpbin-verified export ord 168; the WIDE form тАФ shell.c's MSVC entry is `wmain(int,wchar_t**)`; already importing msvcrt, no new import library). The synthesized fn: allocate locals {argc i32, wargv ptr, wenv ptr, _startupinfo{i32 newmode=0}}, call `__wgetmainargs(&argc,&wargv,&wenv,0,&si)` (the 5th arg _startupinfo* MUST be non-NULL тАФ msvcrt derefs .newmode), then `return wmain(argc, wargv)`; the trampoline calls THIS fn as the effective entry (the wmainCRTStartup role). тШЕ DESIGN-AUDIT (aed13cd1, for the rejected option A but its facts carry): PLAN-SOUND-WITH-CORRECTIONS + 2 load-bearing corrections that apply to B too тАФ (1) WIDE-vs-NARROW must be DERIVED FROM THE RESOLVED ENTRY'S SIGNATURE (2nd-param pointee width: wchar_t**тЖТwide __wgetmainargs / char**тЖТnarrow __getmainargs), NOT a format-level `wide:true` bit (which would silently miscompile the EXISTING narrow `main_argc_argv` witness тАФ pass wchar_t** to char**); (2) REGRESSION GATE тАФ adding the pe args mechanism makes the startup emit the CRT call for EVERY pe program incl. main(void) ones (windows_predefine_macro, shipped_windows_crt_batch) тАФ those must still exit 42 (C-legal: main(void) ignores the arg regs). CLOSURE GATES: (a) wide/narrow from the entry sig or fail-loud on mismatch [never silent wchar**тЖТchar**]; (b) RSP 16-aligned at the __wgetmainargs call [B gets this free from callconv]; (c) _startupinfo real zeroed non-NULL local; (d) every main(void) pe example still 42; (e) a host-independent structural pin (the synthesized fn / trampoline emits the __wgetmainargs call + arg-reg loads, red-on-disable every leg); (f) all-CI-green before CLOSED. WIDE-vs-NARROW derives from the entry's argv ELEMENT width (u16тЗТ`__wgetmainargs`, char/i8тЗТ`__getmainargs`), never a format flag; a non-char/non-u16 argv fails LOUD. Wired at BOTH lowering seams via ONE shared function тАФ merge (program.cpp, NтЙе2, pre-optimize so DCE roots the Global synth fn) + single-CU (compile_pipeline.cpp lowerCuMirToAssembly, N==1, post-optimize so the synth marks its EntryBlock canonically itself). pe64 format.json declares `processArgs{crt-out-param, __wgetmainargs, __getmainargs, msvcrt.dll}`; the ArgsMechanism closed-enum + decode + ProcessArgs fields extend target_schema.hpp / object_format_schema_json.cpp. WITNESSES: `examples/c-subset/main_argc_argv` pe64 flipped `runOn:["windows"]` тЖТ 42 (NARROW, single-CU) ┬╖ NEW `examples/c-subset/wmain_argc` pe64 тЖТ 42 (WIDE тАФ argc==1 тИз argv[0][0] wide-char non-zero тИз argv[argc]==NULL) ┬╖ 4 `SynthPeStartup.*` structural pins (exact per-arm export name + entry retarget + MirVerifier-clean + main(void)/non-CRT no-op тАФ HOST-INDEPENDENT, all legs) ┬╖ the `ShippedMachoExecsDeclareNoneAndPeDeclaresCrtOutParam` + enum-round-trip descriptor pins ┬╖ a manual 2-TU merge-path wmain proof (argc=1; +mallocтЖТheap RW OK). Gates aтАУf met: (a) wide/narrow from the sig, fail-loud else тЬУ; (b) RSP-16 free from callconv тАФ the calls execute тЬУ; (c) `_startupinfo` zeroed non-NULL local тЬУ; (d) main(void) pe still 42 (windows_predefine_macro + shipped_windows_crt_batch) тЬУ; (e) the structural pins тЬУ; (f) 3-leg gate тЬУ. тШЕ SCOPE: this closes the ARGS WALL ONLY тАФ sqlite3.exe now BUILDS+LINKS (4.7MB PE) and the synth delivers a VALID argc/wargv (proven in isolation), but `sqlite3 --version`/SQL still 0xC0000005 in shell.c `wmain`'s kernel32 CONSOLE prologue (`WideCharToMultiByte` loop / `GetStdHandle` / `GetConsoleMode` / `SetConsoleMode` тАФ all IMPORTED тАФ or `utf8_main`), a SEPARATE deeper blocker PAST this wall (args + malloc proven working in isolation) = the next sqlite Windows-RUN cycle. [SUPERSEDED OPEN TEXT тАФ split from [[D-RUNTIME-MAIN-ARGC-ARGV]] at c88, 2026-07-02: pe64 `main(argc,argv)` read entry-register garbage; the Windows OS entry receives NO C argument vector, so the ELF stack-vector mechanism cannot apply.]** The Windows loader calls the PE entry via `RtlUserThreadStart` with NO argc/argv anywhere in registers or on the stack тАФ the C runtime must ASK for them. ┬зB FORK (тЙе2 defensible designs, deliberately not half-picked): **(A) msvcrt `__getmainargs(&argc,&argv,&env,0,&startinfo)`** тАФ the classic msvcrt CRT-init call; DSS already imports msvcrt.dll for the whole shipped-lib surface and the trampoline already calls through an import (kernel32!ExitProcess via `call_indirect_via_extern`), BUT the call needs FOUR out-POINTERS to writable stack locals + a 5th STACK argument (beyond the 4 ms_x64 register args) + reloading argc/argv from the locals into rcx/rdx after it returns тАФ the hand-built trampoline LIR would grow stack-slot addressing (lea rcx/rdx/r8 off rsp, a [rsp+32] store for arg 5) it has never needed; feasible with existing opcodes (`lea`/`store`/`load` all encode [rsp+disp]) but a real mechanism step-up. **(B) ucrtbase route** (`_configure_narrow_argv` + `__p___argc()`/`__p___argv()`) тАФ the modern CRT's accessors тАФ the same out-parameter shape, different import library (ucrtbase.dll тАФ a NEW DT-equivalent import DSS doesn't carry today). **(C) kernel32 `GetCommandLineA` + trampoline-side parsing** тАФ rejected: quoting/escaping rules make the parser a small program, absurd as hand-built LIR. Design question for the ┬зB ask: extend the trampoline (A) vs synthesize a tiny compiler-generated pre-main INIT FUNCTION in ordinary MIR/LIR (where stack locals + calls are free) that the trampoline calls тАФ the latter generalizes to future per-OS runtime init (atexit wiring, environ). Until it lands: pe64 declares NO `processArgs` (pinned by `ProcessArgsSubstrate.ShippedNonLinuxExecsDeclareNone`), every `main(void)` program is unaffected (the whole corpus), and `main(argc,argv)` on pe is the KNOWN gap (the corpus witness `main_argc_argv` keeps its pe leg compile-only `runOn:[]` until this closes тАФ flipping that leg to `["windows"]` is this anchor's RED-ON-CLOSE witness). | `D-OPT-RELEASE-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-REG-ARG-DROP` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-07 (T2 miscompile-hunt) тАФ ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED; this single root ALSO closed [[D-OPT-RELEASE-FP-CONSTANT-UNLOADED-XMM]].** ROOT: the MIR `Arg` payload carried ONLY a per-ABI-class register ORDINAL. On a SysV-style CC the GPR/FPR pools count INDEPENDENTLY, so `int a, double x` gives a=gpr#0 AND x=fpr#0 тАФ BOTH ordinal 0. The INLINER (`emitCalleeInst`) mapped a callee Arg тЖТ the caller's actual BY that ordinal, so a mixed int+FP callee spliced the WRONG actuals (`(int)x` read the int arg тЖТ the release build returned 0 for `g(1,47.0)`; ms_x64 immune тАФ its slot-aligned CC makes ordinal==position). Bisect: `[Identity,Inlining]` reproduces, `[Identity]` correct, the verifier did NOT flag it (disasm: `cvttss2si %xmm15` тАФ a GPR ordinal encoded through the XMM field). FIX (independent design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES, F1-F8 folded): the Arg payload now packs `(position<<16)|ordinal` (`core/types/arg_payload.hpp`); `argIndex()`=ordinal (lir_callconv unchanged), NEW `argPosition()`=the FLAT call-operand position; `hir_to_mir` threads a flat position counter through EVERY receive site (scalar/piece/byref/va_list/sret-hidden + advancing for the two no-Arg operand shapes: x8-sret ReadIndirectResult + straddle RecvByValueStackParam); the inliner's gate + splice map by POSITION (+ a gate type-consistency refusal backstop); тШЕ F1 (audit CRITICAL): `addInst` REFUSES Arg (dedicated builder) so the 3 `addArg` re-encode sites (`mir_rebuild_helper.cpp` тАФ EVERY pass; `mir_merge.cpp` тАФ the 2-TU path; `inlining.cpp` MultiBlockInliner host) thread the position or it dies before Inlining runs; F5 a MirVerifier duplicate-position check (`I_ArgPositionDuplicate=0xA012`); F2 mir_text round-trip. WITNESSES: repro `g(1,47.0)` release 0тЖТ47 + `isum(2,10,3,20,43)` 78 on x86_64 AND arm64 (both = gcc); corpus `inline_mixed_class_args` (release arm, all legs) + `inline_mixed_class_args_crosscu` (the 2-TU merge witness) + `mixed_class_stack_overflow` RE-ARMED with its release arm (was omitted for the FP-constant bug, now fixed); MIR-tier `Inlining.{MixedClassCalleeSplicesFpArgByPositionNotOrdinal (operand-identity), ArgPositionSurvivesRebuildRoundTrip}`; 3-leg gate. тШЕ F8 opened: [[D-OPT-LIR-ARG-REGISTER-CLASS-MISMATCH-FAILLOUD]] (the encoder silently accepted a GPR ordinal through an XMM field тАФ the missing backstop). | тЬЕ (CLOSED) | T2 miscompile-hunt 2026-07-07; `core/types/arg_payload.hpp` + `mir/mir.{hpp,cpp}` + `mir/lowering/hir_to_mir.cpp` + `opt/passes/{inlining,mir_rebuild_helper}.cpp` + `mir/merge/mir_merge.cpp` + `mir/mir_verifier.cpp` + `mir/mir_text.cpp` + `core/types/parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` + examples + `tests/opt/test_inlining.cpp`; ORIGINAL discovery p19 c77 verifier a1b4246e (proven pre-existing on c76). **PRE-FIX detail below (kept for the audit trail):** ЁЯФ┤ **OPEN / SILENT MISCOMPILE (release pipeline; discovered p19 c77 verifier a1b4246e, 2026-07-01; PRE-EXISTING, NOT a c77 defect).** On x86-64 SysV (elf64) under the `release` shipped pipeline, a function with a leading GPR (integer/pointer) arg FOLLOWED by an FP arg DROPS the FP arg. Minimal repro `int g(int a, double x){ return (int)x; }` тЖТ release SysV returns 0, gcc returns x's value (47); DEBUG is CORRECT; Windows ms_x64 CORRECT тАФ only SysV-release wrong. PROOF pre-existing (NOT c77): the repro has ZERO spilled call args so c77's SpillSlotRef path is never taken; stashing c77 + rebuilding the c76 baseline yields BYTE-IDENTICAL wrong values (p4a=6/p4b=197/z=0 on both). Likely the REGISTER-arg facet of the SysV mixed-class family тАФ DISTINCT from the CLOSED overflow-offset [[D-FF3-SYSV-MIXED-CLASS-STACK-OVERFLOW-OFFSET]] (this is IN-REGISTER args, release-ONLY тЖТ an OPTIMIZER├Чmixed-class-arg interaction, possibly kin to [[D-OPT-RELEASE-FP-CONSTANT-UNLOADED-XMM]]). Impact on sqlite-green: sqlite is int/pointer-heavy (few mixed int+FP fns) тЖТ a RUN-green concern IF such a fn runs under release, in-scope then [[feedback_address_any_sqlite_green_blocker_2026_06_30]]; NOT the compile blocker (c78 = the x86_64 encoder gap). FIX (its own cycle): root-cause the release mixed-class register-arg placement/read (why an FP arg after a GPR arg is dropped under the optimizer). | (OPEN тАФ a full /dss-cycle: root-cause the release mixed int+FP register-arg drop, gcc-cross-check 3 legs RUN, a red-on-disable witness) | discovered p19 c77 verifier a1b4246e (proven pre-existing on c76); a release-optimizer ├Ч mixed-class-arg concern; `src/mir`/`src/lir` arg placement/read + optimizer; plan 12 or an optimizer plan / SQLite-green RUN phase |

WITNESSED: `realpath(".", NULL)` through the c82-shipped stdlib.json symbol returned NULL/errno=EINVAL on native x86_64-elf тАФ the binding landed `realpath@GLIBC_2.2.5` (the pre-2.3 compat version that REJECTS a NULL resolved buffer) instead of the `realpath@@GLIBC_2.3` default; qemu-aarch64 was UNAFFECTED (aarch64 glibc baseline 2.17 has a single version). EXPOSURE (enumerated against the current shipped surface): exactly TWO dual-versioned intersections тАФ `realpath` (REAL: the NULL-buffer form degrades; sqlite shell.c:25343 calls exactly `realpath(zPath, NULL)` on its .open canonicalization path тЖТ a run-green concern the moment that path executes) and `memcpy` (2.14 vs 2.2.5 тАФ LATENT-SAFE: the old binding is semantically correct for conforming non-overlapping callers; sqlite is clean). libc carries ~217 dual-versioned names total, so the class GROWS with every shipped symbol. **c87 UPDATE (libm joined the import surface via D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED):** libm.so.6 adds FOUR dual-versioned intersections тАФ `pow`/`log`/`exp` (GLIBC_2.2.5 compat vs the 2.29 defaults) + `fmod` (2.2.5 vs 2.38) тАФ ALL VALUE-SAFE empirically: a 29-probe bit-exact comparison (DSS's unversioned binding vs gcc's default binding, glibc 2.39; pow├Ч6/log├Ч5/exp├Ч4/fmod + the single-versioned rest; scratchpad c87libm/vercheck-verdict.txt) came back BYTE-IDENTICAL тАФ glibc's compat wrappers route to the SAME __ieee754_* cores, so the residual divergence is SVID errno/matherr handling on DOMAIN-ERROR inputs only (e.g. the errno shape of pow(-1,0.5)), plus any future glibc that changes the cores without re-versioning the compat aliases. The anchor stays OPEN on the realpath-class REAL degradations; the libm additions raise the surface, not the severity. FIX (its own cycle, linker feature work): emit `.gnu.version` (one u16 per dynsym) + `.gnu.version_r` (a VERNEED block per DT_NEEDED lib naming the required version per import) so every import binds its DEFAULT version тАФ the standard toolchain behavior; needs the per-symbol version STRING (readable from the build machine's libc at descriptor-verification time, or pinned in the descriptor). **(2)** IMPORT-side undefined-extern naming is moot тАФ the ET_REL path REJECTS `externImports` before the symtab is built (`elf.cpp`: "externs flow to the linker only via ET_EXEC / ET_DYN"), so no undefined symbol arises to name [blocked by object-path extern support тАФ the [[D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH]] relocatable variant]; **(3)** [[D-LK-OBJECT-RELOC-ADDEND-CROSSTOOLCHAIN]] тАФ a DSS `.rela.text` `call rel32` PC32 reloc carries r_addend 0 where the x86-64 psABI / gcc's ld need -4, so a DSS object with ANY intra-object call SIGSEGVs under gcc (exposed the instant this anchor made the link succeed); **(4)** static/internal-linkage functions are emitted STB_GLOBAL with a unique `sym_<id>` name (the name-only carve-out тАФ `isExternallyVisible`=false тЖТ the fallback), NOT STB_LOCAL: benign for the single-DSS-object testfixture route (unique `sym_<id>` names never collide with user/libc symbols), but two DSS `.o`s linked together by a foreign linker could multiple-define `sym_<id>` тАФ flipping to STB_LOCAL needs the locals-first symtab reorder + `sh_info` recompute across all 3 formats [low priority; trigger: linking 2+ DSS objects under a foreign linker; the anchor's original contract explicitly permits static "may stay internal", so this is a separate binding axis, not sliced naming work]. тАФ ORIGINAL (2026-07-07): ЁЯФ┤ **OPEN (discovered 2026-07-07, SQLite codegen-validation session).** DSS relocatable-object emission (the non-`-exec` format `elf64-x86_64-linux`) writes externally-visible GLOBAL FUNC symbols into `.symtab` under DSS's INTERNAL numeric IDs (`sym_8`, `sym_10`, ...) instead of the source-level C identifier names (`foo`, `bar`). The object is otherwise structurally valid (`file` -> `ELF 64-bit LSB relocatable, x86-64`, Type `REL`; `readelf -s` -> binding/type correct `GLOBAL`/`FUNC`, `Ndx=.text`), so DSS's own linker resolves intra-DSS references and the `-exec` legs + the `sqlite3` executable build+run cleanly тАФ the gap is INVISIBLE to intra-DSS linking. It only bites CROSS-TOOLCHAIN object interop: `gcc main.c dss.o` -> `ld: undefined reference to 'foo'/'bar'` (C names absent from `.symtab`). VERIFIED via `nm dss.o` (-> `T sym_8` / `T sym_10`) + `readelf -s` + the failing gcc link. Secondary ELF interop nit, same run: DSS objects omit `.note.GNU-stack` -> `ld` warns 'missing .note.GNU-stack implies executable stack'. Likely also latent in PE/macho object output (the internal-ID naming appears to live in the shared symbol layer тАФ verify per-format on close). **Impact:** blocks linking a DSS-compiled object into a foreign-toolchain image тАФ specifically the DEFINITIVE SQLite validation: building `testfixture` (gcc + `libtcl`) against a DSS-compiled `sqlite3.o` to run SQLite's own 1,190-file TCL suite against DSS codegen (all prereqs present on the WSL box: 1,190 `.test`, 44 `src/test*.c`, `tclsh` 8.6.14, `tcl.h`). Until closed, DSS codegen is validated by DIFFERENTIAL testing vs gcc on the SAME amalgamation (2026-07-07: 107 statements across 2 corpora byte-identical incl. FP->text 17-sig-fig + 100k-iter recursion; 0 miscompiles) тАФ strong, but not the official suite. **Trigger:** fires when any real consumer needs a DSS object consumed by an external linker (the testfixture/TCL-suite route, or shipping a DSS `.o`/static lib for foreign linkage). | Carry the source-level C identifier name into `.symtab` `st_name` for every externally-visible (non-`static`) symbol in relocatable-object output тАФ thread the real name through the shared symbol layer for the object emitter, not just the internal `sym_N` ID (static/local symbols may stay internal). Format-neutral fix at the symbol-layer seam, realized per-format (ELF/PE/macho). Also emit an empty `.note.GNU-stack` (SHT_PROGBITS, flags 0) on ELF objects. Verify: (1) `nm dss.o` shows real names; (2) `gcc main.c dss.o` links+runs; (3) build `testfixture` from DSS `sqlite3.o` + gcc harness + `-ltcl`, run `veryquick`/`full`. Priority: normal (trigger-gated; no in-tree consumer today; unlocks the definitive SQLite validation). | `D-LK-ELF-SYMBOL-VERSIONING` (sibling ELF-symtab interop row); `D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH` (IMPORT-side extern-name resolution тАФ this is the EXPORT-side dual); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` (symtab/strtab producer); `src/dss-config/object-formats/elf64-x86_64-linux.format.json` |
| `D-LK-OBJECT-RELOC-ADDEND-CROSSTOOLCHAIN` | тЬЕ **CLOSED c140 (2026-07-14, ELF) тАФ the ELF `.rela.text` producer now BAKES the target schema's `addendBias` into the emitted `r_addend` (`rel.addend + targetSchema.relocationInfo(kind)->addendBias`), so a FOREIGN linker receives the full psABI implicit addend.** FIX (`src/link/format/elf.cpp`, the `!isExec` `.rela.text` loop): a `call`/`lea` rel32 (R_X86_64_PC32, addendBias -4) now emits `r_addend = -4` (byte-matching gcc); abs64/abs32 (addendBias 0, loader-guaranteed by the `addendBias != 0 тЗТ pcRelative` invariant) unchanged; + a defensive `K_RelocationKindMismatch` fail-loud if the target schema lacks the kind's `TargetRelocationInfo`. DSS's OWN in-place exec applier (`applyExecRelocations`) is UNTOUCHED (it keeps `addend + addendBias` separate and emits no `.rela`); DSS never re-reads its own `.o` (the FFI reader parses only a FOREIGN lib's `.dynsym`/`.dynstr`), so the baked addend is consumed exclusively by external linkers тАФ no double-count. PE/Mach-O objects are STRUCTURALLY unaffected: they store addends IN-FIELD (REL32/BRANCH relative-to-instruction-END conventions) and fail-loud on a non-zero `rel.addend`, so a call (addend 0) is already correct; the RELA explicit-addend model is ELF-only. WITNESSES: `ElfWriter.RelaRecordsNativeRelocTypeAndSymtabIndex` (rel.addend 0 тЖТ r_addend -4; red-on-disable тАФ reverting to `rel.addend` verbatim emits 0 тЖТ fails) + independent silent-failure review (VERIFIED-CLEAN) + ctest 639/639. тШЕ END-TO-END on WSL gcc 13.3: `dss_part.o` (`dss_add` calls the static `dss_helper`) тЖТ `readelf -r` now shows `R_X86_64_PC32 sym_8 - 4` ├Ч2, and the gcc-linked program RUNS тЖТ **exit 42** (was SIGSEGV/139 before the fix). Unblocks running ANY DSS object with intra-object calls under a foreign linker тАФ the next step toward the SQLite testfixture route. тАФ ORIGINAL (discovered 2026-07-14): ЁЯФ┤ **OPEN (discovered 2026-07-14 by [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES]]'s end-to-end witness).** DSS relocatable-object `.rela.text` emits a `call rel32` R_X86_64_PC32 reloc with `r_addend = 0`, but the x86-64 psABI (and gcc's `ld`) require `r_addend = -4` for a rel32 field тАФ the field is relative to the instruction END, 4 bytes past the reloc offset (a gcc reference object of the same source shows `R_X86_64_PLT32 helper - 4`). DSS's OWN linker applies its relocs with a compensating convention (the whole corpus runs green), and the EXEC path applies relocations IN-PLACE (never emitting `.rela`), so this is INVISIBLE to intra-DSS linking + every CI leg. It only bites cross-toolchain: the instant [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES]] made `gcc main.c dss.o` LINK, a DSS object with ANY intra-object CALL SIGSEGVs at runtime тАФ the call lands 4 bytes into the callee (mid-instruction). VERIFIED (WSL gcc 13.3, 2026-07-14): `dss_part.o` (`dss_add` calls static `dss_helper`) тЖТ `readelf -r` shows `R_X86_64_PC32 sym_8 + 0` ├Ч2 тЖТ the gcc-linked program SIGSEGVs (exit 139); the LEAF variant (no intra-object calls тЖТ zero `.rela` entries) runs тЖТ exit 42. **Impact:** blocks running ANY realistic DSS object (sqlite3.c has thousands of intra-object calls) under a foreign linker тАФ a hard prerequisite for the definitive testfixture/TCL-suite validation. **Trigger:** FIRED (the witness above). | Emit the psABI-correct `r_addend` for PC-relative relocs in the `.rela.text` producer (`-4` for a rel32 call/jmp field; verify per reloc kind AND per arch тАФ ARM64 branch/ADRP relocs use different addend conventions), OR normalize DSS's own reloc-application convention to the ELF standard so DSS's linker and gcc's `ld` agree on one `.rela`. Verify: `gcc main.c dss_part.o` with an intra-object call runs тЖТ 42; a gcc-vs-DSS `.rela` addend diff is byte-clean. Priority: normal (trigger-gated on the cross-toolchain route; no in-tree consumer until testfixture). | [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES]] (exposed by its link-success тАФ the EXPORT-naming dual); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` (`.rela.text` `appendI64LE(relaText, rel.addend)`); `src/asm/` (the reloc `addend` origin in codegen); plan 14 linker / SQLite testfixture route |
| `D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-CALL-RELOCATABLE` | тЬЕ **CLOSED c141 (2026-07-14) тАФ extern FUNCTION calls now lower into an ELF relocatable object (`.o`) that a FOREIGN linker resolves: `gcc extmain.c dss.o` (default PIE) links + runs.** FIX: **(1)** the relocatable `elf64-x86_64-linux.format.json` declares `externCallDispatch: "direct-plt"` (so MIRтЖТLIR lowers an extern call to a plain `call rel32` + reloc тАФ the SAME call-site as exec, but the FINAL linker builds the PLT, not DSS); **(2)** `elf.cpp` тАФ the ET_REL path's blanket "externs flow only via ET_EXEC/ET_DYN" reject is LIFTED for a format that declares `externCallDispatch` (the exec-dynamic path moved into an `else`), + a fail-loud guard still rejects an extern DATA import (`isData` тАФ needs copy-reloc/GOT, unhandled); **(3)** `object_symbol_names.hpp` тАФ `externName` reintroduced (the undefined extern carries its real `mangledName`; `sym_<id>` fallback); **(4)** PLT32 тАФ a new OPTIONAL `pltNativeId` field on `ObjectFormatRelocationInfo` (+ JSON loader); the PC32 row declares `pltNativeId: 4` (R_X86_64_PLT32), and the `.rela` loop emits it for a rel32 reloc whose target is an undefined extern (built once into `externTargets`) тАФ a bare PC32 against an undefined symbol errors under a foreign `-pie`; same S+A-P formula + `-4` addend as PC32. WITNESSES: `ElfWriter.ObjectExternCallEmitsUndefImportNameAndPlt32Reloc` (UNDEF extern name + PLT32 type-4 reloc + SHN_UNDEF + `-4` addend; red-on-disable) + `ElfRelWriter.ExternDataImportFailsLoudOnEtRel` (the `isData` guard, red-on-disable) + the pltNativeId loader; ctest 639/639; independent code-review. тШЕ END-TO-END on WSL gcc 13.3: `extern int rand(void); int dss_rand2(void){return rand()+rand();}` тЖТ `.o` with `readelf -r` = `R_X86_64_PLT32 rand - 4`, `nm` = `U rand`; `gcc extmain.c externcall.o` (DEFAULT PIE) links + runs тЖТ **exit 0** (previously the COMPILE fail-loud'd on the missing externCallDispatch). Unblocks compiling a libc-calling `.o` тАФ the NEXT SQLite-testfixture blocker is object-path DATA-section emission (globals). RESIDUAL (pinned): extern DATA imports in a `.o` (fail-loud today; needs copy-reloc/GOT тАФ a sibling of [[D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT]]). | (closed for function externs; extern-data residual pinned) | [[D-LK-OBJECT-EXTERN-SYMBOL-NAMES]] + [[D-LK-OBJECT-RELOC-ADDEND-CROSSTOOLCHAIN]] (the cross-toolchain object arc); [[D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH]] (the exec-side dispatch this extends to ET_REL); `src/link/format/elf.cpp` + `src/link/object_format_schema.{hpp,cpp}` / `object_format_schema_json.cpp` (`pltNativeId`) + `src/dss-config/object-formats/elf64-x86_64-linux.format.json`; plan 14 linker / SQLite testfixture route |
| `D-PP-ELIFDEF-ELIFNDEF` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-09 тАФ the C23 ┬з6.10.1 `#elifdef`/`#elifndef` directives are implemented; the silent miscompile is FIXED.** Config-driven: 2 OPTIONAL `PreprocessConfig` directive words (`elifdefDirective`/`elifndefDirective`) routed through the existing conditional-group state machine `sbHandleElif` with `SbIfKind::Ifdef`/`Ifndef` + the shared `sbEvalDefinedName` тАФ the SAME direct definedness test `#ifdef`/`#ifndef` use, NOT the `#if` expression evaluator; ZERO engine identity branch. TWO dispatch arms sit BEFORE the `stackActive()` gate so BOTH sub-paths close: the dead-enclosing silent-skip (a false `#if`/`#ifdef` arm no longer falls through a true `#elifdef` to `#else`) AND the live-after-taken spurious-unsupported path; fail-loud NAMES the right directive on a missing/malformed identifier (never a silent skip). Negative miscompile-pins (double-traced red-on-disable): `examples/c-subset/preprocessor_conditionals_elifdef` (42тЖТ2) + `_elifndef` (42тЖТ3). Independent design-audit + code-audit both GO; 3-leg gate ctest **606/606** (Win Debug + WSL x86_64 + arm64 qemu) + sqlite 3.54.0 + CRUD 42 on all 3 legs. Opened [[D-PP-SYNTHBUILDER-PREDEFINED-DEFINEDNESS]] (the pre-scan `isDefinedTok` predefined-macro gap the mirror INHERITED тАФ fail-loud, not silent). ORIGINAL: ЁЯФ┤ **OPEN (discovered 2026-07-08, hardened dss-state C23 battery).** The C23 preprocessor directives `#elifdef`/`#elifndef` (C23 ┬з6.10.1 тАФ shorthand for `#elif defined(X)` / `#elif !defined(X)`) are NOT recognized by the FC14 conditional-compilation substrate (which shipped `#if`/`#ifdef`/`#ifndef`/`#elif`/`#else`/`#endif` + `defined()`; `D-PP-CONDITIONAL-COMPILATION` тЬЕ). тЪа **SILENT MISCOMPILE, not fail-loud:** an unrecognized `#elifdef` is not treated as a conditional arm, so when the preceding `#if`/`#ifdef` arm is FALSE the true `#elifdef` group is SKIPPED and control falls through to `#else` тЖТ the wrong branch compiles, rc=0. VERIFIED by the `c23_elifdef` battery probe (`#define FEATURE_B` then `#ifdef FEATURE_A / #elifdef FEATURE_B / #else` тЖТ selects `#else`, exit **2** not 42, 2026-07-08). Witness is `#elifdef`; `#elifndef` is its unshipped C23 pair (same substrate gap). **Trigger:** FIRED тАФ any C23 TU using `#elifdef`/`#elifndef` mis-selects today; the probe is in-tree. | Extend the FC14 condition-stack dispatcher (`MacroExpander`) so `#elifdef X` is `#elif defined(X)` and `#elifndef X` is `#elif !defined(X)` тАФ SAME balance-tracking + `stackActive()` gating as `#elif`, operand resolved via the existing `defined` path BEFORE expansion; a real branch, FAIL-LOUD on a missing/malformed identifier (NOT a silent skip). Verify: `c23_elifdef` probe returns 42 + a fail-loud red on `#elifdef` with no identifier. Tracked as plan-23 **FC17.5** (feature) + plan-00 stepper **T2.3** (miscompile tier). Priority: normal тАФ silent-miscompile class; close before any C23-conformance claim. | `D-PP-CONDITIONAL-COMPILATION` (тЬЕ FC14 тАФ the substrate this extends); `D-PP-VA-OPT` (sibling C23-preprocessor addition); plan-23 FC17.5; plan-00 stepper T2.3; `src/analysis/preprocess/` MacroExpander condition stack; dss-state probe `c23_elifdef` |
| `D-PP-SYNTHBUILDER-PREDEFINED-DEFINEDNESS` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-PP-ELIFDEF-ELIFNDEF]]).** The SynthBuilder PRE-SCAN's bare-name definedness check (`isDefinedTok`) consults only `localMacros`, NOT the predefined macros the `#if defined()` path (`definedCb`) is aware of тАФ so a `#ifdef`/`#ifndef`/`#elifdef`/`#elifndef` guarding a quote-`#include` and testing a PREDEFINED macro can diverge from the authoritative pass IN THE PRE-SCAN. тЪа **PRE-EXISTING тАФ inherited verbatim from `#ifdef`** (the elifdef fix MIRRORED it, did not introduce it); worst-case symptom is a LOUD parse error (an unspliced quote-include тЖТ stray tokens), NEVER a silent miscompile. **Trigger:** a real consumer hits the pre-scan divergence for a predefined-macro-guarded quote-`#include`. | Unify `isDefinedTok` with `definedCb` so all four bare-name forms (`#ifdef`/`#ifndef`/`#elifdef`/`#elifndef`) agree with `#if defined()` in the pre-scan. Priority: normal (tracked limitation, not a blocker). | opened-by [[D-PP-ELIFDEF-ELIFNDEF]] (the elifdef cycle that surfaced it); [[D-PP-CONDITIONAL-COMPILATION]] (the FC14 substrate); `src/analysis/preprocess/preprocessor.cpp` (`isDefinedTok` vs `definedCb`); dss-state preprocessor surface |
| `D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-09 (FC17 cycle 1) тАФ C23 ┬з6.4.4.6/┬з6.2.5 `nullptr`/nullptr_t: the predefined null-pointer constant + its distinct type + one-way conversions + comparison rules + a fail-loud operator gate.** NEW closed-enum `TypeKind::NullptrT` (append-only after VolatileQual), **SEMANTIC-TIER-ONLY**: the KEYSTONE (Fix 1(a)) lowers the `nullptr` literal DIRECTLY to the target-agnostic integer-0 null constant at the HIR tier (`cst_to_hir` `lowerLiteral`, keyed on the core kind), so NullptrT NEVER reaches MIR тАФ reusing ALL existing null-pointer-constant lowering (coerceтЖТPtr, null comparison, ternary null-materialization, `if(nullptr)`тЖТfalse). Config-driven: `nullptr` keyword + operand.alt + `literalTypes {NullptrKeyword,NullptrT,0}` + `coreTypeFromName` + a NEW `pointerConversions.nullPointerConstantFromNullptrT` flag (default false тЖТ toy/tsql inert). Conversions via ONE `isAssignable` chokepoint (PtrтЖРNullptrT, ONE-WAY тАФ nothing converts TO nullptr_t) covering init/positional-init/assign/call-arg/return; `isExplicitCastable` (T*)nullptr only. Fail-loud: an EXPLICIT `S_NullptrInvalidOperand` (0xE033) SE-phase gate in `pass2Post` rejects nullptr as an arithmetic/bitwise/shift/relational/compound-assign operand, unary -/~, and ==/!= against a non-ptr/non-nullptr peer (WITHOUT it Fix 1(a) would silently compile `nullptr+1` as `0+1`); the gate ADMITS ==/!= vs ptr/nullptr, plain-assign, comma, logical &&/||. `combineTernary` NullptrT arm (Ptr-vs-nullptr тЖТ Ptr, both orders). MIR backstop `I_NullptrTypeInMir` (0xA014) тАФ a never-fires tripwire (red-on-disable unit test). Runtime witness `examples/c-subset/nullptr_basic` exit 15 (opaque volatile pointer source so `p != nullptr` is a genuine runtime compare, release arm) + 2 diagnostic-corpus goldens (`nullptr_int_assign`тЖТS_TypeMismatch, `nullptr_arithmetic`тЖТS_NullptrInvalidOperand) + `test_type_rules`/`test_semantic_analyzer_c_subset`/`test_mir_verifier` unit tests + red-on-disable (flip the flag тЖТ S_TypeMismatch). Deferrals opened: [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-BOOL-CONVERSION]], [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-NONLITERAL-OPERAND]], [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-T-DECLARABLE]], [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-CONSTEXPR-FOLD]]. Independent design-audit (Fix 1(a)) + code-audit (VERIFIED-CLEAN; 2 MEDIUM folded тАФ the FnSig/Array Eq/Ne peer-decay false-positive + a config-load cross-check requiring `nullPointerConstantFromIntegerZero` when the nullptr flag is set) + 3-leg gate ctest **607/607** (Win Debug + WSL x86_64 + arm64 qemu) + sqlite 3.54.0 + CRUD 42 on all 3 legs. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; plan-00 stepper T3.2; `src/core/types/type_lattice/core_type.hpp` (NullptrT) + `type_layout.cpp` + `type_reintern.cpp`; `type_rules.hpp` (isAssignable/isExplicitCastable arms); `cst_to_hir.cpp` lowerLiteral (Fix 1(a)); `semantic_analyzer.cpp` (pass2Post gate + combineTernary + variadic reject); `mir_verifier.cpp` (tripwire); `c-subset.lang.json` + `grammar_schema_json.cpp` + `semantic_config.hpp`; `D-LANG-NULL-POINTER-CONSTANT` (the integer-0 sibling) |
| `D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-BOOL-CONVERSION` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]]).** C23 ┬з6.3.2.3.2 says `nullptr` converts to `bool` (yielding false), but the c-subset has NO scalarтЖТbool conversion at all (`bool b = 0;` is itself S_TypeMismatch), so admitting ONLY nullptrтЖТbool would be inconsistent AND hit the unrealized `TruncтЖТBool` codegen form. Dropped from this cycle's `isAssignable`/`isExplicitCastable` arms (Ptr-only) so `bool b = nullptr;` stays a clean S_TypeMismatch (consistent with `bool b = 0;`), rather than admit-at-semantics then fail-at-codegen. тЪа Costs NO real behavior: nullptr in a CONTROLLING expression (`if(nullptr)`, `nullptr ? a : b`, `!nullptr`) works via the HIR condition lowering (nullptrтЖТ0тЖТ`Ne(0,0)`тЖТfalse), independent of this arm. **Trigger:** general scalarтЖТbool conversion lands (then add the BoolтЖРNullptrT arm + `(bool)nullptr` alongside). | Add the `BoolтЖРNullptrT` `isAssignable`/`isExplicitCastable` arms + the codegen (nullptrтЖТbool via the general scalarтЖТbool normalization). It therefore does NOT catch a NullptrT value arising from a NON-literal subexpression used as an arithmetic/relational operand тАФ `(c ? nullptr : nullptr) + 1` or `(x, nullptr) < p` (astronomically rare; no real code writes it). тЪа Under the Fix 1(a) lowering these degrade to DEFINED 0-arithmetic (`0 + 1`), never a crash or wrong-value miscompile тАФ a silent-ACCEPT of ill-formed code, not a silent miscompile. **Trigger:** a real program hits it. | Extend the detector to cover NullptrT-typed non-literal operands (stamp NullptrT expression results so the O(1) already-stamped check catches them, or a bounded typed check). Priority: normal. | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR]]; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`pass2Post` nullptr operator gate `isNullptrOperand`) |
