| `D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17) тАФ a GENERIC speculative/backtracking capability for `{"optional"}` grammar clauses.** A `{"optional": X, "speculative": true}` clause is TRIED after the shared prefix and ROLLED BACK to the sibling continuation when X does not match тАФ the reusable parser improvement the enum underlying-type feature rides (its `enum <tag> :` prefix collides with the anonymous enum bit-field `enum C : 4;`). Engine (Fork B, audit-revised): (1) `PositionBuilder::build` extracts `applySpeculativeFlags(body,posId)` shared by the `"alt"` AND `"optional"` builders (a plain optional stays byte-identical тАФ the bit is set only when `speculative` is present); the optional records its skip/continuation branch (`Position::setSkipBranch`, the 2nd branch == `cont`). (2) THE AUDIT FIX тАФ `collectAltBranchRules` EXCLUDES a SPECULATIVE optional's skip branch from its OWN candidate enumeration (gated `posId==startPos`, so an enclosing non-speculative optional still reaches those rules; the non-speculative `altRuleBranches` route stays byte-identical), so a peek matching only the continuation yields `candidates=[]` тЖТ the nullable skip fires тЖТ the body parses NON-speculatively (avoids the D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER c25 cliff on the common bodied enum). (3) `parser.cpp stepOnce` speculative arm adds a nullable-fallback after the candidate loop (KEEP; the naive edit-(i) direct-descent guard was DROPPED per the audit). CRUX safety (verified empirically тАФ 30/30 broad ctest): all shipped speculative alts are non-nullable-tailed, so the parser edit is inert for them. Keys ONLY on generic `Position` properties (`speculative()`/`nullableTail()`/`skipBranch()`) тАФ never a rule/language identity. WITNESSES: synthetic `kSpecOptionalSchema` in `test_parser_speculation.cpp` (clause-matches, backtrack-to-sibling [red-on-disable for the nullable-fallback], body-over-budget). | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; `src/core/types/{compiled_shape.hpp,grammar_schema.cpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp}` + `src/analysis/syntactic/parser.cpp` + `tests/analysis/syntactic/test_parser_speculation.cpp`; consumed by [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-09 (FC17) тАФ C23 ┬з6.7.2.2 enum with an EXPLICIT underlying integer type (`enum E : unsigned char { A, B }`).** The underlying type fixes the enum's size/representation (scalars[0] on the interned Enum type тАФ `sizeof(enum E)==1`/align 1, not the default int 4). Grammar: new `enumTypeSpecifier = [Colon, castTypeBase]` with `commitRequiresTypeName{requireKnownType}` (MIRRORS `alignasTypeName`), wired into `enumSpec` as a SPECULATIVE optional ([[D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL]]) so the anonymous enum bit-field `enum C : 4;` still parses (the clause rolls back on a non-type). Semantic: config-driven `DeclarationRule.enumUnderlyingType {rule,typeChild}` (sibling to `bitfieldSuffix`); `semantic_analyzer` resolves the clause via the shared `resolveTypeNode`, gates on `isIntegerKind` тЖТ threads the kind into `enumType(name, underlying)` (the type substrate was ALREADY generic on scalars[0] тАФ layout/reprKind/enumUnderlyingOrSelf/reintern), and range-checks each enumerator (`enumeratorValueFitsUnderlying`) ONLY when explicit (default-int enums UNCHANGED). Fail-loud: `S_InvalidEnumUnderlyingType` (0xE034, non-integer) + `S_EnumeratorValueOutOfRange` (0xE035, overflow), both UNSUPPRESSABLE. тШЕ **CODEGEN FIX (beyond the design's edits тАФ the design's "mir_to_lir already generic" claim was STALE):** `requireNativeIntWidth` in `mir_to_lir.cpp` gated intтЖФenum CONVERSIONS on the RAW `interner.kind` (тЖТ Enum ordinal 23, "no encoded conversion form") instead of `reprKind` (тЖТ underlying) тАФ so a NARROW-underlying enum was RUNTIME-UNUSABLE (even `(int)v` fail-loud). Fixed to read `reprKind` at every kind test in that gate (identity for all non-enum kinds тЖТ zero effect elsewhere), so a `enum E : unsigned char` routes through the realized U8 conversion forms. WITNESSES: 12 `test_semantic_analyzer_c_subset` tests (scalars/distinct/anonymous/non-integer/struct/OOB-256/boundary-255/default-never-fires/bitfield-width/the fork pin/bare-id rollback), `TypeLayout.EnumFollowsExplicitU8Underlying`, `examples/c-subset/enum_underlying_type` (RUNTIME wrap-at-256 тЖТ 42, release arm all 4 targets), 4 diagnostic-corpus goldens. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17; plan-00 stepper T3; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` + `src/core/types/{semantic_config.hpp,grammar_schema_json.cpp,parse_diagnostic.*,unsuppressable_codes.cpp}` + `src/analysis/semantic/{semantic_analyzer.cpp,type_rules.hpp}` + `src/lir/lowering/mir_to_lir.cpp` (reprKind conversion-gate fix) + tests + example + goldens; rides [[D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL]]; opens [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-OPAQUE-FORWARD-DECL]] + [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE-QUALIFIERS]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-OPAQUE-FORWARD-DECL` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE]]).** A BODYLESS enum with an explicit underlying type used as an OPAQUE forward declaration тАФ `enum E : unsigned char;` (C23: an enum with a fixed underlying type is a COMPLETE type even without a body, so it can be forward-declared). Today it PARSES (the underlying clause + no `{тАж}`) but falls to the existing enum tag-miss fail-loud (Enum lacks the two-phase forwardComposite/completeComposite that Struct/Union have), so a bodyless `enum E : T;` is a LOUD rejection, never a silent wrong type. **Trigger:** real source needs an opaque fixed-underlying enum forward declaration. Resolution: give Enum the forwardComposite/completeComposite two-phase mint (mirroring struct forward-decl, D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION), keyed on the fixed underlying making the tag complete. | (gated тАФ no shipped consumer; a loud rejection today) | opened by the enum-underlying cycle; `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (Enum forward/complete two-phase); cross-ref `D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION` |
| `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE-QUALIFIERS` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE]]).** A `const`/`volatile`-qualified underlying type on the clause тАФ `enum E : const unsigned char { тАж }`. The clause is `[Colon, castTypeBase]` (the qualifier-free base type-name), so a qualifier before the base is NOT admitted тЖТ the speculative clause does not match and the parse rejects it (LOUD, never a silent drop of the qualifier). C23 permits any integer type-specifier-qualifier sequence; the qualifier is semantically irrelevant to the underlying (an enum's underlying is an rvalue integer). **Trigger:** real source writes a qualified underlying type. Resolution: widen `enumTypeSpecifier`'s base from `castTypeBase` to a qualifier-admitting form (e.g. the head-qualifier `{repeat headQualifier}` prefix castTypeRef uses) and strip the (ignored) qualifier at resolve. | (gated тАФ trivial widen; no shipped consumer writes a qualified enum underlying) | opened by the enum-underlying cycle; `src/dss-config/sources/c-subset.lang.json` (`enumTypeSpecifier` base) |
| `D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-CHAR-BOOL` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-09 by [[D-CSUBSET-ENUM-UNDERLYING-TYPE]], code-audit LOW finding).** `char` / `_Bool`(`bool`) as the enum underlying type тАФ `enum E : char { тАж }` / `enum E : bool { тАж }`. C23 ┬з6.7.2.2 permits both (`char` and `bool` ARE integer types), but DSS's `isIntegerKind` (the underlying-type gate) is `I8..U128` only тАФ it EXCLUDES `Char`/`Bool`/`Byte`, consistent with DSS's deliberate "`Char`/`Bool` are NOT arithmetic-integer ranks" posture (they reach the int ranks only via the `charConvertsToArith`/`boolWidensToArith` config gates, not natively). So `enum E : char` is REJECTED with `S_InvalidEnumUnderlyingType` (LOUD, never a silent wrong-width enum). **Trigger:** real source needs a char/bool-underlying enum, OR native Char/Bool-as-integer-rank support lands. Resolution: admit `Char`/`Bool` in the enum-underlying gate + map to their byte width in `enumeratorValueFitsUnderlying`, once they participate in the integer ranks. | (gated тАФ a loud rejection today; consistent with the Char/Bool non-arithmetic-rank posture) | opened by the enum-underlying cycle (code-audit); `src/analysis/semantic/semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`isIntegerKind` underlying gate) + `type_rules.hpp` (`enumeratorValueFitsUnderlying`) |
| `D-CSUBSET-EMPTY-INITIALIZER` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-10 (FC17.5) тАФ C23 ┬з6.7.10 `{}` empty initializer + the SCALAR brace initializer.** `int z = {};` zero-initializes (6.7.10p11), `int v = {42};` / the scalar compound literal `(int){42}` initialize with the single expression (6.7.10p12), `int *p = {};` zero-fills to null тАФ all via a NEW scalar arm at the TOP of the ONE `lowerBraceInit` funnel (cst_to_hir.cpp `lowerScalarBraceInit`; all 4 routes тАФ decl init / call-arg ├Ч2 / compound literal тАФ inherit it). **F4 CLOSED ALLOWLIST** (`isScalarBraceInitKind`: Bool/I8..U128/F16..F128/Char/Byte/Enum/Ptr тАФ exactly what `synthZeroOrError` can zero-mint); Void/FnSig/etc. keep the aggregate gate's fail-loud reject (`(void){}` stays H0009-loud). 0 elements тЖТ the SHARED `synthZeroOrError`; 1 undesignated NON-brace element тЖТ `lowerExprOrBraceInit` (byte-identical to `= expr`); >1 / designator / **N2 nested `{{42}}`** тЖТ `S_InvalidScalarInitializer` (0xE03F, plain Error тАФ the lowering returns an Error node either way, no silent-accept path). **F3**: `validateConstexprDeclarator` gains an EMPTY-brace arm (`constexpr int x = {};` / `constexpr int *p = {};` = zero = valid; `{5}` folds via the normal descent тАФ the now-green delta pin). Aggregate `{}` zero-fill was ALREADY green (regression-pinned). Tests: the FLIPPED `ScalarCompoundLiteralLowersViaScalarBraceInit` (was the aggregate-only fail-loud pin) + 3 malformed-shape 0xE03F pins + the `(void){}` F4 pin + `empty_init_scalar` runtime example (exit 42, 4 targets + release) + 2 constexpr semantic pins. Empirical: 8/8 pe64 runtime probes + elf64 emits. |
| `D-CSUBSET-FUNC-PREDEFINED-IDENTIFIER` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-10 (FC17.5) тАФ C99 ┬з6.4.2.2 `__func__` (+ the GNU `__FUNCTION__` alias, config vocabulary `semantics.predefinedFunctionNames.identifiers`).** Pass 1 binds per spelling a SYNTHETIC SymbolRecord into a function DEFINITION's own scope BEFORE the params (**N4**: a param named `__func__` collides тЖТ S_RedeclaredSymbol at its own span): kind=Variable, **F1 `isConst=true`** (SE4's const check catches `__func__ = x` / `+=` тЖТ S_ConstViolation тАФ isConst is the ONLY guard, both pinned + corpus-goldened), type=`Array<narrow-string-core, len+1>` (**N5**: the element core from the language's config string-literal core тАФ `SchemaIndexes::stringLiteralElementCore`, never hardcoded Char). HIR FOLDS a read at the identifier-operand path to a string-literal-shaped `HirLiteralValue` (BYTE-IDENTICAL to `lowerStringLiteral`'s narrow arm) тЖТ rodata/decay/`__func__[i]`/sizeof/`&__func__` (LEGAL, the Literal-lvalue arm) all ride unchanged. **F1 guard**: `++__func__`/`--`/compound-assign classifiers тЖТ `S_PredefinedIdentifierNotAddressable` (0xE040) at the ONE `simpleLvalue` chokepoint (returns {sym, InvalidType} so both classifiers bail with no second engine error; previously an engine-level MIR "no storage slot"). **F2 (C REQUIRES `__func__ == __func__`)**: `materializeStringLiteralGlobal` MEMOIZES by (array TypeId, byte content) тАФ identical string literals now share ONE rodata global module-wide (C 6.4.5p7 permits; also a size win тАФ sqlite's repeated literals collapse); red-on-disable MIR pins (1-global counts) + the runtime identity term in `func_name_basic` (exit 42, 4 targets + release). File-scope `__func__` stays S_UndeclaredIdentifier (the binding is per-function-definition, pinned). NOTE тАФ `__func__[0] = 'x'` (the rodata-WRITE class) rides the PRE-EXISTING [[D-CSUBSET-INCDEC-CONST-LVALUE]] const-lvalue family gap: not newly opened here, closes with that class. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA` | ЁЯЯв **C1a + C1b + C2 + C3 + C4a + C4b + C4c + C5 тЬЕ ARC COMPLETE (C1aтАУC4a 2026-07-13, C4bтАУC5 2026-07-14) тАФ `int a[n]` + MULTI-DIM RUN + `sizeof <vla>` runtime + ptr-to-VLA `int (*p)[n]` (LOCAL + PARAMETER) + VLA typedef `typedef int R[n]; R a;` + array-param `static`/cv-qualifiers/`[*]` (`int f(int a[static 3])`/`[const n]`/`int a[*]`, all decay to a pointer) on all 3 legs (LEAF scope); `__STDC_NO_VLA__` REMOVED, `c99_vla`+`c99_vla_multidim`+`c99_vla_ptr`+`c99_vla_ptr_param`+`c99_vla_typedef`+`c99_array_param_static` GREEN. тШЕ C4c ALSO landed a GENERALIZABLE parser capability (the schema compiler honors `speculative` on inline repeat-alts) via 2 user ┬зB decisions. C5 (block-scope teardown) тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-14 тАФ the FULL VLA arc is COMPLETE. тАФ arc OPENED, ┬зB forks DECIDED. тШЕ The substrate probe CORRECTED the stale premise: `int a[n]` PARSES clean + is rejected ONLY at semantic `S_NonConstantArrayLength` (0xE00B тАФ the `S000B` render is the 0xE high-nibble stripped by `parse_diagnostic.cpp:409`, NOT parse `P_UnmatchedClose` 0x000B; verified via `--suppress=0xE00B` compiling to an object) тЗТ the arc is TYPE+SEMANTIC+CODEGEN, ZERO grammar for the core `int a[n]`. ┬зB DECIDED (user 2026-07-13): FORK A тЖТ `TypeKind::Array` + a `kVlaLength=-2` sentinel + an out-of-band size side-table (the incomplete-array `-1`-sentinel precedent; low blast radius over the 83 Array dispatch sites; `computeLayout` already nullopts on `sc[0]<0`); FORK B тЖТ TRUE dynamic stack (runtime-size `Alloca` operand тЖТ aligned `sub rsp,<size>` + a frame pointer [RBP] + SP save/restore тАФ best-long-term, + it UNLOCKS the `alignas` over-aligned-local frame-model the `L_OverAlignedStackLocal` fail-loud waits on; NO dynamic-SP precedent exists today). Cycle split: **C1a** (type-rep + the 2 semantic accept-arms `semantic_analyzer.cpp:2313`+`:3298` + CSTтЖТHIR size-expr un-skip `cst_to_hir.cpp:170` + MIR runtime-operand `Alloca` + verifier) / **C1b** (LIR/asm dynamic-alloca: frame pointer + `sub rsp,reg` + SP save/restore, agnostic ├Чx86_64/arm64├ЧPE/ELF/Mach-O) тЖТ C2 sizeof-on-VLA тЖТ C3 multi-dim `a[n][m]` (runtime stride) тЖТ C4 ptr-to-VLA+VLA-typedef+`[*]`/`[static n]` тЖТ C5 block-scope teardown. Full design [[project_vla_arc_design_2026_07_13]]. тШЕ **C1a тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-13:** the `kVlaLength=-2` sentinel + `vlaArray`/`isVlaArray`; both accept-arms + the Pass-2 `validateVlaDeclarator` owning file-scopeтЖТ`S_NonConstantArrayLength`, non-integerтЖТ`S_VlaSizeNotInteger` 0xE053, static/externтЖТ`S_VlaWithStaticStorage` 0xE051, multi-dim/array-of-VLAтЖТ`S_VlaMultiDimUnsupported` 0xE052; CSTтЖТHIR size-expr un-skip + a SymbolId-keyed side-table; MIR runtime-operand `Alloca` (total-byte-size, decl-point-emitted via the hoist-skip) + the verifier invariant `I_VlaAllocaOperandInvalid` 0xA016; the named C1b boundary = MIRтЖТLIR fail-loud `L_VlaDynamicAllocaUnsupported` 0xB00D. Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (scope gate = the existing agnostic `fileScopeOf`; 3 fail-loud gaps folded pre-code) + code-audit (caught+fixed a CRITICAL non-integer-length SILENT-truncation тАФ `int a[1.5]`/`int a[nullptr]` were `FPToSI`/`SExt`-truncating to 1-element/0-byte past a dead `mapCast` guard тЖТ now rejected at the SEMANTIC tier by `S_VlaSizeNotInteger` + block-scope goldens; the fix also cleared a pre-existing `H0009` on `_BitInt` bounds via `resolveScalarIntKind`; 2nd-order self-review CLEAN). 628/628 ├Ч3 legs + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч3; `__STDC_NO_VLA__` STAYS =1 (`FC175StdcNoVlaDefined` green). тШЕ **C1b тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-13** тАФ the dynamic-stack RUN: a `framePointer` CC field + 2 OPTIONAL LIR ops (`sub_sp_reg` [arm64 EXTENDED-register `0xCB2063FF`, reg31=SP тАФ the naive reg-reg form targets XZR] + `sp_copy`) emitted by ONE config-resolved handle; a per-function VLA-ONLY conditional frame pointer (┬зB тАФ regalloc pool + rewriter-scratch reserve + force-saved); a SITE-SPECIFIC SPтЖТFP base-switch at every fixed-frame ref + a completeness verifier; `lowerVlaAlloca` (alignUpтЖТ`sub_sp_reg`тЖТbaseтЖРSP). LEAF scope: fail-loud `L_VlaNonLeafFrameUnsupported` (0xB00E) on VLA+call/`va_start`/over-align/own-`__try`(SEH). Independent DESIGN-audit folded 4 CRITICAL silent-miscompiles PRE-code (SP-relative spills; unsaved caller-FP; prologue-order clobber; the non-leaf outgoing-args collision тЖТ the LEAF scope-cut); the mandated SPILLING test caught a rewriter-scratch FP-leak the spec itself missed; the CODE-audit caught 1 missed base-switch site (`recv_by_value_stack_param`) тЖТ folded (a leaf by-value-struct-param VLA now RUNS). `c99_vla`/`_spill`/`_structparam` RUN тЖТ 42 on x86_64-elf + arm64-elf(qemu, debug+release тАФ the extended-register SP encoding proven by EXECUTION) + pe64; 631/631 ├Ч3 legs + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч3; a non-VLA function BYTE-IDENTICAL; agnostic (no arch/format identity in `src/lir/`). New anchors `-NONLEAF-CALL-FRAME`/`-WIN64-UNWIND`/`-WIN64-STACK-PROBE`. тШЕ **C2 тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-13** тАФ `sizeof <vla-object>` is a RUNTIME `size_t`: `lowerSizeof` records (SizeOf HIR node тЖТ the VLA operand SymbolId) via `vlaObjectOperandSymbol` (descend transparent grouping wrappers to the bare identifier; a COMPOSITE operand тАФ `sizeof(b,a)`/`sizeof(0,a)`, тЙе2 sub-exprs тАФ is REJECTED so it FAILS LOUD, since C decays a composite value to a pointer тЖТ never a silent wrong size); `vlaAllocaForLocal` FREEZES `count*stride` (= the exact C `sizeof`; the 16-align is LIR-only) into a hidden 8-byte U64 slot ONCE at the decl (┬з6.7.6.2p2, side-effect-safe for `int a[f()]`); the MIR SizeOf case Loads it (+a desync fail-loud). тШЕ THE INVARIANT: const-eval keeps DECLINING a VLA sizeof (the SizeOf node's `vlaArray` TypeRef is LEFT UNCHANGED + NO const-eval path touched) тЖТ `_Static_assert(sizeof a==K)`тЖТ`S_StaticAssertFailed` "not an integer constant expression" (`S0029`); never a wrong constant. Object-form ONLY; the type-form `sizeof(int[n])` isn't even constructible in the c-subset (parse `P0009`) тЖТ honest deferral `-SIZEOF-TYPEFORM`. Design-audit GO (invariant CONFIRMED-SAFE by construction; the mid-function fixed size-slot IS frame-summed + FP-relative in the VLA frame; `bytes`==exact sizeof). Code-audit caught+fixed a CRITICAL silent-miscompile (a broad-DFS operand match mis-keyed `sizeof(0,a)`тЖТ`a`'s frozen size; the wrapper-peel now rejects composites тЖТ fail loud; red-on-disable pin `SizeofOfCompositeVlaOperandFailsLoud`). 632/632 ├Ч3 legs + `c99_vla_sizeof` RUNSтЖТ42 (x86_64-elf + arm64-qemu[debug+release] + pe64) + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч3; pins `SizeofOfVlaLoadsDeclFrozenSizeSlot` + `StaticAssertSizeofVlaIsNotConstantFailsLoud`. New anchor `-SIZEOF-TYPEFORM`. тШЕ **C3 тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-13 тАФ multi-dim VLAs RUN with a RUNTIME inner stride** (`int a[n][m]`, `a[n][m][k]`, `a[n][5]`, `a[5][n]` тАФ all block-scope multi-dim shapes; LEAF scope). ┬зB DECIDED (user 2026-07-13): **storage = Option 1** (store cumulative row-strides ONCE at the decl, Load at each index) + **`a[5][n]` INCLUDED (no-compromise)** via a transitive `typeContainsVla`. Single-tier (semantic + `cst_to_hir` + `hir_to_mir` + interner + verifier; NO LIR/asm/target/format тАФ a runtime stride is just more MIR `Mul`/`Load`). Mechanism: nested `vlaArray(vlaArray(int))` type (the fixed `Index(Index(..))` + `indexResultType`-peel + byte-`Gep` machinery composes for free); lift the 4 `S_VlaMultiDimUnsupported` rejects; `interner.typeContainsVla` (recursive `ops[0]`/`isVlaArray` walk, does NOT over-fire on `int[5][5]`) threads 8 `isVlaArray` gates (the 4 rejects + `validateVlaDeclarator` + `captureVlaSize` call + sizeof-recording + the MIR verifier alloca rule) so top-fixed `a[5][n]` works; `captureVlaSize` collects EVERY suffix (outerтЖТinner vector); `vlaAllocaForLocal` evaluates all dims ONCE (┬з6.7.6.2p2, side-effect-safe), folds cumulative row-strides bottom-up, freezes one slot per runtime level keyed by **`(baseSymbol.v, elemTy TypeId.v)`** (the interned peel тЖТ store/load keys MATCH by construction); тШЕ CRITICAL-1 CONVENTION (design-audit caught a self-contradictory level map тАФ `a[1][0]`тЖТ`a[0][1]`): scale a subscript / size a row by the runtime `sizeof` of the SUBSCRIPT-RESULT type тАФ a VLA-row elemTy Loads its `(sym, elemTy)` slot, a FIXED elemTy uses compile-time `elementStride`; this UNIFIES index + row-`sizeof a[0]` + whole-`sizeof a` (the C2 total slot IS the whole-object entry). Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (3 CRITICAL folded pre-code: the level-map, the verifier `a[5][n]` reject, the 4-gate a[5][n] leak) + code-audit SHIP (stride arithmetic hand-verified per shape; keys match; evaluate-once holds; typeContainsVla complete+non-over-firing; 3 MINOR тАФ the misleading "internal desync" message for the C4-deferred typedef-VLA/ptr-to-VLA shapes REWORDED to honest fail-loud; `S_VlaMultiDimUnsupported` 0xE052 now UNEMITTED but kept-reserved for C4 `[*]`). Orchestrator-verified INDEPENDENTLY: the off-diagonal `a[1][0]`==77 (NOT the transposed 88) on all 3 legs; per-dim non-integerтЖТ`S0053`, file-scopeтЖТ`S000B`, wide-`_BitInt`тЖТ`H0009`, struct-member-VLAтЖТ`S_FlexibleArraySoleMember`, `_Static_assert(sizeof a==K)`тЖТ`S0029` (C2 invariant preserved). 633/633 ├Ч3 legs + `c99_vla_multidim`тЖТ42 (x86_64-elf + arm64-qemu[debug+release] + pe64) + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч3; new MIR pins `MultiDimVlaLowersWithRuntimeStrideSlots`/`FixedMultiDimArrayDoesNotRouteToVlaPath`/`RowSizeofOfMultiDimVlaLoadsStrideSlot`. **C4 (ptr-to-VLA `int(*p)[n]` + VLA typedef + `[*]`/`[static n]`) NEXT.** тШЕ **C4 ┬зB DECIDED (user 2026-07-13): cover ALL 4 shapes across as many cycles as needed** (explorer-confirmed clean tier seam тАФ codegen core vs grammar surface). Split: **C4a** ptr-to-VLA (the substantial codegen core; FORK-1 = a per-pointer runtime-row stride slot FROZEN at the decl, reusing the C3 `vlaStrideSlot`/`scaleIndexToBytes` substrate тАФ the type `Ptr<vlaArray>` already builds; the only gap is `p[j]`/`p┬▒j` scaling by the runtime pointee size; **source scope = LOCAL + PARAMETER** [a local slot at the decl, a param slot in the callee prologue; globals/struct/cast/ternary bases fail loud]) ┬╖ **C4b** VLA typedef (`typedef int R[n]; R a;` тАФ a NEW freeze-at-typedef-declaration size capture; the only silent-miscompile surface) ┬╖ **C4c** `[*]`+`[static n]` (grammar-only prototype-param syntax, decays to a pointer, ZERO codegen). Full substrate map + FORK-1 options in the C4 explorer output (2026-07-13). C4a is the substantial one + carries FORK-1. тШЕ **C4a-LOCAL тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-13** тАФ a LOCAL `int (*p)[n]` RUNS: `p[i][j]` scales the outer subscript by the RUNTIME pointee size via a per-pointer stride slot FROZEN at the decl (reuses C3's `scaleIndexToBytes`/`vlaStrideSlot` verbatim). GAP 2 (the slot): the `captureVlaSize` gate widened for `kind==Ptr && typeContainsVla(pointee)`; the stride store at the VarDecl DECL SITE (program order тАФ design-audit CRITICAL-2: `allocaForLocal` is HOISTED тЖТ `n` read before its store = silent miscompile) via shared helpers `widenVlaDim`/`computeVlaByteSize`/`storePtrToVlaStride` (refactored from `vlaAllocaForLocal`, behavior-preserving, keeps all belts); keyed `vlaSlotKey(sym, pointeeTy)` == the subscript's `hir.typeId(Index)` (matches verbatim). Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (2 CRITICAL folded pre-code: the decl-site placement + NOT swapping the init-derivation wholesale [brace-init mass-regression]) + code-audit SHIP (refactor line-by-line behavior-preserving; decl-site correct; key matches; + a deferral-boundary pin). Witness = the ASSIGNMENT form `int(*p)[n]; p=b; p[i][j]`; the off-diagonal `p[1][0]`==77 (not transposed) on all 3 legs; `c99_vla_ptr`тЖТ42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64); 635/635 ├Ч3 + sqlite ├Ч3. **DEFERRED (both fail-loud):** the INIT form `int(*p)[n]=b` (`D-CSUBSET-VLA-PTR-INIT-FORM-TYPING` тАФ the init node is pre-stamped decayed, defeating the init-compat derivation; pinned red-on-disable) + `p+j` pointer-arith (a non-single-root base тЖТ `:4746`). тШЕ **C4a-PARAM тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-13** тАФ a PARAMETER `int (*p)[n]` (+ the adjusted `int a[][n]`) RUNS: the callee `p[i][j]` scales by the RUNTIME pointee row size frozen ONCE in the callee prologue from the incoming `n`; a ptr-to-VLA param emits only a FIXED 8-byte stride slot (NO `sub_sp_reg`) so the callee is NOT leaf-restricted (CALLS freely) and a genuine VLA arg `int b[K][n]` decays + type-checks. GAP 1b = **Option B**: a DISTINCT `paramDecay` bool threaded through `applyDeclaratorSuffix`/`directDeclaredType`/`declaratorDeclaredType` (checked FIRST at the nullopt decision site) builds a present-length PARAM `[n]` as `vlaArray(-2)` [then `adjustArrayToPointer` top-strips a plain `int a[n]` param to `int*` unchanged], while the struct-field FAM path still passes `allowFlexibleArray` (NOT paramDecay) тЖТ BYTE-IDENTICAL (`StructFieldVlaSoleMemberStillFailsLoud` pin). GAP 2 param = the stride store in the prologue param-loop scalar arm (`n` is param 0, placed before `p` тЖТ no hoist hazard). FIX-3 = the `int a[][n]` `captureVlaSize` leading-decayed-suffix skip (`skip = numSuffixes тИТ pointeeArrayDepth`, Ptr-only). Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (config rows DISJOINT; paramDecay-first ordering the crux) + code-audit SHIP (VERIFIED-CLEAN тАФ paramDecay only true via the single `param` config row; struct-FAM byte-identical; plain-decay preserved; tests red-on-disable). `c99_vla_ptr_param`тЖТ42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64); 636/636 ├Ч3 + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч3; pins `ParamPtrToVlaAcceptsAndVlaArgDecays`+5 + new anchor `-FIXED-ARRAY-ARG-COMPAT` (a fixed `int b[2][2]` arg тЖТ `ptr(array)`тЙа`ptr(vlaArray)` тЖТ S0003 fail-loud, stricter-than-C leniency deferred). тШЕ **C4b тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-14 тАФ VLA typedef `typedef int R[n]; R a;` RUNS** (+ `R a; R b;` shared freeze ┬╖ multi-dim `R[n][m]`/`R[5][n]`/`R[n][5]` ┬╖ multi-declarator `R a,b;` ┬╖ `const R a;`). C99 ┬з6.7.7p2: `n` evaluated ONCE at the typedef + FROZEN; every use allocates that frozen size (a probe mutating `n=100` AFTER the typedef keeps `sizeof b==12`, not 400). Mechanism (reuses C1b/C2/C3/C4a substrate тАФ semantic tier had ZERO core gaps): **(A)** a NEW `SymbolRecord::vlaTypedefOrigin` (aтЖТR) set in `resolveDeclTypesPost` under the design-audit I1 gate `declTy==headTy && (isVlaArray||typeContainsVla)(headTy)` тАФ this gate EXCLUDES `R a[m]` (extra dim) / `R *p` (Ptr) for free; the head-descent resolves the alias via the SAME `scopes.lookup(here,name)` the type resolver used. **(B)** HIR тАФ `lowerTypeDecl` captures R's OWN `[n]` (`captureVlaSize(R)`), `lowerVarLikeInto` records aтЖТR into a NEW `typedefVlaOriginBySymbol` side-table + SKIPS `captureVlaSize(a)` (avoids H0009). **(C)** MIR тАФ the `HirKind::TypeDecl` no-op тЖТ `computeVlaByteSize(R, freeze=true)` freezes R's per-level slots ONCE in program order (evaluates `n` once). **(D)** `vlaAllocaFromTypedef` COPIES only R's FROZEN level-slots (`vlaStrideSlot.find`, skip misses тАФ I3) into a's own `(a.v,levelTy.v)` keys + sizes a's alloca from level-0's whole-object total. тШЕ The #1 hoist hazard (C4a-local CRITICAL-2 class) does NOT apply тАФ the entry-alloca hoist SKIPS VLA locals (`aggregateByteSize` nullopts) so a's copy-down emits at the VarDecl site AFTER R's freeze (design-audit independently VERIFIED at `hir_to_mir.cpp:9190`). Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES (тШЕ I1 crux: my original token-keyed capture WOULDN'T FIRE [the `identDeclHead` is Internal] тЖТ moved to `resolveDeclTypesPost`; + I2 multi-dim runtime-inner test, I3 enumerate-frozen-only, I4 own-suffix fail-loud, I5 jumped-overтЖТloud) + code-audit SHIP-WITH-FIXES (NO CRITICAL, no silent seam тАФ freeze-once + the copy-down key-match hand-verified by simulation; folded: the CHAINED VLA typedef `typedef R S;` now fails loud CLEANLY via the `vlaTypedefOrigin` discriminator [`D-CSUBSET-VLA-TYPEDEF-CHAINED`] instead of a C4b-introduced confusing generic `internal:` H0009). Orchestrator-verified INDEPENDENTLY on pe64: basicтЖТ42, freeze-once (n=100 after typedef тЖТ `sizeof b==12`), two-use, multi-dim off-diagonal `c[1][0]`тЙа`c[0][1]`, all deferred shapes loud. `c99_vla_typedef`тЖТ42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64); 637/637 ├Ч3 + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч3; pins `VlaTypedefFreezesSizeAtTypeDeclStatement`/`VlaTypedefObjectAllocaLoadsFrozenSizeNotReLoweredN` + 4 fail-loud (stacked/own-suffix/ptr/chained). DEFERRED fail-loud: stacked-suffix (`typedef int R[5]; R a[n]` / `typedef int R[n]; R a[m]`) + ptr-to-VLA typedef (`typedef int (*P)[n]; P p`) + chained (`typedef R S`). тШЕ **C4c тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-14 тАФ array-parameter `static`/cv-qualifier/`[*]` syntax RUNS** (`int f(int a[static 3])`, `[const n]`, `[restrict]`, `[const static N]`, `int f(int a[*])` тАФ ALL decay to a bare pointer, ZERO new codegen). Parse-permissive + a `paramDecay`-gated `S_ArrayParamQualifierNonParameter` (0xE054) fail-loud for a NON-param use (the `typeSpecifierSeq`тЖТ`S_InvalidTypeSpecifierCombination` pattern; ┬зB Option A over forking a param-only sub-grammar). Shared `arraySuffixBoundNode` skips the leading markers to locate the bound (4 sites). тШЕ The `[*]` form (`D-CSUBSET-VLA-PARAM-STAR`, CLOSED) needed TWO user ┬зB decisions тЖТ a CORE parser-engine capability (schema compiler honors `speculative` on inline repeat-alts) + `arrayStarSuffix` + a guard-less `fnSuffixTail` (the fnSuffix commit-guard would else fire spuriously in the speculative suffix). Deferred: const/volatile pointer-qualifier ENFORCEMENT (`-PARAM-QUAL-ENFORCEMENT`, lenient), outermost-only static (`-PARAM-STATIC-OUTERMOST`, lenient), unnamed abstract `int f(int[*])` (`-PARAM-STAR-ABSTRACT`) тАФ each fail-loud/lenient, never a miscompile. Design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES + code-audit SHIP (engine fix opt-in, all 6 fnSuffix sites, no silent seam); all 3 languages (toy/tsql/c-subset) ctest-verified byte-identical for the engine change; `c99_array_param_static`тЖТ42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64); 63x/63x ├Ч3 + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч3. **C5 тЬЕ LANDED 2026-07-14 тАФ block-scope teardown RUNS on all 3 legs: NEW MIR ops StackSave/StackRestore тЖТ the existing `sp_copy` + a NEW `sp_restore` LIR op (result:none, [SP,saved], both target JSONs); a `vlaScopeStack` of per-VLA-decl watermarks; teardown on natural-exit/back-edge + break + continue + goto (FULL general: forward/outward/backward-over-a-decl) + for-init-VLA at loop-exit (not the back-edge тАФ it persists across iterations); `H_VlaJumpIntoScope` 0xF018 entry-ban (C99 6.8.6.1p1 тАФ the dominance guarantor for legal gotos); computed-`goto *expr` crossing a VLA scope + multi-declarator for-init (`D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-MULTIDECL`) fail-loud; StackSave/Restore join opcodeClobbersMemory (no load sunk into freed stack); inliner bails on teardown funcs; MIR-verifier pairing+dominance check; design-audit GO-WITH-FIXES + code-audit SHIP (zero silent leak/UAF), empirical battery incl. the back-edge-no-UAF clobber witness; 639/639 ├Ч3 legs + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч3. тШЕ THE FULL VLA ARC (C1aтАУC5) IS COMPLETE.**  (Prior: C1b handoff: the `_BitInt`-bound widening arithmetic + Mem2Reg-must-not-drop a side-effecting size (`int a[f()]`) are C1b-verified. тАФ a FULL VLA IMPLEMENTATION arc (user directive 2026-07-11: after the `_BitInt` arc completes, before FC18; SUPERSEDES the prior no-VLA decision recorded below).** Until that arc lands, DSS has NO variable-length-array support (C99 ┬з6.7.6.2 VLAs: a runtime array bound fails LOUD тАФ `S_NonConstantArrayLength`; no silent decay, no guessed length) and now DECLARES it conformantly: **`__STDC_NO_VLA__ = 1`** rides the c-subset `predefinedMacros` config row (C11 6.10.8.3 / C23 6.10.9.3 REQUIRE a VLA-less implementation to define it тАФ the conformance-HONEST line given `__STDC_VERSION__ = 202311L`). A conforming program's `#ifdef __STDC_NO_VLA__` now selects its fixed-size fallback (preprocessor pin `FC175StdcNoVlaDefined`, red-on-disable). The `c99_vla` battery probe stays RED by design. TRIGGER: **FIRED (user directive 2026-07-11)** тАФ a full VLA arc (`int a[n]` runtime length + `int a[expr]` + the C99 ┬з6.7.6.2 variably-modified-type substrate: runtime stack allocation + runtime `sizeof`) is SCHEDULED after the `_BitInt` arc completes, before FC18; its own designed multi-cycle work (TLS/`_BitInt`-scale), never a drive-by. тШЕ WHEN IT LANDS `__STDC_NO_VLA__` MUST be REMOVED from the c-subset `predefinedMacros` (a VLA-supporting implementation must NOT define it) + the `FC175StdcNoVlaDefined` preprocessor pin flipped/retired + the `c99_vla` battery probe flips GREEN. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-FIXED-ARRAY-ARG-COMPAT` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C4a-param; NOT src-cited тАФ test/example-cited only).** A FIXED multi-dim array argument (`int b[2][2]`) decays to `ptr(array(int,2))`, which is a DISTINCT interned type from the ptr-to-VLA param's `ptr(vlaArray(int))` (the `-2` VLA sentinel vs a constant length), so the arg-decay compare (`type_rules.hpp:371`, exact `lhsElem[0]==rhsElem[0]`) REJECTS it тАФ `S_TypeMismatch` (`S0003`), a CLEAN fail-loud, NEVER a silent miscompile. C 6.7.6.2p6 makes a VLA-typed pointer and a compatible fixed-array pointer assignment-compatible at runtime, so DSS is STRICTER than C here (rejects valid C), but the -2 sentinel keeps every accepted call genuinely VLA-shaped (no wrong-stride risk). A genuine VLA arg `int b[K][n]` (rows `int[n]`) DOES decay + type-check (`c99_vla_ptr_param` + the `ParamPtrToVlaAcceptsAndVlaArgDecays` pin); this anchor is only the fixed-arg leniency. Closing work: admit a fixed `array(int,K)` element against a `vlaArray` element in the decay compare when C-compatible (runtime bound check deferred to the callee). Trigger: a real need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-TYPEDEF-CHAINED` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened 2026-07-14, VLA C4b; src-cited `cst_to_hir.cpp` `lowerTypeDecl`).** A CHAINED VLA typedef тАФ `typedef int R[n]; typedef R S;` тАФ where S aliases a VLA typedef with NO own `[n]` suffix (its VLA-ness comes from the head alias R, so the semantic I1 gate stamps `S.vlaTypedefOrigin`). C4b freezes the ORIGINAL typedef's size from its own captured `[n]`; a chained alias has no `[n]` to capture. `lowerTypeDecl` recognizes the chained form by its OWN array-suffix ABSENCE (`declaratorHasArraySuffix` тАФ a self-contained HIR-tier check; the `vlaTypedefOrigin` field is stamped OBJECTS-ONLY, `DeclarationKind::Variable`, so it is never written on a typedef symbol) and FAILS LOUD CLEANLY ("a typedef that aliases a variable-length-array typedef is not yet supported", H at HIR) тАФ NEVER a silent miscompile. Pre-C4b `typedef R S;` was a no-op that compiled but any USE (`S a;`) failed loud; C4b moved the (clean) error to the chained typedef itself. Closing work: freeze S's slots by copying R's frozen slots at S's own TypeDecl statement (reuse the C4b copy-down slot-copy, minus the object alloca), then S-objects copy S's slots тАФ a modest extension of injection (C). Trigger: a real need. Pinned `ChainedVlaTypedefFailsLoud` (red-on-disable). |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-NONLEAF-CALL-FRAME` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C1b).** A VLA function that ALSO makes a call (`hasVla && hasCalls`) or uses `va_start` FAILS LOUD `L_VlaNonLeafFrameUnsupported` (0xB00E, unsuppressable) тАФ NOT a miscompile. Root cause (design-audit CRITICAL-4): the fixed outgoing-args area is reserved at a compile-time `[sp+off]`, but a VLA's `sub sp,<size>` moves SP тЖТ outgoing args would land INSIDE the VLA (clobber), and switching them to the frame pointer breaks the ABI (the `call` runs at the moved SP). NO base choice is correct in the fixed-reservation model. Closing work: the NON-LEAF VLA frame model тАФ a ┬зB-worthy design (outgoing-args placed BELOW where the VLA goes, or a separate outgoing-args pointer; or over-allocate + a two-region frame). Trigger: a real need / a dedicated cycle. Cited `lir_callconv.cpp` + tests (red-on-disable). |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-WIN64-UNWIND` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C1b; design-audit IMPORTANT-4; src-cited `lir_callconv.cpp`).** A pe64 VLA function's `.pdata`/`.xdata` reproduces the FIXED prologue (`sub sp,F` + pushes; `rsp = established + F`) тАФ it does NOT reflect the VLA's dynamic `sub sp,<size>` nor the FP-setup, so an exception/stack-walk THROUGH a pe64 VLA frame mis-unwinds. тШЕ C1b code-audit fold (IMPORTANT-3): a VLA function that OWNS a `__try` (`hasVla && isSehParent` тАФ its symbol is a `sehFuncletParents` parent, PE-only-populated so agnostic) now FAILS LOUD `L_VlaNonLeafFrameUnsupported` (closes the `hasCalls`-isn't-a-sufficient-proxy gap тАФ a `__try` around a trapping NON-call op like `#DE` has `hasCalls=false`; pinned `VlaSehParentFunctionFailsLoud`). RESIDUAL (narrow, still open): a leaf VLA function that TRAPS (no own `__try`) whose CALLER has a `__try` тЖТ the OS unwinds through the imprecise `.pdata`; needs a trapping op + a caller handler (the examples are safe тАФ normal return). Also `isSehParent` assumes every `.pdata`-emitting `__try` produces a funclet-parent entry (true for x64 `__except`/`__finally`). Closing work: emit VLA-aware Win64 unwind (UWOP_SET_FPREG + dynamic-alloca codes). Trigger: the residual SEH case or a real need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-WIN64-STACK-PROBE` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C1b code-audit MINOR-1; NOT src-cited тАФ tracked-only).** On Windows, the prologue `stack_probe` guard-page walk covers only the FIXED frame `F`; a VLA's runtime `sub_sp_reg` descends further with NO page-walk, so a LARGE VLA (past the guard page) тЖТ a stack-overflow access violation. SAFE-ish: a FAIL-STOP (a clean AV / `#SS`), NEVER a miscompile; the `n=6` examples are far under a page. Closing work: emit a `stack_probe`-style page-walk on the dynamic `sub_sp_reg` for the PE profile (`cc.stackProbePageBytes>0`). Trigger: a large-VLA Windows need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-PARAM-STAR` | тЬЕ **CLOSED 2026-07-14** (opened 2026-07-14, VLA C4c; landed SAME-CYCLE via the ┬зB core parser-engine change; src-cited `grammar_schema_json.cpp` + `c-subset.lang.json` arrayStarSuffix + `examples/c-subset/c99_array_param_static`). The bare unspecified-size `*` array-PARAMETER form `int a[*]` (C99 ┬з6.7.6.3, a prototype VLA-parameter size marker) now PARSES, decays to a bare pointer, and RUNS. LANDED via (1) a CORE schema-compiler fix тАФ `grammar_schema_json.cpp`'s `"repeat"` arm now propagates `speculative`/`lookahead` from an inline `{repeat:{alt:тАж}}` body onto the loopEntry AltChoice the parser dispatches at + `setSkipBranch(cont)` (reusing the D-PARSE-SPECULATIVE-OPTIONAL skip-branch machinery; opt-in, byte-identical for every non-speculative repeat) тАФ and (2) a DISTINCT `arrayStarSuffix` = `[BracketOpen,StarOp,BracketClose]` grammar rule listed BEFORE `arrayDeclSuffix` in the now-speculative suffix repeat (directDeclarator/abstractDirectDeclarator), so `[*]` matches it while a real bound (`[N]` / the deref-VLA `[*p]`, both still compile) rolls back CLEANLY to `arrayDeclSuffix`. Semantic: `applyDeclaratorSuffix`'s `arrayStarSuffixRule` arm (new OPTIONAL `declarators.arrayStarSuffixRule` config role) builds an unspecified-size array that `adjustArrayToPointer` decays like a bare `[]` for a param; a NON-param `[*]` fails loud S_ArrayParamQualifierNonParameter (0xE054, the SAME paramDecay gate). Pinned: `SpeculativeInlineRepeatAltBacktracksToSiblingBranch` + `SpeculativeInlineRepeatSeqAltComposes` (engine, red-on-disable), `ArrayParamStarFormDecaysCleanNonParamFailsLoud` (semantic), `DerefSizedVlaStillCompiles` (the `[*p]` regression guard), + `gstar(int a[*])` in `c99_array_param_static` (runtime witness, exit 42 ├Ч3 legs). тШЕ CODE-AUDIT WRINKLE (folded, not in the original agent draft): making the suffix repeat SPECULATIVE dragged `fnSuffix`'s `requireKnownType` commit guard into the probe тЖТ it fired spuriously at the suffix position, breaking `int f()` / casts / every function declarator. FIX = a guard-less `fnSuffixTail` clone (grammar-identical to `fnSuffix`, no `commitRequiresTypeName`) for the suffix position, recognized via a new OPTIONAL `fnSuffixTailRule` config role at the 6 fn-suffix sites (`isFnSuffixRule`); the base-position `fnSuffix` (fnSuffix-vs-parenDeclarator triage + the cross-file-typedef ambiguous-record) is UNCHANGED. Corpus golden `mini_calc.c.tree` + the `test_c_subset` hand-built pin regenerated (named-fn suffix тЖТ `fnSuffixTail`). SCOPE: only `directDeclarator` (the NAMED `int f(int a[*])`) is speculative; **`abstractDirectDeclarator` stays NON-speculative** тАФ the UNNAMED abstract `int f(int[*])` is DEFERRED (`D-CSUBSET-VLA-PARAM-STAR-ABSTRACT`, fail-loud parse; the abstract cast-triage interaction is off-scope). EMERGENT multi-dim `[*]` combos are SAFE (code-audit): `int a[*][3]` decays like `int a[][3]` (accepted, fixed inner stride), `int a[n][*]`/`[*][*]` тЖТ S0003 fail-loud (NEVER a silent stride; pinned `ArrayStarOuterDecaysInnerStarFailsLoud`). Independent code-audit VERDICT SHIP (engine fix opt-in; all 6 fnSuffix sites covered; no silent seam). |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-PARAM-STAR-ABSTRACT` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened 2026-07-14, VLA C4c).** The UNNAMED abstract form of `[*]` тАФ `int f(int[*])` (a star-modifier on an ABSTRACT param declarator, no name) тАФ is DEFERRED: `abstractDirectDeclarator`'s suffix repeat is kept NON-speculative (only `directDeclarator`'s, for the NAMED `int f(int a[*])`, was made speculative), so an abstract `[*]` FAILS LOUD at parse (P-error), NEVER a miscompile. Reason: making `abstractDirectDeclarator` speculative interacts with the cast-vs-value type-name triage (the abstract declarator is the cast/sizeof type-name tail) тАФ a separate, riskier change than the named-param path; the named `int f(int a[*])` is the common form. Closing work: extend the `fnSuffixTail`/arrayStarSuffix speculative-suffix treatment to `abstractDirectDeclarator` with a cast-triage regression sweep. Trigger: a real need / a dedicated cycle. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-SIZEOF-TYPEFORM` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C2; NOT src-cited тАФ tracked-only).** VLA C2 makes `sizeof <vla-OBJECT>` (`sizeof a`, the value form) a runtime value. Two other composite/type sizeof shapes are FAIL-LOUD (never a silent wrong size), each deferred: **(1) the TYPE-NAME form `sizeof(int[n])`** тАФ a VLA type-name whose `n` C 6.5.3.4p2 evaluates FRESH at the sizeof (a DISTINCT mechanism from the object's decl-frozen slot). Not even constructible in the c-subset today: the `sizeof(TYPE)` grammar rejects an abstract array declarator `int[n]` at PARSE (`P0009`), so it can't reach a `vlaArray`. **(2) a COMPOSITE OPERAND** whose value is a VLA тАФ `sizeof(b, a)` (comma), `sizeof(c ? a : b)` (ternary) тАФ where C applies lvalue conversion (arrayтЖТpointer decay) so the answer is the POINTER size, NOT the object's frozen size. The c-subset does not model comma/ternary array decay (a PRE-EXISTING gap affecting even fixed arrays), so `vlaObjectOperandSymbol` rejects composites тЖТ they FAIL LOUD `H0009` (pin `SizeofOfCompositeVlaOperandFailsLoud`). Closing work: (1) accept `int[n]` in the sizeof type-name grammar + a fresh-evaluate `n*stride` MIR arm; (2) model composite-operand arrayтЖТpointer decay (a broader semantic fix). Trigger: a real need for either. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-PTR-INIT-FORM-TYPING` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened 2026-07-13, VLA C4a-local; src-cited `semantic_analyzer.cpp`).** The NATURAL init form `int (*p)[n] = b` (array-to-pointer-decay INITIALIZER of a pointer-to-VLA local) does NOT compile тАФ it FAILS LOUD `S_TypeMismatch` (`S0003`), a CLEAN rejection, NEVER a silent miscompile. ROOT CAUSE: the Pass-2 init-type derivation (`semantic_analyzer.cpp:7564`, the single-child `typeAt` walk) yields `b`'s DECAYED pointer type, not the raw `array(vlaArray(int),2)` the `Ptr<vlaArray> тЖР array(array)` decay compare (`type_rules.hpp:371`) needs. A guarded `subtreeType` override (design CRITICAL-1) was ATTEMPTED but proved INERT: `subtreeType` short-circuits on the SAME stamped node (`:8874`), and the initializer identifier `b` is pre-stamped with its decayed type by an earlier pass (the same stamp that types `p`'s body subscript non-VLA тАФ the "body-typing wrinkle"). Verified empirically: the guarded override left `int(*p)[n]=b` still `S0003`, while brace/struct/nested/scalar/decay/string inits stayed clean, the `int(*p)[5]=b_vla` mismatch still rejected, and `int(*p)[5]=b_fixed` still accepted тАФ so it was reverted (no half-working form shipped). The C4a-local WITNESS uses the ASSIGNMENT form `int (*p)[n]; p = b; p[i][j]` (passes assignability + RUNS the runtime-stride path тАФ `c99_vla_ptr` example + a semantic accept pin). Closing work: stop pre-decaying the ptr-to-VLA initializer's stamp (or a dedicated array-decay-init derivation that recovers the raw array shape) so `= b` matches the same decay compare the assignment form uses. Trigger: a real need for the init form / a dedicated cycle. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-SCOPE` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-14 (VLA C5) тАФ a for-INIT VLA (`for (int a[n]; тАж; )`) now gets FULL for-scope block teardown.** (This anchor was briefly a fail-loud DEFERRAL earlier in this SAME cycle; the code-audit correctly flagged that gate as a REGRESSION тАФ a top-level for-init VLA compiled + RAN pre-C5, function-scoped + epilogue-freed тАФ and it contradicted the user OPTION-C full-general-teardown directive, so it was replaced with real teardown before the cycle closed. The prior "leaks per-iteration" rationale was factually wrong for a top-level for-init.) MECHANISM: a for-init VLA is declared ONCE at loop entry and PERSISTS across all iterations; C5 frees it ONLY on the loop-EXIT edges, NEVER on the back-edge (freeing it there = a use-after-free of `a[]` in iteration 2+). `emitStackSaveForVla` accepts a `ForStmt` scope node (single-declarator for-init: `hir.parent(declNode) == ForStmt`); the For driver captures the VLA watermark BEFORE the init (`f.vlaBase`), so the for-init frame sits at exactly `vlaBase` and the BODY block's per-iteration teardown (whose watermark is DEEPER, captured after the init) can never reach it (the depth bookkeeping keeps them distinct). The For driver emits ONE StackRestore at the loop EXIT block (the merge of the cond-false edge + every `break` тАФ a break already restored the body VLAs to the post-init watermark, so both predecessors arrive with SP at the post-init level); `continue` frees only body VLAs (post-init watermark), keeping the for-init live; a `goto` OUT of the loop restores the for-init via the ancestry walk (`vlaFrameEnclosesLabel` treats a ForStmt scope as whole-for). Pins: `ForInitVlaEmitsForScopeTeardownAtLoopExitOnly` (for-init-only тЖТ 1 save + 1 restore, NONE on the back-edge) + `ForInitAndBodyVlaTornDownAtDistinctPoints` (for-init + body VLA тЖТ 2 restores in DIFFERENT blocks) + the `c99_vla_loop` phase-6 runtime witness (a for-init VLA in a 400000-iteration outer loop, written at k=0 + read at k>0 тАФ catches BOTH a back-edge-free [use-after-free тЖТ wrong total] AND a leak [overflow]). Multi-declarator for-init VLAs remain deferred тЖТ `D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-MULTIDECL`. |
| `D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-MULTIDECL` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened 2026-07-14, VLA C5; src-cited `hir_to_mir.cpp` `emitStackSaveForVla`).** A VLA in a MULTI-declarator for-init clause (`for (int a[n], b[m]; тАж)`) is FAILED LOUD at HIRтЖТMIR (H at the VarDecl), NEVER a silent early-free. WHY: cst_to_hir wraps a multi-declarator for-init in a `Block` of VarDecls (whose parent is the ForStmt); that Block is lowered via `lowerForClauseNode` during For phase 0 and drains fully (its own Block-scope teardown fires) BEFORE the loop header/body тАФ so a VLA there would be freed at the END OF THE INIT, before the first iteration (a use-after-free for the whole loop). The single-declarator for-init (`for (int a[n]; тАж)`, the common form, `hir.parent == ForStmt`) gets full for-scope teardown (D-CSUBSET-VLA-FOR-INIT-SCOPE). Detection: `emitStackSaveForVla` recognizes the wrapper by `hir.kind(scopeNode)==Block && hir.parent(scopeNode)==ForStmt && hir.forInit(that)==scopeNode`. Closing work: give the for-init wrapper Block for-scope (not block-scope) teardown semantics тАФ push its VLAs onto the vlaScopeStack without the Block driver's per-exit restore, and free them at the For driver's loop-exit. Trigger: a real multi-declarator-for-init-VLA need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-FOR-INIT-SCOPE` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-13 (user-reported bug).** C99 6.8.5.3: each `for`-statement opens its OWN scope, so SIBLING/nested `for (int i = ...)` loops re-declaring the same loop name are VALID (the 2nd `i` is a distinct object in a distinct scope). ROOT CAUSE: `forStmt` was ABSENT from the `c-subset.lang.json` `scopes` list тЖТ the for-init `int i` LEAKED into the enclosing BLOCK scope тЖТ two sibling `for(int i)` collided (2nd loop: uses reported undeclared `S0002` + decl reported unused `S000A`; braced or braceless, no binary emitted). Slipped past the empirical battery (declare-then-assign convention) + sqlite (C89-style loop vars). тШЕ FIX = add `"forStmt"` to the config `scopes` list тАФ config-driven + AGNOSTIC, the ROOT-CAUSE fix (not a name-reuse special-case): the ONE shared `nodeOpensChildScope` predicate (`semantic_analyzer.cpp:533`, `scopeByRule` from `cfg.scopes:926`) drives all 3 passes (Pass1 mint `:4046`, Pass1.5 `:6406`, Pass2 `:8074`) тЖТ the new for-scope is re-derived CONSISTENTLY. Also correctly fixes shadow-then-use-outer (`int i=5; for(int iтАж){} use(i)` тАФ was a false collision). Independent design-audit + code-audit SHIP (per-pass consistency + cond/incr/body see `i` in braced+braceless + out-of-scope-after-for FAILS LOUD `S0001` + NO leak-reliance regression across examples/tests/sqlite; while/do/switch deliberately NOT added тАФ no init-declaring clause + their braced bodies already open a `block` scope). Pins `SiblingForInitSameNameHaveDistinctScopes` + `ForInitVariableOutOfScopeAfterForRejects` (red-on-disable) + runnable `for_init_sibling_scope`тЖТ42 (x86_64-elf/arm64-qemu[release]/pe64), 634/634 ├Ч3 legs + sqlite 3.54.0/CRUD-42 ├Ч3. |

A MAJOR: N-bit arithmetic semantics (promotion rules DIFFER from standard integers тАФ no integer promotion above int for _BitInt), storage/ABI layout per target, and codegen for non-native widths. Trigger: FC18+ full-C23 arc prioritization; requires its own designed cycle with the type-lattice substrate (a new TypeKind axis or a width-parameterized integer core) decided at a ┬зB gate. |

BLR; ZERO new encoder). тШЕ Mach-O is a THIRD TLS model тАФ it stores a REAL descriptorVA in symbolVa (ordinary reloc kinds 2/3), so it OPTS OUT of the tlsSymbols/XOR backstop [CRIT-1] and runs only the decoupled arm-(a) data-item belt on a macho-local tlsDataSymbols set (`&t`тЖТS0048 + K_RelocationKindMismatch; plain thread_local links clean); word2 reuses the addTlsTemplateOffsets ARITHMETIC only (0-based, no TCB bias) [CRIT-2]; one `__tlv_bootstrap` bind per descriptor at word0 [CRIT-3, exact two-underscore name verified vs Apple's threadLocalVariables.c]; over-aligned macho TLS >16 тЖТ fail-loud K_ThreadLocalOveralignedForFormat 0x8016 [M-3, `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-MACHO-OVERALIGN`]. | `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-DYNAMIC-TLS` | тП│ **GATED (recorded 2026-07-10 тАФ the ┬зB-accepted arc caveat).** The arc is LOCAL-EXEC only: the exec's PT_TLS block joins glibc's static-TLS set allocated for EVERY thread at pthread_create, so worker threads stay correct absent dlopen (the C1 pthread witness proves it). dlopen'd modules carrying TLS need dynamic TLS (`__tls_get_addr`/TLSDESC). | `D-CSUBSET-DECL-QUALIFIER-BEFORE-STORAGE-CLASS` | тП│ **GATED (recorded 2026-07-10, surfaced by TLS C1 witnesses).** C 6.7p2 lets declaration specifiers appear in ANY order, but the c-subset grammar requires storage-class-first: `const static int x;` / `const thread_local int y;` (qualifier BEFORE the storage-class) are LOUD parse errors (P-tier), while `static const` / `thread_local const` work. Pre-existing class (predates TLS тАФ `const static` fails at pre-arc HEAD identically). Loud, never silent. Trigger: the FC18 conformance sweep or real-code need. |

fn(); ret 1` тАФ an indirect Call through a ptr<void> callee, D-CSUBSET-FNPTR-INDIRECT-CALL; minted above the recipe max, emitted only when a call_once recipe is present; all 3 Args emitted so ordinals stay contiguous тАФ DCE keeps Args as roots, D-OPT-VARIADIC-RELEASE-ARGINDEX) ┬╖ **thrd_join = the first MULTI-block recipe** (entry `WaitForSingleObject(t,INFINITE); CondBr(res!=0)` тЖТ then `GetExitCodeThread(t,(LPDWORD)res via Bitcast)` тЖТ join `CloseHandle(t); ret thrd_success`, built with the plain MirBuilder createBlock/addCondBr/addBr API + `rederiveStructCfMarkers(mir)` module-wide after finish() тАФ the merge-path per-pass MirVerifier enforces stored==derived, codegen ignores markers so the single-CU post-optimize path is idempotent). threads.json += thrd_create/call_once DUAL entries + thrd_join pe entry; | `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TIMED` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened by C11-threads Cycle 1, 2026-07-14).** The TIME-conversion pe recipes are elf-FFI-only this cycle: `mtx_timedlock`/`cnd_timedwait` (abstimeтЖТrelative-ms) AND `thrd_sleep` (a `struct timespec` read тАФ its Windows time_t/long field widths are UNVERIFIED from a Windows host, and a wrong offset is a SILENT MISCOMPILE, so thrd_sleep carries only an elf entry rather than risk a wrong struct read). A pe reference to any of these fails loud (undefined symbol тАФ not injected on pe). | `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE` | тП│ **GATED тАФ best-effort, named (opened by C11-threads Cycle 1, 2026-07-14).** On pe64 EVERY mtx_t is a Win32 CRITICAL_SECTION, which is ALWAYS recursion-capable тАФ so `mtx_init(&m, mtx_plain)` yields a mutex that a same-thread re-lock will NOT deadlock (C11 leaves a plain mutex's same-thread-relock behavior UNDEFINED, so a conforming program must not rely on the deadlock; a recursive-capable plain mutex is harmless for conforming use). The `type` argument is accepted + IGNORED (mtx_plain/mtx_recursive/mtx_timed all map to the same CRITICAL_SECTION). Not a miscompile тАФ a deliberate, documented Win32 semantic. Closing work: if strict mtx_plain non-recursive semantics are needed, a manual owner-thread/recursion-count guard around the CRITICAL_SECTION. Trigger: a real program relying on plain-mutex same-thread-relock deadlock. | Manual non-recursive guard if strict semantics needed. | [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-HEADER]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TSS-DTOR` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened by C11-threads Cycle 2 code-audit, 2026-07-14).** Cycle 2 upgraded `thrd_start_t` to the concrete `ptr<fn(ptr<void>)->i32>` (so a conforming `thrd_create(&t,worker,&arg)` accepts a bare function CAST-FREE тАФ fnтЖТvoid* is NOT an implicit conversion in DSS тЖТ S0003), but `tss_dtor_t` (the `tss_create` destructor param) stayed the Cycle-1 opaque `ptr<void>`. A NULL destructor coerces fine (the `c11_threads` Cycle-1 witness passes `0` тАФ a null pointer constant coerces to any pointer), but a NON-NULL real destructor `void dtor(void*)` would hit S0003 at the call site. A pre-existing Cycle-1 residual (config/descriptor-only тАФ cited nowhere in src), out of scope for Cycle 2. Closing work: type `tss_dtor_t` as `ptr<fn(ptr<void>)->void>` (the thrd_start_t mirror, threads.json) + a witness passing a real tss destructor that runs at thread exit. Trigger: a real non-NULL `tss_create` destructor, or the FC18 conformance sweep. | Type tss_dtor_t as a concrete fn-ptr + witness a real destructor. | [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-HEADER]] |
| `D-LK3-MACHO-ARM64-OBJECT` | тП│ **GATED тАФ high (opened 2026-07-14, user-flagged during the macho-threads cycle).** ASYMMETRY: `src/dss-config/object-formats/` ships a Mach-O relocatable-OBJECT (MH_OBJECT `.o`) format for x86_64-darwin (`macho64-x86_64-darwin.format.json`, plan 14 LK3) but NOT for arm64-darwin тАФ only the arm64 EXEC (`macho64-arm64-darwin-exec`). So `arm64:macho64-arm64-darwin` (an object-emission target `test_target_spec.cpp` already PARSES) has no backing format тЖТ fail-loud "format not found". Mostly mechanical: the arm64 EXEC format ALREADY declares the full `ARM64_RELOC_*` vocabulary WITH the MH_OBJECT `nativeId` packing `(r_type<<28)|(r_length<<25)|(r_pcrel<<24)`, so the object format mirrors the x86_64 object's MH_OBJECT structure (header filetype=MH_OBJECT, section_64, nlist_64, relocation_info) + the arm64 cputype (0x0100000C) + those relocations. NOT a file-drop: MH_OBJECT emission is a DIFFERENT macho.cpp code path than MH_EXECUTE (writes real relocation_info records vs applies relocs in-place), UNVERIFIED for arm64 тАФ closing MUST ship a witness that emits a correct arm64 `.o` (assert mach_header + relocation_info records; ideally the in-tree linker consumes it). Orthogonal to threads (threads uses the exec format). Closing work: author `macho64-arm64-darwin.format.json` + an arm64 MH_OBJECT emission test/corpus. Trigger: user-directed NEXT cycle (2026-07-14). | New arm64 macho object format + a `.o`-emission witness. | [[D-LK3-RODATA]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO-MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE` | тП│ **GATED тАФ best-effort, named (opened by C11-threads macho, 2026-07-14).** The macho pthread `mtx_init` recipe passes a NULL mutexattr тЖТ PTHREAD_MUTEX_NORMAL (non-recursive), which is CORRECT for `mtx_plain` (the tested + common mode) but means `mtx_init(&m, mtx_recursive)` on macho yields a NON-recursive mutex тАФ a same-thread re-lock **DEADLOCKS at runtime**. This is a SILENT behavioral divergence (not a fail-loud, not a compile error): the mtx type is a runtime arg the single-block shim cannot statically reject. UNOBSERVABLE this cycle тАФ Cycle 1 has no `thrd_create`, so the program is single-threaded and no recursive-relock path executes. (This is the MIRROR-IMAGE of the pe wart [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE]], but the OPPOSITE direction: pe's always-recursive is benign [recursive тКЗ plain], macho's always-plain deadlocks on a recursive request.) Chose NULL-attr over an always-recursive `pthread_mutexattr_init`/`settype`/`destroy` dance because the latter needs a stack `Alloca`, breaking the pass's single-block/alloca-free invariant. Closing work: honor `mtx_recursive` via a mutexattr (once multi-block shim recipes are in scope) OR a branchless attr-select. Trigger: `thrd_create` (Cycle 2, makes it observable) or a real program using a macho recursive mutex. | Honor mtx_recursive via mutexattr (needs multi-block shim). | [[D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-PE-OVERALIGN` | тП│ **GATED тАФ trigger-gated (opened by TLS C3, 2026-07-11).** A PE `thread_local` whose required alignment exceeds the x64 loader's guaranteed TLS block-base alignment (16 bytes = MEMORY_ALLOCATION_ALIGNMENT) is FAIL-LOUD this cycle (K_ThreadLocalOveralignedForFormat in pe.cpp тАФ the IMAGE_TLS_DIRECTORY64 has no field to request block over-alignment, unlike ELF's PT_TLS p_align which C1/C2 honor). тЙд16-aligned TLS (every normal scalar/pointer/small-struct) is unaffected. Closing work: a block-base over-alignment mechanism (a padded-slot / loader-cooperative scheme) if a real >16-aligned PE TLS need (SIMD/atomics) fires. Trigger: that need. |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-PE-LARGE-ZEROFILL` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened by TLS C3, 2026-07-11).** PE TLS embeds the WHOLE per-thread block (tdata template + tbss zeros) as raw `.tls` bytes with `SizeOfZeroFill=0` тАФ correct on every thread incl. the main thread's early static TLS (the empirically-observed loader-doesn't-zero-early-main-thread-fill case; matches MSVC for small TLS), but costs file bloat for LARGE zero-init TLS. Closing work: emit a real `SizeOfZeroFill` tail once the loader-zeroing is verified safe across all thread-creation paths (incl. early main-thread). Trigger: a large zero-init PE thread_local where the bloat matters. |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-MACHO-OVERALIGN` | тП│ **GATED тАФ trigger-gated (opened by TLS C4, 2026-07-11).** A Mach-O `thread_local` whose required alignment exceeds 16 (the malloc/TLV-block-base guarantee) is FAIL-LOUD this cycle (K_ThreadLocalOveralignedForFormat 0x8016 тАФ the format-generic gate C3 introduced). macho CAN express section-align, but whether dyld over-aligns the per-thread TLV block base beyond 16 is UNVERIFIABLE without a Mac; conservative + consistent with the C3 PE gate, never a silent under-align. Closing work: verify dyld's TLV block-base over-alignment on real hardware, then align word2 + set section_64.align + lift the gate + a CI `_Alignas(32)` macho example. Trigger: that Mac-verified behavior. |
| `D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-MACHO-TDATA-RELOC` | тП│ **GATED тАФ normal (opened by TLS C4, 2026-07-11).** A reloc-bearing macho TLS template (`thread_local char *msg = "hi";` тАФ an initialized pointer into rodata inside __thread_data) FAILS LOUD via buildExecDataSection(allowItemRelocations=false). PIE-rebasing a per-thread template pointer (the loader copies the template per thread; a link-time abs pointer + a DIR64-analog rebase into a per-thread copy) is unverifiable without a Mac. Closing work: verify dyld's handling of a rebased pointer in a copied TLV template on real hardware, then allow item relocations in the __thread_data template + emit the rebase. Trigger: that Mac-verified behavior (or a real consumer needing a pointer-typed macho thread_local). |
| `D-PIPELINE-CU5-MULTIFILE-EXTERN-DATA` | тП│ **GATED тАФ TRIAGE (recorded 2026-07-10, surfaced by the TLS C1 cross-CU E2E pin).** The API-only `compileFiles`-with-N-sources surface (CU5: ONE multi-FILE unit тАФ the CLI never routes there; every multi-source CLI build goes `routesToMultiUnit`тЖТ`compileUnits`/LK11) mints a SURVIVING extern-data import for a sibling-FILE definition instead of resolving it intra-unit. For a THREAD-LOCAL extern the TLS C1 linker gate correctly fail-louds (K_FormatLacksThreadLocalSupport citing [[D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL-INITIAL-EXEC]]) тАФ LOUD, never a silent mis-bind. Whether PLAIN (non-TLS) extern data on that same CU5 path silently becomes a copy-relocation library import (vs an intra-unit resolution) was NOT isolated. Closing work: probe the non-TLS shape; either resolve sibling-file externs intra-unit on CU5 or fail loud. Trigger: any real API consumer of multi-file `compileFiles`, or the FC18 sweep. |
| `D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE` | тЬЕ **LANDED 2026-07-10 (FC17.5 cycle 2) тАФ C23 ┬з6.7.9 `auto` type inference (`auto x = expr;`).** The audit-revised design (GO-WITH-FIXES, 30 probes): a HEAD-LESS `autoInferredVarDecl` grammar rule (`localDeclSpecifiers initDeclaratorList ;`) + the `autoInferredForDecl` for-init mirror; the type is INFERRED from the sole declarator's initializer at the declaration's own Pass-1.5 visit (`resolveAutoInferredDeclaration` тЖТ the inferred type plays the head's role and the ORDINARY declarator fold applies it тАФ one proven write path). GENERIC engine capability: `DeclarationRule.inferTypeFromInitializer` (loader waives the declarator-mode head requirement for it; `head тИз infer` тЖТ C_ConflictingField; the flag on a non-declarator row тЖТ loud) + `DeclarationRule.requiredSpecifierToken` (loader-resolved token-kind NAME тАФ the constMarker idiom; never an engine keyword identity). тШЕC1 the presence gate: `static x=5;`/`register y=2;`/`alignas(4) z=9;`/`[[maybe_unused]] w=3;` parse into the headless rule and STAY errors (S_AutoInferenceInvalid) тАФ C89 implicit-int never resurrects. тШЕC2 branch order `[autoInferredVarDecl, varDecl, attributeDeclaration]` (+ the forStmt inline speculative mirror `[autoInferredForDecl, forDecl, forInitAmbig]`, gatedMarkers copied тАФ `for (static auto i=0;;)` stays S_StaticStorageInForInit): autoInferred fast-fails тЙд3 tokens on real-typed decls; varDecl is the declared-LAST structural candidate so the all-fail REPLAY (parser.cpp ~:1984) re-parses it committed with NO budget тАФ the >4096-token `static const int big[]={тАж}` pin compiles (EMPIRICALLY red under varDecl-first: P0009; sqlite's largest is 3918/4096 so the sqlite gate can't catch the cliff). тШЕC3 the full normalization: braced-single unwrap (`auto x={5};`; `{}`/`{1,2}`/designated/nested тЖТ shared S_InvalidScalarInitializer) тЖТ literal PRE-STAMP via the EXTRACTED shared `typeLiteralIfAny` chokepoint (pass2Post's literal-typing block, byte-identical тАФ at Pass 1.5 the Pass-2 blanket stamps don't exist; probe-confirmed `typeof(42) a=7; int arr[sizeof(a)];` fails S000B at HEAD = the backfill false-green the audit's p1 hit) тЖТ `subtreeType` тЖТ DECAY ArrayтЖТPtr<elem> (`auto s="str"` is char*) + FnSigтЖТPtr (`auto f=fn` callable) тЖТ REJECT LOUD Void / NullptrT (folded into [[D-CSUBSET-NULLPTR-T-DECLARABLE]]) / invalid incl. self-ref `auto x=x;` тЖТ stripVolatile (6.7.9p2 drops top-level qualifiers). Diagnostics 0xE041тАУ0xE044 (S_AutoRequiresSingleDeclarator / S_AutoRequiresPlainIdentifier / S_AutoRequiresInitializer / S_AutoInferenceInvalid) ALL UNSUPPRESSABLE (the тШЕC3 backfill seam: Pass 2's decl arm backfills `rec.type = initTy` for any unresolved declarator-mode symbol тАФ a suppressed reject would silently adopt it; closed-table 96тЖТ100 + named membership pins). hirLowering rows тЖТ VarDecl (zero HIR change тАФ lowerVarLikeInto reads rec->type). Tests: 16 unit pins (incl. the Pass-1.5-VISIBILITY `typeof(x) y;` discriminator + the >4096 replay pin + C89 `auto int x;` + constexpr-auto + volatile-strip тАФ 3 pin groups verified red-on-arm-disable) + 5 diagnostic goldens + the `auto_type_inference` 4-target runnable example (exit 42 debug AND release; width/deref/array-decay/fn-call/for/static/constexpr/braced/visibility all runtime-witnessed) + battery `c23_auto_type` GREEN (130/133). Deferrals opened: [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-QUALIFIED]], [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-DERIVED-DECLARATOR]], [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-FILE-SCOPE]]. | (closed) | plan-23 FC17.5; `c-subset.lang.json` (grammar + 2 decl rows + 2 hirLowering rows) + `semantic_config.hpp` (2 DeclarationRule fields) + `grammar_schema_json.cpp` (loader relax + 3 cross-checks) + `semantic_analyzer.cpp` (`resolveAutoInferredDeclaration` + the `typeLiteralIfAny` extraction + the resolveDeclTypes arm) + `parse_diagnostic.{hpp,cpp}` (0xE041тАУ0xE044) + `unsuppressable_codes.cpp` (+4 тЖТ 100) |
| `D-CSUBSET-AUTO-QUALIFIED` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-10 by [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE]]).** `const auto x = 5;` / `auto const x = 5;` / `volatile auto тАж` тАФ a QUALIFIED inference declaration (C23 6.7.9 allows the type-qualifier composition) is a LOUD PARSE ERROR today (the headless rule has no head-qualifier slot; `const`/`volatile` are not localDeclSpecifiers) тАФ pinned loud by `AutoFileScopeAndQualifiedStayLoudParseErrors`, never silent. **Trigger:** a real TU declares a qualified auto object. Needs a qualifier slot on the inference rule + the const/volatile symbol marking WITHOUT re-opening the c58 initializer-const-leak (the row deliberately declares NO constMarker/volatileMarker тАФ the headless fallback scan spans the initializer). Priority: normal. | Admit head qualifiers on the inference form; mark the symbol const/volatile from the PREFIX slot only (never the whole-decl scan). | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE]]; the c58 `D-CSUBSET-INITIALIZER-CONST-TOKEN-LEAK` scan scoping |
| `D-CSUBSET-AUTO-DERIVED-DECLARATOR` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-10 by [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE]]).** `auto *p = &x;` / `auto a[] = тАж` / parenthesized declarators тАФ the WG14 v2-paper DERIVED-declarator extension to 6.7.9 (strict C23 requires a plain identifier). Fails loud `S_AutoRequiresPlainIdentifier` (0xE042; golden + unit pins incl. the `auto f(void);` prototype shape) тАФ a strict SUBSET of C23, never a conformance regression. **Trigger:** a real TU uses the extension (GCC/Clang accept some forms). Needs per-layer inference (unify the declarator fold with the inferred base). Priority: low. | Fold the inferred base through the declarator's pointer/array layers instead of rejecting non-plain shapes. | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE]] |
| `D-CSUBSET-AUTO-FILE-SCOPE` | тП│ **GATED (opened 2026-07-10 by [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE]], the design-audit's M2 тАФ deferring is admissible, leaving UNNAMED is not).** C23 ┬з6.7.9 (N3007) ALLOWS file-scope `auto g = 42;`; DSS keeps the pre-existing LOUD P0009 parse reject (the top-level grammar has no inference form) тАФ pinned loud by `AutoFileScopeAndQualifiedStayLoudParseErrors`. Block scope + for-init only this cycle. **Trigger:** a real TU declares a file-scope auto object. Needs a topLevel inference rule (the topLevelDecl ordering analysis тАФ its OWN тШЕC2-style order audit against the >4096-token file-scope static-init class) + tentative-definition interaction (6.9.2 тАФ an inference decl always has an initializer, so it is always a real definition). Priority: normal. | Mirror the block-scope rule at file scope with its own branch-order audit + linkage/tentative rows. | opened-by [[D-CSUBSET-AUTO-TYPE-INFERENCE]] |
